### jvine — 2024-12-05T05:15:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussie-thyroid-questions.29925/post-343305>

Hi everyone, Just posting regarding my wife who has recently had some blood tests done and her Thyroid seems to be concerning her doctor. Neither of us trust centralised medical approaches or advise. Im basically wanting to know which blogs and information of Jacks is best to read and learn everything I can about thyroid, there are many that mention thyroid so not sure which ones are best to understand the full picture and solutions. Our background is we live in NSW Australia (so no points for guessing why we are having problems). We have been following Jacks podcasts and website for about 6 months and have become, sunrise, light, water, magnetism, bitcoin, grounding savages. We have completely transformed our family life and been at the beach every morning sunrise without fail with the kids for a 1-2 months now, we all drink fluoride / bromine free RO or High Lat water. I have told her to up her seafood intake. She is grounding each morning at the beach and trying to do another 5 mins or so later in the day. We are also seeking the sun as often as we can. My best guess so far is that we obviously continue the path we are on and she needs to up the seafood? Happy for any other thoughts or if anyone can nudge me in the right direction for information. The centralised doctors next steps are a scan/injecting dye then a following up blood test in 6 week...Then no doubt pharmaceuticals which we do not want to do.

My wife's Thyroid labs came back as follows:
TSH - 0.06 mIU / L (very low)
FT4 - 17 pmol / L (within limits)
FT3 - 6.3 pool / L (within limits)
Anti Thyroglobulin Abs - 4.0 IU / mL (within limits)
Anti Thyrodial Peroxidase Abs 88 IU / mL (elevated)

She has been experiencing temperature issues (often cold), dizziness, fatigue, brain fog / fuzzy in the head, low weight (difficulty putting on weight), low libido  changes in menstrual cycle and low energy.

Thanks Everyone

Lots of love from Downunder

### Magpie — 2024-12-05T10:52:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussie-thyroid-questions.29925/post-343313>

I would be wondering about her workplace. Likely excessive exposure to blue light from screens. You have not mentioned if she has adressed blue light toxicity. Too much inside time behind glass. Too much time in front of a computer monitor radiating blue light onto the thyroid?

I would be cautious with supplementing iodine because she has subclinical hyperthyroidism. At this stage, it is difficult to deterine if this is Hashimotos or Grave's disease. I would also be cautious with high iodine foods like seaweed. Have fish and seafood but don't go overboard.
Just follow a good epipaleoRx type diet.

Agree here. EMF and blue light is huge. Cover up the thyroid at night especially if watching tv. I have issues and stopped watching tv at night and wear a scarf around my neck. It is hard at first but then you never want to go back to TV. Even if it is cold outside, I always make sure my thyroid is exposed to the sun especially sunrise and sunset. I am 59 and have seen some improvement after many years of low thyroid hormones. I am also on desiccated thyroid replacement therapy but have some physical issues (surgery when I was 5) that may have caused scar tissue around my thyroid. Tried everything else first. I tried going without HRT for three years but thyroid numbers did not budge. I recommend a consult with Dr. Kruse and then maybe talking to Dr. Elizabeth Bright who does telemedicine and works specifically on thyroid. She believes in all of Jack's recommendations as well. ### Magpie — 2024-12-17T08:38:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aussie-thyroid-questions.29925/post-344016>

I worry about my sister's thyroid while she works as a nurse in GP land. Sitting in front of screens all day and not being provided with protection such as Iris mini pro or a physical barrier. I suggested that she could put the face masks to good use and just wear it around her neck so that it is covering the thyroid. I have some lovely black cotton face masks from Bonds. If you wore one covering your thyroid while you work in front of a screen, perhaps you will look like its there to be handy to pull up onto your face as needed. I think that you could get away with it. Otherwise its skivies at dawn and you need to take care to style your outfits so that you don't look like a member of the Wiggles!

I will mention that my mother developed Hashimoto's in around 1994. She had no screen exposure. There were no phones, no computer. She made my university studies hellish because she refused to let me have a computer to write my essays (it was compulsorary to submit essays that had been wordprocessed). There may have been too much inside time behind glass. But as teachers they had to do yard duty at lunch time. And she always took the family dog for a walk in the mornings, so had early morning sunshine.

But if I explore her health problems a little more, must have been very sunlight deficient because she had chronic plantar fasciitis.

Her allergist and immunologist (seen for food sensitivites and allergic rhinitis) provided the opinion that she was at higher risk of Hashimoto's because she was exposed to a lot of DDT as a child while living in a country trying to tackle malaria. While I see our light environment as the biggest health threat, there are biochemical toxins in the environment which cause biological damage. Clean up the environment in all ways possible. Diet, air, nnEMF (this includes artificial light), toiletries, cleaning products. Examine everything in your life and discard everything that is unnatural. And address the fact that all the water coming out of the taps and falling from the sky is too high in deuterium. When Jack started taking about the deuterium issue in the Australian water cycle, he advised that it was more important to drink lower deuterium waters that to eat organic food. Someone like me just ran with it and started drinking low deuterium waters (most often European spring water) and continued to eat organic food. But this has a massive impact on your social life so many people are going to hesitate to do it. Or do it half assed. I don't go to a cafe and have anthing to drink apart from an imported mineral water. Because of the deuterium and the fluoride.
It's also important to look at lowering the deuterium load in your diet. MInimise the high deuterium foods.

It would be interesting to know if your wife's longer term diet was historically low in nutrients like iodine and selenium. What sources of iodine have been in her diet over the long term.

So everytime I left the house for work, I carried a 1 litre thermos of herbal tea along with insulated bottles of water. I took all of my own meals. I hardly ever ate out. It was a lot of work. My idea of take away was buying a kilo of prawns off a trawler. Living in Australia is like living on Mars in many ways. And that is just the biological side.

# Thread: Watching Jack's predictions come through in real time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watching-jacks-predictions-come-through-in-real-time.29926/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Magpie — 2024-12-05T10:35:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/watching-jacks-predictions-come-through-in-real-time.29926/post-343312>

Surviving the year 2024 is an enormous achievement. I went to two funerals this year for beautiful women who were both colleagues and friends. One died in her mid 50s of pancreatic cancer leaving behind her husband and two teenage children. She taught me so many things about obstetrics and gynaecology when I was a first year medical student on elective. I learned how to assist a caesarian section by assisting her. And I know how to examine a woman for preeclampsia. She was both smart and funny and had an incredibly kind and caring approach to all the women she looked after. She also made a point of finding new things to teach me when she was one of the consultant OBGYNs responsible for me during my 4th year O&G rotation. She died far too early and her death was a preventable tragedy. It makes me angry. No one should be dying of pancreatic cancer in their 50s. I think that SV40 was the invisible smoking gun.

My other colleague/friend died of a brain tumour. She never had the usual headaches. It was only discovered because she developed a patch of visual loss a scotoma. The tumour was in the visual cortex. My colleague Jack says it would have been caused by jump conduction. She had stopped working to avoid having any COVID vaccinations which were mandated. She would have liked me to join her practice and I would have except that I noticed a cell phone tower sitting above the strip of shops where her "Doctor shop" was located. She lasted only four months after diagnosis. My last memory of her was meeting her at the fish counter in a favourite small independent supermarket where she told me she was hosting a lady from the countryside in her home while the lady sought care for cancer. I spent 6 weekends learning Emmett Technique this year because Kathy informed me it existed. She was a very popular integrative GP that included acupuncture and Biobalance (targeted nutritional supplements for mental health) in her repertoire and my mum was one of her patients. We had great fun sitting in my family room in 2020 doing our Biobalance refresher via Zoom (Biobalance courses were once upon a time face to face at a nice hotel on the Gold Coast). And attending performances of the Queensland Medical Orchestra became a regular occasion to catch up. She left behind her son, daughter in law and two grandchildren. No one should by dying at age 69 of a brain tumour.

The last death I am grieving for this year is the death of my beloved dog Poppy. My poor dog had three cancers. A gastrointestinal stromal tumour of the jejeunum (GIST) almost caused her to bleed out in early 2022, but she survived the surgery to remove the tumour and did not have any recurrence of the GIST. However she had required a blood transfusion so had anaphylaxis when she was given antivenin for tick paralysis in October 2022. (Australia is a very dangerous place for dogs as they did not evolve here). She survived the tick paralysis due to brilliant ICU care. She had been very well and continued to enjoy long early morning walks on the beach even though she looked terrible with her fur shaved every 3 months for review ultrasounds for surveillance because of the GIST. I started worrying about her in September 2023, because she was breathing too quickly at rest on a cool Sunday evening after I had returned from a weekend conference. Emergency vet said to change her diet from raw to cooked. She became worse despite having her diet changed to lightly cooked Lyka. I noticed muscle wasting and progressive worsening of exercise tolerance. After several visits to the vet the cause was only found after I asked for her thyroid function to be checked. It was very very low, but in dogs this can be caused by something else. Her adrenals were enlarged on ultrasound and blood tests confirmed Cushings disease. She was put on medication to suppress the adrenal function.

Over Easter, an abdominal wall tumour that had not been there 8 months prior started bleeding so I took her to the emergency vet. The vet said that the tumour was most likely a malignant haemangiosarcoma due to the way it was behaving. There was induration surrounding the lesion. The plan was for the tumour to be surgically removed the following day. However the vet rang me in the morning to say that she was showing neurological signs and the vet nurses that knew her so well were worried about her behaviour. They requested to do a CT of her head which showed a large pituitary adenoma which was behaving like a space occupying brain tumour causing the neurological signs. I decided to take her home and have a lovely local vet do a home visit for euthanasia. She was suffering too much for me to be selfish and have the tumour treated with radiotherapy which would have required an anaesthetic with each round. Poppy would have turned 13 in May. But no-one's pet dog should have had three malignant tumours. The modern world is a very toxic place.

I have now removed myself from the environment that caused the early death of my dog and my lovely friends. We all had a wonderful time at the Giving Thanks event. And I would like to give thanks to my colleague Dr Jack for his wisdom and insightful analysisis of all of the problems that he has identified in our modern world. I have never gone live on a pow wow to ask questions about myself. I occasionally ask questions about things relating to my patients but Christy tends to misinterpret the questions I have her ask. (No my doctor wasn't suggesting that I take ozempic. I was getting sick of my patients demanding it and wanted to clarify Jack's concerns regarding this pharmaceutical)

I have been following Jack's work since 2011 ( I know because I remember when he was kicked of the cruise that time). I mostly listened to podcasts but joined Patreon when he started the blog there. I finally joined as a member in 2019, because I decided that it would be nice to be able to post and discuss things on the health practioners forum. Sometimes I wondered why I bothered because I kept hearing him berating members for not moving and it wasn't fun listening. Informative but not fun.

Jack was telling us some years ago that our pets are like canaries in the coal mine indicating that there are invisible dangers in our environment. He suggested that we talk to older vets in the community to ask what unusual things they are seeing or if they are seeing more of certain conditions. The GP vet I took my dogs to just retired at age 82. He described seeing more unusual skin tumours but I'm not a vet and they were not familiar pathologies for me. I imagine that over his career the reasons for surgical intervention changed. Dr Feeney was an exceptional surgeon. He served the small beachside pocket community where my mom and my step dad lived. One of the neighbours dogs, a schnauzer, was attacked by an unknown dog. She was taken to Dr Feeney who said that she was very badly injured and he would have a go operating but did not think she would survive. Saffy did survive because Dr Feeney trained in an era when a GP vet was expected to be a good surgeon. Now pets are more likely to need surgery for cancer. Dr Feeney was excising the skin tumours himself.

The emergency vet that I took my dog to was co-located with a state of the art oncology clinic for pets. They offer chemotherapy and radiotherapy as well as advanced surgery for pets with cancer. I have met many people with their pets in the waiting room while I waited for Poppy to have her ultrasounds or waited to pick her up after an admission. A Veterinary oncology clinic such as this one did not exist in the past. Such a business model was not viable in the past because there were not so many pets developing cancer like they do now.

Please do not delude yourself into thinking that things are OK in your current environment. Moving for most people is very inconvenient, but so is a terminal illness. Some podcasters seem to think that Jack is joking when he suggests people purchase term life insurance if they elect not to move. He is not joking, he is providing some sound financial advice. I decided that I was not going to stay in the bad environment waiting for a malignancy to eventuate.

If you consider how poor my environment was, I should probably have died of some type of cancer already. But I have an advantage that many of you do not. The ovum that I originated from came into existance at latitude 1.290270. My mother and grandmother were born at latitude 1.290270. And my great grandmother was born at 5.285153. Unfortunately this trend was broken. I was born at latitude -37.840935 because my mother thought this was a good place to go for her tertiary education.

My dog and I slept in a bedroom that was shielded with several layers of Y shield paint and had an EMF blocking film on the windows. The nnEMF in the bedroom measured low. I had hardwired internet with absolutely no wifi. No smart devices. I kept the electrical circuits to the bedroom off. But we lived too close to an international airport and there were enough cell phone towers in the vincinity for the building biologist to comment on the numbers. The neighbourhood we lived in was not a high density neighbourhood as it had lots of parkland due to flood plains and was near an private Catholic boys school with enormous grounds (13 rugby fields a 9 hole golf course and tracts of remnant untouched forrest along with acres of marginal swamp land.). But sometimes I had ridiculous leg cramps at home despite taking good quantities of magnesium. I think this was from the neighbours doing something like switching on one of their smart devices or a wifi modem

I had a liver resection 20 years ago for what turned out to be a hepatic cystadenoma. Rare but considered to be pre-cancerous and was not even listed in my pathology book when the intern came round to visit me in my hospital bed with the histopathology results. It was case reports only for hepatic cystadenoma. So I have periodic US. The one done in October reported mild hepatic steatosis. It must be the blue light and spending too much time in an office with windows that don't open. Because I actually managed to lose a good amout of weight and do a good leptin reset in May. I had the pleasure of meeting Johan when I was in Oslo in September. He was emphasising that no matter how much you try to spend time outside it is not enough to be enough to mitigate against the toxic blue light. Even though my lunch breaks were taken on the beach in minimal clothing.

I never posted on the public forum until recently. I didn't feel safe to do so, because the government where I lived is the opposite of benign. At the clinic where I worked, we had a trainee registrar for about three weeks before he was suspended from practice for speaking out against the COVID vaccine mandates. If I had prescribed hydroxychloroquin for a COVID infection, I faced a 6 month jail sentence. I would have been suspended if I prescribed ivermectin. We were only be allowed to prescribe ivermectin for scabies after topical treatments had failed. We were not even permitted to suggest anything natural like vitamin C for COVID infection. I was still self employed so I didn't have an employer dictating how I practiced. But socialised medicine means that the government threatens to audit you if they notice too much deviation from the norm in your practice.

I like to learn from other people's mistakes, because it means that I am less likely to need to make those mistakes myself.

Don't keep living in a toxic environment and hoping that things will be ok. They won't. I watched Jack's predictions come through in real time.

# Thread: Curly Hair Help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-06T00:02:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/post-343356>

Hi! I’ve been trying really hard to find the optimal hair care routine for my curly/wavy hair. I’d love tips on curly hair and favorite way to wash & style.

I’m only wearing makeup once a month these days and embracing my natural beauty more and more. Loving this part of my journey.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-12-06T01:00:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/post-343359>

You can just use coconut oil about a teaspoon to damp hair.. I do that. I was taught this trick by someone in the beauty business

As for washing. You can either try to go without washing it for six to seven weeks , only water no shampoo or use the shampoo bars that are made with decent ingredients

I happen to have long wavy curly hair. I tend to over brush it. It's worth just leaving it naturally...

### Sirena — 2024-12-06T06:03:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/post-343377>

Bask in your curls. Many women pay big bucks for curls. If you want to tame your curls a bit, try a few hairstyles until you find one that gives you power. Perhaps decorate or braid your hair with colorful ribbons or flowers. Play around.

### eaandkw — 2024-12-07T04:37:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/post-343475>

Katherine Sewing on YT talks about her curly hair routine and seems relatively natural.

### [Katherine Sewing](https://www.youtube.com/@KatherineSewing)

Hey! I'm Katherine, and I create historical, natural hair care videos, and historical hand-made fashion videos. I share the tips that I have personally used to grow my hair from chin to hip length, as well as how I sew my historical, vintage-inspired wardrobe.

 www.youtube.com

### Pratima — 2024-12-07T04:53:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curly-hair-help.29930/post-343478>

>
> You can just use coconut oil about a teaspoon to damp hair.. I do that. I was taught this trick by someone in the beauty business
>
> As for washing. You can either try to go without washing it for six to seven weeks , only water no shampoo or use the shampoo bars that are made with decent ingredients
>
> I happen to have long wavy curly hair. I tend to over brush it. It's worth just leaving it naturally...

Also got curly hair.......ACV and water leave-in every so often.......no washing......

# Thread: Congenital central hypoventilation syndrome
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-central-hypoventilation-syndrome.29936/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-06T21:50:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-central-hypoventilation-syndrome.29936/post-343454>

Uncle Jack… I’m listening to your podcast with Alexis Cowan and you mentioned central sleep apnea. My daughter, Parker, has CCHS and I am so curious about this issue.

The pulmonologists and “experts” have always said they have no drive to breathe and it’s worse depending on mutation. She is the most mild mutation (20/25) and breathes just fine while awake. It’s only when she’s sleeping.

I’ve seen firsthand that environment matters because there are so many different phenotypes within the same genotype of these kids. But to what degree could this be an epigenetic condition?

I’m not expecting miracles but I do want to understand this better. Would a consultation be appropriate for this to simply understand what we could leverage here?

I’m working my way through the blogs!

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-07T02:25:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-central-hypoventilation-syndrome.29936/post-343464>

Blue light destroys FGF family of genes and desmin is one of the family members.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-07T02:25:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-central-hypoventilation-syndrome.29936/post-343465>

### [Desmin gene expression is not ubiquitous in all upper airway myofibers and the pattern differs between healthy and sleep apnea subjects - European Journal of Medical Research](https://eurjmedres.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s40001-024-01812-9)

Background Desmin is a major cytoskeletal protein considered ubiquitous in mature muscle fibers. However, we earlier reported that a subgroup of muscle fibers in the soft palate of healthy subjects and obstructive sleep apnea patients (OSA) lacked immunoexpression for desmin. This raised the...

 eurjmedres.biomedcentral.com

### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-07T03:41:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-central-hypoventilation-syndrome.29936/post-343474>

Thank you for your reply. I’ll read this.

I had a suspicion I caused this from working in family practice for 40 hours a week. Under blue lights and staring at computers. Everyone has always assured me I didn’t do anything to cause it but I knew nature doesn’t make mistakes intuitively.

That’s a tough pill to swallow but I guess there are millions of women who could commiserate with me.

We’ve made significant strides in andopting a circadian lifestyle and now I have a 5 year old in short sleeves in 20 degree weather. Kids barefoot. Swinging from trees. All because of you. Because I was reminded of nature and her beauty and comfort. I see every sunrise and am in awe of the beauty. We sit and watch nearly every sunset and talk about what we are grateful for. We drive nearly every weekend to go hiking in nature.

The gift you’ve given me is priceless even though the cost of arriving to find it has been steep. I’m grateful for your wisdom and dedication to helping others.

### Rachel Martin — 2025-01-19T16:10:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/congenital-central-hypoventilation-syndrome.29936/post-345386>

I have a follow up question.

She has a mutation in the phox2b gene that helps develop the nervous system. So it’s formed and it’s wrong - she doesn’t sense CO2 changes while sleeping.

Is this a pure genetic mutation (you’ve mentioned CF and other mutations in your Breedlove podcast) and it is what it is?

I no longer necessarily believe them when they say there’s nothing that can be done. But perhaps there isn’t anything… other than lifelong ventilator support while sleeping.

Today I give you a gift. It is my mitochondriac anniversary on April 2nd. I post this for my Mom. She used to call me at this time every year. Now i am calling you, my tribe. It is time to wake up.

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

# Thread: Thoughts on light & darkness deficiency
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-light-darkness-deficiency.29942/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-08T15:48:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-light-darkness-deficiency.29942/post-343522>

I’ve been reading the blogs (thanks to whoever posted the study guides) and binge listening on airplane mode to all the podcasts I can get my hands on..

Here are my musings-

We are indoors with shitty lighting and basically starved of essential light nutrients (like UV light, red light, infrared light). We are also bingeing on blue light, which I’ve begun referring to as the white food of the light world.

At the same time, we are also deficient in darkness. Lighting up the night with our artificial sun thinking we are so clever.

And we are overdoing it with carbs. Cozy all the time at 68-70 degrees.

Our bodies are stuck in eternal summer and mismatched with our environment. It’s so obvious once it really hit me. It explains everything.

I can appreciate in podcast 24 with Alexis how Uncle Jack says that light is more important than water and magnetism (not that those aren’t important).

The steps are brutally simple. The lifestyle changes though are tough with people who (as you’ve mentioned before) just simply can’t be inconvenienced. It’s a damn shame.

I can’t believe I was convinced that it was food and exercise at first. Then I was convinced it was stress. Now I know that all plays a role but sunlight is king. Which honestly is refreshing because it FEELS good to be in the sun. This aligns with my intuition that “it shouldn’t be this hard” when I was formerly trying to use elimination diets, gut protocols, and other nonsense to help myself and patients.

Nothing has helped me more than simply getting the fuck outdoors.

### Tyler Hanson — 2024-12-08T22:45:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dengue-fever-and-ivermectin.29944/post-343554>

Hello!

Down here in the tropics for the winter.

Currently in Mexico and next month headed to El Salvador!

Heard lots of talk about Dengue Fever in Mexico, my wife got a bunch of mosquito bites a few days ago and is coming down with some ill feelings.

I've heard that Ivermectin is both a good prevenativive and could be helpful if someone has it?

Ya'll know anything about this? I've searched the forums and blogs but couldn't find anything.

Thanks,

Tyler

### Marshall_Sponder — 2024-12-09T03:36:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dengue-fever-and-ivermectin.29944/post-343563>

> Hello!
>
> Down here in the tropics for the winter.

>

>
> Ya'll know anything about this? I've searched the forums and blogs but couldn't find anything.
>
> Thanks,
>
> Tyler

In Mexico, Ivermectin (IVM) and Hydroxychloroquine (HCQ) are inexpensive and easy to obtain. The dosage depends on body weight; typically, a 6 mg pill covers about 60 pounds. For instance, if you weigh 180 pounds, you'd take 3 pills. Some people recovering from COVID-19 may take 2 to 3 times that amount for a few days. There's plenty of information online about dosing, so I recommend looking into that for more guidance.

**[Now here we have this study, published in ARSH 2012 that clearly demonstrates that Ivermectin has clear antiviral properties against both HIV and Dengue Fever.](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3327999/)** You can’t make this up.

### The fact that Ivermectin is a KNOWN broad-spectrum antiviral by not only Fauci but the entire global pharmaceutical industry tells us in no uncertain terms that Ivermectin should have been IMMEDIATELY looked to as a possible treatment and prevention of any authentic and non-weaponized viral **outbreak**, but that its continued suppression and vilification points to habitual capital-level criminality predating the CoronaScam by decades, namely Fauci’s “Death by AZT” scam, and the WHO’s Dengvaxia crime in the Philippines, where criminal charges were filed.​

This also goes back to covering for BigPharma and the WHO and the disasterous Dengvaxia roll-out that killed children in the Philippines by – wait for it – causing an AMPLIFIED risk and hyper-immune reaction to Dengue Fever when encountered in the wild post-vaccination. Sound familiar?

Here’s an article from NPR – yes, National Public Radio, the hard-leftist American government-funded agit-prop machine from May 3, ARSH 2019. Read every word of this. **Key excerpts below:**

[https://www.npr.org/sections/goatsa...unch-has-deadly-repercussions?t=1630335896583](https://www.npr.org/sections/goatsandsoda/2019/05/03/719037789/botched-vaccine-launch-has-deadly-repercussions?t=1630335896583)

> The French pharmaceutical company Sanofi Pasteur spent 20 years — and about $2 billion — to develop Dengvaxia. The company tested it in several large trials with more than 30,000 kids globally and published the results in the prestigious New England Journal of Medicine.

> But halfway around the world from the Philippines, in a Washington, D.C., suburb, one scientist was worried about the new vaccine.

> “When I read the New England Journal article, I almost fell out of my chair,” says Dr. Scott Halstead, who has studied dengue for more than 50 years with the U.S. military. When Halstead looked at the vaccine’s safety data in the clinical trial, he knew right away there was a problem.

> For some children, the vaccine didn’t seem to work. In fact, Halstead says, it appeared to be harmful. When those kids caught dengue after being vaccinated, the vaccine appeared to worsen the disease in some instances. Specifically, for children who had never been exposed to dengue, the vaccine seemed to increase the risk of a deadly complication called plasma leakage syndrome, in which blood vessels start to leak the yellow fluid of the blood.

> (…)

> The increased risk seems small. The vaccine raises the risk of hospitalization after a dengue infection from about 1.1% to 1.6%, the follow-up study from Sanofi found. So out of 1 million kids in the Philippines, the vaccine would cause about 1,000 to be hospitalized over five years, Sanofi estimated. (On the other hand, the vaccine would prevent about 12,000 hospitalizations for a new dengue infection in children who have had a prior dengue infection during this same time period.)

> **But in the world of vaccines, that’s not an acceptable risk. A risk needs to be exceedingly small to be tolerated….**

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-09T15:19:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dengue-fever-and-ivermectin.29944/post-343579>

Because of what I posted before.......if you have a turbo cancer and you have exposure to SV40 the first line treatment should be Ivermectin and maybe add Fenbendazole. Dr. Makis has put out some good info on this combo and virally induced cancers.

We will never see clinical studies because Ivermectin for cancer, HIV, or Dengue because it is off patent and cheap.

Merck, which used to have a patent on Ivermectin, has partnered with Moderna on mRNA Cancer Vaccines, estimated to cost 400,000 GBP per treatment. So the incentive is built in to not study IVERMECTIN on anything when the DoD and BigHarma are pushing expansion of the mRNA platform. For this reason everyone who has been diagnosed with a turbo cancer, Dengue, even HIV should consider this before you do anything else. Protease inhibitors work well for HIV so one has to discuss this with patients during informed consent discussions.

Ivermectin studies on mice include: Pancreatic cancer, Breast cancer, Colon cancer, glioblastoma, glioma and leukemia.

I have not seen IVERMECTIN studies on Lymphoma, Testicular Cancer, Sarcomas but that does not mean one should not consider it.

IVERMECTIN acts on Cancer mainly by inhibiting signaling pathways involved in cancer proliferation (Akt, Wnt, mTOR) and by inhibiting CANCER STEM CELLS.

More good news with this drug: it’s practically impossible to overdose on ivermectin, as it has a half-life of 18 hours and it’s cleared from your body within 2 days.

Fenbendazole was discovered completely accidentally. It was back in 2017, a gentleman in Oklahoma, [Joe Tippens](https://forbiddenknowledgetv.net/a-cheap-cure-for-cancer-that-will-probably-disappear/), a gentleman in the 60s, I believe, was diagnosed with terminal cancer, Stage 4 small cell lung cancer, and he was sent home to die by his doctors, and he had a veterinary friend of his who said, “Listen, there’s a dog medicine, dog dewormer medicine, that was discovered accidentally to have very strong anti-cancer properties. Why don’t you try it? You have nothing to lose.”

And he put together a protocol with this fenbendazole medicine and also with [curcumin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curcumin), [CBD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannabidiol) oil, vitamin E, and he cured his terminal cancer, that he had basically less than 1% chance of surviving. And so that was back in 2017, and so this has been known for a few years, now.

There is a Facebook group called the Fenbendazole Cancer Support Group. It has a hundred and ten thousand members. A lot of them are cancer patients, a lot of some of them are family members of cancer patients, so this is a large community, and it works.

In fact, there was a group of Stanford University medical researchers who were so impressed by fenbendazole that they found three patients who had taken fenbendazole, Stage 4 cancer patients, who took fenbendazole, cured their Stage 4 cancers after they had failed every chemotherapy regimen known to mankind, and they published this case series. Now what’s funny is that they were not able to recommend fenbendazole to these patients, because it’s not FDA-approved.

Now, there is an FDA-approved version of it called “mebendazole”, which is virtually identical. Unfortunately, it goes by the brand name of [“Vermox”](https://www.webmd.com/drugs/2/drug-7087/vermox-oral/details). It’s much more expensive than fenbendazole, but you can now get generic versions of both for, you know, a dollar a pill.

# Thread: Newish Member in Alaska

### Rmyfld — 2024-12-09T03:39:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newish-member-in-alaska.29946/post-343564>

Hi, my name is Rebekah and I recently moved to Kasilof, Alaska 3 years ago after being in Texas all my life. I became a member 3 months ago and have been learning a TON. I’ve been an Esthetician for 17 years and have made an incredibly successful business in Texas and now in Alaska. I’m trying to figure out how to navigate this new found knowledge into my practice. I have changed all my light bulbs out in my spa and home, am wearing Lucia Eyes blue light blocking glasses and have been eating like a great white shark. My question is: Is there anyway possible to build my solar callus here until I’m able to buy a second house in El Salvador? I’ve been investing in bitcoin too! On sunny days I get out in my Kiniki swim suit and lay out for maybe 30 min because it’s soo cold. I’m really trying everything! I have been tanning a little bit in tanning beds but all the bulbs are mainly UVA/Red Light even when they’re advertised as Vitamin D beds. I just ordered the Sperti (suggested by Dr. Alexis Jazmyn) and I get my face and eyes in the AM and PM sunlight but can’t get my naked body in it as it’s way too freaking cold. I’m in excellent health but my 2 boys and husband have some health problems and I’m trying to get them all on board. Any alternative suggestions? I really love it here in Alaska and am trying to figure out a way to stay here (at least as my home base) until I can buy a second house! Do you think there would be opportunities for waxing/lashing people in El Salvador? I’m probably going to be quitting doing skin care that that my eyes are opened to the truth unless I can figure out a way to do it that’s healthy. Thanks for reading!

### AKJOHN — 2024-12-09T07:10:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newish-member-in-alaska.29946/post-343566>

Many welcomes, look at a few of my posts for some Alaska tips. I have been in AK since 1979. I am in Anchorage. I do CT every other day. I can now do 20 minutes of less than 50 degrees. I also do red light and near infrared everyday. I am 66 , and one of the few Alaskans that are in about perfect health. I take no medications and I wake up feeling great every day. After CT and Light therapy, I feel even better. Make sure to eat lots of Alaska fish. It's one of the best things we have going for us with the high DHA content. I also do grounding. It's great to have a grounding pad feeding you electrons throughout the day. We take at least one trip a year to get as much sun as we can, or I tell my wife we just wasted a trip. I am living proof that good health is possible in the far North, and I only first heard of and started dong Jack's tips since about July.
ps I am a Sons of Texas as my family members were massacred at Goliad.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### René Løweneck — 2024-12-11T09:32:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emitted-blue-light-from-led%C2%B4s.29947/post-343684>

Thank you for bringing this article, Jack. It did not answer any of my questions though, so I have been poking a little around myself and got the answers ;-)

I found a lot of confirmed cases of emitted blue light poisening, allthough it´s not officially defined as that, of course ;-) Oficially it´s defined as (sun)light deficiency and in my part of the world, called winter depression and in your part of the world, it´s called Seasonal affective disorder (SAD). Even that I think it has always been like that, I think that the disorder is a lot more widespread nowadays, due to the (forced) use of LED´s. Even though this is not proving, that emitted blue light is adding up, inside our bodies, it proves, that we need workarounds, when the sun is´nt an option.

That leads me to my next finding. The emitted blue light from LED´s, is 458-460 Nm. So I made a test yesterday, where I tried my blue light detox on, sort of speak ;-)
And it went well beyond my expectations. I felt nearly as good, as the day before yesterday, when the sun showed up, for a couple of hours
However, I can´t follow up on the tests for the next days or week, because I´m treating my dog´s Pyometra.

### rk257 — 2024-12-11T15:09:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emitted-blue-light-from-led%C2%B4s.29947/post-343695>

I used to work in a lab which used primarily blue lasers (~460nm.) Lots of time spent staring at the intense light reflected from paper and other stuff for calibration etc... really should have worn those safety glasses, looking back.

Definitely noticed a fuzziness to my vision after a particularly intense week of calibrations. Eye gymnastics helped to bring them back in line but I'm unsure what the effect on my health was.

It had been commented that all of the principle investigators looked older than their years would suggest, seems likely that all the time spent absolutely _bathed_ in EMF amongst all the lab equipment and power coils/electromagnets and staring at blue light takes its toll

# Thread: Hi everyone.  This is my mom Luba (Russian for love) 11 months after a metastatic SCLC to her brain.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone-this-is-my-mom-luba-russian-for-love-11-months-after-a-metastatic-sclc-to-her-brain.29948/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jon Hinman MD DVM — 2024-12-09T19:57:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-everyone-this-is-my-mom-luba-russian-for-love-11-months-after-a-metastatic-sclc-to-her-brain.29948/post-343591>

Hi everyone. Jon Hinman here. This avatar includes my mom in 2009, 11 months after her craniotomy for a plum-sized metastatic SCLC. Full brain radiation was strongly recommended. Radiation dementia took her before her heart ceased to beat. Even with tools such as being a veterinarian later MD anesthesiologist, pain doc I wasn't equipped to navigate this for her. It is one of many forces guiding me to do a better job in helping people. So, I continue to evolve, study, learn, try new and old things. So, here I am diving into Jack's Universe with an enthusiastic mind and heart.

### [Animal Husbandry](https://www.mvmd.com/animal-husbandry)

 www.mvmd.com

I've been following MVMD since early March 2020 as I was highly interested in the success of ivermectin to treat Covid. At the time they were trying to figure out funding and developing their Quiksol technology for delivering ivermectin for people and animals. I think they ran into a lot of "red tape" of where they could trial and it morphed into animal husbandry in India for development.

I think one of their original intents was potential for treating certain types of cancer. [https://www.globenewswire.com/news-...-on-Three-Variants-Tested-With-Ivectosol.html](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2021/05/18/2231755/0/en/Mountain-Valley-MD-Receives-Successful-Results-From-BSL-4-COVID-19-Clearance-Trial-on-Three-Variants-Tested-With-Ivectosol.html)

Interestingly, they have exposure to the cannabis and cbd/thc industry. Not sure if Kevin McKernan has heard of them before. # Thread: Questions from a Newcomer
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-a-newcomer.29949/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rk257 — 2024-12-09T21:09:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-a-newcomer.29949/post-343594>

Few questions after burning through the early blogs on EMF and CT:

\=> How does one measure if/to what extent you're hitting the Ancient Pathway without labs? (Plan to get some ASAP but now is not a good time)
\- My sleep seems more efficient, in the sense that I seem to wake up earlier naturally
\- However, I also feel like I'm "detoxing;" it's mentioned in CT6 that _"if you have been an eostrogen colletor (???) as a warm adapted hominid you will notice bumps as you adapt. Fear not. It soon shall pass."_ what does this look like and is it a signal I'm moving in the right direction? It seems that in the pathway we burn WAT, am I just releasing a load of toxins stored in fat tissue?

\=> If the leptin-melanocortin pathway is designed for cold-adapted mammals, does this mean we need to maintain a 12C internal temperature? If we just "fool" our systems into using this pathway when they're not actually that cold, then I would imagine we'd be running pathways for which we lack the "relative thermal stability" due to the ambient temp... presumably this is what the warm adapted mode is for, nature makes no mistakes, right?

\=> As a reasonably fit and healthy individual, I don't need to lose much weight. It's said in places that rice and potatoes can be eaten eventually. How strict with carbs do I need to be in winter? I'm tempted also to eat more cruciferous greens, it just feels healthy and they have iodine. Anything to watch here?

\=> Water... if anything I don't drink enough water, but I'm also very concious of fluoridation locally, are there good sources of water available in the UK?

Hope you guys don't mind the deluge of questions, figured I'd throw it at the wall and see what sticks

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-11T18:50:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-a-newcomer.29949/post-343714>

Are you symptoms worse or better......if they aren't then hire a nnEMF engineer before labbing up because labs won't point out the problem it will show evidence of the problem that you still have not addressed in the environment.

Symptoms are not about weight. They are about your N=1. I am not sure why everyone make that false equivalence.

### rk257 — 2024-12-11T19:23:47+0200

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=343714)

> Symptoms are not about weight. They are about your N=1. I am not sure why everyone make that false equivalence.

In any case, the "detox" symptoms have passed and I am working with more energy than I have with some time. Thanks Jack, you're on another level.

**Do you still recommend "HIIT with heavy weights" once leptin sensitivity is in evidence?
**Intense exercise seems to be a touchy subject around here.

and, from a more theoretical perspective:

**If the leptin-melanocortin pathway is designed for cold-adapted mammals, does this mean we need to maintain a 12C internal temperature? Otherwise do we not "fool" our enzymes into using a mode for which they lack thermal stability?**
(i.e. because the body temp is too high, even if the hypothalmus is signalling for it?)

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2024-12-12T01:26:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-a-newcomer.29949/post-343766>

The halogens (/ˈhælədʒən, ˈheɪ-, -loʊ-, -ˌdʒɛn/\[1\]\[2\]\[3\]) are a group in the periodic table consisting of six chemically related elements: fluorine (F), chlorine (Cl), bromine (Br), iodine (I), and the radioactive elements astatine (At) and tennessine (Ts), though

Fluoride is a halogen. Along with chlorine

The pf2 filter

Eliminates: Fluorine, Arsenic V and pre-oxidized Arsenic III, as well as other residual heavy metal ions

I was recommended Maps.me for offline navigation. It was was sold to a bigger group.
The original founders of maps.me now are offering a new open source alternative for offline navigation. Any experience w/ these?
I used libreoffice and openoffice instead of ms office. Those worked fine for me.

### Rusty — 2024-12-10T11:38:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-343621>

Perplexity for AI search (not open source but you can search without logging in)
Brave browser for Ad free Youtube (works on iPhone/Android/Mac/Windows)
Signal for messaging
Bitwarden for password manager

### paintballtao — 2024-12-11T12:02:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-343690>

you can look into the kickstarter brax3 phone

### pietermj — 2024-12-19T04:33:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-344102>

F-Droid is good. It indicates if any app contains undesired features (like tracking, ads, ...). Apps are compiled/build directly from source and reproducible builds are offered. This means that the source the app is built from actually does correspond to the source the developer claims it comes from. Use it in combination with [https://reports.exodus-privacy.eu.org/en/](https://reports.exodus-privacy.eu.org/en/) to check for app apk permissions and trackers.
But of course nothing beats installing linux on a mobile with hardware switches for wifi etc...
I use
\- firefox with ublock addin (yes you can install any addin on mobile firefox!) (not f-droid but fully open source) also addin allows neaarly ad free youtube and screenless continuous playback (or try revanced).
\- keepassxc on desktop and one of their approved apps on android (eg Keepass2android).
\- amethyst nostr client (over tor)
\- F-droid client over tor
\- syncthing-fork (keeping "backups")
\- unstoppable crypto wallet (bitcoin)
\- anysoft keyboard (slightly more privacy than built-in keyboards)
Ik ben blij hier ook eens iemand van België tegen te komen

### [x.com](https://x.com/pieter_mj_j)

 x.com

### Honeybadger — 2025-11-27T20:56:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-363289>

Aurora store works great as an alternative to google play.

### urko — 2025-11-28T10:05:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-363297>

Graphene OS is an easy to install operative system to google pixel phones, it gives you a free of google environment and lot of turn on/off features like mic, camera, etc...

### Michalis — 2025-11-28T10:53:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-source-alternatives-f-droid-etc.29950/post-363298>

e/OS is also de-googled

### Honeybadger — 2025-11-28T10:56:57+0200

> [urko said:](/goto/post?id=363297)

### Chirotheca — 2024-12-11T06:45:46+0200

Greetings from a norwegian biochemist, exiled in Thailand!
I was contacted through Jack Kruse's twitter account, and had someone(video of Jack) call me on telegram, posing as Jack. No sound, so we continued chatting. Very cleverly done using AI.

### Magpie — 2024-12-11T07:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-thailand-also-scam-warning.29956/post-343680>

Lovely to see you here. Make sure you go through the back catalogue of pow wows and hear what Jack says about SE Asia.

Yeah Jack doesn't contact members like that. He is just too hectically busy. He invites us to member events and we have the monthly pow wows. And the member events are more about meeting other members face to face and the presentations. It was was very enjoyable.

Was it like a deep fake video? They are starting to target doctors and using videos of clinicians and researchers being interviewed to produce some interesting deep fakes. It's just another form of advertising and the advertising industry has always involved lies.

Such as the academic journals being used to advertise pharmaceuticals with clinical trial reports that are full of lies that you can discover if you get your hands on their raw data and you have the statistical knowledge to interpret the data

Can you tell us did this scammer actually fake it well enough to provide some good mitochondriac style education?
What were they trying to scam.
I would have kept that scammer on the call for as long as possible allowing them to think they were getting somewhere just to keep them fruitlessly busy. As long as I could continue to feel the sun on my skin.

You can get very convincing deep fakes like the one reported in the screen shots below. Unfortunately these entities are never going to do deep fakes of an endocrinologist telling people to avoid dirty electricity, nnEMF and blue light because you can't monetise it:

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-13T18:28:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-for-good-enough-xmas-lights-garden-lights.29959/post-343848>

Not sure why you are measuring RF. The problem with lights - other than the artificial light itself - is electric fields. Any light that runs off AC power, that is not grounded - which is most/all “Christmas” lights - put off a significant 50/60Hz electric field. Did I see 99V/m and 128Vm - not good. Death by “Christmas” lights.

IF you want seasonal lights go for battery operated (red) ones.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-14T15:49:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-for-good-enough-xmas-lights-garden-lights.29959/post-343880>

I see an extension cord in the background of the photo. IF it is plugged in it will affect your electric field readings being that close. Also you holding the meter will affect the reading. Put the meter in the middle of a table or the floor, then put the lights down next to it ( do not touch the meter) and see what the reading is.

# Thread: PLASMA CAN BE UNCONTAINED IN NATURE
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasma-can-be-uncontained-in-nature.29960/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-11T22:47:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasma-can-be-uncontained-in-nature.29960/post-343743>

PLASMA CAN BE UNCONTAINED IN NATURE

Interesting paper out that challenge more beliefs: [https://phys.org/news/2017-11-stable-plasma-air.html#google\_vignette](https://phys.org/news/2017-11-stable-plasma-air.html#google_vignette)

### rk257 — 2024-12-12T01:20:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasma-can-be-uncontained-in-nature.29960/post-343765>

Sharp RF peaks with Lithium niobate is interesting, it's piezoelectric and has interesting optical properties.

Stared at it for a while, some sort of radial resonance between the plasma frequency and the piezoelectric response of the dielectric seems likely. 10MHz might be the first resonance clear of the water jet after which it slowly dies out...

Will revisit in the morning, I'm wondering what the inspiration behind this idea was, or what they were trying to do in the first place.

### rk257 — 2024-12-12T01:30:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasma-can-be-uncontained-in-nature.29960/post-343770>

Actually I think it's the other way around, the 36 MHz peak is the small purple ring (smaller radius, higher frequency, duh...) which would explain why both have matching (noisy) peaks around there... don't know about the other peaks, it's weird. Cool as hell though

### rk257 — 2024-12-12T21:23:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasma-can-be-uncontained-in-nature.29960/post-343800>

It's unlikely to involve plasma as above, however:

If the electrical signals generated by the cerebral cortex can couple to the skull, one would expect that this would piezoelectrically transduce mechanical vibrations in the skull, vibrations which would then bounce around the skull like a whispering gallery or EMF fields around the atmosphere (fractal evolution...)

Piezeoelectric emission from the skull might then couple back into different locations in the cortex across the brain and allow all cells on the cortex to plug in to our own personal broadband radio. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) did they ever publish _how_ brain cells can communicate remotely (through "telepathy," I forget the techical term) as you discussed with Rick and Andrew on the podcast?

With your knowledge of the wetware in our skulls, is this feasible, does the cortex generate enough voltage at the interface to be picked up by "skull transducers"?

This would also directly couple the brain into the rest of the body.

### rk257 — 2024-12-13T15:11:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plasma-can-be-uncontained-in-nature.29960/post-343833>

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1053811910007743](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1053811910007743)

_"In this paper, we show that bone piezoelectricity—a phenomenon in which bone polarizes electrically in response to an applied mechanical stress and produces a short-range electric field—may be a source of intense blast-induced electric fields in the brain, with magnitudes and timescales comparable to fields with known neurological effects."_

This paper suggests that bone can indeed produce EMF strong enough to influence the brain. The question is if the brain can produce EMF strong enough to induce piezoelectric phonons (the solid-state, mechanical version of a photon) in the skull. With both these in place, it strikes me as a natural way to communicate on a "system-wide" scale, rather than point-to-point neuronal signalling.

### NickD — 2024-12-13T22:52:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplement-stamets7.29964/post-343861>

I looked into Stamets stuff a while ago, and it was going to get too expensive. I ended up with Lifecykel products. But, it's one of those things that you can't really "feel"...

### Adamo — 2024-12-13T23:18:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplement-stamets7.29964/post-343863>

I hate to be the contrarian here, but separate Stamets and his knowledge/experience with mushrooms and his brand/product.

Mushrooms can be grown on any substrate. If you have grown your own, you know. Creating a supplement for health, growing them on rice as the substrate and adding in the mycelium is NOT something traditionally done in China or Japan. Using mushrooms medicinally is most commonly recorded in those countries, and is ABSOLUTELY valid. Mushrooms of many different varieties work very well, and the quality in the "market-place" is very, very different. (The spectrum is great.)

I understand that for business there is a scaling and speed issue at hand, but if we are all here trying to bring natural healthy/balance back to the body based upon what nature intended us for, why take something grown in a non-naturally occurring manner?

They are even growing cordyceps in liquid substrates and trying to synthetically propagate Chaga mushrooms. You might register the same chemicals in a lab-study/test, but does that mean the effect is the same as a cordyceps grown on a live insect or chaga on a birch tree in the colder climate (all natural occurrences)? I doubt it.

Barefoot doctors would rarely put that many different mushrooms or principal ingredients in the same herbal formula as well. Nor do all of those mushrooms grow in the same environment together. Again, we need to use discretion here.

There are small companies that focus on wild-harvested or grow mushrooms outdoors on logs. They are often a bit more pricey.

FYI: I fully believe in and use adaptogenic, natural compounds such as mushrooms, maca, etc. I'm not against them at all.

# Thread: Finally discovered my mom prob got blue light/EMF poisoned
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-discovered-my-mom-prob-got-blue-light-emf-poisoned.29967/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### madliv — 2024-12-13T14:57:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-discovered-my-mom-prob-got-blue-light-emf-poisoned.29967/post-343832>

Not sure if this is the right thread for this but I wanted to share something that it is been puzzling me for years until I saw the light!

Just wanted to comment the case of my mother... just to put a tinny bit of context my family is from Spain and in both sides there are examples of generations of elderly people in great health condition (mentally and physically). The father of my mother died by a stupid mistake at 98 (year 2004) quite deaf but with a very clear mind and quite well preserved motor skills (during many years he would go daily for his morning walk to the beach under the Eastern Spanish sun...).

So my mother was always in great health condition but suddenly she started to lose weight at 73 (she has always been in good shape but then she started to look too skinny) and was diagnosed with diabetes type II (which was unexpected) but so small that she has to have the bare minimum medication to avoid hypoglycaemic episodes - that she actually now and then has -. So she is now 83 and for many years I have been scratching my head wondering where all of that came from! I thought it was stress related as she is a quite nervous person but it didn't quite fit... until I started listening to Jack Kruse and I realised my mom had been poisoned by the light. Also important to notice that my mother was from 3 years old (in the mid 1940s) to 15 (in the early 60s) living in a town in the north of Spain (post civil war) with no electricity! with icy winters and not entirely warm clothes yet very healthy entirely eating locally and basically living outdoors with poisonous snakes running around - that didn't kill anybody because the locals knew what to do to avoid them - haha. She has told me stories about being wet feet frozen in the snow and picking up whatever the trees had of season with her friends... my great grandmother had natural remedies for anything and make do with whatever they had to make creative recipes everyone enjoyed.

So, back to the future, what happened around that time my mom got her diagnose...in 2014... I am guessing must have been around the time my dad decided to switch to all led lights at home and they both got smart phones and tablets to read books... also my mom always using sunglasses didn't help.

So now I am trying to get my mom back to the sun with no make up… not an easy task but I am working on it. Also she got cataracts surgery a bit ago with all the light blocking that that implies.
My dad is a bookworm, as well, so I am trying to dump as much information as I can but with the language barrier it is not as easy as it could be but hopefully I can get him on board and get them both into their terrace in the AM light. I also got them blue light blockers recently.

Thanks for reading if you did


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Found someone in Athens who does zirconia implants and i am considering that route.

The material they use is
Y-TZP (Yttria-Stabilized Tetragonal Zirconia Polycrystals) with composition

Zirconia (ZrO₂): The base material.
Yttria (Y₂O₃): Acts as a stabilizer to maintain the tetragonal crystalline phase at room temperature.

Continue researching if this is a good replacement for a missing tooth.

### digitald3 — 2025-03-26T01:58:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dental-implants-titanium-zirconia.29968/post-349353>

I shy away from recommending Zirconia implants as I don't see much benefit health wise compared to titanium when one is needing to replace a tooth or teeth... they are a replacement of no teeth. Zirconia implants are difficult to put teeth on which means they have less options and often require cement to adhere the crown to the implant.... cement and dental implants have issues if not handled well . The larger issue that rarely do I hear anyone talk about is they are twice as stiff as titanium implants.. the modulus of elasticity of bone is 10-20 Gpa(Gigapascals), titanium is at around 100 gpa and Zirconia is 200gpa.. which means when the bone flexes they don't bend with it.. zirconia implants do have higher failure rates in the literature...primarily due to implant fractures and lack of osseointegration... but the longevity in the literature is terrible as I see failing dental implants daily and when they are removed, I know for sure nobody is tracking its failure.. nobody is looking at the affects of nnEMF on dental implants whether they are titanium or zirconia.. most dentists relate poor dental hygiene to their failure, my hunch is that the reason the original teeth were lost will be the same reason the implants failed and it has nothing to do with a toothbrush and more so the environment and the redox potential of the mitochondria.. regardless of the choice the redox and environment must improve to have a successful outcome

# Thread: Hi All,
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all.29970/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### annav — 2024-12-13T23:10:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all.29970/post-343862>

I am new member here although I was on the forum back in 2021 for about six months. I solved some of my issues and learned a lot along the way. I am back because I want to learn more, heal more, help my family and share my knowledge with others who want to listen.

I wanted to post something that dawned on me the other day. My Mom had early Alzheimer's that showed up in her early 60's (I am 59) and three of her sisters also had Alzheimer's. She passed away in her 70's. I always thought my Mom's Alzheimer's was due to bad genes, chronic stress, medications and not eating enough seafood. Don't get me wrong, those are still factors, but after listening to Jack and reading his blogs, I believe the bigger factors were: circadian mismatch (from Italy and moved to Chicago), not outside enough, always used suntan lotion and she wore sunglasses when outside.

Guess what? I was headed down the same path! Luckily, I learned about the benefits of light three years ago and I changed my habits. I see sunrise every day, sunset most days, outside as much as possible, eat within an hour of sunrise, ground, no sunglasses, no suntan lotion, shut wifi off at night (hard wire is next), respect darkness at night and I am no longer in front of a computer 16 hours a day. I have resolved a lot of minor health issues but still working on a few and hope to keep improving. What's really nice is my husband has finally turned the corner. Once he listened to Jack's explanation of the history including MKUltra, Robert Becker and the Church Committee, it all made sense to him and now he prioritizes the sun and even takes his shirt off! We are a work in progress. I am jealous and admire all of you moving to a tropical climate and maybe that will be us one day.

### Adamo — 2024-12-13T23:26:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all.29970/post-343865>

Welcome back!

I have seen similar things in my family as well. I tend to remind them about their agrarian lifestyle in 'the Boot' and how they did things to respect the natural biorythym, and when them came to the Americas, it all went out the window. It's tough to get people to acknowledge that we need a change and a lot of our modern habits/addictions are actually harming us, but glad to see so many people embracing the changes and knowledge.

Illinois has a similar environment as Ontario, Canada, so I can relate in many ways.

### annav — 2024-12-15T22:17:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all.29970/post-343954>

> [Adamo said:](/goto/post?id=343865)
>
> Welcome back!
>
> I have seen similar things in my family as well. I tend to remind them about their agrarian lifestyle in 'the Boot' and how they did things to respect the natural biorythym, and when them came to the Americas, it all went out the window. It's tough to get people to acknowledge that we need a change and a lot of our modern habits/addictions are actually harming us, but glad to see so many people embracing the changes and knowledge.
>
> Illinois has a similar environment as Ontario, Canada, so I can relate in many ways.

Thanks Adamo!

You're so fortunate that you can at least try and teach them. My parents passed before I could share this knowledge. My Mom would have embraced all my recommendations. My dad would have been more challenging. I still have family in Italy. They walk everywhere, cook and eat outside. Those three fairly simple habits would have definitely helped my Italian parents. I have found that when I wear my blue blocking glasses I get a lot of questions and it's a perfect time to give the elevator pitch about light to friends and family. Good luck to you!

I’m getting ready to go outside for my morning walk and this is what I see. What defense do we have against this? Our beautiful Carolina skies! I’m just sick over it! And no one talks about it at all. I have videos and they are still spraying. It’s the worst I’ve seen today.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-14T22:28:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/defense-against-chem-trails.29972/post-343903>

Yes there are chem-trails . . .
But . . . most photos I see are actually con-trails. They can stay in the sky for long periods of time depending on conditions in the upper atmosphere. If you live in a flight path there can be many and if you live under where flight paths cross, N/S and E/W you will see a checkerboard.

### Adamo — 2024-12-14T22:36:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/defense-against-chem-trails.29972/post-343905>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=343903)
>
> [View attachment 29663](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/29663/)Yes there are chem-trails . . .
> But . . . most photos I see are actually con-trails. They can stay in the sky for long periods of time depending on conditions in the upper atmosphere. If you live in a flight path there can be many and if you live under where flight paths cross, N/S and E/W you will see a checkerboard.

In addition to what you are showing here, it's kind of a dead give-away when the plane is not flying or appearing "horizontally" and is ALWAYS going "vertically" and at the same time directly across the Sun. And then there are multiple planes doing the same thing.

I have personally noticed a pattern of this happening every time we have bouts of solar flare activity. Not sure if there is a connection here, or coincidence.

There appears to be more than just "chem-trailing"... it is a type of geo-engineering that has been admitted by governments all over the world, including the Spanish, Canadian, Chinese and Emerati nations. There has to be several other motivating factors at play... I doubt it is just to "poison" people with chemicals.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-17T06:55:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/defense-against-chem-trails.29972/post-344015>

I am not denying in any way that Chemtrails and geo engineering are real, they are VERY real. I have photographs/videos of planes with tanks in them and pilots admitting to flying them.

Every 1,000ft of altitude the temp. drops 2º.
Most new commercial aircraft are approved to fly at a maximum of around 42,000 feet, with this maximum figure also being known as a 'service ceiling.' For example, for the double-decker [Airbus](https://simpleflying.com/tag/airbus-a380/) A380' superjumbo' quadjet, the service ceiling is 43,000 feet. Meanwhile, for the [Boeing 787-8](https://simpleflying.com/tag/boeing-787-8/) and [\-9](http://simpleflying.com/tag/boeing-787-9/) 'Dreamliner,' it is 43,100 feet. However, the stretched-fuselage [787-10](https://simpleflying.com/tag/boeing-787-10/) comes in a little lower, at 41,100 feet.

To use another example, earlier Boeing 737 family aircraft (up to the -500 variant) have a service ceiling of 37,000 feet. This ceiling was increased to 41,000 feet with the introduction of the 737-600 model, which was the first and smallest variant of the 737 'NG' (Next Generation) family. When I was flying on 747’s our altitude was typically around 35,000 feet.

There are several reasons why airlines would want to fly their planes at higher altitudes. The primary purpose of such operations is to optimize efficiency. At higher altitudes, the air is thinner. This produces less resistance, allowing the aircraft to fly faster using less fuel. Jet engines also operate more efficiently at a higher altitude, as they can be run closer to their maximum capabilities.

The difference between 35,000ft and 42,000ft can mean contrails disappearing after a few aircraft lengths and staying in the sky for as far as you can see. Chemtrails are real . . . but not every line in the sky is a chemtrails. To have an effect in changing things and to be taken seriously it is important to get our facts right.

I have two options for glass bottled water in UK that are financially sensible. Highland spring is what i've bought for awhile, i use VOSS to make survivor soup and to drink every so often, but i recently found a brand called cheddar spring that has a high mineral content and is half the price. As far as I'm aware highland spring has 0.005 fluoride content, cheddar valley has 0.05. Is this even worth considering over whether i should spend more or save with the one with slightly higher fluoride with higher mineral content at half the price? I don't know if im splitting hairs over nothing or this is a considerable difference in PPM that i should be considerate of.

Martha, you might find this article on DMSO of interest:

_•The standard approach for treating pain and musculoskeletal injuries typically involves giving NSAIDs (e.g., ibuprofen), and in more severe cases, opioids. Unfortunately, these drugs are extremely dangerous (e.g., each one kills tens of thousands of people each year), but nonetheless have remained the standard of care for decades.

•DMSO is a remarkably effective pain-killing agent, in many cases allowing individuals who’d been disabled for years by their pain (e.g., a failed spine surgery or severe arthritis—DMSO’s most popular use) to get their lives back. Furthermore, it can treat many types of pain other therapies do not work on (e.g., complex regional pain syndrome).

•DMSO is a highly effective therapy for healing wounds and creating healthy scars, making it particularly helpful for recovering from surgery.

•DMSO is incredibly effective for healing a wide range of acute and chronic musculoskeletal injuries (e.g., arthritis, headaches, neck and back strains, restless leg syndrome, sprained ankles, trigeminal neuralgia and numerous traumatic injuries). It typically has an 80-90% success rate and often has an instant and dramatic effect. This use was particularly popular with professional athletes, as it allowed many of them to quickly return to the field rather than be out for the rest of the season.

•In this article, I will review the scientific literature that explains how DMSO provides pain relief and healing, the vast body of evidence (comprising of thousands of patients) showing it indeed does, and our preferred DMSO home treatment protocols for pain, arthritis, and musculoskeletal injury (along with the best sources for procuring DMSO)._

# Thread: Want cancer success?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-cancer-success.29985/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-17T17:44:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-cancer-success.29985/post-344032>

Want cancer success? Begin here by plugging into the decentralized network of the sun.

ATTENTION TRIBE: GET SUN OR GET CANCER

When considering your exposure to the sun, make sure it's your choice, not your dermatologist's.

In this paper, which your dermatologist ignores, we see that....

"The findings of the current study confirm solar UV-B radiation is associated with reduced risk of cancer of the breast, colon, ovary, and prostate as well as non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Eight additional malignancies exhibited an inverse correlation between mortality rates and UV-B...."

**This means more sun = less cancer risk.**

The paper goes on to say that....

"**Conclusions:** The results of the current study demonstrate that much of the geographic variation in cancer mortality rates in the U.S. can be attributed to variations in solar UV-B radiation exposure. Thus, many lives could be extended through increased careful exposure to solar UV-B radiation..."

So, if you'd like to get cancer faster but have lighter to vampire-like white skin (depending on what skin color you are starting with), by all means, follow your dermatologist's advice and avoid the sun.

But if you would like to extend your cancer-free time here on Earth, maybe do the opposite and follow the mitochondriacs advice that is blasted all over this website.

So possibly try feeding your cat some raw fresh meat and then put her/him out in the sun to laze around , it may work. The cat will take what it needs possibly with this nudge.

# Thread: Quasiparticles discovered that make up all magnetic materials
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quasiparticles-discovered-that-make-up-all-magnetic-materials.29991/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### petrichor — 2024-12-18T20:10:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quasiparticles-discovered-that-make-up-all-magnetic-materials.29991/post-344086>

### [Tiny particle, huge potential](https://showme.missouri.edu/2024/tiny-particle-huge-potential/)

University of Missouri researchers Carsten Ullrich and Deepak Singh have discovered unseen interactions that could impact the future of electronics.

 showme.missouri.edu

These particles, among other things, could allow us to employ the use of spin electronics--using the natural spin of electrons rather than their electrical charge to store and process information.

> “The spin nature of these electrons is responsible for the magnetic phenomena,” Singh said. “Electrons have two properties: a charge and a spin. So, instead of using the conventional charge, we use the rotational, or spinning, property. It’s more efficient because the spin dissipates much less energy than the charge.”

Here's the paper: [Emergent topological quasiparticle kinetics in constricted nanomagnets](https://journals.aps.org/prresearch/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevResearch.6.043144)
