Not by me, but others have done blood work. Look at your blood under a microscope. You will be able to see a difference after some time.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-25T06:46:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unvaccinated-blood.29996/post-344329>

So when blood work is done in the checking of these (and other) devices, what are the lighting conditions? Does the subject or tester have a cell phone in their pocket? Is there a computer on the desk with a WiFi router close by? Are there any 50Hz/60Hz AC electric & magnetic fields from the equipment in the room? Dr. Jack has made many comments about the conditions under which many “scientific” tests are done.

I have muscle tested one of the strongest natural weight lifters in the world. I could hang from his out stretched arm. I did one move that unbeknownst to him broke his energy field and 30 seconds later I could push his arm down with one hand. Needless to say he was shocked. I reestablished his energy field and his strength returned. The mind is a powerful thing . . . Both the mind of the subject and the mind of the tester.

I believe many traditional treatments are bogus and many “alternative“ decentralized treatments are valid . . . But we must be careful with test results and our understanding, interpretation of them.

### Caitlin (Metabolic Mastery) — 2025-01-15T13:40:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unvaccinated-blood.29996/post-345230>

> [Villiam said:](/goto/post?id=344130)

>
> I wanted to start this thread bc my biggest concern is if I need untainted blood where can I go to receive it. My blood type is on the rare side with only 4% of the population having it. Not sure if we can start something here or at least start talking about it.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-useful-to-put-mineral-water-in-a-glass-bottle-and-put-it-in-the-sun.30000/post-344173>

# Thread: Non-smoker, athletic, healthy diet, low cholesterol and BP---- Ischemic Stroke @ 46...WTF?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-smoker-athletic-healthy-diet-low-cholesterol-and-bp-ischemic-stroke-46-wtf.30001/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thadius — 2024-12-20T20:12:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-smoker-athletic-healthy-diet-low-cholesterol-and-bp-ischemic-stroke-46-wtf.30001/post-344181>

Greetings all,

Newbie here, coming to you from Colorado in the U.S. I've been following JK for a while now and I am intrigued with his positions. Likely for the same reasons most of you are.

As someone who rarely joins forums on anything of substance, I wanted to take a moment to say hello, extend the proverbial olive branch, and expand my horizons of knowledge.
**To be clear, I am joining the Forum with an objective in mind.** Two actually. If either resonate with you, the following story will not be "TLDR". If the title alone doesn't capture your attention, no worries. I hope you find what you are seeking.

That said, if I connect with one person who shares a common experience as mine, or with someone who I can inspire or who can inspire me to challenge my methods of thinking, "GREAT SUCCESS!" (-Borat)

**About me:**
\-20+ years in the financial industry in Sr. Management. Great money, all the fixings, travel, cars, etc. I had an epiphany along the way and left the industry for sake of mind, body, and soul.

(Why does someone leave a mid 6 fig job without a safety net in place? Ask me and I'll tell you. Hint, it has to do with centralized industry. I assume I will find camaraderie here.)

\-All previous health indicators (centralized medicine's indicators, e.g., BP, Chol, Diet, BMI, exercise, etc.) were/are within good to excellent range.

\-Recently suffered a bout of long Covid (6+ months of pure exhaustion and poor lung/circulatory function) which I believe led to prolonged extreme inflammation (of which I also believe) that led to sudden bouts of gout and eventually, a stroke. In that order and with no history of medical issues, EVER. No meds, no flu or flu shots, rare cold (every few years). No allergies. You get the picture.

\-Mentally and physically, I felt like S\*@T **ALL** the time. Yes, I probably drank too many craft beers, so sue me  And, I think you will agree, that is likely not the culprit of reason #1 I am joining this forum.

**Reason #1 for joining the Forum**

**I had an ischemic stroke at 46 YOA** (Warning - centralized explanation here): Full loss of motor function on the left side. After IV brain and heart catheter surgeries (I won't call them minimally invasive as I can already hear Dr. K saying "MAXIMALLY INVASSIVE"!!) I have since regained 99% motor function and cognition and am faring very well. In fact, better than most. For that, I am eternally thankful.

Not one doctor can tell me why I got a blood clot however they can tell my why it led to the stroke. Is that actually helpful going forward, NO. **I want to know WHY?**

**Reason # 2 for joining the Forum**

To date, I feel "better", but not "healthy" (low energy & brain fog from before the stroke still remain) although I am in even better shape (appearance wise) than I was before the stroke.

I need to formulate a new strategy since my medical team's answer is making me a lifetime customer of big Pharma. (Statins for someone with low BP? Blood thinners for an active person with no history of any cardio or neuro issues? **These medications? For life? It just doesn't "feel" right.**

Lastly, this is more than anything, a newly invigorated pursuit of truth, justice, and knowledge. If I can get an ounce of each, I will be better off and can pass it along to those I care about.

Thank you advance and I look forward to whatever comes next.....

~TR

### Ozzy1 — 2024-12-20T22:20:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-smoker-athletic-healthy-diet-low-cholesterol-and-bp-ischemic-stroke-46-wtf.30001/post-344186>

A warm welcome to you, glad to read that you recovered well. I know if you get gold membership you can submit a question to Kruse in the monthly QnA and there is a path to get direct consultation. I feel very lucky I found this place I hope it serves you well too

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-20T23:08:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-smoker-athletic-healthy-diet-low-cholesterol-and-bp-ischemic-stroke-46-wtf.30001/post-344188>

Welcome . . . As I put it . . . “You hit the wall” . . . That has a tendency to get someone’s attention.
Use the search feature and put in Jack’s name for his thoughts on the subject you have questions about.
For some (many/most) reducing nnEMF specifically 60Hz electric fields in their sleep area helps with recovery.
If I can help let me know: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

25% of your body's total cholesterol is in your brain

Blood thinners well you got a clot, and it's only been six months or 5. That can be revisited later

### Chlo_e — 2024-12-22T19:54:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-smoker-athletic-healthy-diet-low-cholesterol-and-bp-ischemic-stroke-46-wtf.30001/post-344256>

Agree with comments above, regardless of the lag time it's the most likely precipitating factor.
As for any predisposing factors the usual suspects apply- lack of sunlight and poor circadian habits, frequent air travel, cumulative and ongoing EMF exposure, accumulated deuterium, booze, cigs, other shit in our environment and water, previous vaccines/abx/meds, pre-conception/maternal health - tick any and all that apply. An event like this usually requires a deep re-evaluation of lifestyle and environment- would a consult with JK help you?

If we bounced the question back to you, why do you think you've had a stroke? If it was the vaccine, why you specifically and not your contemporaries? Even if you didn't have a PFO (which is v unlucky in this context), the DVT would probably have travelled to your lungs instead and caused a PE. I'm hoping your docs at least checked you for the rare coagulation disorders some young people have.

JK has written a lot on this- be worth having a read. A mid-western doctor on substack has also written a lot on this including damaged zeta potential following the jabs- could be worth a look as well.

# Thread: Sun protocol for my daughter? (9 months old)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-protocol-for-my-daughter-9-months-old.30003/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### martin.v — 2024-12-21T04:26:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-protocol-for-my-daughter-9-months-old.30003/post-344199>

Hey mitochondriacs.
I have a baby girl who's 9 months old and tomorrow the summer officially starts.
She's pale white (caucasian), blue eyes, we're in Argentina at latitue 34 south. Now the UV is very strong (peaks at index 12).

I have doubts on what protocol to follow with her. I see that her arms, legs and face already have a darker tone because of natural comes and goes around our big backyard, we're not even putting her under the sun as me and my partner usually do.
Should I avoid direct sun on someone that young? I don't want to use sunscreen but my partner is now "worried" because her arms look burnt (they're not), but I suspect is the old paradigm knocking on her head.

Perhaps my old paradigm is the one that made me at least come here and ask you what you would do in my situation.
Thank you all!

### doctorwu85 — 2024-12-21T04:35:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-protocol-for-my-daughter-9-months-old.30003/post-344200>

We had our kid out in the direct sun from birth and she is Fitzpatrick 1 (or maybe a light 2). She's had a nice tan for basically her entire life, and has never had what centralized medicine would call a sunburn. We're at 34N.

Don't do something drastic like go from 0 direct sun to 5 hours in one day, but look up Jack's solar callus blog and follow that protocol. The sun healed my daughter's eczema when she was an infant and it has so many other beneficial properties. She loves playing outside in direct sun, and when she's done, she just says "home" or walks into the shade herself. 9 months is approaching the age where your daughter will be able to communicate that she wants to change venues once she's had enough.

# Thread: Favorite Homeschool Resources
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-homeschool-resources.30006/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-22T03:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-homeschool-resources.30006/post-344240>

Hi! Jack has inspired me to plan homeschooling my kiddos. I realized they are in an elementary school for 8 hours with hundreds of tablets and WiFi and crappy lighting.

Do any current homeschool families have curriculum they use and like?

I will work part time and homeschool so I need a low-prep or grab-and-go option. Textbook only.

I’ve found Oak Meadow (mostly Waldorf) and The Good and the Beautiful (non-denom Christian and more Charlotte Mason) that both appeal to me.

### Jessnet3 — 2024-12-24T16:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-homeschool-resources.30006/post-344307>

> [Rachel Martin said:](/goto/post?id=344240)
>
> Hi! Jack has inspired me to plan homeschooling my kiddos. I realized they are in an elementary school for 8 hours with hundreds of tablets and WiFi and crappy lighting.
>
> Do any current homeschool families have curriculum they use and like?
>
> I will work part time and homeschool so I need a low-prep or grab-and-go option. Textbook only.
>
> I’ve found Oak Meadow (mostly Waldorf) and The Good and the Beautiful (non-denom Christian and more Charlotte Mason) that both appeal to me.

Rachel, the beauty of leaving traditional school is that the whole library of Alexandria may now be open to you and your children without restriction. I would personally not narrow yourself to following any one curriculum, though I understand this appeals to families that are just starting out. Most specifically, I would not restrict yourself to just textbooks. I find textbooks at this age are often where learning goes to die.

You mentioned a draw to Charlotte Mason, and if you get into the weeds on her educational philosophy, textbooks are the antithesis of that. Fill your children’s minds with living books that provoke thought and inquiry. Narration is critical with her approach - have them read works that they then narrate back to you.

Ambleside online is a free resource. It has a Christian bent but one can easily make it secular by disregarding the religious texts and solely following their classical literary suggestions. Their music and art study are also excellent. Importantly, subject matter is effortlessly interwoven through the texts and thus it is absolved from modern silos of “now we are learning history, now we are learning literature”. I appreciate the classical bent as the language and learning is rich while not being overly simplified, as modern offerings tend to do.

When it comes to mathematics, many homeschool programs do fall flat in my experience and so it’s worth the time to really investigate what might fit your child. We use Dimensions Singapore (there are a few others worth their salt for sure, but I’ve found this one works with a variety of different learners). It is rigorous, so be sure to appropriately follow their guides for placing your child as often they are a full grade below if coming from a traditional school.

I mentioned in another post how we approach “science”.

It is a lot more work to tailor education to your children than simply opting for an open and go program. But I assure you though this approach requires more legwork initially, it has the capacity to bear a lot of fruit. Once you lay the groundwork, you should be able to easily integrate this into your schedule.

### Rachel Martin — 2024-12-27T23:22:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-homeschool-resources.30006/post-344400>

Thanks for the information about Singapore Math. I was intrigued and impressed with what I read.

# Thread: Finding Low EMF laptop with dedicated graphic card
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### eve001 — 2024-12-22T15:20:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/post-344248>

What would be some specs to take into account regarding light, flicker and EMFs?

Refresh rate: What would be the best for least health impact?

What are the components that create the LF magnetic and electric fields?
(How) can we take these into account when choosing a laptop to increase our chances of a suitable device?

I want a laptop that allows for using Lr and Ps.
RAM minimum 16GB and a seperate dedicated graphic card.

In laptops I tested often LF magnetic fields are situated under the trackpad, close to the space bar.
This is annoying: on the go we cannot always plug in a seperate keyboard to create distance.

The loudspeakers too give LF magnetic fields so they better be postioned far away from mouse and keyboard.

Any recommendations for specific devices?

The BenQ gaming screens have been recommended regarding flicker and eye strain, however I am looking for a laptop not a desktop w/ seperate screen.

### eve001 — 2024-12-23T16:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/post-344280>

Maybe it is possible to attach an external graphic card to a laptop in order to make it suited for graphic work?

### [The Best Ways to Connect an External Graphics Card to Your Laptop](https://www.howtogeek.com/295442/the-best-ways-to-connect-an-external-graphics-card-to-your-laptop/)

Laptops, especially gaming laptops, are a study in compromises.

 www.howtogeek.com

I know of a laptop I can work with with few EHS symptoms, but it has no dedicated graphic card.
It has 16GM RAM so for my Adobe Ps/Lr usage that's enough.

This would be awesome.

Anyone got good experience w/ this?

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-23T23:50:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/post-344292>

It is my understanding if you hard wire (Ethernet) any laptop and either use it on battery or a grounded power cord, you should have little to no nnEMF. On battery power any electric and/or magnetic fields would be DC not AC. I know some people are sensitive to DC EMF but for most this set up solves their issues.

### Rusty — 2024-12-24T09:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/post-344301>

Wired Mouse
Wired Keyboard
Ethernet to the Router, WiFi OFF
3 PIN PRONG for the charging adapter
Above setup grounded for AC electric fields using an aligator clip with a grounding cord.

You can't do much about the ELF fields originating from the processor clocking at high speeds. If you add in a GPU (GFX card), there will be more ELF-EMF 100%, that's why you need a WIRED Mouse/Keyboard

### michellejorge — 2025-01-21T20:36:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/post-345522>

I’m trying to learn and not techie. So I have a router/ WiFi combo that’s a couple months old. It’s an Arris. If I get a long enough cable, I can use my laptop and Ethernet and some how disable the WiFi? How would I do so? Then I could work without worrying! Any hacks for the I phones? Thanks!

Are there tips for newbies in one centralized place?

### eve001 — 2025-01-21T21:11:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/post-345525>

Hi [@michellejorge](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/36737/)

In my case I went to my online account of my internet provider and there switched off my wifis.
Here, these days modems sometimes also emit wifi for a public network so that the clients of the provider can have wifi on the go and use this wifi from your modem as they drive/walk past your house. It's a common practice still of providers to guarantee free internet everywhere.
If this is the case you will see several wifi networks on your online account from your internet provider.
So better switch them all off.
Some modems can have a wifi-off button. I have not seen any of those yet.
You can get ethernet adaptors for your devices: phone, laptop, tablet.
Well grounded devices reduce also LF EMFs (fields from wires)
You better get a meter for the wifi and other wireless radiation = RF EMFs. Radiofrequency EMFs.
That way you are sure your modem is not emitting wifi.
The safe and sound pro 2 is often recommended. It also detects short pulses from radar.
Also switch off bluetooth from your computer and other devices, as well as find my device for healthy low EMF living.
Good luck!
Till you figure this out you can put a mylar sheet or some metal grid between you and your modem to lower your wifi exposure.
No centralized place I am afraid!

### rtd — 2025-01-30T23:32:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/post-346004>

### bennyhoffman — 2025-02-01T05:24:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/post-346065>

> [Rusty said:](/goto/post?id=344301)
>
> Wired Mouse
> Wired Keyboard
> Ethernet to the Router, WiFi OFF
> 3 PIN PRONG for the charging adapter
> Above setup grounded for AC electric fields using an aligator clip with a grounding cord.
>
> You can't do much about the ELF fields originating from the processor clocking at high speeds. If you add in a GPU (GFX card), there will be more ELF-EMF 100%, that's why you need a WIRED Mouse/Keyboard

Do you have any thoughts on e-ink devices or the Daylight Computer (which uses a transflective LCD rather than e-ink)? There’s a lot of hype around Daylight, but I imagine it’s basically a glorified iPad—just without blue light from an EMF standpoint. It might even be worse in that regard since you’re constantly holding it, making more surface contact with your hands.

E-ink devices are interesting because they use almost no power to display an image, like a book page, and only consume small amounts of energy for annotations or writing—unless WiFi and Bluetooth are enabled. One concern, though, is that the pen might rely on Bluetooth, which could be a drawback.

### bennyhoffman — 2025-02-02T04:47:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-low-emf-laptop-with-dedicated-graphic-card.30007/post-346105>

> [bennyhoffman said:](/goto/post?id=346065)
>
> Do you have any thoughts on e-ink devices or the Daylight Computer (which uses a transflective LCD rather than e-ink)? There’s a lot of hype around Daylight, but I imagine it’s basically a glorified iPad—just without blue light from an EMF standpoint. It might even be worse in that regard since you’re constantly holding it, making more surface contact with your hands.
>
> E-ink devices are interesting because they use almost no power to display an image, like a book page, and only consume small amounts of energy for annotations or writing—unless WiFi and Bluetooth are enabled. One concern, though, is that the pen might rely on Bluetooth, which could be a drawback.

Quick update on the e-ink device. I own both a Remarkable 2 and a Remarkable Paper Pro. I also have a Trifield meter and the Safe and Sound RF detector, so I tested the devices for EMF emissions. The only measurable EMF came from the magnetic field emitted by the pen holder and the pen itself, averaging around 10 mG with peaks of about 25 mG.

I originally thought this wouldn’t be an issue, but now I’m reconsidering. While e-ink devices seemed like a clever workaround for reducing EMF exposure, the pens use EMR technology, which is likely better than Bluetooth but still emits levels I find concerning. Given that I just purchased it, I might return it. Unfortunately, no other e-ink devices currently offer a wired pen option, which would be a great addition if a company decided to implement it.

I also recently ordered the Daylight Computer, but I don’t have high expectations. It will likely have RF and magnetic field emissions similar to an iPad or iPhone. I tweeted at Daylight about EMF testing—they liked the tweet but didn’t follow up.

That said, I do really like transflective LCD technology. I believe it’s the future of screens alongside e-ink, especially with upcoming advancements once patents expire this year (I believe). You’re probably familiar with transflective LCDs, but for context: they emit zero blue light due to the amber backlight, and the outer transflective layer absorbs ambient light like paper, making them highly usable in sunlight.

### bennyhoffman — 2025-02-02T04:48:06+0200

>
> Quick update on the e-ink device. I own both a Remarkable 2 and a Remarkable Paper Pro. I also have a Trifield meter and the Safe and Sound RF detector, so I tested the devices for EMF emissions. The only measurable EMF came from the magnetic field emitted by the pen holder and the pen itself, averaging around 10 mG with peaks of about 25 mG.
>
> I originally thought this wouldn’t be an issue, but now I’m reconsidering. While e-ink devices seemed like a clever workaround for reducing EMF exposure, the pens use EMR technology, which is likely better than Bluetooth but still emits levels I find concerning. Given that I just purchased it, I might return it. Unfortunately, no other e-ink devices currently offer a wired pen option, which would be a great addition if a company decided to implement it.
>
> I also recently ordered the Daylight Computer, but I don’t have high expectations. It will likely have RF and magnetic field emissions similar to an iPad or iPhone. I tweeted at Daylight about EMF testing—they liked the tweet but didn’t follow up.
>
> That said, I do really like transflective LCD technology. I believe it’s the future of screens alongside e-ink, especially with upcoming advancements once patents expire this year (I believe). You’re probably familiar with transflective LCDs, but for context: they emit zero blue light due to the amber backlight, and the outer transflective layer absorbs ambient light like paper, making them highly usable in sunlight.

Hi there,

I own a daylight tablet. I did some measurments some months ago but don't recall them really well.
The daylight really makes a huge difference for my eyes!!

By contrast: I feel annoyance from my new laptop though it has an OLED screen and permits a night mode that seems full red.
The refresh rate of the screen of this laptop may be the problem. It can be set to 120Hz or 48Hz.
It affects my eyes which the daylight does not.

This laptop for some reason - all RF off and wired ethernet - feels VERY addictive to me. I wonder why.
Just for being new? Or is more going on?
I measured for DE with this AM radio and I think it makes lots of DE noise - but find it hard to interpret since this is new to me.
I have no idea what would be the standard for okay DE.
Also, how come it makes DE when it is not plugged in?
Or am I measuring something else.

Its screen seems to have some AI or tech in it to adapt w/ the camera.
I taped the camera but the luminance of the screen still changes now and then.

The blue backlit keyboard for some reason switched itself off automatically... or by error.
Which I do not mind.

May try install Iris - the software said to cut out blue and reduce flicker.

JK said on a podcast last autumn - I think - that red LEDs too can be used for manipulation........

Can I set my screen to violet?

For all devices I am afraid they are best when you do not touch them.
For TV I imagine a beamer would be much better than a screen.
Jeromy Johnson has a post about a beamer kind of screen for a PC.

Thanks, this info is useful to me!

For many years, I've noticed significant disruptions to my energy when using computer screens and LED/OLED cell phones. Last year, I got a 65" LG OLED TV that's 3 meters from me, and this solved most of that problem. I suspect that the reason it still bothers me a bit is that it occasionally sends out huge spikes of rf radiation, no matter if I turn it off in the TV's settings.

I've also tried using a projector for a computer screen. The projector was 1m in front of me, and the screen 3 m in front of me. At first, it felt worse than using a regular monitor, but when I put it in an aluminum box with a small hole in front for the light to come out of, it was the best feeling I've had with any kind of pc screen setup so far! Unfortunately, the picture wasn't very sharp, so it bothered/fatigued my eyes.

So the 65" TV is much sharper, but not quite as good energy-wise.

At this point, I think there are 4 different types of nnEMF that are bothering me. Magnetic fields, electric fields, dirty electricity and rf.

The first 3 fall off so strongly with distance that having the 3m distance solves most of the problem. The rf is still strong enough to bother me at 3m distance.

Next test, when I get more living space, will be to get an ultra short throw projector and a 120"/3m sized screen, and put the projector in an aluminum box that is open enough for the light to get out, so that there is no straight path from the projector to my body. Theoretically, that could provide the best result for low emf, and acceptable sharpness and brightness.

I was wondering about the daylight device, and I might still try it, but the high dirty electricity you're measuring might mean that it could be unacceptable for me. I still don't know if the problems my cell phone is causing are due to DE or electric fields. It's always on flight mode, and I've measured that it doesn't put out rf while in flight mode (contrary to the iPhone I've measured which still puts out rf in flight mode).

### Marshall_Sponder — 2024-12-23T22:15:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/danny-jones-podcast-w-calley-and-mary.30011/post-344287>

> [TheOnlyEZ said:](/goto/post?id=344268)
>
> Listening to the new podcast of Danny Jones with Calley Jack and Mary on a Watch Party with Alexis Cowan (lol) and woah buddy Calley is toast. Could not be more blatantly lobbyist mumbo jumbo cya

Yeah, I likewise listened to it via the Patreon link. I didn't know about the Watch Party (on YouTube?), or I might have joined.

I had a lot of thoughts and wanted to watch it again and see how much of the YouTube version remains and what is cut out. I know that two minutes of Jack's first interview with DJ was cut out except for Patreon members.

So far, I can say that Calley didn't convince me he was sincere or transparent, even with himself (that's the scariest part about it - not to mention he has the ears of a lot of important people). He sounded (as was noted) like a lobbyist who has come to believe in indoctrinated storylines he has grown up around and used to propel himself up the ladder. In other words, what is going on the outside and what is what he feels internally to be true are incongruent and can appear inauthentic, but it actually seems like he fails to own what he really believes or feels fully. In fact, in the end, Jack and Mary tell him that they are more concerned for him due to the Jabs than he is for himself - because he doesn't realize he's a genetically modified organism, etc.

At this point, I'm really interested in the comments, not just here but on YouTube when it's posted. I want to see what people overall make of this podcast.

What's also scary is that you can have someone THAT disconnected from themselves and their history that, in a way, they are in denial of it - they can't see it - who would want to see it - it's painful to be that disconnected from oneself and one's story. It was painful to watch, especially as Calley melted as the interview went on. Jack was relentless, but he had the right to be, because he was advocating for his patients, for people who have been injured, while Calley was focused on politics, mainly.

And as someone who used to go to marketing conferences, I knew of Edelman and the Trust Monitors, but I didn't realize that Edelman PR was a CIA-operated MKULTRA Shop; oh well, why should I be surprised the people in PR are full of bullshit would also be MKULTRA'd!

I have watched Darkhorse podcasts on and off, and I must say that Bret is very articulate and knowledgeable about the topics he discusses. He provides answers that make sense. Interestingly, he has acknowledged that some people perceive him as controlled opposition, although he doesn’t agree with that characterization.

One of the most fascinating aspects for me was that he and Chris Martensen were invited to the Darién Gap last year to investigate the sources of immigration. They ended up doing firsthand reporting on what they observed during their visit.

However, I don’t agree with him on everything, and I have little interest in Eric Weinstein. In fact, it’s hard for me to believe that Bret and Eric are brothers.!

# Thread: Friendly warning about leveraged ETFs like MSTU and MSTX.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/friendly-warning-about-leveraged-etfs-like-mstu-and-mstx.30015/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patrick S. — 2024-12-25T00:23:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/friendly-warning-about-leveraged-etfs-like-mstu-and-mstx.30015/post-344317>

I’m currently listening to the December ’24 PowWow where the 2x leveraged ETFs for MSTR (MSTX and MSTU) were discussed. Here are my thoughts:

These types of ETFs provide **2x performance only on a daily basis**, not over longer periods (e.g., two days or more). They are designed as **day-trading vehicles**, not for long-term holding.

Here’s why: For a 2x Bull ETF like MSTU, if MSTR goes up 10% on one day and then falls 10% the next day, **the two-day loss for MSTR is 1%, but for MSTU, it’s 4%.**

Here’s the math:

• **MSTR**: (1 + 10% ) x (1 – 10%) = 1.1 x 0.9 = 0.99, a 1% loss

• **MSTU**: (1 + 20%) x (1 – 20%) = 1.2 x 0.8 = 0.96, a4% loss.

Thus, over a two-day period, MSTU’s losses are **4x the loss of MSTR**, not 2x. This effect occurs because leveraged ETFs rebalance daily, which can amplify compounding losses over time.

So, be cautious with these types of ETFs. If you want leverage, a better approach might be to go long on MSTR using margin.

### Marshall_Sponder — 2024-12-25T05:51:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/friendly-warning-about-leveraged-etfs-like-mstu-and-mstx.30015/post-344325>

> [Patrick S. said:](/goto/post?id=344317)
>
> I’m currently listening to the December ’24 PowWow where the 2x leveraged ETFs for MSTR (MSTX and MSTU) were discussed. Here are my thoughts:
>
> These types of ETFs provide **2x performance only on a daily basis**, not over longer periods (e.g., two days or more). They are designed as **day-trading vehicles**, not for long-term holding.
>
> Here’s why: For a 2x Bull ETF like MSTU, if MSTR goes up 10% on one day and then falls 10% the next day, **the two-day loss for MSTR is 1%, but for MSTU, it’s 4%.**
>
> Here’s the math:
>
> • **MSTR**: (1 + 10% ) x (1 – 10%) = 1.1 x 0.9 = 0.99, a 1% loss
>
> • **MSTU**: (1 + 20%) x (1 – 20%) = 1.2 x 0.8 = 0.96, a4% loss.
>
> Thus, over a two-day period, MSTU’s losses are **4x the loss of MSTR**, not 2x. This effect occurs because leveraged ETFs rebalance daily, which can amplify compounding losses over time.
>
> So, be cautious with these types of ETFs. If you want leverage, a better approach might be to go long on MSTR using margin.

That's what I heard too, I ended up going with MSTR because I still a newbie at this - just wish I had bought the required amount last Friday instead of last Monday - oh well, let's hope and pray it goes way, way up in 2025~

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-344355>

Hello All,

I saw Uncle Jack's post about a deviated septum from 2019. I am looking for some feedback about surgery from Uncle Jack and the forum.

Do you have a deviated septum? Did you have surgery? Why or why not? Has anything helped (besides redox, sun, etc)? I have read the blogs relating to this and learned a lot but still contemplating the best approach to all the congestion and night wake ups.
I don't know if this is something that requires a consult, but I thought I would start by putting my question here. A little bit about my situation. An ENT recently confirmed a "moderate" deviated septum and swollen/inflamed nasal turbinates. I knew this but wanted to get a specialist's opinion. Of course, he recommends surgery.

What I have tried besides Jack's protocol:
\-Salt, saltwater rinses
\-All nasal rinses
\-No dairy
\-Elevated bed
\-Nasal strips
\-Mouth taping at night (still do this as it helps the mouth breathing)
\-Chiropractic help
\-Allergy medications

I am weighing the pros and cons of surgery. My biggest concern is I have to go under the anesthesia which is a concern for someone withs Alzheimer’s with my mom and all her sisters. It is very strong in my family history. Would I be better off with the surgery? I have read the blogs related to this issue (hypoxia blogs, sleep blogs) and some threads. My conventional ENT does not think I will get better without surgery. Jack's description of a deviated septum is me except for the nosebleeds and I don’t think I have sleep apnea. I don't snore, I don’t wake up gasping for air and feel 80-85% rested when I wake up. I have noticed slightly elevated BPM (60’s). The 2-3 wake ups to urinate are probably preventing deeper sleep and longer REM. I temporarily wore an Oura Ring and my sleep scores were 82-88 for what it is worth. Deep sleep is around an hour as well REM. I have persistent congestion or a runny nose, sinus headaches (improving!). It is hard to breath through my nose when I exercise but can when I clear my nose, and I am not exercising.

I have been following Jack's protocol for approximately 3.5 years and seen improvement in many areas. My Mom came from Italy (37 latitude) and I live in Ohio (41). I never miss sunrise/sunset, frequent walks outside, a great tan in the spring/summer/fall (need to get a Cool Tan suit), darkness at night, blue blockers, reduction of nnEMF (little tv time, less time on my computer, Wi-Fi off at night, cold showers, face dunks, grounding, eating within an hour of sunrise, in the process of trying DDW etc.) in the hopes that I could solve the congestion and night wake ups.
Other than the congestion and night wake ups, I feel my health is better than most 59-year-olds with no autoimmune conditions or other diseases and no meds accept BHRT. Potential setbacks include lots of dental work, good size scar on my neck from a surgery when 5 years old, 20+ years of excessive blue light exposure and currently live within ½ mile of high-tension wires. In the past I had low Free T3 and high Reverse T3. Free T3 improved to normal with BHRT and light protocol. I am very tan 8 months out of the year, tan holds and semi-tan 4 months of the year.

In conclusion, the last thing I want is surgery, but I am concerned about hypoxia at night and wish the congestion during the day is a challenge. My Mom had Alzheimer's as well as her three sisters. The deviated septum is from my maternal side.
Any input, success, experience with a deviated septum is greatly appreciated!

Thanks! Anna

### rtd — 2024-12-26T19:41:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-344362>

> [annavargo said:](/goto/post?id=344355)
>
> Do you have a deviated septum? Did you have surgery? Why or why not? Has anything helped (besides redox, sun, etc)? I have read the blogs relating to this and learned a lot but still contemplating the best approach to all the congestion and night wake ups.
> I don't know if this is something that requires a consult, but I thought I would start by putting my question here. A little bit about my situation. An ENT recently confirmed a "moderate" deviated septum and swollen/inflamed nasal turbinates. I knew this but wanted to get a specialist's opinion. Of course, he recommends surgery.

I have a deviated septum (right nostril, almost fully closed off) from a car air bag. Pre jab, I had a nasal scan that showed the problem is structural and I did not have inflammation. I did not get the surgery, which I now regret. Post jab I have had respiratory issues. 2025 I plan to get a turbinate surgery so I can breath through my nose. BTW, my resting BPM are 80-95. Too high! I hope full nasal breathing will bring down my BPM. I do low, slow cardio like walking and cycling, zone 2. I used to do yoga with different nasal breathing, as well a breath of fire. I cannot do those breathing techniques effectively any more.

### Richard.B — 2025-02-10T19:29:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-346546>

I have a significant left deviated septum, resulting in near-complete obstruction of one of my nostril since childhood. This primarily manifests as intermittent breathing difficulties (characterized by one nostril being partially open while the other is largely obstructed). Apart from that, I don't have any other symptoms.
Not jabbed

My ENT told me that the only solution for this deviated septum would be surgery (septoplasty and turbinoplasty) and that it would substantially improve my quality of life with minimal risk of adverse effects. But I don't trust centralized medecine as they are incentives by the surgery.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) what would you do if you had a deviated septum that impeded your breathing ?

My apprehension arises from the possibility of physiological adaptation to the chronic airflow restriction or that resulted into this deviated septum. I am concerned that the resultant increase in nasal breathing following surgical intervention could lead to supraphysiological oxygen concentrations, potentially predisposing to an increase in free radical generation and oxidative stress.

### Adamo — 2025-02-11T04:44:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-346589>

Chiming in and would love to know about this as well, as I have several family members facing the same issue.

### meredith24 — 2025-02-12T19:11:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deviated-septum.30020/post-346635>

I also have a very deviated nasal septum. I catch myself mouth breathing often. I likely have UARS. My sleep scores are always terrible with Oura ring. I am considering functional septorhinoplasty but have not been convinced 100% yet.

I am currently reading Breathe through your nose don't pay for it and it emphasizes the use of rhinomanometry to measure flow to even determine if surgery would help. I am also going to read Dr. Kruse's viewpoints on the matter.

Curious to hear others thought processes and experiences.

### Anthony Smith — 2024-12-26T20:30:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coming-to-el-salvador.30021/post-344368>

If you stay in Atami or Shalpa you will meet many Mito’s on the beach at dawn.
I found most locals do not speak English but are happy and helpful with communications. A language app is an often used tool to bridge the gap.
Jack travels a lot so may or not be there at that time. Even if he is the odds of running into him are remote. My understanding is he does most (all) his consults over the phone/internet even with people who are in ES. Just so you know.

The new Cornet Quad for $200 (+/-) gets the job done. Buy BitCoin with the money you save . . .

How does one stay away from power lines, are you suggesting that houses should be build entirely off grid and never connected to power lines?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-13T20:05:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-ed88t.30022/post-350621>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=350588)
>
> How does one stay away from power lines, are you suggesting that houses should be build entirely off grid and never connected to power lines?

Off grid with an all DC system would be good but no that is not what I am saying.
IMO - Low voltage, wires to your house ok. Medium voltage 50-100ft ok,
High and extra voltage - stay away, way away (It is not just the electric and magnetic fields, they Ionize the air which then can drift hundreds of feet.

### 1\. **Low-Voltage (LV) Lines** ​

-   **Voltage Range**: Up to 1,000 volts (1 kV)
-   **Usage**: Primarily used for distributing electricity directly to homes, small businesses, and streetlights.
-   **Examples**: Residential power distribution (e.g., 120/240 V in the US).

### 2\. **Medium-Voltage (MV) Lines**​

-   **Voltage Range**: 1 kV to 69 kV
-   **Usage**: Distribute electricity from substations to neighborhoods or industrial areas.
-   **Examples**: Power to local industrial plants or commercial complexes.

### 3\. **High-Voltage (HV) LinesVoltage Range**: 69 KV To 230 KV​

-   **Usage**: Transmit electricity over moderate distances, typically between cities or to larger substations.
-   **Examples**: Regional power transmission lines.

### 4\. **Extra-High Voltage (EHV) Lines**​

-   **Voltage Range**: 230 kV to 765 kV
-   **Usage**: Transmit electricity over long distances between power plants and major substations.
-   **Examples**: National grid lines for intercity or interstate transmission.

Solar panels are the go-to for off-grid homes. What's the mitochondriac alternative?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-23T07:21:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-ed88t.30022/post-351139>

Solar panels CAN also BE the mitochondriac alternative - IF - DC solar panels feed into a DC power grid in the home (no DC to AC to DC inverters) . Most of our gadgets and gizmos run on DC so why have the “AC“ middleman?

### Rusty — 2025-04-23T19:12:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-ed88t.30022/post-351159>

> [solsearcher said:](/goto/post?id=351138)
>
> Solar panels are the go-to for off-grid homes. What's the mitochondriac alternative?

Solar panels themselves are almost silent on the nnEMF front. The trouble starts with the electronics that manage & monitor them, especially the inverter and any wireless reporting gear. Dirty electricity becomes the heavyweight concern, followed by occasional RF bursts that send performance data to the installer or the utility. Magnetic and electric fields are usually minimal if wiring is done in metal conduit and the current on the outgoing and return conductors is balanced.

\- Pick a low-noise inverter
\- Whole-house DE filter at the panel (Satic Power Perfect Box, PQFilter)
\- Park the inverter outdoors, in metal conduit

Enphase micro-inverters & SolarEdge HD Wave string inverters put out the least Dirt electricity compared with most competitors; Sunny Boy from SMA in Germany is also relatively quiet.

# Thread: Low EMF WiFi Router (Don't buy one)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-emf-wifi-router-dont-buy-one.30023/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rusty — 2024-12-27T09:22:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-emf-wifi-router-dont-buy-one.30023/post-344380>

**Today let's talk about the JRS eco Low Radiation WiFi router that boasts**

— [90% reduced pulse frequency](https://www.jrseco.com/faqs/eco-wifi-90-reduced-pulse-rate/) compared to ordinary wifi routers
— [Full Eco standby mode with measurably zero wifi radiation](https://www.jrseco.com/faqs/the-jrs-eco-100-wifi-router-100-radiation-free-in-stand-by/) when no wireless devices are connected
— Auto sleep mode

**{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**

It all sounds like a groundbreaking leap forward in reducing WiFi radiation.

_Or is it?_

Let's see..

**Claim Number 1:**

_**All models of JRS Eco Wireless routers cut down on electrosmog by reducing the beacon pulse frequency by 90%**_

**What is a beacon pulse?**

A beacon is a specific control signal sent out by the router (technically an access point) to let nearby devices know that the router exists and that they can connect to it. This signal is a short pulse that’s transmitted continuously, usually about ten times per second (10 Hz), and often at full transmitting power. It’s important to remember that this is just a control signal—it doesn’t have anything to do with the actual data being sent to or from the router. In other words, it’s just one part of the overall radiation coming from the whole Wi-Fi system you’ve set up, including all the devices connected to it.

AP = Access Point (Router)

**The Problem**

The smart engineers behind the eco-router decided to set the beacon signal to transmit once per second (1 Hz) instead of every 0.1 second (10 Hz), and then claimed they were “reducing electrosmog by cutting the beacon pulse frequency by 90%.” The truth is, this is a pretty simple trick because the Wi-Fi specification doesn’t strictly define the beacon pulse frequency. Most devices use a 10 Hz frequency to make it quicker for devices to find and connect to the router.

As a result of this “smart” tweak, you’re now getting one beacon pulse every second instead of 10, which means the average power from the beacon pulses is reduced by 90%.

If you check the two major biological standards, (1) EUROPAEM 2016 and (2) Building Biology Standard 2015, you’ll see that biological effects are tied to the peak power of the pulsed signal, _not the average power._ This is why we need the Peak-Hold (or Max-Hold) function in meters to properly assess the biological effects of pulsed digital signals.

So, has the peak power of the beacon pulse changed with the “eco” router? No.

Another crucial parameter for evaluating pulsed signals and their biological effects is the **PAPR (Peak to Average Power Ratio)**. This metric highlights the abrupt changes in signal strength with each pulse. EMF sources like radars and smart meters exhibit particularly high PAPR values because they emit extremely short, powerful pulses followed by periods of almost no radiation. As a result, while the average power remains low, the peak power during each pulse is exceptionally high. The shorter the pulses and the longer the intervals between them, the greater the PAPR.

Research underscores the significant biological impact of such signals. For instance, when engineers designing eco-routers reduced the average power of beacon signals by 90% but left the peak power unchanged, they inadvertently _**increased the PAPR of these router beacons 10X**_. Smart design? Not quite.

Let's use the example of Chinese Water Torture

Imagine each water drop represents a single beacon pulse. If the interval between drops is extended from one second to ten seconds, does it transform the experience into "eco-torture," "safe-torture," or "smart-torture"? According to the marketers of these devices, they’ve “reduced the torture by cutting the water drop frequency by 90%.” Feeling relieved? You shouldn’t be.

Dimitris J. Panagopoulos has published extensive research quantifying the biological effects of man-made EMF radiation. He demonstrates how pulsed signals affect cellular ionic channels. [In the paper from January 2020](https://www.emfsa.co.za/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/Panagopoulos-et-al-Sensing-of-Earthquakes-STOTEN-2020-3.pdf), he presents the mathematical model for assessing the bioactivity of the pulsed electromagnetic fields:

_**Pulsed EMF-Bioactivity = 2k E0 / ν**

where k is a constant quantity for any specific ion channel (i.e., Ca, Na) in the vicinity of a voltage-gated ion channel in a cell membrane, E0 the intensity amplitude, and νthe frequency of the applied electric field pulse._

The equation shows that the EMF pulsed radiation's bioactivity is proportional to the **electric field pulse maximum amplitude** and inversely proportional to the **electric field pulses' frequency.**

Now the engineers behind these low EMF routers was to reduce the frequency of the beacon pulsed signal 10X, _without changing its maximum amplitude_.

Taking the above equation and reducing the pulse frequency 10X inevitably results in **rising the bioactivity of the signal 10X

_The 90% reduction in beacon pulse frequency is a clever marketing spin that overlooks the increased bioactivity caused by higher PAPR. Instead of reducing biological impact, this adjustment may exacerbate it._**

This was claim number 1. Next we will talk about claim #2

The bottom line is the root 'chakra' of a person - the bottom of the spine, the top is the crown 'chakra', and if we were writing by hand, there'd be a ---------------- line in the middle if we were doing calligraphy, thats the heart 'chakra'. The bottom half is therefore the 'personality self'/lower 3 chakras, and the top half of the line is the higher 3 chakras, and the heart being the centre-point that transfers info between the two.
The circular shapes in the alphabet are the feminine/receptive attributes of the character, and the straight line shapes are the masculine/transmitting shapes.
So when a baby screams at the top of its lungs 'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa' when its crying out, it's really expressing its receptivity to the physical world through the lower three chakras; as demonstrated by the circle there. When someone demonstrates their opinion of something that someone else cannot see, but is my own informed perception of the world, and only 'I' can see it (straight line character) - this is discerning and discriminatory, I draw the line down from the crown to earth.
And you can get a feel for each character about what they show/express if you just imagine them emanating from your spine; that's really all this is. Takes a fair bit of practice though and it's weird at first because no one taught us this as a skill.
so the 'g' descending is important because it's the first letter to have fallen, and if it hadn't had fallen; the circular shape would be 'receptive' to the higher self. The Power 'p' would have been able to stand, erect, Percieving from it's place on-high. The 'q' would be upstanding and PROTECTED by the line in-front of it, and the 'y' would be able to give the reasons in the past and future, in the higher world's as to why it's standing here and now. The 'f' would be able to start from the ground and do its leap upwards, instead of trying to jump up from below its starting point, and the 'j' can do it 'j'ump for 'j' to the point from the ground instead of below it.
Point is, this changes everything because the machine-code has been updated. And this will help bring awareness to what needs to be said and expressed if we're to bring a new world about. Try writing in your notes like this for 30 days and see if you can go back.
This is about character development, and if 5 billion people are using the wrong shapes in their mind, then 5 billion people are reinforcing the wrong character development that the western world is 'supposed' to play out.

### LuciizaP — 2024-12-31T23:01:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-344571>

Next up! Let's make our language get to the point waaaaay faster (less letters used), and with more depth of expression (taller characters, that top half of the line). The way we do this is by adding in capital letters: the things that we already have, but for some reason only use for the beginnings of names or places or beginnings of sentences. It's almost as if this has been right in front of our eyes the whole time and its just been waiting to be discovered! No new characters either; we know them all and use them all, but with no logical useage.
It's gonna feel a bit strange at first as you're a beginner at this, but stick with it and your mind with be far more one-pointed and authoritative in its expression.
So for the sake of typing on a keyboard: g is now 9 (kinda looks like an uplifted g, so we'll use it), a lower-case p is now a capital one - P, q = Q, y = Y, j = J. obviously youd just use the uplifted lower case characters and not the capital ones, but as I said, keyboards are limited.
So now we'll have 52 characters of expression instead of only 26 to spell things - that's an extra 26 more characters to express ourselves in all the ways we want to. The capital ones are the one where you can express your self more fully and directly and passionately and 'off the cuff' - we aren't thinking about things and mulling over them to say - we're getting straight to the point!
so a, b, d, e are pronounced ah, buh, deh, eh etc. - likes the way babies say the letters (c is ommited, because its the same sound as k; that's just one of the rules, which we can go into later). A, B, D, E is just like the normal alphabet song letters: ay, bee, dee, eee etc.

shO = show
9lO = glow
fO = foe

### LuciizaP — 2025-01-19T22:32:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-345411>

"Pfff, this is just some weird way of typing the same thing in shorthand".
I can't convince you until you experience it for yourself, because that's what it is - felt experience. These characters are used to sense the world both spatially and temporally.
N9l is an electromagnetic language and the updates it brings is one that makes one electric/magnetic depending on any given circumstance. Once again, I believe it has been given to us to discover 'under our noses this whole time' and it's fitting that I'm revealing it to you all on this forum given its topics.
So you don't have to write/type using it, or any of that (until you naturally want to, of course). But do this one thing when you listen to videos on your tech: convert what you hear into the words you'd see in English in your minds eye. Then update the characters into N9l. Do this as much as you can persistently day by day, even if its just a few sentences at a time in a day when listening to a video (I wouldn't recommend it in real life interactions; that is filled with too much sensory info as it is! especially when practicing). More N9l example words to come. Just practice with the vowels for now. You won't be able to keep up at the pace in which people are speaking as you'll be figuring out how to spell out the N9l words and updating it spatially, but that's OK. Try and go as fast as you can, and you'll get faster. Enjoy the rewiring!

### LuciizaP — 2025-03-01T15:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-347642>

Magic E
Why is the word 'dove' (the bird) pronounced like 'duv', but the word 'hope' pronounced more like it sounds?
From what I've laid out already, in N9l, you can get rid of that pointless e on the end. the character 'e' is an emergent, amplifier word. a power word. take a look at these adjectives ending with e

1.  Amuse
2.  Awake
3.  Blaze
4.  Cope
5.  Dine
6.  Dose
7.  Dove
8.  Enrage
9.  Escape
10.  Gape
11.  Impose
12.  Invade
13.  Love
14.  Misuse
15.  Observe
16.  Pose
17.  Rave
18.  Reuse
19.  Sieve
20.  Survive
     If those aren't powerful words, I dont know what are!
     But we're talking about the corrected language here - so there still need to be emphasis on those strange words like dove, above, glove, have, lose.
     This is the part of the story where the language evolves into a 'strange' pronunciation (atleast initially it sounds strange!). dove is now dOv,... abOv, 9lOv, hAv, lOs. so it sounds like "d'oh'v", "ab'oh'v", "gl'oh'v", "h'ay'v", "l'oh's". this way we cut the crap of the extra e, whilst empowering the word with the vowel sound like e intends.

a-9 (a to g)
Is it a coincidence that the first fallen characer is the 'g', and it's capital version is the 'G' - the character to show the heart of spiral; the masculine and feminine in balance? What happens when we raise the 'g' so it's a '9'? Well, we can achieve spiral dynamics when we act upon what it is showing us.
a-9 is a progression of that little circle we first see in the 'a' (circle in bottom half of dotted line in the centre), to its uplifted state, which is '9' (circle in the top half of the dotted line in the centre). This is the first time the feminine circle shape appears in the top half of the dotted line. It is therefore the journey from being open/feminine in the 'lower' world (not that it's 'lower' in a hierarchical sense, but it is more to do with 'physicality') to that of the 'higher' world (nonphysical/nonlocal/etheral senses).

fasinAtin9 wot a fYU sQi9ls kan dU tu chAn9 PPls lIvs. thats BkOs thAY Arnt Just sQI9ls, thAY'r si9ils.
(fYU because the Y is really just a lower case y but uplifted, which cant be done on the keyboard, U = ooo, see previous post)
(sQi9ls, Q is lower case q but uplifted, which cant be done on keytboard)
(PPls = second p is a lower case because of keyboard)
(BkOs = O, which is the sound the 'au' in because changes to because the e is a 'magic e' and capitalises the vowel before it, which the closest sound)
(thAY = the upper case Y is a y because of the keyboard, and gives emphasis after the A, to give a more 'proper pronunciation sound')
(Just = just, J because of the keyboard).

Now imagine you're a baby - an intense world you're feeling all around you, and suddenly you have an intense desire for some MILK! aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa! The Centre. It sees just like your own vision - more information further away - in this case, a gradual progression down to the lower-mind, where at the bottom where the two lines stop. It see's all.

### LuciizaP — 2025-05-31T14:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-353632>

Next up is b/B.
Afer that a comes b. So there's still the circle in the lower world i.e. receptivity. But when before drawing that circle, we go up in that straight line at the very beginning to touch the top line (on the left side), then come back down to make that circle. Now with the sound 'b', pronounced like 'buh' - it's kind of a weak sound. Emotionally reliant. For example, (I'm not saying this is the case with all words), but the beginning letter of a lot of words are a reflection of their qualities... but baby is bugging me for my boobs, or should I give them the bottle? This analogy is used because of the 'aaaaa' sound above of a baby crying out for milk. That's what that line at the beginning represents - the higher world of the boobs/bottle being able to provide the milk. However, because it's on the left side (which represents the past); it is indicative that this is a reliable thing to quench the thirst of the 'aaaaa' - it has been provided many times in the past - it can look 'up there' to the caressing and nurture of the mother to provide the boobs/bottle.

On to the B.
Well, this one is encoded into the very word itself 'Be', as in to be in non-resistance in the present moment. Non-judgement, perfectly divine, eyes-closed-in-meditation, nothing needing to happen, just Beingness. This is represented in the circle hanging off the line in the higher as well as lower worlds. This time, no boobs/bottle are needed. You've matured. The sense of Beingness IS the boobs/bottle. That higher circle is receptivity, balanced by the receptivity of the lower world = aah, I've been in this difficult situation before, I need to remember to just 'B'; I can accept my fate in the lower-struggle realm, as well as completely be non-resistant in the higher-realm, and realise that to stop is the balance that is required here.

### LuciizaP — 2025-06-07T16:22:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-354188>

c/C

Imagine all the lowercase letters are written by hand. All lower case letters in cursive start off on the bottom line and rise up. So for that c, there's a bit before it that rises up to that top bit of the c.
c is the anomoly letter in this whole way of writing/speaking.
As a word, it's 'see' or 'sea'. What is the shape doing? It's 'seeing' whats below it, but never really interacting with it in anyway. It's closed, and non-participative - it goes to that top bit, see's the 'ground's' state, and then curves back down to that state. So it bypasses any sort of function.
The only time c is used in N9l is next to the letter h, e.g. in church, chat, cheek etc. - it cannot get out without that h. All of the words that nonsensically have either 'c' or 'k' sounds are now just all 'k's', so now cut becomes kut, cab becomes kab etc.
This is because we don't want to use the 'c' (or 'C') any more because it is a non-participative word. The whole point of N9l is to not 'see' anything (dualistic), but to be as, with, in, immersed.
So we don't need C either - this shape represents seeing from a higher source. So don't mistakenly use C when you hear is e.g. Cd = seed, no - it's sEd, Cl = seal, no it's sEl.

### LuciizaP — 2025-06-10T21:32:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-354447>

d/D
I'm going to start typing in N9l from now on. If you've made it this far your interest is piqued anyway, so it's time to start diving in! If you have any question on why a letter is a particular way, just feel free to ask. I see from this point on being the great filter - yes it's a pain to read >at first<, but it gets easier. It's more aesthetically pleasing in written form too when the f, j, g, p, q, y are in their proper forms and you can feel the relationship easier between the joined up letters. But anyway..
One more rule I need to say before continuing - when 'e' and 'a' are next to each other, it's always pronounced as the 'ee' sound, which in N9l is 'E'. For example, read --> rEd - I lIke rEdin9 books. lead --> lEd - YU lEd the wAY!
But I can also say read and lead for the past e.g. I've read book/you lead us that way. So that'd simply be: I'v red that book/YU led us that wAY.
Another thing is emphasise the Y. You could just say 'sA' for 'say', but because N9l is all about proper pronunciation, 'sAY' would the emphasis with that biting 'Y' sound. So without further ado...

e/E
'e' is the furst tIme that a JumP is atMPted. wE sE thu ful JumP lAter on in the '9/G' - wich wEl 9et tU. its like a mini/PraktIse atMPt at 'rIsin9 uP' tU thu chalN9e! wE sE it flik uP and then cirkle bak rownd after its atMPt.
wN this is achEved wE sE a fowndAtional shAPe that is solid on all thrEe leveLs - thu lOwer, thu hIer, and thu intermEDArY mIdle bit - thu 'E'! E is 9rownded on the Earth, thu sPirit, and thu hEart! it sEs all Platforms ov oPerAtion and haz rFerence PoYns from thu PRst of how to 'B' at NE stA9e. it iz an EQualIzin9 leter in that is mAks sYURe that thu lOwer, mIdle PoYnt and hI9her Are all rLatIve tU Echother. if thu small 'e' is suksSful in its JumPin9, it then has the rFerense PoYnts nEded to sE whAre thu hI9her lIn nEds tu B Plased Bkoz thAY Are rLatIv tU the lOwer Ones!
this lEds us nIslY onwards tU thu f/F and thN...... thu 9/G!

### LuciizaP — 2025-07-11T22:48:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-356192>

OK well I think it's probably best that I write stuff in English and have the N9l translation so people can translate as they go, without sacrificing N9l's integrity.

So I'll translate the above:
When I say "DP, Dd, Dl?" it's not really talking about the capital D there, it's talking about the line coming off the circle in the lower case d - it's about endings. A Deep Deed is often a risk, a gamble - to cut something off, or do something a bit radical that you wouldn't usually do, when that is achieved, you become the D - as if the full radar and area is now available to you now that you've cut away the unnecessary.

e/E
e is the first time a jump is attempted. We see the full jump later on in the 9/G, which we'll get to. It's like a mini/practice attempt at 'rising up' to the challenge! We see it flick up and then circle back round after it attempt/ When this is achieved we see a foudnational shape that is solid on all three levels - the lower, the higher, and the intermediary middle bit - the 'E'! E is grouned on the earth, the spirit, and the heart! It sees all platforms of operation and has reference poynts from the past of how to 'B' at any stage. It is an equalizing letter in that it makes sure that the lower, middle point and higher are all relative to eachother. If the small 'e' is successful in its jumping, it then has the reference points needed to see where the higher line needs to be placed because they are relative to the lower ones!

### LuciizaP — 2025-08-01T18:00:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-357335>

Now we get to f/F.
A slingshot upwards. Flinging - Flying - Feel it? The e was just the practice run, but the 'f' is when we are moving on up towards the higher half, the higher self. Before the jump, there's a little flick down and then up again before drawing the line up (if you were to do it in hand-writing). The line then goes all the way to the top, and then with a little flick with a 'blow off top'. The same flick is like digging yer heels in before leaping and the blow off top is like the mirror of that.
The F is the completion of that - two lines this time unlike the capital E; no bottom line. The bottom line isn't needed any more which gave the groundedness to the three lines - how can one be grounded when we're flinging upwards?? Let's go!!
now wE 9et tU f/F.
A slin9shot uPwards. flin9in9 - flYin9 - fEl it? thu e was Just thu PraktIs run, but thu 'f' iz wN wE Ar mOvin9 on uP tuwords thu hIer half, thu hIer sLf. bFOr thu JumP, thEr's a lItl flik down and thN uP a9An BfOr drawin9 thu lIn uP (if YU wEr tu dU it in hand-wrItin9). thU lIn thN 9Os all thu wAY tu thu toP, thN with A lItl flik with A 'blO of toP'. thu sAm flik iz lIk di9in9 Yer hEls in bFOr lEPin9 and thu blO of toP iz lIk thu miror ov that.
thu F iz thu komPlEshun ov that - tU lIns this tIm unlIk thu kaPital E; nO botom lIn. thu botom lIn isn't nEded NE mOr wich 9Av thu 9rowndednS tU thu thrE lIns - how kan On B 9rownded wN wE'r flin9in9 uPwards?? let's 9O!!

### LuciizaP — 2025-08-06T22:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-357695>

And now....... the post you've all been waiting for. We arrive .... at the '9/G' - woohoo!
All the work so far from a to f has been in preparation for getting that circle from the lower half of the lined page to the upper half - we now finally have it in it's righteous place, where it should be. Obviously ours has descended 'g' and so never actually arrives this uplifted place. Let's recap:
a - open and receptive circle 'clinging on' to the waveform
b - open and receptive circle 'clinging on' to the lined-structure in-the-past
d - open and receptive circle 'defended' by eliminating the unnecessary future
e - first attempt at a leap, a 'safe' leap
f - flight and full-fledged leap
Remember: the top and bottom vertical direction signifies space - higher space and lower space
the horizontal direction signifies time - left the past, right the future and the middle, now.

more to come

### LuciizaP — 2025-08-08T15:23:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-357823>

The 'g' has been uplifted now in N9l to '9'. But with this uplifted version, you do not start writing it with the circle and then descend down to the tail. No, no. You start at the tail and uplift it to draw the circle last. This signifies the upliftment of the circle shape from the lower world to the higher world - open and receptive to it.
Now let's put it in context for you and your life. What higher purpose have you cut yourself off from? Maybe you had a dream when you were a kid in school and you abandoned it. Why? Maybe not solely because of the descended 'g', but because you may have encountered 300 kids on a playground who had all learnt the descended 'g', as well as the conditioning from their parents who had all learnt the descended 'g'. Relating to this forum, they didn't receive the light, water and magnetism guidance to be high dopamine enough to encourage their kids to go for what they were designed by god/nature to do. Any initial attempts at creativity might've been shut down, ridiculed, be criticized unfairly by arrogant 'authorities'. There are a million ways to spin it and a web of interconnectivity that may have shut down ambition - whether that be a long-term plan, a decision to try something new in the moment, or anything in between; it doesn't matter. As long as you and others have had that descended 'g' - the receptivity hasn't been able to reach the higher world, the higher self which absolutely KNOWS the most optimal path for you because your intuition feels it in your very bones, your very soul.
That's why the uplifted version is the 'G' - the heart of the spiral. The descended 'g' knows nothing of the spiral because it's completely out of relationship with the context of mind/body/feeling in-the-now. The uplifted '9' is perfectly aligned and can see where to go and what to do be essence of feeling. The spiral is a gradually progressive shape - not too easy and not too difficult. Ones grows day after day, week after week, month after month and year after year. But it's consistent - it's spiralling round a little bit at a time. That's satisfying. That's fulfilling. Improving what you want to improve in your life, and letting life unravel as you see each time you come round the spiral to move on to bigger and bigger heights. That's how nature intended all things to be.
Unforunately we rarely if ever get to that point.

### LuciizaP — 2025-09-25T10:50:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-360029>

Take a seat...

h/H

h
The seat's a little bumpy, but it's good enough. You can relax now you've realized the 9/G. Quite stable - back legs and fore legs.

H
Bridge between the past and future, higher and lower. Ultimate stability. The past can now be bridged to the future.

### LuciizaP — 2025-10-25T18:13:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shape-shift.30024/post-361596>

now lets start embodying this 9 shall we?

i/I
People would think that when you're talking about 'I' it's related to ego - something generally considered negative. Although people in recent times (atleast from what I've seen on social media) have taken a contrary stance on this, and rightly so. Let's take it in it's simplicity - "I'm going to the toilet" - there was a need there to communicate this, stand up, and walk in the direction of the socially acceptable place to urinate to alleviate the pressure down there. What would the consequence of not doing this be? Well, you'd be in pain... for longer.
Why would you want to be in pain for longer? Well, adhereing to social rules probably. You didn't want to get up whilst they were speaking. It's best to finish a meal before getting up. Getting up now might be rude.
Are those things true? It's not for me to decide, only the 'I' in you.

# Thread: New member as of today

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2

### boerekamp — 2024-12-30T18:17:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-as-of-today.30032/post-344484>

Hi All,
Let me shortly introduction myself:
I am born and raised in The Netherlands.
Studied Chemical Engineering at the Technical University Eindhoven.
Specialization Material Science.
Worked for Philips Electronics (&Lumileds) in The Netherlands and Germany for almost 30 years in several R&D and production facilities mainly in Ceramic materials, Glass and Metals:
1\. Soft magnetic material development (ferrites) used in inductors
2\. Sintered Bioceramics (HOAp) for implants
3\. Glass and glass to metal seals for Incandescent, Fluorescent lighting, High pressure sodium SON and CDM. a.o.
4\. Fluorescent materials for Energy saving lamps and LED lighting.
5\. Scintillator materials for Medical Systems, CT, PET and SPECT.

Retired a few years ago and currently mainly working in and around my residence, my vegetable garden and perennial garden.
Furthermore I like to ride my off road bike

That's it for now,
Rgds, Jack

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-30T22:54:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-doge-elon-and-vivek.30036/post-344514>

Where do Elon and Epstein collide?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-01-01T14:50:05+0200

U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) engages in radar and atmospheric research, but for legitimate military and scientific purposes. Projects like HAARP were initially DoD-funded but are now research-oriented under the University of Alaska Fairbanks. Agencies like the CIA are known for their work in psychological operations and disinformation. High-frequency Active Auroral Research Program (HAARP):

One of the most discussed technologies is HAARP, which uses high-frequency radio waves to temporarily excite a limited area of the ionosphere. While HAARP is primarily for research on the ionosphere, conspiracy theories often allege its use in weather manipulation, communication disruption, or creating visual phenomena. These are facilities like HAARP that can modify ionospheric conditions. The manipulation can lead to changes in radar echoes or signals, but the technology is far from creating complex images or holograms.

Yet. They say... Then there's Project Blue Beam. Project Blue Beam is a conspiracy theory proposed by Serge Monast in the 1990s suggesting that advanced technology would be used to fake an alien invasion or religious events to usher in a new world order. It includes the use of holograms and mind control. There's no credible evidence that such a project exists or was ever implemented by any government agency. But with the technological advances that are just being shown today, the government itself is usually 20 to 30 years ahead of what the population is actually aware of.

Directed Energy Weapons (DEW) and Sky Projections DEWs include lasers, microwave, and particle beam technologies used for precision targeting. While they could theoretically interact with the atmosphere, their use for creating visible illusions or manipulating radar on a grand scale is speculative. Israel announced their new “Iron Beam” weapon was being deployed, which you can see in action at the beginning of this video. It's the first energy based weapon in world history. The weapon uses a high-powered laser to destroy a projectile from the moment of its launch. The cost of each pulse is $2.50.

The U.S. is also spending billions and already has active armament of lasers/ DEWs attached to everything you can imagine, from airplanes to jets, tanks, and also mounted on ships. If they had the technology in space, on satellites, do you think they would tell the American people? While the technologies to manipulate the ionosphere, radar, and project images exist or are theorized, their application in the manner described by conspiracy theories like Project Blue Beam are still considered deep black projects and not a lot of information is declassified or available to the public yet. Other weapons are in development that can manipulate human behavior and even project voices into people's minds.

Yes, all this sounds like science fiction, but the technology is real, and other industry other than the military are also getting their hands on this technology for business and advertising purposes. One thing to take into consideration is all the warnings from prominent people of the past, warning about project blue beam and other dangerous technologies. Especially people like Werner Von Braun, father of rocketry itself and director at NASA who was brought to the U.S. through Operation Paperclip.
