### Jack Kruse — 2025-01-08T16:22:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-doge-elon-and-vivek.30036/post-344885>

Why does Trump want Greenland & Eastern Canada? Think Lousiana Purchase or Alaska. Did you know that the oldest crustal portions on Earth exist in Eastern Cananda and Greenland. Crust that is 4 billions years old and has not been subducted as yet iremains in this area and it means that it is cversion territory for exotic minerals that the Big Tech addicts around Trump need to run their monopolies. **The** **oldest** substantial chunk of **Earth**’s **crust** **has** been found in **Greenland**, and dates back at least 3.8 billion years. The find is important because it is of a type known as an ophiolite – _Ophiolite_ is thrust sheets of ancient [oceanic crust](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/earth-and-planetary-sciences/oceanic-crust) and upper part of mantle rocks that have been uplifted and exposed above sea level and often emplaced on top of the [continental lithosphere](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/earth-and-planetary-sciences/continental-lithosphere). These layers are untouched by modern man. This means they contain rich mineral depositis associated with igneous rocks. Ophiolite is composed of green-colored altered spilite (fine-grained oceanic basalt), [pillow lava](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/earth-and-planetary-sciences/pillow-lava), [serpentinites](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/earth-and-planetary-sciences/serpentinite), gabbros, and [chert](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/earth-and-planetary-sciences/chert). It occurs close to the oceanic ridges, [orogenic belt](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/earth-and-planetary-sciences/orogenic-belt), within mountain belts of Alps and Himalayas.

# Thread: FOR THE PEOPLE WHO THINK VIRUSES DO NOT EXIST...........

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T14:45:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344543>

I call them Koch heads... bring your hurt. I a, glad you folks exist as we need people questioning everything. However, I have found the “virus doesn’t exist” arguments unconvincing scientifically. The virus isn’t the only cause of disease may have some ground. I remain open to new papers on this.

Set some rules. This was established in 1884. Watson & Crick discovered DNA structure in 1953. He did not contemplate unculturable organisms... the vast majority of organisms are not culture-able. Viruses by definition cannot be cultured. They require a host cell to culture.

What are Koch's postulates?

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T14:48:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344544>

This is where the debate gets dirty. In order to culture the virus you need to find cells/organisms it can infect but not be a pathogen to those cells. But Koch wants to demonstrate the pathogen creates symptoms of the disease which be counterproductive to culture.

This is like the uncertainty principle. The cell line/model organism you choose to culture the virus, can’t be extremely affected by the virus or the culture won’t succeed. Therefore the vector for the disease is unlikely to be the best place to look for the disease symptoms.

As a result we have folks discounting Vero cell (monkey kidney cells) studies for viral isolation. By this same logic, all animal models are thus insufficient as well. Any argument that Vero cells are not identical to patients must also reject all animal models.

Medical ethics prevents us from infecting real patients with a virus suspected of pandemic potential. This leaves us with Ex-vivo culture of sick and healthy patients samples. Fairly invasive research. Needs IRB approval. [https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lanres/article/PIIS2213-2600(20)30193-4/fulltext](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lanres/article/PIIS2213-2600\(20\)30193-4/fulltext)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T14:49:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344545>

This extracts epithelial cells from patients respiratory tract and cultures those cells as a model for viral infection and replication. It has been done in COVID-19 and I’m open to Koch Folks scrutinizing it’s short comings.

Ideally, we would have highly purified virus from the diseased and be able to put it into a human cell line and demonstrate replication and illness and re-isolate and confirm its the same. Since we can’t infect humans, we resort to human cell lines.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T14:52:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344546>

We have new tools for understanding purity today. We can sequence all RNA in a patient and see the complete virome present. Based on the sequence we can predict proteins we should see with Mass spectrometry after successful viral replication in a host.

### [Shotgun Transcriptome and Isothermal Profiling of SARS-CoV-2 Infection Reveals Unique Host Responses, Viral Diversification, and Drug Interactions](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.04.20.048066v5)

The Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) has caused thousands of deaths worldwide, including >18,000 in New York City (NYC) alone. The sudden emergence of this pandemic has highlighted a pressing clinical need for rapid, scalable diagnostics that can detect infection...

 www.biorxiv.org

Masons lab goes on to build beautiful whole transcriptome pictures of each SARs patient. Over 96% of the patients had C19 sequence in their Bronchial Lavage (BAFL). This is not Vero cell culture. Patient cells. Whole SARs genomes from patients and it’s the dominant RNA present.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T14:54:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344547>

Some will say... you don’t know it’s the causative virus from this. Highest prevalence does not = causative agent. But since we have whole transcriptomes we can see there is no other sensible hypothesis which is why I call viral deniers Koch Heads. No other viruses present. The RNA has been isolated but not the virus.

On the flip side, images of fuzzy crowns on EM, I find unconvincing as well. You can’t identify a virus with a photograph. All taxonomy is ultimately DNA/RNA based. So how do we catch the virus in the act of infection?

Spatial transcriptomics- This is the art of sequencing in situ.

You infect cells with the purified virus and you perform spatial transcriptomics on those cells. This is a new, explosive and exciting field that SOLiD sequencing is still being used for.

[https://www.science.org/content/webinar/mining-transcriptome-using-spatial-transcriptomics-comprehensive-2d-3d-visualization-all-mrnas-tissue-sections](https://www.science.org/content/webinar/mining-transcriptome-using-spatial-transcriptomics-comprehensive-2d-3d-visualization-all-mrnas-tissue-sections)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T14:58:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344548>

It’s worth understanding the technique. It enables you to harvest RNA from cells and maintain their spatial coordinates in the cell with 10 um and eventually 1um resolution. It’s unlikely to get below the diffraction limit of light (250nm) in terms of resolution.

Here it was deployed on COVID-19 patient biopsies. We have spike protein histochemistry co-localized with C19 RNA sequence in infected patients. Not all biopsies succeeded. Lots of tissue heterogeneity but a good piece of the puzzle is present. [https://www.medrxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.11.04.20225557v1.full.pdf](https://www.medrxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.11.04.20225557v1.full.pdf)

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T15:00:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344549>

Now many are of the opinion that we need purified virus particle for the story to be complete. I think this statement is based on a perception of science stuck in 1884. Viruses evolved from Viroids. Viroids are just RNA. No capsid. Small RNA alone can infect cells.

[https://apsjournals.apsnet.org/doi/full/10.1094/PDIS-03-19-0530-PDN](https://apsjournals.apsnet.org/doi/full/10.1094/PDIS-03-19-0530-PDN)

So we have to keep our eyes on the code of the disease, not it’s wrapper. The code dictates its proteins and identity. The wrapper is a non-specific, low information content camouflage that provides nice eye candy for journalists but provides little signature for function or ID.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T15:01:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344550>

So when people say, “you haven’t purified it”. You can’t claim A causes B because you can’t assure me A is only A. This is misunderstanding what Whole transcriptomes do. When you sequence everything, you can then choose to synthesize the single RNA you think is causative.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T15:02:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344551>

his is as pure as you can get. Even more pure than a cesium gradient isolation of the particles as the biological isolation will have biological contaminants not found in a synthesized from scratch genome. We can also test the hypothesis. Knock out a gene and see what happens

### Jack Kruse — 2024-12-31T15:07:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-344554>

Here is an important detail in that paper. After inoculation, they track the viral genomes growth through qPCR. You can see it climbing in copy number and decay over time. The increase is important as it can't be explained by the dilution of the inoculation into the host. This means the RNA sequence replicated. This is definitive proof.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-14T16:08:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-the-people-who-think-viruses-do-not-exist.30040/post-346713>

LOVE CAN SHIFT YOUR WORLD.............AND GIVE YOU MORE TIME.

Today, is Valentine's Day. Here is some wisdom for those who I have loved in my life.

View gallery of the loves you’ve had in your life. For me, since 2020 I realized what made me a savage was how I loved my tribe of misfits. This forced me to do some unusual things. On July 4th, 2020 I began this journey in front of 120 of my decentralized savages. Today, you'll read where I am in that journey of love.

My wisdom is this today about your love: Assess the failures carefully. Why did YOU fail them? Adapt quickly once you get that answer.

Failure in love isn’t fatal, but failure to change might be damaging to your ability to love again. Fight like hell to get out alive to live again.

If you must record some greatness in your account before you leave this earth, you must value time and understand the importance of time conversion. You need to love to make this happen.

What do I sell people? Wisdom on how to maximize time.

In my opinion, one can become a great leader by multiplying yourself through time conversion into wisdom and ideas but that is only be possible if we understand the value of time. If those you love do not value their time, they are devaluing your time and they disrespect your love. It builds resentment. You can choose to stay for their pain or leave and reserve your time. I am sorry to say they’ll be no sacrifice today. I want to save my time and-give some love again to those whose time is coming to a close.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-32-77747149](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-32-77747149)

First principle thinking of the mitochondriac mindset is a shifting event. It requires one to embrace the suck of a heinous situation. This version of thinking is 100% stochastic and is a form of reasoning where you begin by identifying and understanding the fundamental principles of a problem or situation. Instead of relying on analogies or conventional wisdom, this approach encourages you to start with what you know to be true and reason up from there. 2 hours of talk from me to you about how to reserve your TIME IF YOU GOT JABBED.

### JHackett — 2025-03-07T02:14:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/app-for-red-screen.30045/post-348004>

### Mat.I — 2025-03-07T02:45:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/app-for-red-screen.30045/post-348008>

Re: flicker, the issue is the PWM dimming. There are still ways to minimise the flicker intensity (slightly via certain settings). Noting phones on the market that use DC dimming should be the choice if it is your main concern.. OR generally anything LCD due to backlight & current based dimming should be ok. E.g. the OLED's with PWM flicker Hz could be in range of 100Hz to 480Hz or higher (low screen brightness = higher perceivable flicker intensity due to > screen off time vs screen on time %). Check phone specs prior to purchasing..

### Mat.I — 2025-03-07T02:50:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/app-for-red-screen.30045/post-348009>

If you are very sensitive, the LCD option may be the best one.. There are quite a number of phones on the market that still have LCD's... However, check the specs & contact tech support as some may still use a form of PWM for backlight dimming.. Therefore if this cannot be confirmed or verified DC dimming with OLED may be the option..

### Aaron K — 2025-03-12T15:08:16+0200

### Rusty — 2025-03-14T09:09:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/app-for-red-screen.30045/post-348582>

4 things to reduce flicker on your iPhone

1\. Disable auto-brightness
2\. **Reduce White Point:** Go to **Settings** → **Accessibility** → **Display & Text Size** → Enable **Reduce White Point** (adjust slider). This dims the screen without lowering brightness through hardware.
3\. Keep brightness high and light natural light sources in the background. Higher ambient light makes your pupils constrict, reducing how much they dilate in response to screen flicker. Smaller pupils = less flicker sensitivity
4\. Turn on “Dim Flashing Lights”
Anytime it senses a flashing or strobing lights in video content, i.e. Flicker on the screen, it dims the display.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-03T02:09:01+0200

### jackfoster — 2025-01-03T02:32:19+0200

Thank you for that it is well written but I am just unsure if you mean that simply bringing in a canopy or tent will somehow raise electric fields even if they are not grounded via a wall plug?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-03T03:32:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-emf-canopy-or-tent-should-i-get.30046/post-344665>

A canopy shields you from RF because it is metallic - it reflects the RF back. BUT - being metallic it attracts electric fields. SO - you want to turn OFF the source of the 50/60Hz AC electric fields in the room. Remember “THEY” say you should get rid of metal bed frames and metal spring mattresses, so how can a metal canopy attached to a metal wire be good? “THEY” say all you need to do is ground the canopy BUT joining your metallic canopy to the homes electrical system - (in a 1,000 ft2 home thats about half a mile of wire) attaches you to an antenna array. IF you throw a wire (50ft +/-) out the window to ground you are now attached to a 50ft antenna.

If you need a canopy to protect you from RF ok but . . . TURN OFF the 50/60Hz electric field.
Ground outside in Nature not to a wire.

>

> Ground outside in Nature not to a wire.

Great explanation thank you but wouldnt the electric fields also be reflected and kept on the outside? Are the increased electric fields you're talking about able to be seen by being measured before and after there is a canopy surrounding a spot?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-03T21:28:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-emf-canopy-or-tent-should-i-get.30046/post-344683>

> [jackfoster said:](/goto/post?id=344680)
>
> Great explanation thank you but wouldnt the electric fields also be reflected and kept on the outside? Are the increased electric fields you're talking about able to be seen by being measured before and after there is a canopy surrounding a spot?

A canopy reflects the RF away but attracts and absorbs the electric field which then radiates on the inside. These can all be measured with a RF meter and Body Voltage meter. Because many people do not measure and just set up canopies they can actually be making their nnEMF exposure increase. With a canopy the RF should go down BUT measure the electric field before and after because it often goes up BEFORE turning off the circuits.

Don’t guess - measure and you will KNOW.

### jackfoster — 2025-01-03T21:48:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-emf-canopy-or-tent-should-i-get.30046/post-344684>

With a canopy the RF should go down BUT measure the electric field before and after because it often goes up BEFORE turning off the circuits.
>
> Don’t guess - measure and you will KNOW.

Per my cornet ED88T my bed shows measurements of less than .01 - .05 average mw/m2 with random highs of around .08
Lf600 gauss mod shows consistent .1 uT with lf30 mode showing .05-.06 uT
The elf mode shows 0000. - 0006. With it much more consistently being at or closest to absolute zero.
I've done everything I can with unplugging things and moving my bed into a different position to get the lowest readings.
Would a canopy be a safe bet in this situation and would I benefit from it?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-03T22:18:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-emf-canopy-or-tent-should-i-get.30046/post-344688>

Your readings look to be “good” just depends how low you want to go.
RF - Max <1µW/m2 (= 0.001 mW/m2) is no concern
100µW/m2 - 1µW/m2 (= 0.001 - 0.1mW/m2) slight concern
Gauss <0.5mG is what I go for (lower the better)
ELF is not the same as measuring body voltage. I aim for a BV <100mV (lower the better)
BV is 1000x more sensitive than an EMF meter that only goes down to 1Vm (1000mV in a V)

### jackfoster — 2025-01-03T23:11:55+0200

> ELF is not the same as measuring body voltage. I aim for a BV <100mV (lower the better)
> BV is 1000x more sensitive than an EMF meter that only goes down to 1Vm (1000mV in a V)

so should I be getting a bvk and testing my bv in my bed before getting a canopy? also for rf you said .001 mw/m2 is no concern but mine is ranging around .01-.05 or even to around .08
Looks like the BVK from your website is back ordered?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-04T01:34:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-emf-canopy-or-tent-should-i-get.30046/post-344700>

BVK’s are back ordered because our supply chain is running slow. First come first served when shipment arrives.

### jackfoster — 2025-01-04T01:45:53+0200

### Jessnet3 — 2025-04-03T21:45:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-literate-physicians.30048/post-350029>

What a read, Jack.

Probably about four years ago now, I learned of an astrophysicist and an astrobiologist (whose names I now forget but I’m sure you know) who proposed the notion that cancer is a form of atavism. I was drawn to this idea as it came from outside the confines modern medicine’s groupthink (or as I’ve come to appreciate now, from a more decentralized understanding). My good friend was undergoing treatment for stage 4 thymoma, and I was trying to broaden my understanding of what cancer actually was. The realization that it may be a reactivation of ancestral survival pathways was fascinating -an “aha!” moment for me as a layperson - but also, in retrospect, obvious. The rapid proliferation, resistance to apoptosis, hypoxia tolerance, metabolic independence, immune evasion etc… it took someone from outside the cancer world to call out the glaring resemblance between cancer cells and what a middle school biology student could tell you about bacteria.

I appreciated Seyfried and D’Agostino’s insistence that we needed a paradigm shift in cancer research away from the genetic mutation model. But the trend shifted to focus on insulin resistance and food-based interventions, which are woefully incomplete… as food-centric frameworks are. It didn’t explain how or why these primitive programs become “stuck” in the body in the first place. Despite the inordinate amount of money we’ve spent on cancer research, we’re no closer to answering this.

What finally bridged the gap for me was yesterday’s blog: the idea that cancer is the failure to complete the regeneration loop - that these cells have lost their electrical and spatial map. It didn’t just fill in what others missed… it reorients how we understand healing itself. Where Becker revealed the role of electrical polarity in regeneration, you’ve shown how disrupted light, circadian rhythms, and redox states prevent that loop from resolving. Though none of the “root causes” should surprise anyone in this group, the lens of failed regeneration is profound. My hope is that others will have the intellectual honesty and curiosity to pursue the research paths you’ve outlined - because we’ve remained stuck in this outdated paradigm for too long.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-live-in-summers.30050/post-344675>

I live in south Florida but starting to struggle being outside during the very hot summers. Is it a good plan to spend summers in a place like the mountains of North Carolina, or is the higher latitude and altitude a worse environment and I should just tolerate the hot summers in Florida?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-09T23:55:12+0200

Higher the latitude the lower the UV . . . BUT
The higher the altitude the higher the UV . . .
Just look up the UV index of the place you have in mind compared to where you are and you will have your answer.
The UV you get is better than the UV you don’t because it is too hot!

### Rusty — 2025-01-10T02:39:34+0200

>
> Thanks for the advice! are you aware of a good website to easily look at historical UV indexes by location?

I don't think you'll find a resource like that, you'll to run the numbers

Lower Latitude = **Higher UV**
Higher Altitude = **Higher UV**

Lower Latitude + Higher Altitude = **Peak UV**

UV increases about **2%** for every **1000 ft. (300 mtrs) increase in elevation**

Do the math.

### Lisa K — 2025-04-09T05:55:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-live-in-summers.30050/post-350349>

> [Rusty said:](/goto/post?id=344990)

> Higher Altitude = **Higher UV**

>

>

Hi, we've been looking at this recently - deciding if/where to move. So if the UV index is say "9" at sea level, it only increases to say 9.18 (9 x 102%) at 300m at the same latitude? We'd seen some stuff saying you ideally wanted to be 1,500m above sea level, but on that logic, its only a 10% improvement. In summer months in Southern Australia that doesn't seem like a problem, but in winter it is. The average UV index in winter is 2-3 in Sydney (34s) compared to Darwin (13s) at a UV of 8. On this basis, practically speaking, its very difficult to use elevation to solve for lack of latitude.

Have I understood this correctly?

### Rusty — 2025-04-09T09:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-live-in-summers.30050/post-350355>

> [Lisa K said:](/goto/post?id=350349)
>
> Hi, we've been looking at this recently - deciding if/where to move. So if the UV index is say "9" at sea level, it only increases to say 9.18 (9 x 102%) at 300m at the same latitude? We'd seen some stuff saying you ideally wanted to be 1,500m above sea level, but on that logic, its only a 10% improvement. In summer months in Southern Australia that doesn't seem like a problem, but in winter it is. The average UV index in winter is 2-3 in Sydney (34s) compared to Darwin (13s) at a UV of 8. On this basis, practically speaking, its very difficult to use elevation to solve for lack of latitude.
>
> Have I understood this correctly?

Yes, you are correct.

### You can’t use elevation to fully substitute for latitude​

Lower latitude = big UV gains
Higher elevation = small UV gains

**Only scenario where elevation makes a massive UV impact is:**

-   Already high baseline UV (e.g., tropics)
-   Plus significant altitude (like Andes, Himalayas, Ethiopian Highlands)
-   Plus reflective surfaces (e.g., snowfields)

Thanks - just wanted people to be aware of the potential issues. I've been reading the ubiquitation blogs (there are ~25 blogs) so it was on my radar.

### IamGrootsWife — 2025-01-03T16:49:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-from-cold-wi-usa.30051/post-344676>

Good morning, from cold Wisconsin. New member, married, two grown sons. Right now, my head spinning trying to focus on what is most important to change! WOW, all I can say is WOW. 2025 is going to be wild! Thankful to be here now to learn.

First realization: Lasted 20 seconds in the freezing grass barefoot. Walked outside with the dog, windy and 13 degrees only showing my face (would of loved a scarf) for about 20 minutes (no glasses).... that was my morning sunrise..... COLD is not my thing.... so searching where to go during winter and/or sell house and move.....? I see sunrise every morning in some degree daily since I learned of this being the most important. Have to learn how to do this efficiently.

Questions:

Who has magnetico CORE sleep pad? What do you take to offset the detox (heavy metals)? Cant find DMSA....

Is there a post that talks about labs to get? I hear several labs being discussed, what would be the basic list to get at first?

Tinnitus I have -- honestly, after I read Wim Hoff book and started cold therapy and breathing.... I got Tinnitus, when I reached out to the support, they said this does happen.... good question for Dr. Jack, why? I do not like cold therapy at all, I am cold all the time---hope this changes with sunrise time and less blue light.

Just bought EMR TEK 300 watt, where do I learn how best to use?? I read, it should be used correctly.... but not sure what correctly means.

Thinking about getting the Sperti Vit D lamp for winter here.... good? bad? I have to find some hacks to get thru the cold days until we can move.

Will buy the emf reader--- I see posted on the forum so I can test my area.

Where do you buy a swim suit that allows UV light.... not in the naked club yet... lol

so much to absorb, I work in GOV'T... remote for now. I have purchased all blue light blocking screen protectors (for all 3 screens.... way to much tech), blue light blocking glasses for day and night (cheap ones), switching some light blubs....but not sure we are buying the right ones. Have purple light and red light blubs. blue blocking i phone screen protector. (I dislike being on tech in general). How do we buy the new daylight computer Dr. Jack?

I have to learn how to join the gold group forums and connect with some of you to help mentor me up fast!

Thank you for any suggestions/help!

Peace and Love, Kris

### friendlyBrownCat — 2025-01-06T15:54:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-from-cold-wi-usa.30051/post-344778>

Webinar i found about labs = [https://optimalklubs.com/about-labs-webinar/](https://optimalklubs.com/about-labs-webinar/)

Summary of labs follows, I am checking other podcasts and will update as i see more detail. Also perhaps someone might add more detal around ranges for tests if known.
Another detailed post on this seems to be here: [https://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/](https://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

### **. Comprehensive Lab Panels**​

-   **Description**: Dr. Cruz describes using a wide-ranging initial panel to gather a comprehensive health picture. Over time, he has narrowed this to a core set of labs addressing most health issues (90–95%) while reserving "zebra labs" for unique cases like biotoxin illness.
-   **Optimum Ranges**: Not provided.
-   **Relevant Detail**: Labs are part of a holistic health assessment and must be interpreted alongside family history, symptoms, epigenetics, and lifestyle factors. Labs are compared to paint on a canvas, helping to create a picture of health. He does not specify what labs are Comprehensive panels but i am looking in other places. Based on my research it usually means:
-   Code:

    ```
    Complete Blood Count (CBC): Evaluates overall blood health, including red and white blood cells, hemoglobin, and platelets.
    Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CMP): Assesses organ function (liver, kidneys), blood sugar, electrolytes, and proteins.
    Lipid Profile: Measures cholesterol levels, including HDL, LDL, and triglycerides.
    Thyroid Function Tests: Includes TSH and sometimes free T3/T4 to evaluate thyroid health.
    Vitamin and Mineral Levels: Often includes Vitamin D, B12, and iron panels.
    Inflammatory Markers: Such as C-reactive protein (CRP) or erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR).
    Hormonal Panels: Such as sex hormones (estrogen, testosterone), cortisol, or insulin.
    Infection or Immune Markers: Sometimes panels include tests for specific infections or immune function
    ```

    .

* * *

### **2\. Highly Sensitive CRP (C-Reactive Protein)**​

-   **Purpose**: Measures systemic inflammation.
-   **Optimum Range**: CRP values should ideally be **very low**, such as **<0.5**.
-   **Relevant Detail**:
    -   CRP is described as a key marker of inflammation, which Dr. Cruz frames as the foundation of disease.
    -   Inflammation is likened to a "house fire," with CRP acting as the "lighter."
    -   He highlights the importance of CRP in understanding cellular signaling disruptions.

* * *

### **3\. Vitamin D**​

-   **Purpose**: Monitors immune system health.
-   **Optimum Range**: Not explicitly provided.
-   **Relevant Detail**:
    -   Vitamin D levels indicate whether the body has enough "firefighters" to manage inflammation effectively.
    -   Dr. Cruz links Vitamin D deficiency to immune system dysfunction and notes its importance in seasonal testing (e.g., addressing low levels in the fall).

* * *

### **4\. DHA (Docosahexaenoic Acid)**​

-   **Purpose**: Reflects metabolic efficiency, sleep quality, and gut health.
-   **Optimum Range**: Suggested range is **60–701** (based on lab reference values), with abnormal levels such as **\>120** associated with gut and sleep issues.
-   **Relevant Detail**:
    -   DHA is central to the hormone chain and provides insights into overall hormonal health.
    -   Elevated DHA suggests specific issues like poor sleep or gut dysfunction, which can impact metabolic efficiency.

* * *

### **5\. TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone)**​

-   **Purpose**: Evaluates thyroid function.
-   **Optimum Range**: Dr. Cruz suggests **0.5–1.0** as optimal, which is narrower and lower than the standard medical range of **0.5–4.5**.
-   **Relevant Detail**:
    -   He links TSH to symptoms like low energy, libido issues, and eyebrow thinning, even when levels are technically "normal" by standard ranges.
    -   This narrower range aligns with his emphasis on addressing subtle dysfunctions.

* * *

### **6\. Cholesterol Panel**​

-   **Tests Included**: LDL, HDL, Triglycerides, CRP.
-   **Purpose**: Interprets cardiovascular health.
-   **Optimum Ranges**:
    -   **CRP**: Low, ideally **<0.5**.
    -   **LDL**: No specific target provided; context matters more than the absolute value.
    -   **HDL**: Higher levels are preferred for a healthier lipid profile.
    -   **Triglycerides**: Lower levels are favored.
-   **Relevant Detail**:
    -   He frames LDL cholesterol as part of the body's natural healing response during inflammation or acute illness.
    -   Emphasizes inflammation (CRP) over LDL as the key cardiovascular risk factor.

* * *

### **7\. Quarterly Lab Monitoring**​

-   **Purpose**: Helps patients track health trends over time.
-   **Frequency**: Every quarter for 12–18 months.
-   **Relevant Detail**:
    -   Patients should learn to correlate lab trends with how they feel at different values, building a "library" of personal health data.

* * *

### **8\. Seasonal Testing**​

-   **Purpose**: Addresses seasonal health challenges (e.g., vitamin D levels in fall).
-   **Relevant Detail**:
    -   Dr. Cruz mentions using cold exposure to address seasonal drops in free T3 levels and their associated symptoms.

* * *

### **9\. Paleo FX "Cheap Dirty Paleo Hack"**​

-   **Tests Included**:
    1.  Highly Sensitive CRP
    2.  Vitamin D
    3.  DHA
-   **Purpose**: Provides a quick and cost-effective snapshot of systemic health for generally healthy individuals.
-   **Relevant Detail**:
    -   He emphasizes that these three markers together provide valuable insights into inflammation, immune health, and metabolic function.

* * *

### **10\. Estrogen-Progesterone Ratio**​

-   **Purpose**: Evaluates hormonal balance, particularly in women.
-   **Optimum Range**: Not specified.
-   **Relevant Detail**:
    -   Many women have "upside-down" ratios, which can lead to health issues.
    -   The ratio serves as a guide for treatment decisions but is not elaborated on in detail.

### Kristine 2025 — 2025-01-26T18:11:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-from-cold-wi-usa.30051/post-345771>

Thank you! I plan to write an optimal journal soon and been reading as much as I can. I found the daylight computer but also got all blue blocking screen protectors for all screens. I did get a sperti vit D lamp... being in the cold -- it should help. Learning more on red light therapy... did Not buy the magnetico yet. Been looking at places to visit during our winter for now... everyone talks alot about panhandle of Florida.... anyone look at Gulf Shores? I have not been there. They just had snow too.... but we actually had -22 degrees in northern WI this last week. I don't know how to handle this cold. I go outside everyday with my dog... but I have hats, gloves and a warm coat. I put my barefeet in the grass, snow, cement for maybe 30 seconds to 1 minute in morning. This doesn't sound like enough sunrise/grounding... but it is something!

> Any recommendations for the best place to stay in the Florida panhandle in February? So, we can get some sunrise sun? We need a place to drive with our pup....so flying isn't an option. Any suggestions are helpful. We keep hearing Destin.... any other place or stick with Destin area. Thanks!!!!

My wife and I used to have a house in Panama City Beach, FL in the panhandle. Our community was Carillon Beach Resort. It has some nice homes but the panhandle in general is cold in February, and it's off season. So, you can expect some places to be closed or have limited hours that time of year. You'd be better off if you kept driving and got to the gulf coast.

### JHackett — 2025-03-03T19:36:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-from-cold-wi-usa.30051/post-347813>

> [Kristine 2025 said:](/goto/post?id=345964)
>
> I am working on changing my working arrangements, until I have this "changed" I work at 6AM, in 43 latitude USA it is dark at this time.... I already stopped online school to get away from screen time but now I am in a panic. Does wearing the night blue blockers work in AM while it is still dark? I will have to go into no open windows, all jabbed crazy people (shedding is real) office soon. I will change my hours to start after sunrise soon as TINA is real. What are some of you doing to stay healthy when you have to go into the office and deal with these health risks. I asked for retirement papers... this will take a few months to get a leave date. I need to hack my transition time or a new job... now.

Since you seem healthy (huge caveat - disease requires radical change), just take it one day at a time. The "red pill" is a lot to swallow. Don't do anything super rash. Take a vacation, get your eyes and skin in sunlight for a while before really meditating and pondering on the next steps. You don't want to make big decisions in Wisconsin in winter, your cognitive tracts aren't at their best. There are varying levels of steps you can take.

Just as an example, I live and work at the 41st latitude, nearly in Oregon. So, my light sucks! BUT, I live on the coast with zero population density and can embrace the weather as it is, since the average winter's day is about 50 degrees. Cold plunging, surfing, working outside all day without a shirt on are all accessible to me still, which helps a ton vs a place like Wisconsin in winter. Currently, my wife and I are still too busy as young physicians to spend too much time in the tropics, so we bought a place in Todos Santos, an hour north of Cabo. This is by far the easiest, fastest and cheapest place for us to fly to get our redox fix for 4 weeks or so over winter.

I believe building this mitigation strategy while you figure out life is critical. You start to recognize what "better" feels like and can chase it while still achieving financial, professional, parenting, etc goals.

Just $0.02

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-03-03T23:40:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-from-cold-wi-usa.30051/post-347823>

> [Kristine 2025 said:](/goto/post?id=345964)
>
> I am working on changing my working arrangements, until I have this "changed" I work at 6AM, in 43 latitude USA it is dark at this time.... I already stopped online school to get away from screen time but now I am in a panic. Does wearing the night blue blockers work in AM while it is still dark? I will have to go into no open windows, all jabbed crazy people (shedding is real) office soon. I will change my hours to start after sunrise soon as TINA is real. What are some of you doing to stay healthy when you have to go into the office and deal with these health risks. I asked for retirement papers... this will take a few months to get a leave date. I need to hack my transition time or a new job... now.

I wear my blue blockers until Daybreak. For example last night I conked out at 21.30

I woke up to pee at 2.23

I couldn't get back to sleep, my mind was working overtime so I slipped down to the deck at 4.30 , and took in the cool breezes and caught up on email. Looking back I should have taken my daylight down instead of the kindle. Or both, so I stargazed and cooled down until the signs of dawn appeared
But I was wearing my blue blockers because nobody else at Coatepeque turns off the lights at night. And they're the Led ones. So I kept them on and I realised that the light I could see through them was the same as sodium lamps

There was a reason sodium gas lamps were used. They didn't interfere with sleeping. Of course the "watermelon greenies" wanted pure white light out there to keep us sleepless

So I would say until Daybreak.

Have purple light and red light blubs. blue blocking i phone screen protector. (I dislike being on tech in general). How do we buy the new daylight computer Dr. Jack?

> Peace and Love, Kris

EMR are about to release a course on their panels it comes out on March 11 I think. In any case, a session in the morning and at night on bare skin will work. I use mine when I know I won't get much time outside. If you haven't swapped out your lights inside, then I'd flood the room with the panel light to help offset the blue light emitted from the standard LEDs etc.

### TomBear — 2025-03-04T19:21:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-from-cold-wi-usa.30051/post-347873>

> [NickD said:](/goto/post?id=347856)
>

I use mine when I know I won't get much time outside. If you haven't swapped out your lights inside, then I'd flood the room with the panel light to help offset the blue light emitted from the standard LEDs etc.

I just purchased the EMR-Tek Inferno - Red and Near Infrared Light 1400 watts / Inferno pro and very new to this. My first source is morning sunrise for red light. I get a lot of light through a big 8' window where I spend morning hours, but if my guess is correct getting sunlight through a window is not optimal if at all useful. I spend my workday in my home office upstairs in front of several monitors so too much blue light. I have blocking glasses which fit over my regular glasses, but still have to remove them sometimes to see distinctions in color which are important. **What is the best way to use my new red light to counter the blue light which surrounds me** (TV, LED lights although my home lights are rarely on as I prefer and have sufficient daylight. Tom

### NickD — 2025-03-05T16:08:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-from-cold-wi-usa.30051/post-347903>

> [TomBear said:](/goto/post?id=347873)
>
> I just purchased the EMR-Tek Inferno - Red and Near Infrared Light 1400 watts / Inferno pro and very new to this. My first source is morning sunrise for red light. I get a lot of light through a big 8' window where I spend morning hours, but if my guess is correct getting sunlight through a window is not optimal if at all useful. I spend my workday in my home office upstairs in front of several monitors so too much blue light. I have blocking glasses which fit over my regular glasses, but still have to remove them sometimes to see distinctions in color which are important. **What is the best way to use my new red light to counter the blue light which surrounds me** (TV, LED lights although my home lights are rarely on as I prefer and have sufficient daylight. Tom

Hey Tom,
If there is a way to open the window, I'd do that - or get outside for 20min or so for the sunrise. See if you can have sessions outside (meetings, phone calls, breaks etc). That should be the main source - as you know.
I'd use the panel to also flood the room with red, to compensate for the blue from the screens etc. Just have it somewhere that it's not interrupting the work you do. I'm currently on night shift (in Australia) I use it behind me to offset the screens.
Then try a morning and evening session in front of it with as bare skin as possible. They should have come with eye protection so you don't look directly into it.

### Joe7 — 2025-01-04T03:52:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-can-tell-im-new-here.30054/post-344707>

By making an Avatar as requisite to the forums, and I take seriously already, having watched the 4 hrs. of Dr. Kruse (twi..about the 3rd time for soaking in...) on the Danny Jones podcast. Well...the Avatar seems to be our own pic. _Duhh._ I had to get into the forum to see what's in the forum, ok. Or that's why i chose the cartoonish guy w/light bulb (specially after watching podcast/Kruse/Jones. Ok. Because i have been protesting to not lose or give up +/or by no choice at the same time of having them take down yet another goodie of light when it comes to the incandescent light bulb! I was trying to exclaim the big deal of that such as the big heavy bulb carrying up here-as some sort of iconic "save" 'keep it!' Not to go on and on about that (just) one issue, but truly at the time of those curly-coned light bulbs, i remember getting one in the frekn' mail!!! A warning not from the mail but someone was alerting if you must use those bulbs, don't sit directly under them! I kept thinking by all account, when people saw them and that hype they would surely not go for it. But they seemed to and before you looked up they were hanging just about every where. Sobeit. Anyway, i'll have to edit and put up my own pic. like the people in the forums and I'm 'in' i mean, i sent that video i just watched, today, last night and day before, to only few people around me that I know who'd probably take me serious on 'just wait till you see this one!' Despite being 4 hours, well, hey how about being on here to learn 400 x that + right!

# Thread: Does anyone have any experience with SSRIs?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-any-experience-with-ssris.30057/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ajohnson90 — 2025-01-04T19:49:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-any-experience-with-ssris.30057/post-344718>

I’ve always had a belief that living within right relation with nature is the best way to be well.

I’ve also had a strong skepticism towards pharmaceuticals, my entire life. It’s possible that stems from my granddad, who despite living his life outdoors and teaching me about the importance of nature, developed rheumatoid arthritis after a spinal injury.
He said to me, while taking his medication one day “ you don’t ever wanna take this stuff. Do everything you can to avoid taking medication for as long as possible”

I’ve also read enough to have a legitimate skepticism about pharmaceuticals- anatomy of an epidemic points out how manipulative and fraudulent the pharmaceutical industry is. I think even SSRIs don’t have enough research to back them up as an effective therapy. It’s a theory based on a medication essentially. From what I understand, there are no tests to determine whether or not you need them based on neurochemical function. Essentially, I don’t know that the neurochemical imbalance theory is correct, based on my research, or correct enough to pursuit treatment based on the theory

I’ve been dealing with health issues for the last decade, and discovering Dr. Kruse has been a big part of my health journey… man I hate that word lol.

I started watching the sunrise in 2009 in high school when I had the notion inside me that it was something important I should be doing. That same year I also started a cold water therapy, but that’s because our water heater broke one day and I took a cold shower and it felt great.

For a reason, my sleep really fell apart a decade ago, and it’s caused a tremendous amount of issues, some of them being mental health issues. Anxiety, depression, panic, suicidal ideation. Etc

I feel like I’ve tried. “everything”. I’ve gone through the epi-paleo program, I’m outside for the majority of the day and I limit NNEMF.

But the depression and the suicidal ideation are too great and I am honestly worried.

My doctor has recommended SSRIs, my therapist, who I would say is more “holistic”, is recommending it as a temporary way to get through what I’m going through.

I’m so hesitant, for some reason, but I also don’t want to lose my life.

So I’m curious what you’re all experienced with SSRI has been and how they’ve helped you if they have.

### La-Shawn Hill — 2025-01-05T00:38:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-any-experience-with-ssris.30057/post-344728>

Hello, there. As soon as you admitted to having suicidal ideation, your doctor and therapist became liable for making sure you don't harm yourself. At the end of the day, there's only one way for you to find out how you'll do on an SSRI. Five years ago I was loathe to try medication, but my anxiety was through the roof. I would take the medication, feel better, then stop it because I didn't want to become addicted. I kept on doing this "stop-go," but the meds are strong and eventually I started developing withdrawal symptoms in the form of "brain zaps," which made me feel like a junkie and regret having gotten on the meds in the first place, altogether failing to acknowledge how bad my mental health was.

I want to share my personal mantra with you. Whenever I'm in a dark place, I tell myself "I'M SAFE...I'M STRONG...AND EVERYTHING I'VE LOST WILL BE RETURNED TO ME." Don't be ashamed of trying medication. For the longest, I thought that refusing to take meds for my mental health meant that I was still "strong." I had to defeat my own ego in order to get myself the help I needed. If you don't already, I would highly recommend that you start walking every single day. I really wish you the best. (hugs)

### ajohnson90 — 2025-01-05T18:40:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-any-experience-with-ssris.30057/post-344743>

> [La-Shawn Hill said:](/goto/post?id=344728)
>
> Hello, there. As soon as you admitted to having suicidal ideation, your doctor and therapist became liable for making sure you don't harm yourself. At the end of the day, there's only one way for you to find out how you'll do on an SSRI. Five years ago I was loathe to try medication, but my anxiety was through the roof. I would take the medication, feel better, then stop it because I didn't want to become addicted. I kept on doing this "stop-go," but the meds are strong and eventually I started developing withdrawal symptoms in the form of "brain zaps," which made me feel like a junkie and regret having gotten on the meds in the first place, altogether failing to acknowledge how bad my mental health was.
>
> I want to share my personal mantra with you. Whenever I'm in a dark place, I tell myself "I'M SAFE...I'M STRONG...AND EVERYTHING I'VE LOST WILL BE RETURNED TO ME." Don't be ashamed of trying medication. For the longest, I thought that refusing to take meds for my mental health meant that I was still "strong." I had to defeat my own ego in order to get myself the help I needed. If you don't already, I would highly recommend that you start walking every single day. I really wish you the best. (hugs)

Thanks, I am working with my medical team to stay safe. I understand where you’re coming from and your experience and it’s something I’m keen on avoiding as well.

Ironically Ive been walking between 7-10+ miles a day the last 4 years and my mental health is worse than ever

### La-Shawn Hill — 2025-01-05T23:08:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-have-any-experience-with-ssris.30057/post-344753>

John Lennon said, "Everything will be okay in the end. If it's not ok, it's not the end." I know this mental health journey is tiresome, but we can't give up. Stay vigilant until you figure out how to recover your health. Your mental health was fine 10 years ago. You just have to figure out how to recover it. Another forum member gave me the following advice: "Redox before detox." Search Dr. Kruse's website and Patreon for blogs on the redox rx and melanin renovation. The tropics are calling for us.

I was on Sertraline for 6 years. The centralised theory behind it is that theres not enough serotonin and therefore the serotonin specific receptor inhibitors, are there to mop it up and keep it from decaying.

They have a wide variety of side effects, and the one I found the most troubling as a young person was the lasting in sex. Orgasm is delayed and sometimes absent.

In fact there is a syndrome called Post ssri something something called PSSD Look it up

My depression lifts when Im in the sun, in fact there might be a post in here about how someone did all the protocols and got rid of their depressive/ post traumatic state.

Id get out of Utah to begin with youre in a desert and that is absolutely no good for you

Move Im doing the move this month I cant wait to get back to El Salvador and relax and feel better

### Chlo_e — 2025-01-04T20:24:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-aldehydes-and-further-weaponisation-of-vitamin-a.30059/post-344720>

Diving down a lot of deep rabbit holes since 2022..

In my research and observations I have been led to a potential link between vaccinations (possibly all vaccines including covid jabs) and aberrant vitamin A behaviour following exposure to said vaccines. JK has already laid out extensively how nnEMF and artificial blue light are interacting with the opsin-vit A complexes and generating abnormally high levels of aldehydes from vit A breakdown which are incredibly toxic and destroy our non-visual photoreceptors.

I am concerned that another potential mechanism for significant vaccine harm could be through a similar process- but possibly liver-mediated.

I have found several research papers from a fellow named Anthony Mawson- he seems to be one of the few unicorns to get some of his research through the peer-review process when it comes to adverse vaccine effects. I have linked one of his papers below. In essence- he is pointing out that as part of immuno-surveillance, vitamin A is deployed in response to what is perceived to be a threat, or non-self (several forms of chemo are/have been based on vitamin A derivatives) and that introduction of foreign material could lead to significant dumping of vitamin A into the circulation from the liver in order to deal with said threat. He goes into more detail regarding the liver and cholestasis in this regard, and makes links to vaccination in veterans experiencing symptoms of gulf war syndrome.
\- [https://www.researchgate.net/public...ng\_Hypothesis\_for\_a\_Continuing\_Health\_Problem](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/330128567_Gulf_War_Illness_Unifying_Hypothesis_for_a_Continuing_Health_Problem)

Thinking along these lines, if the bodily invasion is such that it is severe or prolonged or difficult for the body to remove- would this mean more vitamin A is potentially being released from storage sites on a continual basis to try to remove the foreign material? Would this lead to overwhelming build-up of aldehydes on an ongoing basis, compounding the effects of blue light and nnEMF exposure?

Specifically regarding the covid vaccines:
\- Introduction of mRNA leading to production of foreign spike protein- is there any good research on how long this lasts for? Would this be more likely to trigger a larger vitamin A response compared to historical vaccines full of toxic metal adjuvants?
\- Would the presence of SV-40 compound this potential issue even further- constantly nudging the body toward malignancy and greater immune response and vit A deployment to try and counteract it
\- If it is true that nano-graphene is present, is this making people more vulnerable to environmental EMFs and further vit-A mediated toxicity? Could it make people sick in the presence of IR? Look at what happened to Michael Mosley.. felt unwell then died of 'heat exhaustion'
\- If the above is happening it would be very difficult to clear all the aldehydes generated which are themselves a neurotoxin and carcinogen- particularly if the stressor and response was sustained

Some final red flags related to this theory are:
\- The WHO initiative to vaccinate as many children in developing countries as possible whilst giving concomitant vitamin A to the children and breastfeeding mothers
'Opportunities to link vitamin A supplementation with immunization' - [https://www.who.int/teams/immunizat...ing-with-other-health-interventions/vitamin-a](https://www.who.int/teams/immunization-vaccines-and-biologicals/essential-programme-on-immunization/integration/linking-with-other-health-interventions/vitamin-a)
\- mRNA vaccines are now being trialled for different cancers - are similar mechanisms being used to try to profit from the initial mRNA-induced malignancies? I.e just hyper-targeting cancerous cells using the same self-destruct processes that were initiated from the original covid vaccines?

Would be interested to hear other people's thoughts on this- aldehyde overload would explain a lot as a common pathway from numerous potential toxic mechanisms within the vaccines.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-06T19:23:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-light.30060/post-344795>

NIR and UV cannot be seen so yes I go by wattage. As far as over all visible light levels go As a photographer I use the F16 rule. Outside in direct sunlight set your shutter speed to match the film speed and the aperture is F16. ie with 100ASA film shutter speed is 1/100 sec, if your light level matches full sun aperture will be F16. If you don’t have a regular camera there are phone apps you can use to measure this. Or just measure the light level outside and match it inside.

### MatthewJohnCosta — 2025-01-08T03:03:12+0200

Dr Jack Says that 380 Nm light controls neuropsin, mTOR, etc. Also different NIR and IR lights add many benefits as well in their own way. All the lights in my house are LED flicker. I can't afford these [https://niralighting.com/](https://niralighting.com/) or any of the other expensive Blue light sales pitch light bulbs that i see on twitter recommended.

### NickD — 2025-01-08T06:02:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-light.30060/post-344877>

I'm a 24/7 shift worker, and we're in the process of organising our lighting at home...
We've swapped out our overhead pendant lights (the normal nighttime lights) to just a yellow light from Block Blue Light, I think they're called "Sweet Dreams" lights. Our bedroom overhead lights are now the same - Sweet Dreams. We've changed our bedside lamps to red lights, also from Block Blue Light "Twilight Red". The red was a little strange to get used to, but they're great once you get used to them!
I also researched for red light panels and ended up with a Krypton & a Fire Dragon from EMR TEK. I use these every morning if I can't make the sunrise and every night before bed. The Krypton has a UVA & UBV light which works great. It was expensive, but I didn't see myself stopping the shift work, so I took the plunge. They gave me a code that others have used "NICHOLAS72365" if you want to venture down that path.
I also stumbled across a site "healthlighting.com" which has full spectrum incandescent lights and I think we may buy stand-up floor lamps and use them during the day.

### Ozzy1 — 2025-01-08T17:25:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-light.30060/post-344886>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=344795)
>
> NIR and UV cannot be seen so yes I go by wattage. As far as over all visible light levels go As a photographer I use the F16 rule. Outside in direct sunlight set your shutter speed to match the film speed and the aperture is F16. ie with 100ASA film shutter speed is 1/100 sec, if your light level matches full sun aperture will be F16. If you don’t have a regular camera there are phone apps you can use to measure this. Or just measure the light level outside and match it inside.

Interesting. I've got an old canon 650d somewhere. What is your recommended spectrometer if i were to just go ahead and get one of those instead?

Pays your money and make your choice.

It seems more difficult than necessary to find information on here. There are like 50 threads on light bulbs, then you find out the one everyone recommends isn't even good. There needs to be place where products that have been tested and or confirmed safe or these are quality recommended products by our internal experts for the whole tribe. Easy to find & easy to trust recommendations. The forum is only growing. Its overwhelming to start changing all the lights in your house or buy red light bulbs for evenings. To do it cost effectively & pick the correct options. I just wish the trusted, confirmed, & recommended options were hosted in one place.

I appreciate Jack & everyone who tries to help eachother out in this forum so much! We all gotta stick together & help eachother out

# Thread: From rubber duckie to black swan mitochondriac
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-rubber-duckie-to-black-swan-mitochondriac.30062/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Carly18 — 2025-01-05T17:48:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-rubber-duckie-to-black-swan-mitochondriac.30062/post-344742>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Hello everyone!
I’d like to introduce myself as I sit in the dark, lit by candles at my kitchen table. My name is Carly and I live at the 53N latitude in Alberta Canada. I’m on the cusp of selling my home from which I’ve lived and worked for the past 11 years, raising my son and trying to find ways to stay out of the meat grinder healthscare system here. I’m a registered massage therapist, completely disillusioned with my industry and the direction it’s trying to take even though I love helping people manage or get out of pain.
I discovered Dr Kruse while I was trying to figure out why my kid wouldn’t sleep and my hair was falling out 2 years ago. His podcast with Rick and Andrew FINALLY made the pieces fit because I knew there was something else that was making all of us sick and it wasn’t just stress, “inflammation”, junk food or lack of pHARMaceuticals.
In 2023 I introduced red light therapy (technically Nov 2022), sunrise viewing, grounding and sun maxing and discovered that summer that I could actually be in the sun all day and not burn or feel uncomfortable. This past year I really committed to being outside as much as possible with the least amount of clothing I could handle, have kept my home completely dark after sunset and before sunrise, use bb glasses if I have to be around ALAN at night and am embracing cold plunging. I’m also no longer a no coiner thanks to Dr Cowan (and Sam Parker) and her Bootcamp Reboot classes. She’s such an incredible teacher with a HUGE heart.
I’m still struggling with some health issues and have decided it’s time to commit to renovate my redox by diving in head first.
I’m just getting to know how to navigate this forum and I’ll try my best to read as much as I can and learn how to be even more of a savage. I’m scared of leaving my family behind in this country but I also want to provide a legacy for my son, turn back my clock and get some skin in the game. I’m not going to be that rubber duck in the middle of the pacific anymore.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Tooth aches with reverse osmosis water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emmanuela — 2025-01-06T01:26:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/post-344761>

Kind of a silly question. But I am doing a good amount of ct and was thinking of reverse osmosis water. I currently get my water from an artesian well but I haven’t gotten the well tested for things like fluoride, etc.
Anyway, when I drink reverse osmosis water without adding sea salt/minerals, my teeth ache.

Any ideas on why that might be?

### Adamo — 2025-01-06T19:26:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/post-344796>

RO water is slightly on the "acidic" side of the pH scale.
I have "sensitive" teeth and have used RO water for 2 decades without that experience.

Are you drinking cold water?
Do you hold a lot of "tightness" in your jaw/skulls/neck/trap (collar bone area)?

There could be several factors to consider here.

Hmm, i do have jaw tightness occasionally.

### Adamo — 2025-01-14T02:55:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/post-345148>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=344947)
>
> Oh, it’s definitely not the real cause. My mineral levels are still recovering from being raised vegetarian. But I was hoping to find a way to overcome that issue so i can take advantage of drinking supercooled water. I guess ill have to try again after eating oysters even more.

For sure, I just want to be clear I'm not an expert... simply personal experience.

You could add fulvic minerals, if you choose. Things such as shilajit as well. But also eating organ meats can help big time with many minerals.
I personally prefer to find natural means, without going the supplemental route. As the gentleman said above, finding a very high-quality sea salt can be added to water, or make your food a little extra salty to get that intake in. You can look at things such as sardines, and certain dried fruits to get other minerals and content, such as apricots. If I am not mistaken, it's always best to get mineral content through soaking the body (skin), such as being in the ocean, and eating food sources that have "processed" them.

I reside about 1 hour or so north-east of your location, so we share a similar environment. Wishing you the best on your journey to recovery!
You will achieve it!

I'm not familiar with "supercool" water, but would love to know more.

yes, incorporating all the above currently, high seafood diet, with organ meats, shilajit, and epsom salt CT

I am interested in tapping into those benefits

> Thank you for sharing. Never heard of this at all, or what the benefits are of the super cooled RO. Interesting.

When water is supercooled, it’s as close to coherent water as possible.
He was saying it’s used for PTSD. It’s in his sleep RX and said it’s beneficial when you are doing CT.

### Adamo — 2025-01-18T16:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/post-345359>

> [Emmanuela said:](/goto/post?id=345358)
>
> When water is supercooled, it’s as close to coherent water as possible.
> He was saying it’s used for PTSD. It’s in his sleep RX and said it’s beneficial when you are doing CT.

Ahhh thank you! I have been filling up quality glass bottles with my RO and putting them outside in the Sun.
Then I drink them.

Not saying this is corollary to your case, but once I opened up the areas around my shoulders/clavicle/neck/jaw, tooth sensitivity was gone for me.
Previous powerlifting and computer work had my upper body very tight. I also had a head trauma over a decade ago, which definitely contributed to this.

Also rebuilding enamel helps, where you can use pure xylitol as a "mouth wash" after meals to cut down the bacteria that may lead to the "acidity" and breakdown of the enamel barrier. Eating many small meals or snacking all of the time doesn't aid in the mouth rebuilding and maintaining it's balance. Also having the mouth open all of time, whether talking a lot, or mouth-breathing, or when sleeping.

**We live in a sh\*t place for sunlight, and that is #1.** I think we have had less than 1 full week of decent light in the past two months. It has been gray skies / covered skies and dark for too long.

For me, EVERYTHING, even the garbage parts of life, feels better when I have Sun. But I have been addicted to the Sun since a child, and have a lot of Leo placements in my natal chart (ruled by the Sun), so for me, no Sun is **hell.**

### MTA321 — 2025-05-31T19:07:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-aches-with-reverse-osmosis-water.30066/post-353644>

> [Redfish3 said:](/goto/post?id=353641)
>
> What does supercool mean? I’m still waiting for access to the past webinar so I can’t check it out. thanks

RO is lower density water and is more coherent. Supercooled RO can stay liquid when put in the freezer but the structure can be easily disrupted making it freeze.

The main benefit to supercooled RO with CT is more efficient semiconduction allowing better transmission of photons/electrons.

### Jacks — 2025-01-19T02:18:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk.30069/post-345371>

Interestingly enough I just finished reading Landlines. The author wrote another book a few years back called The Salt Path - both books are about their long distance walks in the UK. They recount how her husband healed his neurodegenerative condition by going on these long walks. The funny thing is they think the miracle healing is all about the exercise. The food gurus will be horrified because they ate shit food most of the time, so what I think is that they don't realize that it is just living outdoors for long periods of time is what did the trick. They camped for the most part and walked through all weather conditions and cooked most of their meals on a camping stove. So it is possible to heal complex health issues in the UK I guess, but you have to live outdoors for long periods of time and spend time in the remoter parts of the Isles.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-01-19T10:32:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk.30069/post-345377>

Nobody focuses on where they are they focus on what they are doing

I have six good honest serving men they taught me all I know

Their names are Who and Which and Why and Where and When and How

I saw that written on a chalkboard in Stirling university hospital in 1992

I wrote it down and memorised it

It hasn't let me down ever.

### Chlo_e — 2025-01-19T12:26:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk.30069/post-345381>

> [Jacks said:](/goto/post?id=345371)
>
> Interestingly enough I just finished reading Landlines. The author wrote another book a few years back called The Salt Path - both books are about their long distance walks in the UK. They recount how her husband healed his neurodegenerative condition by going on these long walks. The funny thing is they think the miracle healing is all about the exercise. The food gurus will be horrified because they ate shit food most of the time, so what I think is that they don't realize that it is just living outdoors for long periods of time is what did the trick. They camped for the most part and walked through all weather conditions and cooked most of their meals on a camping stove. So it is possible to heal complex health issues in the UK I guess, but you have to live outdoors for long periods of time and spend time in the remoter parts of the Isles.

It's been made into a film which is due to be released soon as well. I'm not sure if you noticed when reading these books- but his neurological condition deteriorated again when he returned to 'civilised' living, and even in the third book when they populate some of their camping with overnight stays in accommodation and can charge their phones- his symptoms worsen. There's a chapter when they're on the return leg from Scotland back down to Cornwall where he's able to research places and routes on his phone and his symptoms are getting worse despite being outside. It's very, very interesting to note these events throughout the 3 books.

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-06-01T08:59:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-uk.30069/post-353678>

> [Jacks said:](/goto/post?id=345371)
>
> Interestingly enough I just finished reading Landlines. The author wrote another book a few years back called The Salt Path - both books are about their long distance walks in the UK. They recount how her husband healed his neurodegenerative condition by going on these long walks. The funny thing is they think the miracle healing is all about the exercise. The food gurus will be horrified because they ate shit food most of the time, so what I think is that they don't realize that it is just living outdoors for long periods of time is what did the trick. They camped for the most part and walked through all weather conditions and cooked most of their meals on a camping stove. So it is possible to heal complex health issues in the UK I guess, but you have to live outdoors for long periods of time and spend time in the remoter parts of the Isles.

The Salt Path is at the cinema now. I went to the afternoon showing. I live in the South West so know the 600 mile coastline really well. They had no choice but to embrace the suck, but his 5 year life expectancy with [**Corticobasal degeneration (CBD)**](https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/corticobasal-degeneration/symptoms-causes/syc-20354767) is now at 10 years. As you say they put it down to the exercise and didn't even think it was sunrise EVERY day and out in all weathers, and cold sea water, no lighting at all, no tv, and as you say shit food, because they were short on money.

Also looked in sadness that we had to go to the upstairs screen and most people were 60+ and struggling with the stairs to get up there, and coming back down them again, seriously only 25 steps max with handrails on both sides, complaining the cinema was cold. Seriously, they haven't a cope of improving their health if 20degs in the cinema is cold.

# Thread: Success Story
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-story.30077/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mitopower — 2025-01-07T22:15:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-story.30077/post-344866>

Hello all, I thought I would share my weight loss journey and now my optimal microcondriac life style change.

I started the traditional diet first before discovering Uncle Jack. You will notice starting Oct 2024 i hit a wall and stopped losing weight even thought i was eating between 800-1200 calories a day!
I am a 6' 1" 48yr old male who lives 20 miles outside Seattle.

5/1/2024 Started ~305lbs cut out fast food.
6/20 285lbs started pill mounjaro/lowered carbs.
7/30 285lbs started injection mounjaro/lowered carbs
8/6 276lbs
8/13 276lbs
8/20 270lbs
9/3 267lbs
9/10 262lbs
9/17 258lbs
9/24 254lbs-256lbs
10/1 254lbs-256lbs
Started eating 800-1200cal/day, treadmill
10/8 254lbs-256lbs
10/15 254lbs-256lbs
10/23 254lbs-256lbs
10/30 254lbs-256lbs
11/3 254lbs-256lbs Started biohacks
ENTER JACK KRUSE!
11/11 249lbs
11/18 244lbs
11/25 241lbs
12/2 236lbs
Reduced mounjaro by half
12/9 233lbs
12/17 232lbs
Cancun Vacation
12/31 233lbs
Now Off Mounjaro!
1/7/2025 228lbs

My typical day in winter here in Seallte involves eating twice a day, breakfast and dinner. I get up a half hour before sunrise and eat breakfast within a hour of waking. This will consist of Salmon, grass fed steak, eggs, or bacon. Dinner is around 4pm-5pm. Dinner will be the same but I will sometimes add more variety like shrimp, sometimes an entrée of mushroom chicken at panda express (once a week), halibut, steelhead. SEAFOOD=DHA. I have seafood probably in ~8 meals a week.

There is not a lot of sunlight right now in seattle hence the Vacation to cancun where I got a little pink!

Before dinner I do,
\*Red Light Therapy for 15min front and back everyday.
\*Sperti UV lamp 5min everyday alternating front one day and then back the next.
\*Some sprints on a treadmill every other day.
After dinner I do,
\*Cold Plunge @ ~47degrees for 20min everyday.
\*bottled reverse osmosis water
\*Bluelight blocker glasses
\*All light bulbs in house changed to no blue light.

After starting this regiment I went from sleeping half the day/taking naps/not having energy/not wanting to do things/getting mad or frustrated easily at wife and kids.
TO having lots of energy/no more napping/wanting to do things/and not getting mad or frustrated easily.
I'm down over 75lbs now in 8 months and still losing weight. Thank you uncle Jack!

LIGHT, WATER, MAGNETISM

\*\*\*\*\* [https://jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/](https://jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/)

\*\*\*\*\* [https://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](https://jackkruse.com/redox-rx/)
