# Thread: Shedding skin I've been picking My scabs again
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/unread>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Carly18 — 2025-01-08T03:28:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-344876>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I’ll start from the beginning… picking out a pair of glasses at the age of 7 because my teacher mentioned I was squinting in class. Myopia and astigmatism. After years of chronic ear infections, school was intimidating but I enjoyed it and now I could see the board too. When they showed up with fluoride mouthwash every morning, I did my part and swished a spit to “keep the cavities away”. But I did have a healthy fear of injections and managed to avoid a few of the immunizations which they did (and still do) in the schools.
A few years later, my smile diminished with crooked teeth. Of course my parents felt the best way to fix this was to get a mean, old orthodontist to straighten them. He decided I needed 5 teeth pulled (1 baby and 4 adult) so there would enough space to straighten them along with breaking my jaw and wiring it shut for a few months. They pulled my teeth but thankfully I pushed back on the jaw reconstruction.
At 13, I started feeling better about myself when my family moved from my small rural town to the big city. This year I ended up with chronic strep infections and subsequently massive depression after all of the antibiotics I had to take. My periods were also incredibly painful, meaning I missed a lot of school every month. The “solution” to this was the pill. It didn’t help much and it caused other problems.
5 years later I finally got all the metal in my mouth removed and graduate high school with an addiction to alcohol. Wisdom teeth get removed and I begin working in the retail grind rather than going to post secondary, ending up in a dark, fluorescent lit warehouse working shift work at any time of the day or night for the next 2 decades.
At 21 I’m diagnosed with an ovarian cyst and gaslit into surgery as the only option. They cut me open from hip to hip like a c section to remove a mandarin orange sized dermoid cyst on my L ovary and a follicular cyst on my R. I had to stay in the hospital for 3 days until I could poop again and while I was packing to leave, I pitched out the prescription birth control I’d been on since the beginning of my teens.
This was the time I finally started getting very disillusioned with the healthcare industry.

### Carly18 — 2025-05-11T02:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-352296>

My 20’s were all about drinking and finding boyfriends. All failed. I did stop drinking, I did get engaged and became a really bad ass martial artist to eventually succumb to more depression and anxiety. I left my fiancé to end up with a complete clown who I married in my mid thirties (after losing 30lbs and started drinking again). 6 months later I get pregnant in an abusive marriage. I’m forced to have a IV of antibiotics because I tested positive for strep while delivering my child. PPD/GAD creeps back in after I deliver and end up with a child with as messed up of a circadian rhythm as mine. We barely sleep properly, I become psychotic and incredibly unstable. I end up going back to work 18 months later and discover my husband is cheating. 6 more weeks off work to start my divorce and try to be a good single mama while working 60+ hours a week with a nursing toddler. Eventually, another 18 months later I end up totally breaking down, living in a new apartment with a toddler trying to navigate an abusive dad freaking everyone out but having to share parenting time. I couldn’t make rational decisions at this point and allowed this a-hole to gain shared custody when I could have eliminated most of his access. 5 years go by and I cannot return to the high paying middle management job I had, still dealing with panic attacks and dropping weight one more time. At this time I think it’s a good idea to start dating again and completely make more and more stupid decisions without being able to support myself. I move onto a new career, begin to run a new business and start making a bit more money to be able to keep my house hopefully. Then COVID hits and I get shut down, with a kid in elementary school, struggling with an ADHD/sensory disorder “diagnosis”. Thankfully, I get a glimpse into the schoolday while my child tried to learn online and immediately decide to homeschool. Unfortunately I can’t run a business from home, homeschool half time and keep myself afloat. 5 years later here I am, my kid living in a rural community, finishing school while living with dad, becoming more healthy and staying away from video games, listening to mama talk about how to eat and be in the sun. Mama also just sold her house, is sitting on a TINY bit of savings which will soon be invested like a savage and am planning to leave Arctic Mexico (Canada) for hopefully some reclamation of time. I’m in menopause, 50 lbs overweight, have a nasty case of eczema in my left ear (especially now in my temp living situation), osteoarthritis symptoms, a tech addiction and just became a Catholic. Welcome to my jungle folks.
(I do have a fabulous tan already though!)

### Carly18 — 2025-06-07T03:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354159>

> [Jojoplachta said:](/goto/post?id=354149)
>
> Good for you! How are you doing now? ❤️

Still struggling. I had a REALLY good set up in my home for nude sunbathing and keeping wifi to a minimum. I don’t any longer but I am always on the lookout for opportunities! I’m contemplating just leaving and heading to ES right away but I want to get my child started on his confirmation journey in Sept so I’ll try to stick it out here a few more months and get down to ES in the fall. ### Carly18 — 2025-06-07T03:49:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354160>

The best part about the past few days is all the comments I keep getting about my tan! It takes me all of 2 hours to be nice and caramel after some cooler temps this month. People think I’m part Native American ~ far from it. My ancestory is Dutch (father), Norwegian and German (mother). Then I start teaching them about how to build a solar callus!

### Carly18 — 2025-06-10T16:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354415>

Firstly, I want to say thank you to Dr Kruse, Chantal and Christy for hosting a wonderful zoom with all of us this past Sunday. The reason why I signed up for the Gold membership was because of hearing Noah's story on Dr Kruse's patreon and I am so happy little Emily has this tribe rooting for her. It also helped me realize that there is no alternative for my health but to head to the equator. Summers in Canada are lovely but the mornings are always around 6-11C (40-50F) so I don't get the full effects of the healing light of sunrise during these months either.

I've been thinking about my life since the chaos of 2020 started. I knew in that year I would have to leave my country. I have been quietly preparing myself for that inevitability but it still punches me in the gut thinking about leaving my family and starting over by myself. The only way I can see being able to make any kind of a legacy for my child though is to start over in a country where our health and investments can flourish. Embracing the suck.

Dr Kruse suggested when answering my question about pain that I read Dr Becker's books. That was such a lovely entanglement moment for me because I had just signed up for Dr Cowan's book club a few days before. We are reading Cross Currents at the moment. My goal for the minimum 6 months in Central America this fall is to discover a country in which I would like to work towards a PR/citizenship, build a citadel, grow a business, improve my health and STUDY. It buoys my spirit to think about all that time in the sun, healing, learning, exploring and making new friends. I need a community that won't roll their eyes at me when I tell them I can't live near a cel tower or eat after sunset or wear sunglasses.

If you'd like to be friends, please reach out. I would adore meeting up with fellow mitos in ES or anywhere in C America later this year.

### Carly18 — 2025-06-10T18:08:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354433>

ok, back to my journal....

Maternal grandmother passed away at 81 after multiple heart attacks, having to spend a few months in the hospital trying to recover. She hated doctors and hospitals. She had osteoarthritis and took medication for it to which she attributed her stomach problems. She had 7 children, oldest and youngest were male and the rest female. My mother was a twin and that was my grandmother's 4th pregnancy. The oldest son never married, lived with and cared for my grandmother his whole life, an alcoholic, had diabetes early and passed before my grandmother of a heart attack. He was also suicidal near the end of his life, going blind and with high blood pressure. The youngest male was 14 years younger then his closest sibling, my grandmother's 6th pregnancy. My grandmother lived on a farm most of her life, did not have running water for the entirety of it and drank spring water. She did have electricity in her later years. She spent most of her time tending the children, her garden, the animals and cooking/baking. As a child, I rarely saw her leave the house except to take care of the animals before I woke or to go to the bathroom (no running water = outhouse year round). She was very pale for the time I knew her and overweight. They grew crops conventionally so likely was exposed to many chemicals all her life. No big electrical towers on their property but there were smaller ones bringing power and telephone access along the highway opposite the large forest between her homestead and the highway. Her marriage was abusive and my grandfather an alcoholic. He passed at 96. His ancestry was Norwegian and they emigrated to the USA before my grandfather came to Canada after the war. Her ancestry is German but we don't have much information about that lineage unfortunately.

My mother had 2 children, starting at age 30. I am the oldest and my brother 18 months later. She's had anxiety all her life (I finally figured this out this year while living with her). Went into menopause around the same age as myself and had a hernia and pelvic floor reconstruction in her 60's. Her deliveries were also quick but she thankfully gave birth without interventions. She had elevated LDL in her 60's and took statins for 12 years before choosing to come off of them. She is now almost 82 and on no medications. She had basal cells removed from her forehead and the side of her nose a few years ago and is now terrified of the sun, wears clothing outdoors and sunglasses, is pale and grey, bitter, resentful and struggles with emotional stability. Her anxiety is off the charts and I am suspecting dementia will likely take her. She is very upset with me wanting to leave the country. She worked in offices, under fluorescent lights most of her life and into my school years and retired when my grandmother passed. She now has an engrained routine she refuses to change, an addiction to technology watching tv most of the day indoors, rarely spends time outdoors and complains constantly. I DO NOT want to end up like my mother. She used to be able to tan so incredibly well and when I was very young I remember her sunbathing often. Her trip to Hawaii in the 80's has some of my favourite pictures of her in a bikini, almost milk chocolate brown and a beautiful smile. My father is Dutch and emigrated here when he was 5. He had a heart attack last summer and is also set in his ways, living indoors but took my advice and did some red light therapy this winter and got a bit of colour back. My Oma passed at 98 without many health issues. She contracted pneumonia a few times near the end and was unable to eat.

Interestingly, all of my family has had core dysfunction in some capacity... father has a hernia (surgery to happen this summer), mother also and pelvic floor prolapse, I have a rectocele and my brother has had 2 hernia surgeries. Collagen failing.... my butt needs some sun!!!!

### Carly18 — 2025-06-12T14:48:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354550>

Since moving into my temp digs, the eczema in my ear is unbearable. I’m able to turn off wifi overnight without causing WWIII with my family now but can I try some topical Benadryl to help with the itch or is that too close to the brain to risk?
It seems to flare more with carbs so I’m eliminating those as well.

I’ll be housesitting all of July so I’ll have much more control over my nnEMF exposure and can tan privately most of the day.

### Carly18 — 2025-06-18T14:50:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-354812>

It’s truly all about the light. Summer for me is by the lake. It’s focused on cold, quiet morning sunrises, paddleboarding, sunning all day long, campfires, reading books and slow, peaceful sunsets.
Nothing else matters. I don’t need much. Only enough for some local food and a roof over my head. That’s it. That’s all that matters.

### Carly18 — 2025-08-17T16:11:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-358369>

> [Carly18 said:](/goto/post?id=352296)
>
> My 20’s were all about drinking and finding boyfriends. All failed. I did stop drinking, I did get engaged and became a really bad ass martial artist to eventually succumb to more depression and anxiety. I left my fiancé to end up with a complete clown who I married in my mid thirties (after losing 30lbs and started drinking again). 6 months later I get pregnant in an abusive marriage. I’m forced to have a IV of antibiotics because I tested positive for strep while delivering my child. PPD/GAD creeps back in after I deliver and end up with a child with as messed up of a circadian rhythm as mine. We barely sleep properly, I become psychotic and incredibly unstable. I end up going back to work 18 months later and discover my husband is cheating. 6 more weeks off work to start my divorce and try to be a good single mama while working 60+ hours a week with a nursing toddler. Eventually, another 18 months later I end up totally breaking down, living in a new apartment with a toddler trying to navigate an abusive dad freaking everyone out but having to share parenting time. I couldn’t make rational decisions at this point and allowed this a-hole to gain shared custody when I could have eliminated most of his access. 5 years go by and I cannot return to the high paying middle management job I had, still dealing with panic attacks and dropping weight one more time. At this time I think it’s a good idea to start dating again and completely make more and more stupid decisions without being able to support myself. I move onto a new career, begin to run a new business and start making a bit more money to be able to keep my house hopefully. Then COVID hits and I get shut down, with a kid in elementary school, struggling with an ADHD/sensory disorder “diagnosis”. Thankfully, I get a glimpse into the schoolday while my child tried to learn online and immediately decide to homeschool. Unfortunately I can’t run a business from home, homeschool half time and keep myself afloat. 5 years later here I am, my kid living in a rural community, finishing school while living with dad, becoming more healthy and staying away from video games, listening to mama talk about how to eat and be in the sun. Mama also just sold her house, is sitting on a TINY bit of savings which will soon be invested like a savage and am planning to leave Arctic Mexico (Canada) for hopefully some reclamation of time. I’m in menopause, 50 lbs overweight, have a nasty case of eczema in my left ear (especially now in my temp living situation), osteoarthritis symptoms, a tech addiction and just became a Catholic. Welcome to my jungle folks.
> (I do have a fabulous tan already though!)

I’m going to dive deeper into how I put the pieces together with my dog’s health and mine. In 2022, I noticed the municipalities were digging up the roads and around utility boxes to install new telecommunications. I wasn’t too concerned because, at the time I lived in a new subdivision and figured we wouldn’t have too many upgrades or be as impacted. Boy was I wrong. That must have been the year they installed all the 5G towers because for a solid week, my son and I suffered the worst “flu” symptoms simultaneously. For him they lasted a week, for me a month and I’ve never been the same since. It felt as if I was being held down by some kind of heavy, oppressive force. I couldn’t move easily, I felt drained and exhausted all the time. I only had energy enough to walk to the park where I would then sit on the grass, exhausted. My dog didn’t get much walking in that month (July 2022). I’ve never had a “summer cold” hit me that hard before. After a few weeks I was able to function again and that’s when I noticed all the 5G towers in my area. My dog had to be put down 6 months later due to what the emergency vet suspected was a brain tumour. That fall was when my hair began falling out and I turned to red light therapy. Thankfully, I was consistent with it and then found Dr Kruse from his Tetragrammaton pod with Huberman. I’ve only slightly been able to recover since that incident in July 2022 and it’s why I’ve sold everything and am leaving for Central America in the next month or so. I experienced that same oppressive, heavy feeling driving into a different city this week, close to where I live now. All of these places make me feel the same way, cause anxiety and migraines and everyone I speak to about this thinks I'm nuts. I HAVE to leave. I can’t keep trading in my time for money now. I’ll end up with same fate as my dog or worse. My menopausal symptoms have INCREASED since selling my home and moving to a temporary living arrangement this year and I’m only able to sleep when I’m in rural areas now.
I’ll be packing away the rest of my life into a cargo trailer and storing it along with my vehicle at a friends home near my current location and flying south within 6 weeks. I’d love to leave sooner but expenses here have stalled that timeline unfortunately.
Anyone else in ES in October and want to meet up and explore? I doubt I’ll be able to do much of anything except recover and renovate for the first few months.

### Carly18 — 2025-10-22T17:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-361412>

I’m a little embarrassed about my tears in the last member Q&A but Dr Kruse always manages to hit me right in the feels.

What I’ve learned over the past 8 months where I have had no control over my environment, trying to farm fiat by selling my time and talking to people about my plans for my future (and ultimately the future of my child):

Building a citadel gives you sovereignty over your environment and is a crucial key to your ability to claw back your time.

Talking to people that have a vested interest in your time with them, will cause a drain on your own time.Choose wisely. Be a good steward of both.

Being able to give to others means you must first secure your sovereignty and the only true source of wealth here is God’s grace. Get right with Him and move forward.

Pray often, with humility and gratitude.

### Carly18 — 2026-03-09T18:31:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-368444>

All the noise has disappeared.

Things I overcame in 2 months while in El Salvador:
\* within the first week, I felt my skin texture improve and the psoriasis in my ear calmed somewhat
\* after 3 weeks, I noticed I had lost some weight, gained some energy and was able to handle the sun much better
\* after 4 weeks, I challenged my panic disorder trigger and found it had disappeared
\* after 5 weeks, all the noise was gone and I could tell if I was cheating or not
\* after 6 weeks, I could handle the noise of the ocean and the feel of the sand (both of which overstimulated me before)
\* after 7 weeks, the chocolate arrived and I was finally able to attain the goal of 4-6 hrs on the beach daily

I chose to leave after 8 weeks to help a friend but managed to maintain my progress while cruising for a month.
I am now back in the environment which got me sick and within 5 weeks, ALL of my issues have resurfaced in some capacity. The only calm left is the focus I have to return (I will be back to ES for 2.5 wks in April), set up some businesses while there and figure out how I am going to make it my home now.

STOP MAKING EXCUSES AND TRYING TO HEAL IN THE ENVIRONMENT THAT MADE YOU SICK. JUMP WITHOUT WINGS.

### Carly18 — 2026-03-24T22:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-369561>

I'm loving the fact that even though I am back in the D- environment, my tan has disappeared and some of the health issues have resurfaced, they have not taken hold as badly as I first imagined they would.
First things to disappear when I landed in ES were my skin issues. My ears, nose and legs were the worst. Psoriasis in my L ear; dry, cracked nose which would not heal even over the warmer summer months in Canada; scaly, dry skin on my legs and 3 lesions which would not heal started to improve within the first week in ES. The more time I spent in the sun (either at the beach, by the pool or in the shade), the more the skin issues improved and by the time 3 weeks rolled around, my nose was completely healed, my legs were beginning to soften and the lesions disappear and my ear was slowly improving. Within 5 weeks, my legs were glowing and iridescent with my darker tan (the lesions still have alot of melanin around them so I assume more sun is still necessary), my hearing had finally returned in my L ear and most of the psoriasis plaque - which had grown onto my cheek near the tragus, up to the choncha symba and down to the lobe - had disappeared except for a small amount internally on the tragus and canal. The most substantial changes occurred when I was finally able to handle the loud surf, the sand on my skin and float in the ocean with my head under the water. All of which was so incredibly overstimulating for me when I first arrived. Christmas week was when MY chocolate princess tan popped off! I can't tell you how incredibly healing ES is, you just have to experience it yourself and go all in.

### Carly18 — 2026-04-29T00:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-skin-ive-been-picking-my-scabs-again.30078/post-372369>

Back in Chernobyl that is Canada after 2.5 weeks in ES again. So many amazing things happened while I was there. Dr Kruse's PowWow reenforced a hunch I had during my time in ES last year, that the magnetism on the beach is what pushed me over the hump to make a good amount of melanin. It took me almost 2 months to get to that ability but knowing just being there was healing things. My callous is still in a good place as I was able to make a decent amount of melanin quite quickly BUT I was already losing hair before I left Canada and that only took 2 months of crap environment to happen again. My skin INSTANTLY becomes ashy and dry (Mars) the moment I get into a shit environment and rehydrates in ES. When I first tried tanning in ES, I got a big heat rash on my chest. My maternal history is inclined to heart attacks and high blood pressure. I burn on my nose and did this time as well and that is one of the first places I have skin issues. I discovered I don't like Atami (except for the incredible community there) but there are some AMAZING places in ES to explore next time. Mitos are unbelievably inspiring people. willing to support one another and have amazing body awareness. Temporary residency in country requirements for a PR in ES are now 90 days vs 8 months which makes it much more doable for my family, friends and clients.
Things I wonder about now:
is the well water in my area so full of iron because of the magnetic declination? Yes. Just like us.
are the dumb people I live near currently suffering from the pole shift? YES. And I pray for them but I have to leave.
if I make my skills available to people in need, no matter where I am in the world, can I help people? Yes. And they get results more quickly!
is the lake I love to summer at drying up because of magnetic declination? yes, and that makes me so sad.
are the farmers in my area not able to grow their crops successfully without chemicals or is it because of the pole shift?

My intuition lead me to look for a new country to live in, somewhere that supports life, with people loving to live in and with nature, who want to protect their culture, their population and their health. Salut El Salvador. I look forward to making your country a large part of the new chapter of my life!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: ALL THINGS GLP-1 drugs..........
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-glp-1-drugs.30079/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-01-08T15:31:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-glp-1-drugs.30079/post-344880>

How much do you know about drug induced blindness. Because this pale woman from Australia is a prime candidate to get this complication.

Recently the PEER literature has reported new data on something I warned about 10 years ago with the GLP-1 drugs. They affect the leptin melanocortin pathways in the retina and lead to an eye disease called h non-arteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy (NAION).

Not how pale this woman is. The same thing is going on in her RPE. This is the first step in NAION development. When you lose melanin in the wrong places you get diseases that show up ou tof the blue and they confound centralized scientists and clinicians.

They are expected complications by decentralized clinicians and this is why I warned about them long ago. in 2015 in Los Angeles I gave a talk to Neil Strauss private longevity group about this coming pandemic and why they banned my TED talk about this pathway in the eye.

Semaglutide causes forced weight loss by BigHarma control their blood sugar by releasing the hormone that makes us feel full while eating. This hormone, called GLP-1, also prompts the body to create more insulin, which reduces blood sugar. Using these drugs in a blue light world which raises both blood sugar and insulin simultaneously makes this situation deadly for the RPE in the retina.

When the body experiences a manufactured change in sugar level via drugs and light exposure, it can affect the shape of the eye’s lens. This change is what causes the initially blurry vision, a side effect of semaglutide. This is how cataracts begin and how degradation of the leptin melanocortin pathways begin.

People who abuse technology and older patients are more likely to experience blurred vision when beginning the medication because their RPE already has a higher heteroplasmy rate to begin with. These two groups of people exhibit increased aging of the anterior eye due to blue light exposure and this causes the eye’s lens to become less flexible as the anterior eye ages, so vision takes longer to stabilize when the body experiences these changes in blood sugar levels. This woman is an actress who is always around blue light due to her occupation and her decisions around lights. The papers have been out for a long time. Now retinal experts act surprised that diabetes drugs cause eye blindness. We'll, I am not.

# Thread: Reliable heavy metal / mercury tests? B4 considering amalgam removal. (Teeth/amalgam/EHS/nnEMFs)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### eve001 — 2025-01-09T22:31:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-344970>

Hi, Who knows of a reliable way to test for heavy metal toxicity in the body?

In a recent podcast by Cameron Borg with Ole Johanson - Swedish EMF expert - I heard that depending on the brand different amalgam fillings have shown to release more or less amalgam in front of tech devices.

I regularly feel weird stuff around one of my two teeth that have amalgam in them.
I wonder if it is useful to have that amalgam filling removed, since I am very sensitive to nnEMFs.

On facebook years ago Dr. Kruse shared this research article on how MRI and nnEMFs release mercury from amalgams fillings.
Incl. bad artifical light. He also mentioned some heavy metal screening. Does anybody knows what he'd recommend? Something with spectroscopy? Where could one get that?

Some context:
I may have extra mercury load from allergy shots I got as a teenager - or not.
I also had braces and after that a metallic thread keeping my lower teeth in place - I had that for over 20 years and had it removed recently.
My tongue hated it and always was going over it. Nowadays my tongue is more relaxed, except when this amalgam filling gets weird.
I asked several dentists who say all seems fine. My other amalgam filling never causes weird sensations in my mouth.
Yesterday I had lots of exposure, to RF and to bad lighting and today this one tooth is feeling weird again.

It sounded as if such a heavy metal screen Dr. Kruse mentioned long ago may be important when deciding whether to remove/replace a filling or not.

I know of the risks of poor removal: the light in the dentist space, the speed of the drill, the poor extraction of the Hg vapor.
I also know that melanin - and sunshine and melanin Rx - are considered great for healing heavy metal toxicity.
I know deuterium is thought of as a heavy metal.

Who knows what heavy metal test would be reliable? And what kind of test Dr. Kruse would recommend today or has recommended in the past?
Is it true that he is not a fan of HTMA?

### pfedorow — 2025-01-10T20:21:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-345020>

Removing them will cause more issues and the risks outweigh any benefits . I don't think he recommends any tests .

> [pfedorow said:](/goto/post?id=345020)
>
> Removing them will cause more issues and the risks outweigh any benefits . I don't think he recommends any tests .

Hi there, he does - or did - but it's rather vague.

You may want to google Dr. Kruse + mercury or amalgam + resonance to research what he wrote.

I will be very happy to learn from people who know more about this.

### Old Dog New Tricks — 2025-01-11T19:52:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-345057>

I had all of them removed and replaced with amalgam by a Natural dentist back in the early 2000's and still doing well at 74. I also regularly clean my mouth and teeth with 3% Hydrogen Peroxide which I told the hygenist when asked what I do to keep them so clean. When I went for check up last June it had been over a year since I saw the dentist and did not need any work done both times. They also don't like that I use a toothpick instead of fluoride covered floss Hehe!

### eve001 — 2025-01-11T21:00:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-345065>

Hi there, thx 4 sharing. I think you mean you had the amalgams removed, not? What did you have them replaced with? The vapour sounds more compromising. I didn't know there may be fluoride on the floss stuff! Good to know.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reliable-heavy-metal-mercury-tests-b4-considering-amalgam-removal-teeth-amalgam-ehs-nnemfs.30083/post-345091>

# Thread: Grounding while cycling?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-while-cycling.30087/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Guy Houri — 2025-01-10T19:07:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-while-cycling.30087/post-345014>

Anyone tried something that worked for grounding their bike?
Im cosidering:
1\. just draggin a copper coil from my feet, but i imagne it can be dangerous if it gets stuck on something so i don't think it'll be a good option ..
2\. Maybe connecting a car anti static grounding strip to the bike's frame? Recently i tried grounding my car with a cheap iron strip from [Temu](https://www.temu.com/goods.html?_bg_fs=1&goods_id=601099613270044&sku_id=17592578841452&_x_sessn_id=d2df3ic12j&refer_page_name=bgt_order_detail&refer_page_id=10045_1736528222191_rxy70e8u4o&refer_page_sn=10045). After one uneasy bumpy road it kinda fell aprart, stayed on the exhaust pipe but the tip stopped touching the ground. So i also need to buy a different one.

What do you guys think?
Thanks

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-10T21:32:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-while-cycling.30087/post-345022>

WHY? . . . I can see so many possible dangers with dragging a wire from a bike. Plus - you cannot ground on asphalt. For optimal grounding you need to be outside, away from buildings & power lines . . . with your skin in direct contact with the earth/ground.

I recommend you enjoy your ride and when you get to a perfect low/no nnEMF grounding spot - stop and put your bare feet on the ground, take a deep breath and relax/enjoy.

### Guy Houri — 2025-01-11T18:22:12+0200

>
> I recommend you enjoy your ride and when you get to a perfect low/no nnEMF grounding spot - stop and put your bare feet on the ground, take a deep breath and relax/enjoy.

# Thread: Anne-Marie’s optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 306
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Woodstock — 2025-01-10T20:20:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-345019>

I am 55 yrs old and have had gut issues, fatigue, POTS symptoms and chronic aches and pains since early childhood. About 5 years ago I tested positive for Lyme which I likely have had since around age 4, based on symptoms and history.

I felt like an 80 year old when I was in my late 20s. (So I am actually doing much better now.) I got the diagnosis of Fibromyalgia from a Chiropractor who took the time to take a full medical history and check pressure points. When I went to the Arthritis Foundation website and read their description of the disease I cried for hours. Mostly of relief that I was not psycho because I had been told previously that this was psychosomatic and “no pain no gain” throughout my childhood while I pushed myself athletically into directions that likely only exacerbated my symptoms while trying to keep up with my non-biological siblings. I felt like a weakling and a failure, while simultaneously knowing I was actually smarter than most people. I just had a delayed reaction in responding and spent a lot of time in my head figuring stuff out. Nowadays this behavior would be diagnosed as on the spectrum, but I was never recognized as having any issues of the sort. It makes sense to me now. I likely had heavy metal poisoning from living in an area that had been destroyed by a tornado when I was 5-6 yrs old. I had some OCD symptoms at that age that improved after I moved.

My current health issues are:
Lyme and co-infections
mold exposure/mycotoxins
high fasting insulin/borderline pre-diabetic
MCAS
candida since age 10
EBV
Strep
Staph
SIBO/gut issues/gluten issues
chronic pain and stiffness in joints, neck and shoulders
liver and gallbladder issues
possible kidney issues
zero libido
menopause-on Bioidentical hormones
gained 30 pounds since menopause and very difficult to lose

since September I have been doing sunrise walks and getting sun on my body as much as possible

I have been working on lifestyle changes to eat more regularly and eat dinner earlier and reduce blue light exposure

about 6 years ago I had a building biologist evaluate and help figure out how to do all the right things. My house was Ethernet wired and we havent used a Wifi router during this time. HOWEVER, when Century Link brought in fiber optic cable, they gave us a new modem which had 5G turned on and the only way to turn it off was to sign in on their website and do it online. We didn’t realize this and it appeared to be off externally. Two days ago I found out that the modem had been putting off a 5G signal for YEARS unbeknownst to me. I have an RF meter from SLT but it only read high right at the source of the modem and was showing ok levels throughout the main areas of the house I use frequently so I just never realized this was happening. But I hated being in that room. I had a desktop computer in there and never wanted to use it. I moved the computer to another room recently but I never wanted to walk through the 5G area to the other part of the house. When I brought the building biologist back and had him go through the house with me, he quickly figured out what was going on and showed us how to turn off the 5G from the Century Link website with the password on the bottom of the modem. I would normally be really pissed off about being radiated for all these years, but that would just be a waste of my energy. It’s finally off now. The worst part of this is that we had a few water leaks and therefore mold issues which are supposedly made 600x worse with Wifi. We remediated the mold years ago but did not throw away everything in our home. We are in a dry climate so we have an evaporative cooler and I always feel worse midsummer when the humidity level gets really high from the cooler being on 24/7 for weeks in a row. The high humidity causes mold spores to reactivate. Hopefully this will improve now!

I am coming to El Salvador for a couple of months this winter. I hope this will be a good jumpstart on getting my health on track. I plan to spend a lot of time in the tropics and on the ocean for at least the next three years or longer. I was already getting the intuition that I needed to do this before stumbling upon Dr Kruse on Twitter back in September.

I will add more details and labs at some point. I just wanted to get this journal started. It has only been two days that my house has been free of 5G, but I am so relieved to know that there was actually something so easy I could do that theoretically should make a huge difference. (We had already done all the hard work but were not reaping the benefit!) Just another confirmation for me to listen to my gut feelings and intuitions and the more subtle things that I feel because there was a massive reason I have been avoiding half of my house for YEARS! I would normally beat myself up for not getting help on this sooner and figuring it out, but I’ve spent 55 years beating myself up and believing my pain was all my own fault and I am so DONE with that self sabotage type of behavior. So much crazy stuff going on in the world but I am going to have my best year ever in 2025. I might even learn to surf. Apparently the beach I will be near is good surf for beginners in Feb/March. I’ve also always felt physically better with a tan. The pieces are coming together.

Thanks for reading.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-10T21:51:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-345024>

>

For you and others reading . . . We have had many clients over the years with Lyme, EHS and/or on the spectrum. Turning off the AC electric circuits/fields and creating an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary has helped many reduce their symptoms. I would recommend EVERYONE with any kind of router - put a shield over it, just in case. Top of page, drop down menu, “office” page for ideas. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Woodstock — 2025-02-05T20:21:51+0200

I have been using my RF meter a lot more and doing everything I can to hack my environment. After discovering a 5G Wi-Fi signal coming from our Century Link modem about a month ago and finally getting that turned off, I have much less arthritic aches and pains! (it was supposed to be off as my whole house is Ethernet wired and I don’t know how it got turned on. It has to be turned on and off through the Century Link website and they don’t make it easy!) So now I have been testing everything. Today I brought my RF AM-11 meter with me on my morning walk. I saw quite a variety of readings which I attribute mostly to smart meters which is pretty intense in the condominium complex I regularly walk by. The worst readings were by the High School and the Rec Center, both with 5G towers, which is next to a nice little lake with a path that would be lovely if it wasn’t so EMF polluted. In from of the rec center was the worst, peaking beyond the scope of the meter! The poor folks playing Pickle Ball behind the building have no idea they are being zapped. I suspected but hadn’t bothered to bring my meter along because it’s a bit bulky and I think I was a bit hesitant to find out. I definitely need to find a new route for morning dog walks. I now understand why my husband and I get into little spats on our morning walks and I often feel anxious. We had a great spot to see the sun rise BUT now that I’ve seen the readings in that spot, I no longer want to see the sun rise there anymore. I will be exploring different routes. The mountain parks trails aren’t far from me, but they can be pretty muddy, wet, and icy this time of year. I hope they will not be so RF polluted. I am going to El Salvador in two weeks and will stay at least a month or two. Hopefully the sun and sea will be a positive for my health. I just ordered the EM3 meter from Safe Living Technologies and will be bringing that as well as a silver canopy to sleep under in El Salvador so whatever I encounter there I should be protected.

I am not going to list all my labs. Just going to make a note here of some things I am concerned about and what I want to retest after I spend the next two months in El Salvador!

insulin is very high. Last check was around 27. it has been as high as 33 and as low as 17.
A1c has been creeping up. Over a year ago it was 5.0 and has gone up .1 almost every time I check and is now at 5.6.
vitamin D is pretty low. I will look up the values and edit this later. Way below what it should be.
ive had issues with hematocrit being in the red and carbon dioxide being too low but those were ok on recent labs.
I had improvement in ECP (eosinophil catatonic protein) over a year ago it was in the red and now low and in range. I was on anti-parasitic herbs recently so maybe that helped.
ALT liver enzyme is high sometimes—other times it’s in range, last check it was high.
I was starting to worry I had Alpha-gal but the test came back negative. I am so relieved. I sure hope it’s accurate!
HS-CRP wasn’t too bad. around 1.6 (I think.). I will double check that. I think ideally it should be under 1.0?
About 3 years ago Alpha MSH was at 8 and a recent test showed 21 so that is good news. (I had an infected dead tooth removed and possibly this was creating a lot of issues for me and why this has improved since getting the tooth pulled.)

i need to work on my omega fatty acids And eat more seafood.
growth hormone isn’t great.
I will look up these values and edit later.

i Am on Bioidentical hormone replacement therapy with a nurse practitioner. Multiple Lyme docs told me I will just shrivel up and get old if I don’t take hormones and also that Lyme won’t heal if they are unbalanced. I m guessing Jack thinks this is a bunch of BS? ( After listening to hormone webinar of 2012 I guess this is ok bu maybe need to fix my P2/E2 ratio before doing testosteron?) Maybe wean off the testosterone for now? I really don’t want to take these but libido is nil and I was trying to fix that. (Also .I now see I need these to fix leaky gut)

My nurse practitioner is not quite a mitochondriac but when I mentioned Jack she knew who he was and his Epi-Paleo book and says she recommends his Leptin prescription all the time. I told her about the Tetragrammaton podcast and she had just heard from another practitioner about it and was going to listen. That was just before the election and I haven’t had an appointment since then to find out where she is at with all this. I will see her in two months after I get these labs done again and talk to her about what effect El Salvador has had on me.

I will re-check some of these concerning values after I go back to Boulder. But I will need to test right away upon return in case Chernobyl has an immediate negative effect. Maybe I should test here prior to leaving but I would rather use the same lab as at home for consistency.

380 nm light also controls the regenerative state of your hormone panel. This was a tremendous optical arrangement for the survival of mammals 65 million years ago, but it is now a big problem when you realize that we are in the great oxygen holocaust due to nnEMF and what dehydration of the heme-containing cytochrome P450scc is designed to do in modern mammals' insides under the power of ALAN, where POMC is boss.

This enzyme carries out the so-called side chain cleavage reaction, consuming cholesterol to produce pregnenolone, the precursor of cortisol, and all other steroids. If melanin is not well hydrated, this cleavage fails, and you get pregnenolone steal syndrome. This is why males are losing testosterone at record rates and why females' sex steroid hormones are being demolished too.

Glucocorticoid synthesis is tightly regulated at the level of cholesterol metabolism, which responds to ACTH stimulation over minutes and ceases equally quickly when this hormone is removed. All require well-hydrated melanin sheets to make Becker's regenerative current. Remarkably, this dynamic process is modulated under most circumstances not by control of the intrinsic enzymatic activity of P450scc, but rather by substrate availability. If that substrate is not there because you're missing Becker's current you are screwed. For this reason, cholesterol transport within the mitochondrion has emerged IN ALL MAMMALS as the key control point for steroidogenesis.

Epigenetic light signals control all your hormone panels. MKULTRA found this out in 1964, and the government has weaponized it to put in screen technology to use against you since screens replaced paper. It was confirmed in Neil Armstrong's testosterone catastrophe when he returned from the Moon. Not only did he have pseudotumor cerebri, but he also had head optic nerve swelling that linked his nnEMF exposure to his acute rapid loss of pituitary function.

DARPA took the lesson and added to another silo they developed in California where screens were moved from green analogy signals to blue lit. This excerpt from another post was helpful for me (that was to some else with hormone issues.)

“Melanin’s Role: Melanin, a semiconductive pigment, absorbs light across the spectrum, particularly UV, and facilitates electron transfer in bioelectric signaling. Its hydration status is critical to your regeneration, That comes from cytochrome C oxidase which comes from rhe red light part of the spectrum. Because your surface is so dark you need way more sun than you think to make the mtDNA make enough water inside for melanin to operate to regenerate you. I have told you that in the consult and you just do not get it. YOU aint getting better at a high latitide because of the biophysics of your skin. Regenerating internal melanin via hydration is critical for managing oxidative stress and supporting mitochondrial electron transport (step 7 in the diagram). Dehydrated melanin—caused by blue light’s lack of hydrating red/UV wavelengths—loses its conductivity, impairing oxygen utilization at cytochrome c oxidase (Complex IV, step 7).

Being dark skinned at a high altitide is giving yourself oxygen toxicity.

Oxygen “Allergy”: Dehydrated tissues struggle to handle oxygen’s paramagnetic properties, leading to an “allergy” to O₂. This manifests as increased reactive oxygen species (ROS) production, as cytochrome c oxidase fails to efficiently reduce O₂ to water (step 9). The energy cascade diagram shows this as a failure to maintain the proton gradient (ΔμH⁺, step 8), reducing ATP synthesis and causing mitochondrial dysfunction.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-03-16T19:57:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-348716>

The only meter I know of that measures above 8 GHz is the new Safe and Sound Pro mmWave meter. $850+
This measures between 20 GHz and 40 GHz which leaves a gap in measuring between 8 GHZ and 20 GHz and above 40 GHz. In America T-Mobile, GCI and Dish all use “V-band” which is 47 GHz - 48 GHz

The AM-11 measures 200 MHz - 8 GHz but if the reading is above 100,000µW you have no idea how high. I have seen 3,000,000+µW on my Safe and Sound Pro 2 when a phone is first turned on. These meters do not tell you the frequency but the new Cornet Quad 5G Pro does.
Remember, the lower frequencies can travel great distances (hundreds even thousands of miles) and go through everything.

I have received multiple calls from people that believed they were being targeted. The resources required to target an individual are extensive. If there is no truck across the street and the neighbors are ok, the odds of “targeting” are remote. I believe most of these people became EHS and have no idea that they are just super sensitive to all kinds of EMF In their home.

There are many kinds of energy. I would need more details but your “blue tornado” experience sounds like some “spiritual” experiences I have heard about.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-03-17T18:55:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-348770>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=348753)
>
> I would LOVE to know what meter measures that shit so I can measure it for myself. When you have a free moment…

These are ALL non-native (man made) NOT NATURAL . . .
In addition to frequency there is power/amplitude and modulation (a frequency within the frequency) the delivery/broadcast method. Continuous broadcast vs pulsed. For example 5G is not a frequency but a technology that can use 600MHz +/- upto 300GHz +/-.

Every frequency/band requires its own meter/receiver. Think AM radio vs VHF vs Ham vs TV vs 3G/4G/5G phones.
Then there is the possible combination effect of two or more of these frequencies working together, harmonics, standing waves etc.

There are no meters for some of this stuff, especially the band/frequencies the military use (Unless you are military with the required clearance).
Where meters are available for some of these frequencies the cost can be 10’s even 100’s of THOUSANDS of $‘s.

Do you really need an analysis, full biological work up of the shit . . . to know it is shit . . . And to avoid the shit?
As Jack says a 5G meter up to 8GHz ($200-$400) should show enough to anyone that gives a damn.
Even IF you could measure all the other stuff what good would that do when people don’t believe it is a problem/issue anyway?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-18T22:32:48+0200

Broadcast Theory, as a concept of ELF signals resonating with natural and biological systems, finds support in Becker’s and Rutger Wever’s human response data and NASA’s VLF-related belt findings. ELF broadcasting from bases can and does influence the ionosphere above cities and states it is used and because of the plasma physics we know the lower Van Allen belts can be altered to cause electron precipitation or scattering, which makes them tunable and subject to human interventions and altering them above transmitter military sites. However, It is beleived that DARPA has black boxed direct NASA evidence for ELF-specific, location-dependent belt changes. It is very hard to get recent data from the public records and from the EPA because all are redacted in FOIA requests. It is also clear that VLF EMF effects are well substantiated in the records and papers when one looks for them.

### Woodstock — 2025-03-20T15:04:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-348948>

Update on my current airbnb in El Salvador. Got here last night. In Xanadu. Pretty low EMFs here. Nice open air place. Except not enough fans in the bedrooms and air conditioning doesn’t agree with me. I didn’t feel comfortable leaving the giant window wide open last night. I guess it’s safe here but I was raised with a lot of fear about such things and it’s hard to shake. Lots of neighbors and people very close. The cell towers are far enough away that I don’t need the EMF canopy. Doesn’t seem like I have to be concerned as much about scorpions, spiders, roaches and the geckos chirping all night long and pooping all over the place like my last airbnb. But I loved that house and watching the surfers and lots of stuff about that place. Feels more sterile here and I was super sensitive to the smelly cleaning crap they used. First day I’ve had any sinus issues since being in ES. I could see the sunrise better from my patio here though. I like that. It’s peaceful and I have an ocean view. I can only afford this place for 10 days. Moving all my stuff and groceries was a pain. It took a lot out of me. I like that I am checking out a lot of places so I can find the right fit for me. But it has a few challenges. I will figure it out. Grateful to be here and have a wonderful friend visiting me and picking up our other bestie today at the airport. After 6 days I will be on my own again. Which is good too. I like that the time has been broken up so I don’t get too stuck in a routine or attached to any one place or habits. Atami is very close and easy to drive over there and go to the Palmarcito beach and hang with the mitos from here. A cat has adopted me. She won’t leave. Been here since sunset last night.

Move to another state? Yeah well that is gonna be difficult because I am not the owner of our home. I think it’s in a trust or something and hubby loves our home. We have created many outdoor spaces to enjoy daily as well as to host friends and have been creating community there since 1996. I have built my life around nature even more so since Covid. I dreamed of having a homestead with a cow and many animals but I can’t do that at my home anyway because it’s in town. I grow vegetables and raise chickens and bees. But I wasn’t getting better despite all this digging in the dirt and also was not focused on the sunrise and was staying up too late in the summer working in the garden coming inside covered with dirt and eating too late. I already figured out this wasn’t working prior to finding you Uncle Jack! Yes I AM listening to nature. I was telling hubby I gotta go to the tropics, but I’m so glad I found you because I might have ended up in a place with lots of military bases. I do love many things about El Salvador and the mito community has been wonderful. I’m not sure how I’m going to afford to move here long term but I will figure something out. I am going to immerse myself into the mito lifestyle at the ideal place I have found at Atami I will be at the last two weeks of my time here. My place here in Xanadu for the next week is pretty ideal as well. I am still transitioning into this life as I tend to do some of my best work at night in regards to art/music/creativity. I will go back for 3-4 weeks and get my jury duty over with and see all my babies. I have dogs too. I need to check on my bees and probably split the hive. I wish the sun would work faster on me so I would be in the mood to take my husband to bed early when I get home…but not for sleeping. I used to use psychedelics and that would help the issue but I don’t do any of that stuff anymore and not for a long time because it was frying my brain and killing my liver and blowing out my adrenals. But it did work in the beginning and at times I felt like what it must feel like to be normal and have feelings of joy. I am really interested in the science behind why MDMA and LSD etc helped my brain so much but only for a while and allowed me to feel human for the first time in my life but then eventually only made it worse. Was this also the dehydration of melanin sheets or something else? Or is there more you can add that is specific to psychadelic use? Just curious. I can’t be the only person fucked up since birth who tried literally everything to fix themselves and was at the end of the rope. I am truly grateful to learn that the sun will fix me. Now it’s just all the other little details and habits I have to change, which I am doing over time. Went to sleep at 8pm but up at 3am because I can’t really sleep for more than 7 hours. Which may be permanent? I think I recall you saying that at some point the sleep mechanism gets messed up and can’t be fixed and that sleep can’t be “made up”. I am hoping it’s not too late and I will be able to sleep soundly for 8-9 hours again someday.

### Revelation — 2025-03-24T04:35:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-349228>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=345019)
>
> I am 55 yrs old and have had gut issues, fatigue, POTS symptoms and chronic aches and pains since early childhood. About 5 years ago I tested positive for Lyme which I likely have had since around age 4, based on symptoms and history.
>
> I felt like an 80 year old when I was in my late 20s. (So I am actually doing much better now.) I got the diagnosis of Fibromyalgia from a Chiropractor who took the time to take a full medical history and check pressure points. When I went to the Arthritis Foundation website and read their description of the disease I cried for hours. Mostly of relief that I was not psycho because I had been told previously that this was psychosomatic and “no pain no gain” throughout my childhood while I pushed myself athletically into directions that likely only exacerbated my symptoms while trying to keep up with my non-biological siblings. I felt like a weakling and a failure, while simultaneously knowing I was actually smarter than most people. I just had a delayed reaction in responding and spent a lot of time in my head figuring stuff out. Nowadays this behavior would be diagnosed as on the spectrum, but I was never recognized as having any issues of the sort. It makes sense to me now. I likely had heavy metal poisoning from living in an area that had been destroyed by a tornado when I was 5-6 yrs old. I had some OCD symptoms at that age that improved after I moved.
>
> My current health issues are:
> Lyme and co-infections
> mold exposure/mycotoxins
> high fasting insulin/borderline pre-diabetic
> MCAS
> candida since age 10
> EBV
> Strep
> Staph
> SIBO/gut issues/gluten issues
> chronic pain and stiffness in joints, neck and shoulders
> liver and gallbladder issues
> possible kidney issues
> zero libido
> menopause-on Bioidentical hormones
> gained 30 pounds since menopause and very difficult to lose
>
> since September I have been doing sunrise walks and getting sun on my body as much as possible
>
> I have been working on lifestyle changes to eat more regularly and eat dinner earlier and reduce blue light exposure
>
> about 6 years ago I had a building biologist evaluate and help figure out how to do all the right things. My house was Ethernet wired and we havent used a Wifi router during this time. HOWEVER, when Century Link brought in fiber optic cable, they gave us a new modem which had 5G turned on and the only way to turn it off was to sign in on their website and do it online. We didn’t realize this and it appeared to be off externally. Two days ago I found out that the modem had been putting off a 5G signal for YEARS unbeknownst to me. I have an RF meter from SLT but it only read high right at the source of the modem and was showing ok levels throughout the main areas of the house I use frequently so I just never realized this was happening. But I hated being in that room. I had a desktop computer in there and never wanted to use it. I moved the computer to another room recently but I never wanted to walk through the 5G area to the other part of the house. When I brought the building biologist back and had him go through the house with me, he quickly figured out what was going on and showed us how to turn off the 5G from the Century Link website with the password on the bottom of the modem. I would normally be really pissed off about being radiated for all these years, but that would just be a waste of my energy. It’s finally off now. The worst part of this is that we had a few water leaks and therefore mold issues which are supposedly made 600x worse with Wifi. We remediated the mold years ago but did not throw away everything in our home. We are in a dry climate so we have an evaporative cooler and I always feel worse midsummer when the humidity level gets really high from the cooler being on 24/7 for weeks in a row. The high humidity causes mold spores to reactivate. Hopefully this will improve now!
>
> I am coming to El Salvador for a couple of months this winter. I hope this will be a good jumpstart on getting my health on track. I plan to spend a lot of time in the tropics and on the ocean for at least the next three years or longer. I was already getting the intuition that I needed to do this before stumbling upon Dr Kruse on Twitter back in September.
>
> I will add more details and labs at some point. I just wanted to get this journal started. It has only been two days that my house has been free of 5G, but I am so relieved to know that there was actually something so easy I could do that theoretically should make a huge difference. (We had already done all the hard work but were not reaping the benefit!) Just another confirmation for me to listen to my gut feelings and intuitions and the more subtle things that I feel because there was a massive reason I have been avoiding half of my house for YEARS! I would normally beat myself up for not getting help on this sooner and figuring it out, but I’ve spent 55 years beating myself up and believing my pain was all my own fault and I am so DONE with that self sabotage type of behavior. So much crazy stuff going on in the world but I am going to have my best year ever in 2025. I might even learn to surf. Apparently the beach I will be near is good surf for beginners in Feb/March. I’ve also always felt physically better with a tan. The pieces are coming together.
>
> Thanks for reading.

Awesome accounting and it helps to hear about modems that have built in trickery. We have ours on a timer, mechanical timer. Powered down from midnight to 7 AM Hope you make fast recovery and improvements.

It is why some ppl get hammered by mosquitoes and some don't.. All ties back to redox state & biophoton emissions are a reflection of your redox state.. It can go the other way bees will be attracted to you, land on you & you can even pat them and they will not sting you.. Birds and other forms of life will be drawn to structured and coherent biophoton emissions & will not see you as a threat. This is a non-verbal communication or presence method.. When redox is good and you are in sync tightly with your environment you will notice things change in areas that are least expecting..

Every morning, I have a group of Crows visit me and I can go about my morning routine out in our yard, they come up close and I can do my movements walk past them and they do not flinch or move (they just watch me).. They land on my ice bath when I'm in it.. Other birds visit as well. Likewise with bees, we have quite a bit of lavender bush and I was noticing bees would land on me very often and my first reaction was to move away, then I didn't and pat them with my finger softly as an experiment lol..

I had stopped getting bitten after a few long days in the sun here. So I think my redox was improving. Then I moved to a different airbnb and felt super exhausted after that. I had non-mito friends visiting and I stayed out after dark and probably lowered my redox again. But also Inwas unlucky and stupid in parking there and opening the door and disturbing their nest. It happened so fast! Within a second there were hundreds on me swarming across my body before I could brush them off. I haven’t been getting mosquito bites anymore here. But I know I need to get back in the sun. I enjoy having my friends here and I’m glad they came. If this had not happened I wouldn’t have learned the lesson on redox I suppose. I surrender to it. It of course could have been worse…if I had SAT down on a fire ant pile and they went in my privates. Lol. Funny thing is that I am a beekeeper when I’m in Colorado. But I’m not that great at it yet. I thought it was about experience and skill but maybe redox is a big part of it. I will see if it’s any different after being here. I will need to attend to my hive as soon as I return. I think my bees survived the winter which is a miracle given the varroa mite that has become a huge problem over the last 15 years. It sucks that I got called to jury duty but I just want to get it over with and also tend to my hive and keep them from swarming which can happen in April if you don’t take necessary steps. I sure hope my bees like me better after improving my redox!

My shoulder has been fucked up for 7 years and has kept me from playing my instrument. It feels better here than it has in a long time. And I can play again. I don’t know how I’m going to make a living out here. Do people even like live music? Where can I busk for dollars? My true love is music and always has been. But much like Jack has said about rock stars, the lifestyle killed them. I went on tour with a band for 6 weeks and it nearly killed me. I’ve been trying to tell this to my parents and docs since I was a kid. I finally feel SEEN. So go ahead Jack, keep punching me in the mouth. I know that’s NOT what you’re doing. Actually Inwas discussing that with other mitos who say NOBODY HAS ONE and that ping pong is GREAT for the brain and they would LOVE it if someone had a ping pong table. If someone doesn’t beat me to it, I AM GOING TO BRING IT. So watch out. If y’all don’t like it, you don’t have to come to my Mito Ping Pong Party. The good news is that mine was 8 in 2021 when there was mold in my house. In Nov 2024 I re tested it and it’s up to 22. Mold was remediated a couple years ago. Just wondering if I need to retest again so soon because that test is pretty expensive. My feeling is that I can hold off because it was already going up and I can check again after I’ve had a full six month stretch here in ES. I am looking at all my flagged levels now and any other concerning labs to re-test immediately after I get back to Chernobyl. Not going to wait for the radiation to have a chance to undo my time in ES. I Already have my ZRT kit being ordered and mailed to me. My NP requires me to retest that anyway. I am weaning off my bio identicals. I am trusting that all I need is the sun. It will be interesting to see if my vitamin D has changed significantly. If not then I guess testing alpha-msh would be the next step. Imy experience is that the sun really does detox mold and mold ihas been a big part of my problem for many decades. Also candida. So it does make sense that the sun is helping with that. Hopefully my brain is improved a little and I can be productive and organized and get my shit together to airbnb out half of my house which requires a shower to be installed. The harder part is getting rid of things and organizing which requires a brain with executive function.

### Woodstock — 2025-03-26T15:17:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-349375>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=349373)
>
> Depemds on the lab you get it drawn at. Each one has a different reference range for this assay

Ok sure. The lab I used says 0-100 is normal. I was at a different lab the first time so maybe it didn’t actually improve. In Nov it was 21.6. I am thinking that you would like to see this closer to the higher end of the range and if so I have a ways to go. It’s expensive so unless you say retest I will wait on this when I’ve had more of this mito lifestyle. I have been eating more seafood so I want to see if my omegas are getting better. And I have been monitoring insulin, lipids, liver enzymes, vitamin D and hormones pretty regularly over the last few years before finding you trying to put the puzzle pieces together. Even Hawaii improved my labs but they quickly went South after returning to Chernobyl. I know I can’t be there for long but I have to do some shit to make living here financially possible. I have been a dependent my entire life due to this sickness. And every time I’ve gotten a job, though I have enjoyed the work, the schedule and having to constantly “mask” (which I have learned about after discovering this is part of Asperger’s/spectrum…ie brain inflammation) it literally sucks the life out of me. And the pay I am able to earn is not enough to buy groceries let alone a place to live. Hubby didn’t like it when I tried to work because I was a zombie after I got home and couldn’t do anything but the job. Same when I tried being a runner. After 2.5 years of going back to school for yet another useless degree under fluorescent lights all I wanted to do was RUN and BE OUTSIDE! I love how my intuition tells me these things. But I was still under the mindset of no pain no gain from the 70s and competing with my super athletic adoptive family and I guess I over-exercised. I gained weight even though I was running 25 miles a week. I did 4 half-marathons and many 10ks and 5Ks in just two years. I could only run every other day and then I had to rest and lie around the day in between to recover. It was like a full time job! BUT the wonderful thing that happened after all that running was that I got pregnant! Now that I understand how the sun heals mitochondria and hormones etc maybe it was all that running outside in the sunshine that made it possible for me to conceive. It’s the only time that I was pregnant ever that I know of. I wasn’t really trying to get pregnant ever because I was terrified I wouldn’t have the energy and health to care for a baby. My baby is so amazing and made me so happy after 36 years of being chronically depressed. He is turning 19. I am missing his birthday but will be home a couple days after so another reason I surrender to the jury duty thing. Last time I got called was maybe 10 years ago so if I get it over with I should be good for the next decade. They aren’t even going to accept me because I used to be a prosecutor in Chernobyl. I will be thrown out on day one without cause. But you still have to show up and go through the process. I was out of town last time and went through it all before. My dad was dying then and it was super stressful because we didn’t know how long he would survive and if I postponed, which I had to then, if they call me again and it’s in the middle of his funeral etc. That is why I’m just going to get it over with now so I can come back without that stress cloud hanging over me. I gotta give my bees away too. Hubby is NOT a beekeeper. Shit to do. Build a shower in the guest room side of the house and start an airbnb. It’s Ethernet wired and no one there knows that Boulder is really Chernobyl. Could make good money renting it out. Then I will be back to the tropics ASAP. I still have 15 days here and I am going to soak it all in—full on mito lifestyle!

### Woodstock — 2025-03-27T15:39:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-349435>

> [MTA321 said:](/goto/post?id=349431)
>
> I know you have some other things to shore up besides jury duty but when I had jury duty scheduled I simply just called them and told them I would be out of the country and they removed me from the list. Unless you just have a strong urge for civil service, it might be worth a call.

What state are you from? They are all different. Not sure if you read the whole thing or missed the part that they wouldn’t excuse me last time when my dad was sick and I was out of state. Didn’t excuse it. Also it specifically says being out of the country isn’t an excuse. You get ONE exemption so YES I could get exempted, BUT then they can call you back at any time during the next 6 months and then there is zero excuse or exemption. I don’t want that hanging over my head. It’s a mental fuck. And I plan on coming back here. It’s only cutting my trip a couple weeks short and my bees might swarm if I’m not back by mid-April. I have a prepaid dental appointment that I will not get my money back on in early May. It’s my baby’s birthday too and I’m still staying two days after that to make it an even 7 weeks and I need two days rest before I have to show up for Jury duty in the 14th so I return on the 11th. They are going to throw me out on day one. It’s not about civic duty. It’s about GOVERNMENT WASTE because I used to be a prosecutor in that very office and I will be tossed without cause on day 1. I fully know how the system works. I was working in the “Chernobyl” prosecutor’s office when OJ made his white bronco car chase run and when Jon Benet Ramsey was brutally tortured and murdered less than two miles from my home. Most of the DA’s thought it was the mom or the little brother. I think we all now know it was the same creepy satanic cult that the “elites” and hollyweird are into and they just meant to suck the energy out of her, not kill her. Big time cover up from the police and likely the head elected DA at the time. I don’t regret for a second leaving that career and walking away from my dream of helping change the system. I knew it was bullshit when I started working there and saw how the local billionaire who owned most of the commercial real estate in town got away with abusing the girl he put up in a condo to screw while married to another and SHE was being charged with domestic abuse after he was threatening her with a gun. I interviewed her many times. And many others. I want nothing to do with all of that. But if somehow they did LET me on a jury ever I would gladly do my “civic” duty because I can see through the bullshit and it’s usually the wrong people who end up going to jail and the rich assholes are the ones that get off. If I could be a part of helping a wrongly accused defendant, I would do it without hesitation or complaint. I will be thrown out on day one without cause because of my history working there. But they aren’t allowed to SAY that’s why they are throwing me out. It’s a a game. A game that costs taxpayers extra money because of all the extra work and people employed to play this bullshit game. I’m not sure if DOGE is doing it for the right reasons but I am all for cutting out government waste. Don’t even get me started on that shit…Oh and the mass shooting that happened a half mile from my home and killed 10 people including my beautiful friend Lonna, I didn’t have the stomach to follow that but the kid was on SSRIs and he got life in prison. I would bet a million dollars (if I had it) that the poor kid was MK-Ultra’d into doing the deed. Probably was being mind controlled by our evil government. I bet that type of evidence never even got presented in the case. Good thing I wasn’t called in for duty on that one. That would have been quite a shit show. I was planning to go get groceries at the time it happened but I lost one of my chickens that morning and I was unusually sad about it. Sometimes you just gotta listen to your heart and feelings. We have infinite eternal wisdom within us.

Thank you! I am hoping the environment doesn’t keep me from being productive. I have a lot to do to make living in ES long-term possible. I’ve been in my home for 29 years and the house is not in my name. I am married to my best friend and our vows are all about supporting each other being most fully ourselves which sometimes means doing things separately if that is in our individual best interests. I manifested it that way from day 1. My life would have been shit without him and vice versa because of our unconditional and non-traditional support and partnership. It weirds most people out and they like to judge me because he pays the bills and think I’m a freeloader taking advantage of him. They don’t know shit about our partnership. I also need to go home and take care of some shit and not just dump all responsibility in his lap. I’ve got my bees and chickens to figure out and maybe I can figure out airbnb on part of our home. That’s my arena of talent not his. I would be dead if he hadn’t been taking care of me the last 28 years. Most likely by suicide. Lyme gets in your brain and causes a lot of depression and mental illness. I can’t imagine how I would have survived the last 28 years on my own. We do amazing things together we would never have done alone. And though he isn’t thrilled he gets that I need to be here or somewhere tropical and low emf to be most fully myself. If I had lots of money maybe it would be easier to just buy a beautiful beach house here. But I wouldn’t give up the last 30 years of all the ups and downs we have had together for a 10 million dollar beach house and enough bitcoin to do whatever I wanted. Sounds lonely.
