### Woodstock — 2025-03-27T21:02:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-349452>

> [Joseph LeBlanc said:](/goto/post?id=349445)
>
> how exactly did you manage to get fire ants all over your arm...

I parked too close to a bush thinking it was a narrow road and I didn’t want to obstruct anyone else’s path, and when I opened the rear passenger door to get my bag out of the car I think I disturbed a nest they had in the bush or something. They were all over me in 2 seconds and I quickly got away and brushed them off but it was too late. They even got under my kiniki suit so it was on my side, back and waist as well. Just glad I didn’t sit in a pile of them. Could have been much worse in that case! Luckily I had brought some anti-histamines from the US because I have MCAS. I didn’t have the Benadryl on me though. From now on I will always make sure to have some. Not exactly “mito” but until I get better, I am more susceptible to histamine reactions. Not as bad as the black widow bite I got 15 years ago. I guess I’m a savage after all. It’s nearly healed now except for the scars.

Lol…just reading all my journal entries and reflecting on all of it because clearly I am releasing a lot of pent up anger and frustration over how my entire life history has been affected by everything Uncle Jack talks about, even from the moment of conception. If I seem crass or whatever other adjective people want to label me as, that is ok with me. I have every right to be angry and Uncle Jack has helped me to further connect the dots as to all the reasons WHY! My first exposure to Jack was a clip on Twitter of the Danny Jones podcast, and the way he talked reminded me so much of myself and all the times over the last decade that I’ve been trying to tell people about SV-40 and the real story behind Oswald and the JFK assassination. I got so much judgment and hate for this and I was absolutely thrilled that an intelligent and respected neurosurgeon was talking about this. And that he was angry too. It’s the first time in a long time I didnt feel alone and I felt validated and excited that there was actually a human alive who could TEACH ME something instead of constantly hitting people on the head with a bat with truth bombs and still they stayed asleep. I had sent the interview from Coast to Coast AM with Ed Haslam who wrote Dr Mary’s Monkey to countless people over the years. Maybe one friend out of all of them ever actually listened to it. i have been shamed and ostracized by the others Or simply discounted. That is a good interview if you haven’t ever read Dr. Mary’s Monkey and don’t get around to it. Haslam goes into a lot of detail and towards the end he talks about AIDS and how that is a bioweapon. It’s an old interview, long before COVID happened. So it’s interesting background. I came across that info about a decade ago while I was desperately trying to figure out what was wrong with me and learned about retroviruses and those breadcrumbs directed me to a doctor talking about SV-40 causing cancer and how it was in the Polio vaccines and passes down through the mother, grandmother, etc. I was shocked to learn that cancer comes from a VIRUS! I was so pissed about how we have been lied to about cancer and all the cures and doctors who got blackballed or killed for the treatments and cures they had that interfered with pharma’s chemo radiation death therapy. This is not about patting myself on the back, Uncle Jack, though I see you doing that for yourself in a lot of your podcast Interviews! Truly I am just thrilled to finally have found others who GET IT as this has been a very long 56 year road if you count my time as a fetus where I was ingrained with the energy that I am wrong simply for existing/being conceived. This is not an easy trauma to shake. I work on this every day. I even tried to call my birth mother last night in attempts to create some positive energy there but she didn’t answer the phone. Her TDS and blue light toxicity is intense. But it’s not her fault. Or anyone’s. We have all been manipulated and genocided and put into slavery at least since the federal income tax and federal reserve was created. I have done tons of therapy to release my birth trauma over the years…soul retrieval, re-birthing therapy, Landmark, bio-feedback, EMDR, and the list goes on…is it truly just the sun, grounding, and eating like a great white? I have seemingly tried everything else and no person or practitioner has ever SEEN me like Uncle Jack does. No one else has ever spoken in a way that resonated with my core like this. The only reason I am going back to Chernobyl for a bit is to do a few things so I can return for longer and longer and fix my brain with the sun and the fish and everything else and finally get my brain and body up to full potential. I have managed to do a lot of amazing things with a fucked up brain and body so it’s going to be really interesting to see what it looks like when I become a fully functioning human!

Feeling super shitty today. Started coming on yesterday. Just lung congestion. Not a cold. There’s a leak in the air conditioning in my airbnb and no ceiling fan. Water dripping down the dry wall. This is mold. Was out in the sun all day. Didn’t help much. I was weaning off Nystatin so I increased my dose again until Incan move to a different place on Sunday. It’s just a wake up call that Jack is right. I am very unwell. And this isn’t going to turn around in two months after being sick for 55 years. Writing all this down so Indont blow it off and forget about it when I start feeling good again.

### Woodstock — 2025-03-29T16:51:05+0200

Mold is freaking evil. But I know if my mitochondria were healthy it probably wouldn’t bother me so much. Thank God I check out if this airbnb tomorrow. Hoping the next one is better. at first I thought I was reacting to the bug fumigation that I could smell when I checked into this place. I woke up stuffy and congested for the first time since I had arrived a month prior where I was in a totally open air place. Lots of scorpions, spiders, and other Critters there, but that place in Mizata was much better for my health. once I saw the air conditioning leaking water in my room I figured it out. But too late. I measured the EMFs when I arrived and picked the room with the lowest readings. Praying I made a better airbnb choice for my last 12 days here. My protocol is Nebulizing silver, taking Nystatin which I was nearly weaned off of a week ago, and going to make some strong tea with whole cloves, cinnamon and green tea I brought with me. And of course sunshine, but that Hasn’t been enough when you’re breathing in mold spores all night. Yeah, I know Uncle Jack. You’re right, I am Very unwell. Two months here is not going to fix me. but all these crazy things like ant bites and reacting to mold despite being here is probably helping wake me up Because sometimes I focus too much on how far I’ve come from my 20s when I had fibromyalgia, POTS and severe depression and couldn’t eat anything without feeling like someone was stabbing me in my stomach with a giant knife. And then I tend to downplay my current sickness because no one but you actually believes I’m sick. Thank you for being the one person who understands. I wrote this a few days ago but was trying to stay offline as much as possible my last few days in El Salvador. Coming back to ES on May 15 for two more healing months. Then have to be in CO for a few more weeks in July and then maybe will have a more long term mito healing location figured out. I’m spending my 56th birthday (June 4) in Atami at the place I rented for two months. Ping Pong will be involved. And cold plunges! Maybe pupusas Too. Benne’s party was pretty stellar so it’s a lot to be inspired by. But I’ve been the Conviviality Queen Bee here in Chernobyl (Boulder) since 1996. So I got skills. Just have to tweak them to be more mito.

note: I’ve only been home a few hours and my right neck pain is back. I’m hoping it’s mostly from the plane radiation and will go away soon. I guess I will keep updating on this. It has hurt for 7 years and went away after 10 days in Costa Rica in December and then again after 10 days coming to El Salvador in late Feb.

from April 9:
Wow. Today is my baby’s 19th birthday. As my 7 weeks in El Salvador comes to a pause until I get back for round 2, I am feeling so many emotions. Yesterday I was very homesick. Also frustrated in how difficult it actually is to find a low emf rental that is the right fit. I have been in Atami for my last 10 days and all the mitos here are so nice and helpful. I think I would have given up without the supportive community here. I’ve been very lonely but I’ve made such good friends in such a short time. Feeling blessed. And I think I’ve got my next stretch out here figured out. It will be so hard to be away from home again but I do think this is making a difference. I’ve tried so many other things to heal for as long as I can remember and yet maybe it’s not as complicated as we think it is? Sunshine and local food. Being outside. Do something you love. There are other places I long to go visit but maybe when I am more resilient it will be ok then. For now, I will do what I can at home. I will work on cold therapy. Richard has helped so much in the two times he has guided me. I feel so lucky to have had that support for my first two times. I have one of those inflatable hot tubs from Costco but the heater has freaking Wi-Fi on it or Bluetooth or something and it can’t be turned off. I am turning it into a CT tub and then maybe just return it! Costco takes everything back. Lol. Not going back to old unhealthy habits. No interest. Just want to keep going and keep healing and not let my location throw me off. There are other things I can do that I’m not currently doing that will help offset the environment for the short time I’m there until I can return to El Salvador…The Savior!

### warriorT — 2025-04-13T12:33:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-350594>

Hey Ann-Marie, so pleased you’re going to keep on healing in ES ! Thank you for sharing your experience, it’s super helpfuI. I am trying to orchestrate a similar process and was wondering have you arranged a medium term rental or are you doing Airbnb’s again? I am concerned about how to find mito friendly accommodation as well and it sounds hard :/

### Woodstock — 2025-04-13T16:56:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-350599>

> [worldoftara said:](/goto/post?id=350594)
>
> Hey Ann-Marie, so pleased you’re going to keep on healing in ES ! Thank you for sharing your experience, it’s super helpfuI. I am trying to orchestrate a similar process and was wondering have you arranged a medium term rental or are you doing Airbnb’s again? I am concerned about how to find mito friendly accommodation as well and it sounds hard :/

I stayed multiple airbnb places, partly to see What area I was attracted to. But when looking for a good spot in Atami it was hard. Most are set up for big families with lots of kids and bunk beds! And there are a lot of cell towers. So if you find places that let you cancel, once You arrive you can drive around with your meters and measure outside of the houses and look for visible towers. Lower elevations where you can’t visibly see a tower is usually the best. then you can ask if you can come by for a tour but I wouldnt mention why. Just that you would like to see it in person before booking. Once you get inside you can ask to unplug Wi-Fi and make sure they don’t have a cell phone on them which will affect your readings. If you are able to find a good one you like, at the end of your stay if you have been a good renter and establish a good relationship, you can often use WhatsApp direct with the owner. Then negotiate outside of airbnb for a better price long term. Or get an El Salvadorian you know to help you find a place from the start because that would have been very helpful. You could also hire Olle on this forum to check places out for you at least from the outside, and then if something looks promising, schedule a time he can go inside and do a full evaluation.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-13T17:12:14+0300

Everyone probably should stay in a hotel first then hire Olle.......and them jump.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-13T17:38:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-350608>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=350600)
>
> Everyone probably should stay in a hotel first then hire Olle.......and them jump.

Yes that is a good plan! Olle is so knowledgeable. I learned a lot from him. He could really Help folks avoid the stress I had to go through. Sometimes the airbnb owners don’t even realize there is a cell tower right in sight blasting on them. It just blends into the background to them. So you can’t trust them to tell you if there is a tower near.

The one thing Olle or anyone could not have helped me with was detecting the mold in the beautiful place I had for 10 days in Xanadu. There was a leak in the air conditioner and I believe mold was inside of the wall which was not visible. My lungs kept getting worse. My two friends who visited didn’t have issues. The lowest emf room was also the one with the leaky air conditioner. I luckily had brought a nebulizer and silver and a tincture of creptolepis which after taking I could feel improvement in less than a minute. That was confirmation that I was dealing with mold and not a “cold” or something else. I know supplements aren’t your thing, and I didn’t take 99% of what I brought to El Salvador, but that one saved me! I was still sick and weak when I moved out of that place and into a place in Atami that thankfully didn’t have mold, and I continued to improve. I worked out a deal with the owner for two more months when I come back in May. I hope I like the rainy season!

so my advice would be shorter stays in the beginning until you find the right fit. Then maybe you can negotiate a deal direct with the owner When you find your match. Good luck mitos! Be savages, persevere, and never give up!

### Woodstock — 2025-04-15T20:26:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-350742>

Listening to this podcast and thinking about what you said Uncle Jack regarding the benefits of cracking the window in the car while driving and wondering if this applies to your home as well. I mean sure it does if you are close to a window, but in a house you might not be so close to windows all the time and also you might have screens on the windows Blocking UV?

Thinking about this brings up the memory of my childhood through age 18 when my mother NEVER opened a window in the house EVER. The house was air conditioned or heated 365 days a year. When I was in high school, I managed to pry my window open because it was painted shut. I wanted some fresh air! Possibly another piece of the puzzle of why I always felt like crap, except in the summer if I could go to the pool that was 5 miles away and be outside and getting tan all day. That and Spring Break in Florida visiting my grandparents. It was all about getting a tan for me. Just not enough opportunities.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-17T01:51:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-350811>

I wonder if it is ok or might be helpful to get an IV just for hydration purposes? so lethargic since the flight home 5 days ago.

Going from such a humid to a very dry climate…I think I am dehydrated. Feeling so exhausted and having POTS symptoms. Almost passed out in Home Depot yesterday. Can’t seem to get hydrated. Doing cold plunges (62 degrees) for 20 min a day helps a little to perk me up. My nurse practitioner has a nice set up for IVs. It’s not cheap but maybe would help me re-acclimate? I am coming back to El Salvador in 4 weeks and have so much I need to get done before radiating myself in more airplanes.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-17T16:14:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-350832>

Successes!

working on COLD PLUNGES at home (Boulder) with the water at around 62 degrees because that’s what it is after sitting in the tub overnight without ice. I’m up to 23 minutes. ordered Ice block molds so I can start getting the temp down to 55 degrees soon. It’s been sunny out so it’s been nice because I am able to keep my tan when I sit in the lounge chair when I get out and it feels great. It’s helped to perk me up a little but I am still super lethargic since getting home 5 days ago. I have Richard from Sweden to thank for coaching me on CT while in ES! I like it.

I gave my BEES away yesterday. This is the first time in 3 years that my BEES SURVIVED the winter which has become very challenging due to the varroa mite—now a major threat to all hives in the US. I wasn’t sad, I was really happy because the man who took them had originally given this genetic line to my bee mentor who had to split the hive last spring and gave some to me. Then she lost her hive to mites so I held the last of this genetic line. The man who came and got them had been injured and then lost all his other hives last year and currently had no bees left! He is recovered now. And he is so sweet and a much better beekeeper than I am. He is a natural with them. He was so grateful to get these bees. And I feel like this was a huge VICTORY for me to save this hive and genetic line and see them go to a good home. I won’t be around enough this summer to tend to the hive while in El Salvador. So it is the best thing for me and the bees. Beekeeping has been a huge struggle for me due to my health and I was never able to give it 100% because you just can’t go in the hive when you are having a flare up of symptoms. It takes a lot of focus and stamina. I learned a lot in the last 4 years though, and I want to try again when I heal my mitochondria.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-21T19:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-351044>

Update on my health:

I think I am seeing some small but significant improvement.

I don’t get up to pee in the middle of the night anymore. Maybe just due to not taking so many supplements anymore. But that wasn’t good for sleep or for healing mtDNA during the sleep cycle. Yay!

Not seeing weight change but clothes fit better. Maybe some loss in overall inches. Better body composition. My back feels straighter without forcing or creating pain to stand or sit straighter.

I only have partial results back but so far seeing that vitamin D has increased a bit. I will post full results when they come in.

### Woodstock — 2025-05-03T10:36:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-351820>

I am currently Being microwaved by my kid who just came home from college. i am so glad he is here, and I was showing him my new meter the safe and Sound Pro 2 which is much more precise and detailed than the one I upgraded from. He has a phone, airpods and a laptop all giving off major Wi-Fi and bluetooth signals so I’m currently being microwaved. I guess it didn’t occur to me how much the signal of devices travels through the walls. It’s undeniable now with this meter. I guess it didn’t matter how careful I was being biohacking the whole house when his signal is just traveling acrcross the hall into my bedroom. It’s totally crazy that he is walking around with these devices in his pockets and backpack all the time. I am leaving in less than two weeks to come back to El Salvador. But I am going to have to move to the other side of the house or somehow get him to unplug and disconnect his tech. He doesnt get it but he does at least have a blue light filter on his computer, so maybe I have some chance of getting through to him? It’s super late but I woke up when he came home and now I can’t sleep. I will have to deal with this tomorrow. But it’s interesting that inside my house is better than outside until you turn on a cell phone. Then you might as well be standing in front of a 5G antenna.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-03T12:34:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-351821>

Do you have any of that thin mylar - wrapped bubble insulation? They sell it in rolls at DIY stores or you can get it online. I lined the wall behind my headboard to shield against some funky rays coming out of the outlets back there, and it seemed to help somewhat. I had borrowed a friend's meter to test the whole house, and gave it back to her when I was done so I'm afraid that "funky rays" is about as technical as I can get to describe what I was mitigating. The best thing would be to see if you can get your son to see, but that's a different challenge altogether. Best wishes.

### ToothDriller — 2025-05-03T16:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-351827>

I think your journey is a testimony to what many encounter. It’s hard to wrap your head around what [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) is saying. It’s a leap of faith and the more you read the more you learn it starts to sink in. I found it’s best to find another person to bounce ideas off of which is what [@digitald3](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/30310/) and I do as we try and fix sleep dentistry.
Jack is trying to tell you your health is most important. If you highly affected by local nnEMF it is basically slowly killing you.
As I am helping my patients understand there is only so much you can do locally to get fixed. If your insulin levels are still high I ask why? Are you still Leptin resistant? Do you see the sun rise? Are
You getting enough UVA and UVB? Do you block all blue light when the sun goes down? Do you eat 50g protein within 30 minutes of waking. Less than 25g carbs and sugar per day? Did you get a reverse T3, what’s your Vit D3?
Jack can get frustrated as I do with my patients when we see patients suffering when they often are getting in their own way. Its like just listen. Don’t think… just listen lol lol hahaha
It’s almost like follow first and learn later.
You are on the right track. Keep going. Took you a long time to get like this will take time to get out.
The key is the sun and block blue and nnEMF
I may of missed it but cold therapy would be a great way to jump start your mitochondria
Based on what you have said that’s a HAVE TO in my opinion. Start with your wrists, work up to your face and then whole body
Lastly if you are surrounded by nnEMF, your genetics and condition will make it impossible to get better it seems.
Keep fighting
Andy aka ToothDriller

Thanks for your input! Yes I am doing most of this stuff and my vitamin D did go up after 2 months in El Salvador. Going in the right direction and getting my stuff together to come back out for two more months. I was dealing with being homesick and feeling down and I can definitely improve on my mito habits this time around. I have been doing cold therapy. This has saved me since being back in Colorado. I see the sun rise every day. Most of the time we take the dogs for a walk but my house and yard is much lower in EMFs than the neighborhood in general. It’s safest inside but then you’re not getting sun. Which is why I’m coming back to El Salvador. Hopefully I will find enough things to keep myself busy with that I won’t have time to be homesick. I have a hard time just rolling around in the sand all day make sand piles! I like to swim in the ocean but the waves sometimes are too much. I sneak into the salt pools when I can. I’m getting better at eating oysters. It’s not practical to eat 30 minutes upon waking because you gotta make your breakfast and then walk down to the beach and then ground in the wet sand for 20 minutes. There is just no way I can eat within 30 minutes of waking and do all the other important stuff. My walk from my rental in Atami is about 15-20 minutes. I am bringing a cold plunge with me so I can do it regularly.

### Woodstock — 2025-05-05T07:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-351921>

The pow wow today was intense. I am feeling sad for those who got the jab and have ended up with very serious issues. I am very grateful to Del Bigtree (born and raised in Boulder) who came out with the movie Vaxxed and confirmed my suspicions a long time ago about the harmful ingredients in vaccines. also I have been very skeptical of government and centralized medicine for a very long time. I knew Covid was a depopulation agenda from day 1. I’ve lost most of the people in my community that I thought were friends because I tried to warn them about all the health dangers and the lies of politicians and their corruption. But they are still asleep. And now it breaks my heart to see people followimg Jack who are waking up to all this because of the serious harm that came to themselves and their loved ones. I am still mad at myself for allowing the doctor to vaccinate my baby 19 years ago and I just feel so grateful that the harm that came to him has been minor in comparison to what others are going through. I know he has been somewhat harmed by those shots and there isn’t much I can do now that he is 19 and I’m just now starting to understand this protocol. He doesn’t want my advice anymore and he likes it here. So I guess I’ve just got to focus on healing me. Ive been trying to plan and prepare for my trip back to El Salvador in about 10 days. My last trip was really stressful for a lot of reasons. But this trip I have the same place for two months in Atami and someone kept a lot of my things for me so I can bring other things along to make it easier this time. I have a portable cold plunge in my suitcase! I’m going to put it in the shower because I have two bathrooms. I have large freezer molds to make ice blocks in my freezer so I don’t have to haul ice every time I want to do a plunge. I have a new tan through swimsuit that should fit better and be more comfortable. I’ve been eating oysters. Whole foods will freshly shuck them for me. I suppose I should increase my oyster intake for the week before my flight. i will try to follow Jack’s jet lag protocol better this time. I have the C0 Q10 ready. I wish my daylight computer I ordered in December was here but that will have to wait. I’m bringing a ukulele so I can play on the beach Because it’s too much to carry a guitar up and down all those stairs. so many things…I know all I need is the beach and oysters but music makes me happy and maybe that’s the missing ingredient.

### Woodstock — 2025-05-07T10:41:23+0300

I was organizing and came across test results from last September that I had done. Not sure how accurate or meaningful they are but after learning more about the brain from the blogs, the results are scary and point to demyelination issues. The test is from Vibrant Wellness and was called a “Neural Zoomer”. I had some other tests done at the same time but it’s many many pages long to post. I hope there will be improvements in my brain spending more time in El Salvador, though I don’t know when or if I will do this test again as it was expensive. The test was done before finding Jack. I just wanted to try to identify what was going on in my body. But my practitioner didn’t know what to do with this information. I knew it was concerning enough to listen to Uncle Jack who understood what was going on with me without ever seeing any test results.

### Woodstock — 2025-05-07T16:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-352097>

Dear Uncle Jack, I am sorry I couldnt feel the music the way you do. I appreciated the post and I’m sorry you wanted to delete It.

I was trying to explain that I am deeply affected by some music in a way most people are not. This all began to make sense to me around a decade ago when I learned about 440hz tuning when playing with a band that went to great lengths to get out of that frequency by tuning a bit sharp to 444hz. The leader of the band is a piano player and it’s not easy to tune a piano to 444hz but even harder to tune down to 432, so they just go up a little. I had my best musical moments in my life with that band. He told me about the book 432 which I read and also researched whatever I could find trying to understand this.

I did listen to your song and I get the attraction to it and all that you said. I am just super sensitive and tend to be very protective of myself around music. Some music is actually painful to me physically and emotionally. If you aren’t one of the 5% that is affected by the harms of 440hz tuning then it might be hard to understand. If my husband has music on that causes a reaction I get really irritable. if the music is more subtle but is affecting me I might not realize it’s the music and just get really moody. I hardly listen to recorded music anymore because of this.

When I practice guitar and make music for myself I always tune up to 444hz. It is a happier place for me and more healing.

I don’t fully understand this because not all recorded music affects me In an undesirable way. But when I hear dark stuff like metal or the music that sounds to me like the whining of Nirvana and other bands of the 90s, I immediately put up a wall of protection. I did listen and to your song and he is crazy talented and the lyrics were spot on. I wanted to put it on my stereo and crank up the volume but I know better. I just can’t go there. It’s for my own sanity.

### Woodstock — 2025-05-08T16:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-352154>

I jokingly used the words “swami Jack” in my journal a while back and now I’m starting to think it’s not a joke. I was listening to the pow wow last night since you said to listen before reading the blog. I don’t ever remember writing in my journal that I had thyroid nodules. You used this as a hypothetical when speaking to me. I dont know if I still have nodules because I don’t see any centralized MDs but about 15-20 years ago I had an ultrasound showing 8 nodules on my thyroid. My thyroid labs have all been normal every time tested. They did a needle biopsy on my thyroid which totally sucked because the next day I could not stop crying. I called the doc office to ask what was up and they said it’s normal because the procedure can release a lot of hormones. Hmmmm….don’t you think that would have been nice to know in advance? I wonder how many people take a gun to their head after such a procedure. If I haven’t already put this little tidbit into my journal, here it is. I don’t know if it’s meaningful but it was pretty intense at the time. I have to wonder based on some of your stories (like the little girl with the methylene blue) if you have medical intuitive abilities. I think we all have intuitive abilities. But, your vast knowledge and understanding of all the science is something no one else in this world currently has. Plus medical intuition is a force not to be reckoned with.

just reflecting a bit on my test results from a month ago after returning from El Salvador. I just had a few things checked. Vitamin D was up but still pretty low and HS-CRP went from 1.8 down to 1.1 which may explain why I was in a lot less pain when I left El Salvador. Some of the pain has returned, which I assume is from inflammation. i am doing my best in Colorado but there are good days and bad days. Scalp and skin issues improved in El Salvador too. the transition from sea level back to 5200 ft was hard. i couldn’t get hydrated. As much as I don’t like humidity I’m ready for it again. I am drying out and shriveling up here. Probably because it’s Chernobyl and I’m being microwaved slowly. I suppose I will still be emitting ultra weak biophotons and get a ton of mosquito bites Now that it’s rainy season in ES. But I made my decision 5 weeks ago and I guess I am ready to embrace the suck. Se y’all soon!

But it’s interesting that inside my house is better than outside until you turn on a cell phone. Then you might as well be standing in front of a 5G antenna.

This is super helpful to know the extent of this Ann Marie, thank you! I am looking for a meter, did you upgrade to this from the safe and sound Classic III? Theres a big price hike to the Pro 2 from the classic III so just wanting to know if that one was no good?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-13T19:14:44+0300

> This is super helpful to know the extent of this Ann Marie, thank you! I am looking for a meter, did you upgrade to this from the safe and sound Classic III? Theres a big price hike to the Pro 2 from the classic III so just wanting to know if that one was no good?

The new Cornet Quad 5G is $200. Measures electric, magnetic, RF and light flicker. I compared it to the S&SPro2 and they are close plus it tells you the RF frequency which the S&S does not - I recommend a body voltage meter also if you can - [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Woodstock — 2025-05-14T05:10:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-352468>

The safe and sound classic 3 is great for driving around or stashing in your bag when you go places to check the RFs. ### Woodstock — 2025-05-17T04:45:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-352681>

I’m baaaaaacck! It feels so damn effing good to be back in El Salvador! The ”embrace the suck” message came at the perfect time as my flight got cancelled and I had to spend the night in Miami last night. My shoulder pain was so intense after no sleep, airports, and a Marriott Courtyard in Miami near airport last night. But the pain is mostly gone now and despite the ferocious waves at Palmarcito this afternoon, I walked past the guard with a smile up to the salt pools and have never felt such an intense feeling of being HOME. What is it about salt water? Electrons? Negative ions with the mist from the crashing waves? So healing! Then I went up to Pelicanos and had 6 oysters. I didn’t mind the humidity being back at all surprisingly. Colorado is so dry. I couldn’t get hydrated There!

The owner of my rental who I negotiated off of airbnb after being a good renter last time added some upgrades like better sheets and a very thorough clean plus provided towels and got rid of the old phoos ball and trampoline that were unusable junk. I am so grateful for all of this, and so grateful for the mito who held onto my guitar and other things I left here just down the road. I am grateful for google translate (even though google is evil) because it allowed me to chat with my driver who didn’t speak English. Carlos was going to pick me up yesterday but then I was delayed 24 hours And he got his friend to drive me. The EMFs at this house are pretty low. My checked bag is still in Miami but I embraced the suck. Actually I’m good tonight With what I carried on. It will just be that much sweeter when the rest of my stuff arrives.

my Birthday is coming up. mito party at my house on June 4! More details to come! My husband will be here then. I will be 56 and he just turned 65 a few weeks ago. So I think this is our yin Yang year 56/65.

my nervous system already feels calmer. the last 5 weeks in Chernobyl have been challenging. Also insightful. Boulder is so damn beautiful. I got a lot planted in my garden and enjoyed the fresh eggs from my chickens every day. I snuggled a lot with my pups and hubby. My 19 year old son said he would miss me. Right now the plan is to go back to Boulder on July 10 and maybe come back here again in early December through May. Maybe spend winters here. If that’s enough. I hope it will be. That is the best I can do right now. But I always leave my options open. And trust the path will be made clear as I go along.

Thank you for this Anthony, very helpful. Have you ever tested on a cruise ship? I am wondering how a 7 day voyage with over 2000 passengers constantly connected to ubiquitous wifi and Bluetooth and likely letting their phones remain continuously seeking cellular signal while crossing the Atlantic (instead of using airplane mode with WiFi enabled) would compare to 2 x 8hr flights with c.200 people in aircraft (that would specifically not be Wifi enabled aircraft)? ### Woodstock — 2025-05-19T22:43:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-352932>

I was reading in today’s blog and came across this:
“Metformin: Activates AMPK, enhancing OPC survival and differentiation. It also supports mitochondrial function, potentially stabilizing TCA cycle activity and UPEs.”

Maybe it’s not relevant right now because I’m in El Salvador but I am going back to Colorado from July through the end of November probably. I wonder if Metformin would be helpful for me at that time?

I have only been back to Atami 3 days and the tension in my neck and shoulders is significantly less. This has been chronic pain and inflammation for 7-8 years.

HS-CRP went down from 1.8 in Nov. to 1.1 in April after my return. I plan to get it checked again here on the beach when the blood work guy comes. Labs are cheaper here so it will be good to get a baseline from labs here and try to test here. I felt so bad in Colorado after my return. I will check Vitamin D for sure as well. And A1c which had been consistently going up by .1 every 3 months. Last A1c check was in Feb and it was up to 5.6. I forgot to order it in April.

Lithium seems risky from reading your blog. I did have a psychiatric nurse practitioner almost prescribe me Lithium after her other pharma garbage she pressured me into only made things worse. I was having some severe grief and depression after my mom’s passing a few years ago. I really did not want to be on anything daily. I just was in major fight or flight. I used propranolol on occasion when my heart would be racing and for stage fright. My regular NP prescribed Valium for occasional anxiety. That works great for me but I have never abused it because I’m sure it wouldn’t work anymore if I did. I get a lot of anxiety in social situations or going to a large stadium. 5mg of Valium makes me a much more relaxed person in those times. I almost feel normal. I don’t want to slow my healing progress down but it’s a balancing act. Sometimes it makes all the difference to have a drink. I was so depressed as a child and when I was 12 my dad gave me alcohol on a cruise ship. I guess he thought I needed to relax and lighten up. He suffered from depression and alcoholism after losing both his parent when he was 12. So I guess he though he was helping me. I realized then that I could lift up out of depression with alcohol. I don’t think I ever really experienced not being depressed except maybe at Disneyland when I was 4. Or when I was 6 and got a sister who was 8 and we played together like puppies. Until school started and I wasn’t cool anymore and she wanted to be with her school friends. Then back to chronic depression I went. Also I should mention that I’m referring to my Dad who adopted me. Not a biological relationship.

I was trying to get organized this morning and spent too much time biohacking my rental house and I started to get a little depressed. It’s a balancing act. The salt pools helped and eating my favorite fish dish on the beach is helping. I want to try and get my cold plunge set up because that energizes me. We will see if I can get enough ice from the tienda to make it cold.

### Woodstock — 2025-05-20T16:02:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-352998>

DOES TARGETING MAKE YOU PSYCHIC?

I’ve listened to the “targeted individuals” podcast multiple times and now listening again to the quantum healthy podcast where Jack discusses targeting. You mention Fibromyalgia as one of the diseases these targeted people get that docs don’t recognize. I had Fibromyalgia, POTS, chronic fatigue, PANDAS and brain fog as a child in the 70’s, long before all the intensity of nnEMFs we have today. I don’t know when I got Lyme but it could have been as early as age 4. The missing first month of my life after being born in a Catholic hospital, never held by my bio mom, and my adoptive parents not being told there was a baby girl waiting for them until I was 30 days old and picking me up from a foster mother named “Mrs. Gerber” (like the baby food)…WHAT WAS HAPPENING TO ME FOR 30 DAYS? I suppose I will never know. Even if I could get my hospital records would MK-Ultra even be indicated in those records?

I also wonder if the blue tornado that tried to suck my consciousness into it when I was 15 was part of this targeting? I felt that it was evil at that time and it only stopped because I exercised my will to not be taken. I lived fairly near Wright Patterson Air Force Base at the time.

Why did Steven Greer (who had worked with the Clintons and Rockefellers on disclosure and maybe DARPA too?) bother to come to my house the day he was fleeing from Boulder (in the late 90’s) to escape from the people he said had bugged his house and murdered his colleagues with turbo cancer? He said he was on his way to DC that day and told me that there was a free energy device there locked up by the government that was supposedly “alien” technology being kept from us to control the energy supply. Seems so strange that this person I had never met bothered to come to my house to meet me under his stated circumstances. Why me?

I get that my brain is compromised and likely has been from birth, probably due to my bio mom’s damaged mitochondria because her dad was a HAM radio operator and had this device in his house from before she was born probably damaging her mother’s mitochondria. Her mom died of ovarian cancer in her early 50s and her Dad died of heart issues in his 60’s. Was their early demise caused by the nnEMF from the HAM radio?

BUT, if my brain is so fucked up, why did I know that 9/11 was an inside job while the towers were burning? Why did I know genocide was coming the second I heard that a virus jumped from a bat to human at a wet market? Why did I know all along Obama was a snake and there was something big that was going to happen related to Osama Bin Laden but it would be a psyop, (even though I voted for him the first term because after the Bush family 9/11 evil I could not stomach another Republican in office.)

what Part of my brain is NOT being compromised by Warburg metabolism that I somehow know things I shouldn’t know and no one else around me knew? I also knew as a young child that the TV was dictating a narrative. What I didn’t understand was how ”they” could get away with lying to us with their narratives and struggled with the belief that “the TV wouldn’t lie”! Until Reagan was shot and I realized the repeated coverage of the same info over and over was all to mess with our emotions and not because the media actually cared. I was 11 years old.

Does targeting make you psychic?

here are the podcasts on targeting:

### [E36 Understanding Turbo Cancer through the Lens of Jack Kruse - A Clarion Call for all Healers](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/quantum-healthy/id1749877805?i=1000704702828)

Podcast Episode · Quantum Healthy · April 24, 2025 · 1h 47m

 podcasts.apple.com

### Bubbie — 2025-05-21T06:18:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-353049>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=345019)
>
> I am 55 yrs old and have had gut issues, fatigue, POTS symptoms and chronic aches and pains since early childhood. About 5 years ago I tested positive for Lyme which I likely have had since around age 4, based on symptoms and history.
>
> I felt like an 80 year old when I was in my late 20s. (So I am actually doing much better now.) I got the diagnosis of Fibromyalgia from a Chiropractor who took the time to take a full medical history and check pressure points. When I went to the Arthritis Foundation website and read their description of the disease I cried for hours. Mostly of relief that I was not psycho because I had been told previously that this was psychosomatic and “no pain no gain” throughout my childhood while I pushed myself athletically into directions that likely only exacerbated my symptoms while trying to keep up with my non-biological siblings. I felt like a weakling and a failure, while simultaneously knowing I was actually smarter than most people. I just had a delayed reaction in responding and spent a lot of time in my head figuring stuff out. Nowadays this behavior would be diagnosed as on the spectrum, but I was never recognized as having any issues of the sort. It makes sense to me now. I likely had heavy metal poisoning from living in an area that had been destroyed by a tornado when I was 5-6 yrs old. I had some OCD symptoms at that age that improved after I moved.
>
> My current health issues are:
> Lyme and co-infections
> mold exposure/mycotoxins
> high fasting insulin/borderline pre-diabetic
> MCAS
> candida since age 10
> EBV
> Strep
> Staph
> SIBO/gut issues/gluten issues
> chronic pain and stiffness in joints, neck and shoulders
> liver and gallbladder issues
> possible kidney issues
> zero libido
> menopause-on Bioidentical hormones
> gained 30 pounds since menopause and very difficult to lose
>
> since September I have been doing sunrise walks and getting sun on my body as much as possible
>
> I have been working on lifestyle changes to eat more regularly and eat dinner earlier and reduce blue light exposure
>
> about 6 years ago I had a building biologist evaluate and help figure out how to do all the right things. My house was Ethernet wired and we havent used a Wifi router during this time. HOWEVER, when Century Link brought in fiber optic cable, they gave us a new modem which had 5G turned on and the only way to turn it off was to sign in on their website and do it online. We didn’t realize this and it appeared to be off externally. Two days ago I found out that the modem had been putting off a 5G signal for YEARS unbeknownst to me. I have an RF meter from SLT but it only read high right at the source of the modem and was showing ok levels throughout the main areas of the house I use frequently so I just never realized this was happening. But I hated being in that room. I had a desktop computer in there and never wanted to use it. I moved the computer to another room recently but I never wanted to walk through the 5G area to the other part of the house. When I brought the building biologist back and had him go through the house with me, he quickly figured out what was going on and showed us how to turn off the 5G from the Century Link website with the password on the bottom of the modem. I would normally be really pissed off about being radiated for all these years, but that would just be a waste of my energy. It’s finally off now. The worst part of this is that we had a few water leaks and therefore mold issues which are supposedly made 600x worse with Wifi. We remediated the mold years ago but did not throw away everything in our home. We are in a dry climate so we have an evaporative cooler and I always feel worse midsummer when the humidity level gets really high from the cooler being on 24/7 for weeks in a row. The high humidity causes mold spores to reactivate. Hopefully this will improve now!
>
> I am coming to El Salvador for a couple of months this winter. I hope this will be a good jumpstart on getting my health on track. I plan to spend a lot of time in the tropics and on the ocean for at least the next three years or longer. I was already getting the intuition that I needed to do this before stumbling upon Dr Kruse on Twitter back in September.
>
> I will add more details and labs at some point. I just wanted to get this journal started. It has only been two days that my house has been free of 5G, but I am so relieved to know that there was actually something so easy I could do that theoretically should make a huge difference. (We had already done all the hard work but were not reaping the benefit!) Just another confirmation for me to listen to my gut feelings and intuitions and the more subtle things that I feel because there was a massive reason I have been avoiding half of my house for YEARS! I would normally beat myself up for not getting help on this sooner and figuring it out, but I’ve spent 55 years beating myself up and believing my pain was all my own fault and I am so DONE with that self sabotage type of behavior. So much crazy stuff going on in the world but I am going to have my best year ever in 2025. I might even learn to surf. Apparently the beach I will be near is good surf for beginners in Feb/March. I’ve also always felt physically better with a tan. The pieces are coming together.
>
> Thanks for reading.

I just wanted to encourage you to try surfing. I have 11 years on you and did my very first attempt at surfing last month in Portugal. My son was there in August and he and 11 buddies all got up on surf boards. When I wondered if I could do it, he thought I for sure could. So rather than a single afternoon lesson, I signed up for a complete week at a surf camp. Aldo’s surf camp in Arrifana Portugal. I had a blast!

Also I wanted to say hello because I’m in Castle Rock CO, not that far from you

Weird but super interesting if that is what happened. I was discussing with other members and someone said that that’s why so many military men have more daughters, because of the EMFs. Makes sense. And out of the 4 daughters my grandfather produced, they only had two children…me and my cousin Jacob who shot himself in the head after going off his SSRIs a week or two later. His mother, my bio-aunt had been diagnosed schizophrenic and had been on meds most of her adult life I think. She was the youngest of the 4 daughters. Poor Jacob was very depressed. I feel lucky to have been given up for adoption, despite the severe trauma it caused. I ended up with very amazing and loving parents and step-parents. And even my bio mom is very caring and looked for me and found me in my 30s. Still, I have been a bit of a train wreck and a “dependent” as I don’t think I would survive long if I had to work a 9-5 job or worse multiple jobs to pay my bills. Sadly I didn’t buy bitcoin 10 years ago when a friend described it to me. That would have made things easier. But then again maybe not because maybe I would have been able to afford centralized medicine treatments. Instead I ran half-marathons, did yoga, and tried practically every diet and cleanse that exists. It didn’t take away the pain. Not for long anyway. Or the anxiety. Reflections on listening back to the Q and A recording:

We have been seeing a lot of butterflies in Atami as well.

Ive been aware of the magnetic reversal and possible CME for a long time now so I do pay attention to these things.

Lots of really cool questions from people and great answers and conversations from Uncle Jack.

I‘m learning a lot listening to this format vs. reading blogs. I am not able to follow the super long blogs and get through that much info because the science is beyond my background and comprehension, but I will go back and try to read them again after listening to this.

I looked up the Danish Winter of 1944 but I haven’t exactly found the piece of it why you are bringing this up. Famine affects a fetus And the effects are different at different stages of the pregnancy. Lots of diseases like diabetes etc. Schizophrenia, etc. But from what I saw, more boys were born than girls during this time so it seems to be opposite from what you told me about repopulation and why my grandfather had 4 girls. Why weren‘t more girls born than boys during the famine? Maybe I just found incorrect information but from what I saw, the birth rate of males was higher than girls during that time. Scratching my head on that. Or maybe there was something else you wanted me to understand about the Danish famine And I missed it?

I am happy to look things up but just not quite connecting the dots here.

### Woodstock — 2025-06-14T06:10:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-354619>

Im not sure if I have put this in my journal yet but I thought of it today. I had preeclampsia when I was pregnant. I developed it sometime in my second trimester. I had flown to Hawaii in about my 5th month of pregnancy and immediately looked more bloated and retaining more water so I wonder if it was the flight. I didnt think it would be an issue in my 5th month but looking back on it, I have to wonder if the radiation from the plane could have triggered the preeclmapsia or if I would have gotten it anyway. I didn’t really notice the symptoms until I returned from the 3 week Hawaii vacation. I only went because I already booked the entire trip the day before I found out I was pregnant. I diagnosed myself with the condition because I knew something was wrong. I had low blood pressure my entire life and I could feel that something had changed and I felt horrible. My doctor didn’t believe me but tested my urine anyway and it had protein.

Is preeclampsia due to nnEMF or just from my lifelong chronic illness I was more likely to develop this condition? Maybe it doesn’t matter but understanding mysel helps me understand the science better.

### Woodstock — 2025-06-20T21:03:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-354922>

This was a really informative and fairly easy for me to follow podcast. I wonder if some of this relates to me. I have some “autistic” traits and have had Dysbiosis since birth. Would a vagal never stimulator help? Obviously sun and grounding and DHA which I’m working on but I have to find a way to afford living in the tropics full time and will be going back to Chernobyl so I might need a few hacks. I plan to drink DDW and do a lot of cold plunging. Further hack my sleeping environment. I have a garden waiting for me to tend to so I will be outside a lot very scantily clad. Somehow I need to find a private place to get my hoo-ha in the sun.

Vagal nerve stimulator? Might this help? Is there a brand?

Here were some notes I took during the podcast:

Vagal nerve stimulator

Autism-carbohydrate craving? That’s me.

De-mylinate the vagus nerve? Esophageal sphincter? EoE??? I have symptoms of EoE.

Dysbiosis-all my life

Microbiome…

De-mylination of the vagal nerve is an issue..could this cause EoE? Dysbiosis…carb cravings…

Would a vagal nerve stimulator help?

Histamine (MCAS) and EoE symptoms even in El Salvador.

I did initially see improvement in making yogurt with l.Reuteri and other specific probiotics according to Dr William Davis’s recipe. (Prior to Ek Salvador) But once repopulated, the benefits were no longer felt.

So what you said in the podcast made sense as it’s all about the myelination and putting more probiotics in my gut may not do much overall. I did feel an initial benefit though.

I would like to discuss this in the next Q and A unless you think this is more of a consult question. I am going to do a consult with you eventually but I know you are insanely busy right now and there are a lot of people much sicker than me that need your time. I am doing what I can to understand the blogs and podcasts are much easier for me to follow. This one really hit home. I don’t have hypothyroidism or hashimotos. And I dodged a bullet by not letting that psych nurse prescribe me Lithium. I have been against the GLP-1 drugs since hearing of them because I heard they cause slow motility and I already have that issue. Is there something else that helps re-myelinate the brain? Maybe I just need more time in El Salvador. Maybe I need to not worry so much about my friends on the GLP-1 drugs? I am scared for their lives and health but maybe it will help them after all….

Doing my best to be a savage. I want to live to my full potential. I have so much life I want to live yet. I have barely seen much of the world. I want to sail in Fiji next summer. It’s something to fantasize about and keep me motivated to do all the mito things. I miss my bestie who lives in Hong Kong who says she will meet me in Thailand and be my tour guide. It’s been years and she can’t get a travel visa to the US and I’m afraid if I entered China they might not let me out. Lol. I’ve never been further than Hawaii. Is it crazy to think I could travel there with the time difference? Is one year enough time for me to turn this around? I have my rental in Atami from Dec-May and the plan is to stay put here for 6 months, no traveling. In between Aug-Nov I am getting my house ready for my son to live there with his two friends and have to remodel a bathroom and purge 29 years of accumulated stuff no longer needed to make room for this. If he lives at home then and rents rooms to his friends this will help make this all financially more feasible. This is my business plan. Beyond that I have not been energetic enough to figure out how to earn a living. But I am always brainstorming this.

### [Sex ratios at birth vary with environmental harshness but not maternal condition - Scientific Reports](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-019-45316-7)

The sex ratio at birth (SRB) may be patterned by maternal condition and/or environmental stressors. However, despite decades of research, empirical results from across the social and biological sciences are equivocal on this topic. Using longitudinal individual-level data from a US population...

 www.nature.com

What are the odds that you go to a far away country and become friends with a person from that country you later learn was named after the tornado that nearly took you out at age 4? The town was only 50,000 people and one of the largest most deadly F5 tornadoes in all of history that killed…yeah you guessed it…33 people. WTF is this crazy place? Is this quantum entanglement? Are we in a matrix?

My takeaway is that I am on the right path because before I even realized the connection, we were talking about geoengineering looking up at the trails in the sky, and since there were 148 tornadoes that touched down that day and the budget for geoengineering had incresed from 3 million to 17 million that year of 1974, I fully see this as the universe/matrix/whatever telling me that those 148 tornadoes were probably manmade.

So, what does this have to do with my health and healing? Everything! No doubt this early trauma as well as the toxins in the water and soil could have affected my health. And if they were geoengineering the skies back then, it’s been a constant source of toxins I have been exposed to. I lived near Wright Patterson Air Force Base and DARPA offices.

also the government already admitted that they geoengineered the rains in Vietnam from 1969-1972. So there’s that…

### Woodstock — 2025-07-04T03:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-355702>

Being in El Salvador has healed me enough to be able to play music again. I have been able to remember or quickly re-learn songs I used to love to play but couldn’t do very much for the last 8 years due to neck and shoulder pain. Also my creativity was flattened. Harder to stay focused. Harder to find my true voice. It hasn’t come back easily. I’ve had to work for it. Part of this has been finding a community that appreciates me here with the mitos in El Salvador and Atami. And also some locals who live nearby. When I was too sick to work 30 years ago, and very depressed, I pulled myself out of depression by building community and hosting live music at my home for many years. It’s not a paid gig but it definitely is a lot of work and takes a lot of heart and soul. When I’m not doing that I don’t feel complete. And the normies in Boulder just don’t appreciate me or see the real me or care anymore. I didn’t know if I would ever find it again.

Tomorrow everyone is coming over to celebrate the freedom we feel living the mito lifestyle. And I’ve got a song I’ve been working on inspired by and dedicated to Dr. Jack Kruse. It’s my take on an old standard. Wish you could be here to hear it Uncle Jack

### Woodstock — 2025-07-15T23:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-356380>

Great Pow Wow! The last live question with the woman from Cape Cod seemed very relevant to me. I also have MCAS, chemical sensitivity, and Lyme. I may also have EoE unless those symptoms are part of MCAS. I have had health issues since birth so the cause of my problem is not the same as the caller’s (benzos) but I do think the non linear optics information may also be key to healing my health issues?

also I have always had issues with flying. And due to many yoga injuries over the years, I have suspected possible EDS, which I see is a collagen vascular disease. So I need to do some research on this as well.

I have spent 4 out of the last 6 months in El Salvador. I did find that things I normally react to I could tolerate better in El Salvador.

I got home to Colorado 5 days ago and I have been drinking DDW. It seems to be helpful. Some people on the forum said that I shouldn’t drink it straight if it’s 5ppm so I have been diluting it by about 2/3 filtered water. I wonder if I actually need to dilute after hearing your advice to the woman from Cape Cod? Though my current symptoms are probably not as serious as hers, I wonder how much of the DDW I should drink daily?

Though I mostly felt better overall in El Salvador, I did not sleep well there. I am concerned about possible grounding issues with my rental house. Also I would have pain in my feet and legs when I first got up. Dehydration? Maybe the increase in sweating caused more water loss? I also had leg cramps that stopped after I added salt to my water. But I still had pain when I first got up or if I got up in the middle of the night. Maybe circulation problems? Hypercoagulation?

I plan to come back to El Salvador from Dec through May, a full six months. I have a lot of things to take care of here in the meantime. Two months is challenging for me to be away from home. Even though I did what I could to make friends and create community, there is a lot of stress with being in El Salvador for me (Loneliness, depression, anxiety). I have to weigh the stress of being there against the toxins and EMFs here in Colorado. Maybe like the caller from Cape Cod, I can research the blogs on non linear optics and understand more how to help myself.

Regarding my question about if GLP-1 drugs would help demylenation, I meant to ask specifically if this would help with scleroderma, because I know someone on the GLP-1 who has scleroderma and I had previously been very worried about her with all the risks of the GLP-1 drug. But maybe her condition is serious enough that it will help her? Also she has had very severe and chronic kidney stones for decades and I wonder if this is somehow related to the scleroderma? She lives in Florida in a rural area. She used to live in Colorado and felt much better when moving to Florida and spending time in nature kayaking on the rivers there. I asked about this just because I am trying to understand autoimmune illnesses as I seem to have several myself. I am not interested in GLP-1 for myself and even if I was, I don’t have health insurance to pay the hefty price of it. I believe the sunlight and grounding protocol will work for me. But I don’t know if my friend has such a severe illness that maybe GLP-1 is the right choice. Long before your blogs on it, I expressed concerns about GLP-1 and she didn’t want to hear it. She believed it was right for her. Maybe she was right after all. She is a professional psychic/intuitive.

Great Q and A powwow. I want to write down what stood out to me about my question so I don’t forget.

1.drink as much salt in your water that you can handle. I probably wasn’t doing it aggressively enough.

2\. Be in water as much as possible. I can use my cold plunge even if I don’t have ice in it to just be in water more. Fill it up in the morning and dip in throughout the day. There is a hot spring about an hour from here with natural vapor caves. Very humid in there. Could be helpful. Take steam showers more. Epsom salt baths or maybe find some Dead Sea salt. I don’t have a hot tub at the moment but I can do that in my bathtub with candlelight.

3\. Get in the water and then get in the sun. Repeat.

4\. Get back to the beach.

5\. Do things to support kidneys (with all the salt intake.) Watch out for swollen ankles.

>
> The UV is stronger in Boulder than in El Salvador. My tan has gotten darker since I’ve been home. Bring on the melanin! This likely only applies in the mid summer months. Good time to be home.

El Salvador’s UV is more consistent year-round, while Boulder’s peaks of UV are confined to summer. Boulder has exceptionally more nnEMF than El Salvador and this degrades any altitude effect Boulder gets in the summer.

### Woodstock — 2025-08-04T19:57:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-357535>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=357534)
>
> El Salvador’s UV is more consistent year-round, while Boulder’s peaks of UV are confined to summer. Boulder has exceptionally more nnEMF than El Salvador and this degrades any altitude effect Boulder gets in the summer.

Yep! Totally agree! I am just glad that the UV is so strong here in the summer because I actually got darker when I got home. And thankfully my backyard is mostly in the green on the Safe and Sound pro 2. I have lived here for 29 years. Hubby does not want to sell. My best plan is to spend 6 months in the tropics and the other 6 here. My tropical home base will be the same place I have been in Atami because being in community helps my mental health. I have been working on my childhood abandonment issues my entire life and though I like some alone time, I like to also have close friends and family to enjoy the beauty of the Earth and all its wonders with. I want to take some excursions from ES with hubby and meet him places during that 6 months. I would love suggestions from anyone who knows of tropical places with lower nnEMF that are easy to fly to from San Salvador direct. Chronic lifelong illness has kept me from seeing the world. I am 56 and would like to see more of our beautiful Earth while I’m still young enough to fully enjoy it! With my Vitamin D up so much, I feel better. I know I have a long way to go still, and I have an idea for a business or non-profit that has been stewing up for 30 years! It’s a concept my hubby came up with and just never materialized. And it works right in with my mito lifestyle plan!

### Woodstock — 2025-08-12T16:45:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-358038>

Decentralized medicine #66! I will do a much deeper dive into this one. But it reminded me of how I dodged the LDN bullet. In 2021 I went to a doc who had changed her specialty to Lyme. She had a medical degree from Harvard and much later realized she contracted Lyme while in med school. But she was way too centralized. She prescribed me low dose Naltrexone. I never took it. My histamine issues made me so sensitive to everything (MCAS) and I was on way too many supplements already. She had me go off everything and then bring things in slowly. But a lot of the things she wanted me to take I just had a bad feeling about. One of them was LDN. I was doing so much gardening at the time and getting so much sun but only in the summer. I grew 100 tomato plants that summer and she told me not to eat nightshades! It was all too depressing. I mentioned using nicotine patches for help with the spike protein and also that it seemed to help my ADD issues and she said “adderal” would be better. I was like “isn’t that just like legalized meth” and she got really angry I asked that question. Just goes to show you that Harvard doesn’t actually train the best and the brightest.

Anne Marie, while I do understand your longing to have adventure, I will tell you the same thing I another mito in El Salvador who keeps flying around instead of staying put - you need to stay put when you get to El Salvador. Stay put for the first 6 mos cycle. Dont go flying off. Every time you fly, you degrade. You need to get well before you adventure. You must believe first that getting well will happen - you must do the things to get well - stay put. Jack has said about the lady on the Q&A that has breast cancer he would sit her butt down on the beach with DDW and tell her to not speak until dinner. I think he would want you to stay put. Let your husband come visit you. Have the community. But do it in El Salvador. Even go for a car adventure once a month for a day trip. But otherwise I think you should stick to the protocol. I may be wrong. Please know that I say all of this with the utmost feelings of fondness for you. And btw I was in El Salvador in Dec for a month and then again in June for 6 weeks and you definitely brought the community together when I was there in June - so just remember that part of your journey is the journey you are providing to others : ) Just take this to heart and think about it - Much love to you...

Interconnections involve quantum mechanisms like electron transfer. Sunscreen and sunglasses block all of UV light = RETARDS DO this. ### Amy Fournier — 2025-08-14T03:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-358190>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=357497)
>
> Great Q and A powwow. I want to write down what stood out to me about my question so I don’t forget.
>
> 1.drink as much salt in your water that you can handle. I probably wasn’t doing it aggressively enough.
>
> 2\. Be in water as much as possible. I can use my cold plunge even if I don’t have ice in it to just be in water more. Fill it up in the morning and dip in throughout the day. There is a hot spring about an hour from here with natural vapor caves. Very humid in there. Could be helpful. Take steam showers more. Epsom salt baths or maybe find some Dead Sea salt. I don’t have a hot tub at the moment but I can do that in my bathtub with candlelight.
>
> 3\. Get in the water and then get in the sun. Repeat.
>
> 4\. Get back to the beach.
>
> 5\. Do things to support kidneys (with all the salt intake.) Watch out for swollen ankles.

Love this summary Anne Marie - thanks for sharing it - and I loved hearing your story of your adventure to El Sal - very courageous!! Would love go connect with you if you'd like! Amy

### Woodstock — 2025-09-02T19:09:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-359051>

> [pfedorow said:](/goto/post?id=358912)
>
> Where are you checking this? I see boulder gets UV 12 for only like a week while el Salvador gets it constant

Possibly this is not correct but this is the UV index I found for Boulder showing 12 for July and August. Since I got a deeper tan when I came back in July, I didn’t question it. [https://nomadseason.com/uv-index/united-states/colorado/boulder.html](https://nomadseason.com/uv-index/united-states/colorado/boulder.html)

Denver only shows 11 uv index for July and August. The elevation isn’t that different than Boulder. I’m not sure why Boulder is higher uv. When it’s cloudy, not so much. But we have partly cloudy a lot of days. So if you go outside and get your sun when the clouds part, you can maximize your exposure. I was feeling really crappy when it was cloudy for a week! Also, I need to be around water. It’s so dry here. Not ideal conditions. Tropics are much better regardless of the high UV index here. I need the ocean!

### Woodstock — 2025-09-02T19:31:05+0300

Good question, Albert! I only tested vitamin D after my first two months in El Salvador back in May. I haven’t tested since I’ve been home. The UV index has dropped here but it’s still supposed to be 10 in Boulder. I plan to test it again but just haven’t gotten around to it. I will let you know here if it has gone up at all. My norm is that I usually get sick sometime in October. And that makes sense since the UV index drops from 10 in September to 7 in October. I am hoping to avoid illness this year. I’ve never had Vitamin D over 70 until this year so hopefully I make it through the season in good health. I know tanning beds aren’t ideal but I will use one if I am unable to get to the beach soon.

### Woodstock — 2025-09-04T00:13:08+0300

All I know is that when the sun is out, it’s HOT and I do get tan. Also this time of year it really depends on clouds. I have not seen any chem trails this week but last week was super cloudy and lots of chem trails too. I will do morning sunrise regardless of clouds and try to be outside as much as I can every day, but I have to take advantage of when it’s not cloudy and work on tanning then. Next month I will need to use a tanning bed or go somewhere higher in UV.

### Woodstock — 2025-09-23T19:12:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-359907>

I am happy to say that overall my health is improved from a year ago. I have had better sleep and my energy is better. Recovery is better. I wonder if this is because my vitamin D went up from 40 to 70 in the first 7 weeks I spent in El Salvador. I haven’t had labs redone since April. Too many other expenses right now. I attribute the health improvement to higher Vitamin D.

im working on my plan to be able to spend more months of the year outside of Colorado. I have hired a friend who does organizing so I can downsize a lot of accumulated and unneeded items from my home of 29 years and reorganize so my son can have more of his own space here when he moves back after the end of the school year And maybe with a friend or two of his moving in. This will help us out financially as well as having people in the home to care for the dogs, chickens and property.

Also, I need to keep brainstorming on how I can personally make an income after 30 years of “retirement”. Somehow I will have to fund living in a tropical environment away from the home that is literally my life’s work and is my citadel. The house isn’t in my name. I can’t sell it. I have no income or assets. And I have been retired from law practice for 30 years. I’m not that business savvy though I’ve thought about starting many businesses over the decades. I just never have the energy or mindset to develop my ideas. My ideas are kind of a joke Most of the time. Just hoping one of them will be crazy enough to actually work someday. Maybe as my brain improves I will figure this out. For now, my business plan is to shift my lifestyle entirely starting in May when my son moves back home. And maybe get a bit of rental income if his friends move into the extra bedrooms.

I have some other ideas. But it takes money to start a business. So that’s a challenge. I wish I would have bought a tiny bit of bitcoin way back in the beginning. But then I would be a billionaire and probably would just be bored with all that money and never have even found Uncle Jack and this community. I always knew there were others suffering like me and that we were being screwed by big pharma but I didn’t fully understand it or know how to help. And until I fully heal, no one I try to tell is going to believe me anyway. So yeah, I kind of have to heal myself first If I want to help others. I am doing my best to get away from Colorado but it’s my home and I dont have the funds or power to change that. A lot of people are in this boat. And as ideal as moving would be for my health, I have no desire to get a divorce and abandon my family. I need to find a way to make this work.

This doesn’t mean I don’t believe you about Colorado, Uncle Jack. But I wasn’t doing that great in El Salvador. I wasn’t sleeping. And being away from my family was difficult for my mental health. It has been amazing to be back at home. I am going back to El Salvador for at least a month this winter. Hopefully I will sleep better this time, and I would love to visit some other tropical places too. I would love to find a place that checks all my boxes. Or maybe several tropical places and I can spend the winters away from here And be back in summers for the higher UV index. I seem to be doing a lot better overall. Not perfect. But the vitamin D boost helped A lot. When I can afford it I will run more tests but right now I would rather spend the money to just go back to the beach.

I would love to know what more I can do while in Colorado to improve my health. Especially as October approaches. I want to avoid getting sick For the first October in 5 years. If anyone has any ideas I welcome them!
