### Woodstock — 2025-09-24T03:11:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-359958>

N=1

just because 7 of your patients died from Colorado doesn’t mean I will be #8.

Did you even read my post? Sure doesn’t seem like it.

4 months in El Salvador so far in 2025. I didn’t sleep well there. But I did many things you suggested and my vitamin D went up 30 points. I don’t take Vitamin D supplements. Sleep is so critical And I didn’t have one good night of sleep in 4 months In ES. I am sleeping much better at home. Maybe it’s because I have mitigated my house well, I have a comfortable bed, a Loving and supportive family, two dogs who missed me terribly, and access to clean water (showering and doing dishes in the nasty Atami water was probably not agreeing with me).

I need to find a way to earn an income. So if you happen to know of a job opening, maybe you can point me in the right direction? i Have drained all excess savings we had and now it’s paycheck to paycheck, of which I CANNOT CONTRIBUTE. My very patient and loving husband has taken care of me for 29 years. Do I now ask him to drain his small retirement fund so I can go to the beach?

possibly you could offer some more reasonable advice that isn’t implying that I need to sell my house/get divorced.

I laid out my plan. By next winter I hope to be able to travel to the tropics 6 months of the year. But I have a lot of downsizing to do here first.

My home and property is my life’s work. You have your medical career. I have my home, family and community. I have put massive effort into this for 29 years. My husband is 65 and he can’t retire because we don’t have the financial ability to do that. Maybe he will decide to sell the house down the road but that isn’t happening any time soon. Nothing I can do.

in the meantime, I plan to find a way to keep my vitamin D up. I might even move out on my own but it will have to be Florida or Hawaii where I am in the US and can actually get a job. I spent the whole day looking at job openings that I could potentially do that would be outdoors. Pool attendant is about the best I can come up with.

If you want to actually help, maybe you could say something HELPFUL. Because I am much more likely to die of suicide if I destroy my marriage and family.

also you don’t seem to get it through your skull that I have said I actually am FEELING BETTER. I was camping at 9,000 ft over the weekend and did not have altitude sickness. My recovery is way better. This is probably the best I have felt in decades.

what I have access to here for winter months is salt baths, cold plunges, an infrared sauna, an EMR-Tek red light, I could go to the tanning bed place to get full body UVB, Whole Foods down the street has oysters, and continue my Morning sunrise routine. When we can afford it, I will go somewhere to a beach for as long as I can.

if you have anything helpful to add, that would be great. Or maybe you know of a great job for a girl with a broken brain where I can make enough cash in the next month to buy a house in the tropics amd pay for my husband to come visit me once a month. Cause right now, that just isn’t possible.

im not one of those idiot multi-jabbed normies on your cruise. I left my family and home to spend 4 months in El Salvador this year. I am a savage. Much more than you know or are willing to admit. I was going to come back to ES in December for 6 months but the guy I was renting the house from turned out to be a lying scammer and I’m still trying to get my money back on that one. I will be back at Atami in February with India. I just need to do the best I can here until then.

I guess I was given too much Tylenol as a kid…because I don’t get it. I have done everything you asked that I possibly can. Other than file for divorce. But where would I even get the money to pay for a lawyer? I am a dependent. And even if a hotel would hire a 56 year old sick lady as a pool attendant for $20/hr, I would most likely die of loneliness or worse If I destroyed my family to do that.

“But Uncle Jack said I would die if I don’t leave Colorado!” What you are forgetting is that I am 100x healthier now than I was in my 20’s when I moved to Colorado as a stressed out lawyer. I had severe Fibromyalgia and POTS, severe hormonal swings with every cycle, was in constant pain every day. I had very long and extreme recovery from intense exercise. I had severe chronic fatigue. Brain fog was indescribable. I had one year of remission my entire life from age 29-30 because of acupuncture and yoga and then my dad passed away and my health had a major relapse. I saw a glimpse of what healthy can look like. And it had nothing to do with being in a low latitude. I got into that remission right here in my current Boulder home.

so what is my N=1?
it sure as shit is not the same as your 7 patients who died. Or maybe it is. Maybe you could tell me more details about their N=1? Because right now I think you’re just not being very nice or helpful.

### AlbertD — 2025-09-24T03:25:34+0300

If you continue planning to travel, one thing if you're comfortable putting your face on the internet, is starting a travel vlog channel and posting the videos online. Vlogging seems like an incredibly competitive space, but I don't see many videos about living in ES. It certainly isn't easy and requires consistent posts with no clear income source at the beginning and even an extended period of time.

Good luck! I am also trying to figure out what to do as well.

It was fun for a few days but after a couple of weeks my brain was literally fried. I hurt all over my body, my nervous system was a wreck, I had viral songs in my head! If I try again it will be YouTube. Actually that is my intention but I need the funds to travel to the places first To even get the content. I really didn’t get much content last trip. It took all my energy to just do the basic Mito lifestyle. I don’t like selfies or hearing my voice recorded. But I Still think I could do it possibly. I appreciate the suggestion. Every time I try to make content I fail miserably. And the tik tok thing was just crazy. But now I understand why young people have such short attention spans and chronic stress. It was a learning experience. maybe I can just make the videos and hire someone to post it for me. The less time spent online for me the better.

### Woodstock — 2025-09-24T15:58:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-359974>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=359969)

I'm yet to visit ES, however based on discussions with others and my own research of underdeveloped areas, typically any electrical wiring best practice or minimum standard goes out the door... Not to mention the likely unbalanced and overwhelmed grid..

I did suspect it might have been the electrical wiring that could have been at issue. I had my own meters and nnEMF was decent but there wasn’t much I could do about ungrounded wiring etc. So I wrote it off as that. This might sound woo woo but I actually think it was the energy in the house. I was being energetically attacked in my sleep. I thought I was renting it from the owner but it turned out to be a property manager who was trying to make a commission when I inquired about a longer term rent. I was not actually wanted in the home by the owner. I had to burn sage every day to keep the negative energy away. I didn’t even know all this about my “landlord” until I came back to Colorado. I could not shake the bad feeling I was having about my planned return there and did some digging to find out I was not actually working with the owner, and the new manager of the property was listing the house on Airbnb even though I had already pre-paid for December and was going to rent for 6 months. Still working on getting a refund. There were a lot of good things about the house so I had just dismissed the other bad vibes I was getting as depression from being separated from my family. I need to trust my intuition when I feel these things. But it was really hard for me to be away and I was “embracing the suck”. I am really good at that.

Thank you for your feedback Peter. Luckily I didn’t pay cash and I have disputed the credit card charge. If he lies to the bank I will report him to the police but I don’t think it’s necessary at this point.

Yes I did drink alcohol in ES and yet my redox improved anyway. I think having a drink or two is much better for my body than taking anxiety meds. When I’m at home in CO, I rarely drink and it’s usually white wine watered down with Gerolsteiner. My liver doesn’t like alcohol. I had pain in my right side as a kid prior to ever having my first drink so I have to be extra careful. In the El Salvador environment, I can get away with a drink or two for some reason. Better redox there I guess. And helps me from getting too depressed when I am separated from my loved ones. It’s a balance.

Also you just made me think about energetic attack I described, which happened my first night back in May and I had not taken a sip of alcohol for a week prior. I think sometimes alcohol can weaken your protective field. But also I was exhausted from travel after my plane got rerouted and it took an extra 24 hours to arrive. I think I went right to the ocean to reset, but it wasn’t enough to neutralize the positive charge.

I was not so excited about the rocky beach and so I spent a lot of time in the salt pools. I spent time in my yard most days so I could tan ALL my parts!!! There were lots of times I was at the beach when you were not so I think we just missed each other sometimes. And I totally agree with you. But for me, I need to be in the salt water and that was hard to do in the actual ocean the last two months I was in ES without getting injured because of the rocks and waves. I would go all the way around through Leona to go into the salt pools most days. I wish they would let everyone use them. Especially when the beach is dangerous.

### Woodstock — 2025-11-24T17:22:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-363089>

No more adding salt to my water. I don’t think my kidneys like it. My eGFR went from the consistent 75-90 over the last two years down to 66 since I started salting my water. Also there is a trace of protein in my urine. Not good. Getting very close to stage 3 kidney disease here. I watched my dad suffer for two years with no kidneys on dialysis. He only stayed alive that long because he didn’t want to leave my mom alone due to her state of mental health. And he went through absolute hell for her.

Yeah I know my environment sucks. But there has got to be something more I can do to mitigate this until I can find a way to afford to be elsewhere for at least half the year.

### Woodstock — 2025-12-29T04:38:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-364313>

WHAT DOES HALF-ASS REALLY MEAN?

Spending 6 months out of the last 12, literally thousands of miles away from my husband, son, 2 dogs and 8 chickens.

Giving up my beehive and tabling beekeeping indefinitely because you need be present to tend to them, or they will die.

Spending hundreds of hours researching locations, airbnbs, and even potential properties to buy if I can find a way to financially support a move to the tropics. I have sometimes done this so much that I become OCD about and it becomes terrible for my health. But I do this because Uncle Jack says I’m gonna die if I stay in Colorado and so I am trying to find a way to make this work financially for more than 5-6 months out of the year which at this point is as much as I am able to do.

My husband works hard in his job and on our home property to maintain it. He has said he never wants to sell it. In the last year I have at least managed to get him on board with joining me in multiple tropical places throughout the year to spend quality time together, hoping that if we travel enough, we will find a place that we both love and are excited about spending half or more of the year at.

I purchased a daylight computer for him so he can work on the beach if necessary. I have spent countless hours discussing with him how he could gradually shift to working remotely.

My N=1 is that I need my family. Until you understand that Uncle Jack, I’m not sure what we have to talk about.

I’m not doing this half-assed. I’m doing what I have to do to live longer. I will quote you in saying in August that people who are in a good relationship LIVE LONGER.

You are way off base with me. But I will chalk it up to me being autistic and a very poor communicator and therefore I will let you off the hook for being totally clueless about what a savage I have been my entire life. You are clueless about me even though I have been a complete open book on this forum and have probably shared more personal information about myself than anyone else here.

But why not share all? I figure…what do I have to lose? My siblings already hate me because I was constantly in pain as a child with likely PANDAS, Lyme, arthritis, POTS, severe IBS etc etc etc and never got enough sunlight and instead of medical help I was considered a “pain” a “whiner” too “negative”. I’ve had to live with being daily shamed and humiliated since early childhood. My siblings still don’t understand me even though one was a doctor—your favorite kind—a retina surgeon. Lol. None of you get me. But you out of all of them should.

But hey all you mito kids out there…just know if you do any of these things it’s only half-assing it. If you don’t actually move to El Salvador TODAY and roll your ass out in the sand for a full 12 hours every single day while eating raw oysters, you will NEVER see any improvement in your autoimmune condition.

Never mind that after 5 months in the tropics you FINALLY have the energy to clear truckloads of old shit and clutter out of your home of 30 years.

Nevermind that in the last few months you transformed several rooms in your home that could potentially now be rented out as a stream of income to support your new beach lifestyle.

Nevermind that when you came home from those many months at the beach and your dog could barely walked that your nursed her back to health, walked her at sunrise and sunset daily, gave her a chiropractic adjustment yourself every day, and used red light and photobimodulation on her to get her walking normally again…..JUST SO YOU CAN NOW LEAVE HER AGAIN AND GO TO THE BEACH.

Nope. All of that is not enough. You’re doomed to a life of autoimmune illness because ALL OF THAT is only doing it half-ass.

### Woodstock — 2025-12-29T23:14:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-364345>

Thanks for the article reference. I will pass it on to my friend. But FYI she is already doing that. She moved from Boulder to Hawaii years ago when she got sick, and now lives remotely in the jungle on a volcano in the Hana area. She is outside all day. But when she said her condition is getting worse, that was when she told me about doing the HBOT and a few other things that may also be pointless or even harmful. She is interested in becoming a member and I told her before the pow wow that I knew you would say HBOT is not going to help but also I am not good at being able to articulate the science to her, the TCA cycle etc. I told her BEFORE the pow wow what you would say but I hoped for maybe a bit a detail about why it wouldnt be helpful and certainly would want to know if it could harm her!

I wanted to share your redox blog with her but in that blog you say that HBOT is GOOD for redox. I had just been telling her redox not detox and trying to explain that to her. So I was like, shit, why does Jack say the opposite about HBOT in the blog of what I knew you were going to say on the call? But I didn’t want to call you out on the zoom for seemingly contradicting yourself. Why don’t you revise your redox blog to explain why SOME people shouldn’t do it?

I already know she needs to be in the sun and SO DOES SHE. That’s why she moved to Hawaii many years ago. She sold her condo in Boulder to move there for health reasons and to have the funds to get medical help because she can no longer do massage therapy as a job anymore. She can’t work at all. I’ve told her about nnEMFs and it could be something in her environment but I wondered if the HBOT might actually be doing some harm to her brain. I thought I recall you saying once that it could do damage In some cases. So if that were a possibility that HBOT could damage her brain further, I hoped that you could explain that.

i don’t need a lecture on getting “outside time”. I am fully aware. And so is she.

if you have anything useful to add and if there is a possible danger to anyone doing HBOT, please share as I am sure others would like to know as well. She isn’t a member yet but likely will be soon. Also I tried HBOT once a few years ago. I haven’t tried it again. So I’m not just asking for a non-member as I would also like to know the answer as to if and why it could be harmful. I realize that you now think it’s useless even though you still advise it in your redox blog. And I tried to find more in the blogs and searches but I didn’t find anything that directly addressed it and I don’t have 500 hours to devote to this when you could easily just tell me if there is a possible danger and why. Even if the answer is over my head at least it would have been on the Q and a recording that I could share with her. Because me just saying “Get outside more” when literally that is what she is doing all day long isn’t helpful advice.

I do believe you want to help. But I think you are enjoying making an example out of me too much to respectfully give me helpful answers.

my friend with Parkinson’s loves Hawaii but she is open to checking out El Sal. But she could never do those steps at Atami. I want to check out Costa Del Sol because I need to be in the water, not just sit on the beach. And being in the water for a long time is very hard to do at Atami. Or even Shalpa. People die there. it was near impossible to be in the ocean in May and June. But I did it anyway, got torn up a few times by the sharp rocks, and still Went to the salt pools daily for my ocean time but I have to wonder if soaking in a tub of vaccinated people is ideal. Despite all that I’m still coming back to Atami in Feb and before that going back to Hawaii in January on the big island to snorkel and be with the fish because they bring me joy and calm my nervous system and I feel better there than anywhere else I’ve been in the world. Maybe if I can learn to surf in Hawaii I won’t be so scared to tackle the waves in Palmarcito. But I don’t have a death wish. Maybe I will get scuba certified in ES because of the lovely family I met there that does scuba certification, even though I have always been terrified to do that because of my ears when flying and it’s difficult to clear them. Also anxiety and claustrophobia of being stuck deep in the water. Savage? Nahhh… Just another one of my half-ass ideas. If I’m going to die, I should at least get started on my bucket list.

I have tried to tell her to get out of her environment before but she was actually doing much better in Hawaii before she sold her condo and had the money to get MRIs and functional medicine and the HBOT etc. she was swimming 2 miles a day in the ocean every day before she added in all the therapies and detox. ### Woodstock — 2025-12-30T02:40:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-364357>

Anywhere can be a bad environment. Including El Salvador. There are pros and cons. I stayed at a beautiful airbnb fully grounded in nature but the whole neighborhood would burn their trash and I would constantly be smelling toxic chemicals from the plastics, etc.

There is no way to know these things in advance. Another airbnb had mold and made me super sick. The EMFs there were fantastic. It was at Xanadu but far away and blocked from the tower. The mold was undetectable and my two friends who stayed there had no reaction to it. Just me. Bad redox? It was not visible mold. I could not have known this in advance.

I am very happy that you are working on the community in Costa Del Sol and anything else like that. It sounds absolutely wonderful. This is definitely needed. When I first decided to come to ES, I tried to get into India’s at Shalpa, but she didn’t have any availability. I came anyway and took my chances because you convinced me that I needed to do this. I have read many blogs but Indont always retain the information.

You can keep giving me a hard time for not being able to retain the information well enough to stack the lessons but treating me that way isn’t actually going to make my brain work better.

I have had to grow a thick skin over the course of my life. I used to be really sensitive. I think this illness has taken away my ability to give a shit.

The only reason I’m still here is because I know you’re right….when you’re actually listening to me and trying to help instead of looking for an excuse to humiliate me.

What’s really sad about it is that I really like you. I think you’re an amazing human with balls of steel to do what you’ve done. I wish everyone was like you. I am tired of the bullshit. I like real people. I don’t know how not to be real. Sometimes I get triggered and say things I don’t mean or the wrong way but my intentions are honorable.

I’m happy to keep being made an example of if it somehow helps others to stack the lessons, even if I’m incapable of it.

but if you are going to dish it out, expect me to dish it back. I’ve had to be on the defensive in survival mode my whole life. I am not sure that I can or should change that. I am not going to worship you or put you on a pedestal, even though I do like you and appreciate you very much. I am very grateful you decided to share your knowledge and wisdom with the world. When I heard you talking about MK-Ultra and SV-40 it was the first time I felt vindicated, understood, and not alone anymore in a decade.

No matter how mean you are to me, no one can take that away. I’m not alone anymore. A reputable doctor is finally talking about the things I’ve been chastised for believing and trying to warn people about for over a decade. That in itself has given me a massive wave of healing even if Ingot stuck living in Chernobyl forever.

### digitald3 — 2025-12-31T02:47:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-364397>

This will sound weird but this is personal for me as well. My dad passed away in 2022. He had some Parkinson’s like symptoms and prostate cancer. In about 2019/2020 be developed some problems from the radiation he received 8 years previous. Had bladder and urination issues. He found someone that suggested he try HBOT for the uriniary problems. He did about 20 dives. I don’t think it helped anything other than give him cataracts. And now I know it most likely made him worse as he was dead a few years after.

When oxygen is in the +3 state from our modern blue lit nnemf environment and one never sees the sunrise to convert back to +2, this state doesn’t deliver oxygen to the tissues crating hypoxia. And when you add more oxygen to this situation you make more inflammation rather then less. This is what makes HBOT problematic. Too much oxygen in an environment where it can’t be used. In 2021 my dad ended up with a ruptured bladder, it’s possible the HBOT contributed along with the old radiation and made through 4 months in the hospital only to get out and die from what Scott Adams is dying from right now. I’ll never know. But my guess is that the HBOT was the worst thing he could have done, there’s a long list, but that wasn’t wise in hindsight.

There’s a reason why we don’t breathe pure oxygen in the environment. We need enough but not too much.

### Woodstock — 2025-12-31T20:13:06+0200

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=364349)
>
> Colorado and Hawaii are nnEMF shitholes. So if she is getting worse there then you know by definition something is wrong.

I agree that Colorado is an nnEMF shithole.

But if Hawai’i were so horrible I wouldn’t have felt so amazing there. Of course some areas are going to suck. I went to Kona specifically for snorkeling and a vacation with my hubby to have quality time. Also, I had a free hotel from points and early December is the cheapest time because it’s between holidays. And the water there is so amazing! I would put it at a 10 against trying to swim in the waves at Palmarcito who’s is closer to a 2 or 3. And it’s impossible to relax and float in the water in Palmarcito unlike Kona where there are many protected coves full of coral, tropical fish and other marine life. You can just float and enjoy the wonders of the ocean. Or paddleboard with the dolphins and whales. It’s also a better place to learn surfing because there are more protected coves. Being in the water all day is a faraday cage. I suppose it could suck if you stayed in the wrong place and didn’t spend all your time in the ocean.

nnEMF is coming to ES. There is a huge plan to put 5G all along the Surf City coast of El Salvador. Someone with inside info got to see their whole plan to destroy the coast with nnEMF. I hope you have some influence with Bukele to stop this. nnEMF is coming to a tropical location near you.

I did however last night learn of an amazing island that is part of Puerto Rico called Vieques. 70% of the island is a protected wildlife preserve. Great snorkeling and diving there. And I did an antenna search and there are areas You can live with no towers in a 2 mile radius. It’s a small island and there is a small airport so like anywhere location needs to be measured. Less than 10,000 people on the whole island. As long as they keep that 70% protected, it might be a good spot. There are NO chain hotels on this island. All privately owned. At least there is a direct flight to PR from Denver unlike El Salvador. And then you can ferry over. It’s a gorgeous island with wild horses inland and protected reefs on the coast as well as some black sand beaches. I am excited to check this out. Maybe Incan document for a YouTube channel. Though I don’t trust that the algorithms would allow me to actual make any money from YouTube. It think I’m already on their shit list since 2016.

HBOT is Orthodontic Expansion and Maxillo Mandibular Advancement surgery on steroids. I am on a mission with [@digitald3](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/30310/) to educate and informed our colleagues about the potential dire consequences. Most won't listen as incentives dictate outcomes. They can't answer how did I go from Mild OSA to Severe in 6 months after expansion? And then how in 4 months to I go to ZERO apnea. It wasn't more oxygen and the surgery they begged me to get...It was sunlight, blocking blue light and mitigate nnEMF.
I asked [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) how can I teach them, and he told me can't. I took that to heart, made a plan and now I just talk to my patients. Yes I lecture nationally and yes I share my story and I am willing to teach. But I don't need healthcare to help me on where I want to go now. Eventually the MD's and DMD's will wake up (or die) to what true informed consent is and how to build a proper foundation of health.
2026 I will continue to fight to reclaim my most precious commodity **TIME**.
Happy New Year Swans,
Andy (your friendly toothdriller)

### Woodstock — 2026-01-02T19:48:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-364466>

I had an epiphany about my brain today.

I was making art today (tie-dye) and because it can be toxic I don’t do this daily. I will do it for a few weeks or a couple of months and then stop because it can be labor intensive and toxic. But I have lately been doing things differently to minimize the toxicity and labor intensiveness.

But still, every time I go back to it it’s like I have to start as a beginner and re-learn everything all over again. Which colors work well together. The little details on dye application to get the effects you want. After a week or two I get into a groove and I can get the results I want. But if I take a break from it, it’s basically starting from scratch all over again.

I was thinking about that this morning and I just had Jacks voice in my head about “you aren’t stacking the lessons” as though I’m lazy or retarded or not “savage” enough.

I can’t stack the damn tie dye lessons either, Jack!

It takes me a while and then I’m maybe getting it and in a groove and then LIFE happens and I lose it all.

Then I have to start over from scratch.

It is not a lack of being savage enough. I am pretty sure it’s because my brain is fucked.

If I can’t stack tie dye lessons and retain it, how am I going to stack brain science when that isn’t even my background?

But I will keep at it. Just like I keep going back to tie dye.

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=345019)
>
> I am 55 yrs old and have had gut issues, fatigue, POTS symptoms and chronic aches and pains since early childhood. About 5 years ago I tested positive for Lyme which I likely have had since around age 4, based on symptoms and history.
>
> I felt like an 80 year old when I was in my late 20s. (So I am actually doing much better now.) I got the diagnosis of Fibromyalgia from a Chiropractor who took the time to take a full medical history and check pressure points. When I went to the Arthritis Foundation website and read their description of the disease I cried for hours. Mostly of relief that I was not psycho because I had been told previously that this was psychosomatic and “no pain no gain” throughout my childhood while I pushed myself athletically into directions that likely only exacerbated my symptoms while trying to keep up with my non-biological siblings. I felt like a weakling and a failure, while simultaneously knowing I was actually smarter than most people. I just had a delayed reaction in responding and spent a lot of time in my head figuring stuff out. Nowadays this behavior would be diagnosed as on the spectrum, but I was never recognized as having any issues of the sort. It makes sense to me now. I likely had heavy metal poisoning from living in an area that had been destroyed by a tornado when I was 5-6 yrs old. I had some OCD symptoms at that age that improved after I moved.
>
> My current health issues are:
> Lyme and co-infections
> mold exposure/mycotoxins
> high fasting insulin/borderline pre-diabetic
> MCAS
> candida since age 10
> EBV
> Strep
> Staph
> SIBO/gut issues/gluten issues
> chronic pain and stiffness in joints, neck and shoulders
> liver and gallbladder issues
> possible kidney issues
> zero libido
> menopause-on Bioidentical hormones
> gained 30 pounds since menopause and very difficult to lose
>
> since September I have been doing sunrise walks and getting sun on my body as much as possible
>
> I have been working on lifestyle changes to eat more regularly and eat dinner earlier and reduce blue light exposure
>
> about 6 years ago I had a building biologist evaluate and help figure out how to do all the right things. My house was Ethernet wired and we havent used a Wifi router during this time. HOWEVER, when Century Link brought in fiber optic cable, they gave us a new modem which had 5G turned on and the only way to turn it off was to sign in on their website and do it online. We didn’t realize this and it appeared to be off externally. Two days ago I found out that the modem had been putting off a 5G signal for YEARS unbeknownst to me. I have an RF meter from SLT but it only read high right at the source of the modem and was showing ok levels throughout the main areas of the house I use frequently so I just never realized this was happening. But I hated being in that room. I had a desktop computer in there and never wanted to use it. I moved the computer to another room recently but I never wanted to walk through the 5G area to the other part of the house. When I brought the building biologist back and had him go through the house with me, he quickly figured out what was going on and showed us how to turn off the 5G from the Century Link website with the password on the bottom of the modem. I would normally be really pissed off about being radiated for all these years, but that would just be a waste of my energy. It’s finally off now. The worst part of this is that we had a few water leaks and therefore mold issues which are supposedly made 600x worse with Wifi. We remediated the mold years ago but did not throw away everything in our home. We are in a dry climate so we have an evaporative cooler and I always feel worse midsummer when the humidity level gets really high from the cooler being on 24/7 for weeks in a row. The high humidity causes mold spores to reactivate. Hopefully this will improve now!
>
> I am coming to El Salvador for a couple of months this winter. I hope this will be a good jumpstart on getting my health on track. I plan to spend a lot of time in the tropics and on the ocean for at least the next three years or longer. I was already getting the intuition that I needed to do this before stumbling upon Dr Kruse on Twitter back in September.
>
> I will add more details and labs at some point. I just wanted to get this journal started. It has only been two days that my house has been free of 5G, but I am so relieved to know that there was actually something so easy I could do that theoretically should make a huge difference. (We had already done all the hard work but were not reaping the benefit!) Just another confirmation for me to listen to my gut feelings and intuitions and the more subtle things that I feel because there was a massive reason I have been avoiding half of my house for YEARS! I would normally beat myself up for not getting help on this sooner and figuring it out, but I’ve spent 55 years beating myself up and believing my pain was all my own fault and I am so DONE with that self sabotage type of behavior. So much crazy stuff going on in the world but I am going to have my best year ever in 2025. I might even learn to surf. Apparently the beach I will be near is good surf for beginners in Feb/March. I’ve also always felt physically better with a tan. The pieces are coming together.
>
> Thanks for reading.

Copper is fundamental to immunity.

This is well worth considering.

### Woodstock — 2026-01-02T22:02:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-364476>

Update on my friend with PD and HBOT:

I told her I asked about it and I strongly encouraged her not to do it, even though I am a failure in stacking the lessons and being able to explain it to her. She appreciated that I tried.

Then she asked chatGPT because that is all the energy she has right now given her daily struggles.

She shared the answer with me and I thought it was interesting. She decided to stop HBOT therapy after reading this. I’m glad that Ingot her to reconsider even if AI had to explain it to her. It’s not a Jack Kruse type answer but alludes to the part about oxygen causing harm. I’m curious as to if the AI answer has any major flaws. Obviously it leaves out hundreds of pages of blogs I am reading and failing to stack. But the chatGPT answer resonated with her because of her insomnia and fight/flight nervous system deregulation.

Also I shared what people said here but she didn’t see that until after consulting with AI. Thanks to all for your help. Glad she is off HBOT. It very well could have been contributing to her decline.

The part that seems critical in the Chat GPT answer is that “high-dose oxygen can cause oxidative stress”. So that I think is the nugget that relates to the Great Oxygenation Event and those blogs. I just have a hard time breaking all that down into a few sentences because of my brain. And I have ALWAYS had problems articulating complex things that in my mind I understand but I just can spit it back out and explain it to someone. It was very difficult in childhood. I would always end up saying something I didn’t mean. In my mind it made sense and I thought I said it that way when I spoke, but I would so often be misunderstood because what I thought in my mind didn’t always sound like what I ended up saying. What will fix my brain? The sun, the ocean, and low nnEMF.

Here is what chatGPT said, just FYI…I don’t use it and I’m not recommending anyone use it unless they have no other resources:

⸻

Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy (HBOT) & Parkinson’s — Shareable Overview

What HBOT is
Hyperbaric oxygen therapy involves breathing high-concentration oxygen (often close to 100%) under increased pressure inside a chamber. This forces more oxygen into the bloodstream and tissues than normal breathing.

⸻

Why some people consider HBOT for Parkinson’s
• Oxygen supports mitochondrial energy production
• May temporarily reduce inflammation
• Some small studies and anecdotal reports show short-term symptom improvement
• Has shown benefits in stroke and brain injury, which sparks interest

Important note: These effects are inconsistent and often temporary.

⸻

Why HBOT is controversial for Parkinson’s

Parkinson’s is not caused by low oxygen. It involves:
• Mitochondrial stress
• Oxidative imbalance
• Autonomic (nervous system) dysregulation

High-dose oxygen can:
• Increase oxidative stress
• Overstimulate vulnerable dopamine neurons
• Trigger sympathetic (“fight-or-flight”) activation
• Worsen sleep, anxiety, or rigidity in some people

⸻

What the research currently shows
• No strong evidence HBOT slows disease progression
• Mixed results for symptom relief
• Not part of standard Parkinson’s treatment guidelines
• Benefits (when present) are usually short-lived

⸻

Common misconceptions
• ❌ “The darkness is harmful” — not true
• ⚠️ The real concern is physiological overstimulation, not the chamber environment itself
• HBOT is very different from natural oxygen obtained outdoors or through gentle exercise

⸻

When HBOT may be higher risk
• Ongoing insomnia
• Anxiety or nervous system sensitivity
• Autonomic instability
• High pressure or frequent sessions
• Expecting long-term or curative effects

⸻

Practical takeaway
• HBOT is experimental for Parkinson’s
• It may help some people temporarily and worsen symptoms in others
• Caution is advised, especially if sleep or nervous system regulation is fragile
• It should not replace foundational therapies

⸻

Safer, better-supported supports for Parkinson’s
• Regular movement (walking, tai chi, dance)
• Sunlight and natural circadian regulation
• Nervous system calming practices
• Sleep protection
• Mitochondrial-supportive nutrition

⸻

Bottom line

Hyperbaric oxygen is not a cure for Parkinson’s and is not universally beneficial. Any consideration should be cautious, individualized, and framed as experimental — not essential or urgent

I have known even before Jack that mold was not the root cause. And either was Lyme, even though my Lyme docs said so. I took all the binders etc and didn’t get better. I knew. I pestered my husband daily for two years before ever hearing of Jack Kruse that I needed to live on a tropical island and could he please find a way to work remotely so we can be “snow birds”.

However, if someone very sensitive to mold due to high heteroplasmy rate from birth and very damaged mitochondria, which led to a weakened immune, which led to Lyme getting out of control partly because their mother kept her inside most of the time as a child and never even opened the windows, not even in summer….well that person just might not be able to get better in a moldy environment until they physically get out of the mold, even if they ARE in the sun 12 hours a day. There’s just no way to heal when you destroy all the good you did out in the sun by going home to sleep in a moldy house every night.

So while mold isn’t the root cause of autoimmune illness, you are not stacking the lessons if you think that living in a home contaminated with black mold is not having any sort of negative effect on your body. You’re just making it harder to heal and for someone who was fucked from birth because of inherited epi-generational fucked mitochondria, your chances of healing are not good.

As far as having an alcoholic drink or two goes, I find it interesting how differently my body reacts to it in the tropics versus Colorado. I have no desire for it Colorado because of how bad it makes me feel the next day or even week after. Even if the consumption is minimal.

I am not perfect by any means. I have a lot of room for improvement. I was extremely depressed all through childhood until I got engaged to my husband. I have had bouts of depression since then with a lot of loss, grief, family trauma, and being gaslit by docs despite being very ill and not being able to even get a Lyme diagnosis until I was 50 when I likely got it around age 4.

Maybe it doesn’t seem like I have been chronically ill and depressed my entire life to new people I meet because I have found a way to look on the brighter side of life and be grateful for all the support I DO have from my husband and a few close friends. I am blessed to be alive and owe it all to my husband who has taken care of me for the last 29.5 years. In sickness and in a tiny bit of remission when I was 30.

If having a couple of beers or a pina colada brings me joy and helps my anxiety, I will use that as a tool to manage my mental health and happiness. And I’m not going to apologize for that.

But what I found in visiting the Kona coast of Hawaii with the goal of spending most of my time in the ocean snorkeling with the marine life was that was all the “anxiety medicine” that I needed. It was very calming to my nervous system.

Being pelted with rocks or almost drowning from harsh waves at Palmarcito in June was NOT anxiety reducing. I got one good day in the ocean on my last day in ES when the waves had calmed enough and it wasn’t as dangerously rocky. I was out there diving into the waves longer than ANYONE else out there that day. Not as relaxing as floating with the fish but it was finally an ocean day that wasn’t going to risk my life and I still got thrashed hard by those waves, ripping off my bikini every time and I didn’t care. Because I was able to be in the ocean. I was a fucking SAVAGE that day.

Admittedly after that, I wanted a Corona with lime.

I am hoping the waves at Costa Del Sol are a bit kinder and gentler. There’s only one way to find out….

Peace and Love to you Peter. Can’t wait to roll around in the sand with you and the beach dogs again! Until then, I will be floating in the heavenly pacific with the tropical fish, turtles, dolphins and whales Happy New Year!

I went to Kona specifically for snorkeling and a vacation with my hubby to have quality time. Also, I had a free hotel from points and early December is the cheapest time because it’s between holidays. And the water there is so amazing! I would put it at a 10 against trying to swim in the waves at Palmarcito who’s is closer to a 2 or 3. And it’s impossible to relax and float in the water in Palmarcito unlike Kona where there are many protected coves full of coral, tropical fish and other marine life. You can just float and enjoy the wonders of the ocean. Or paddleboard with the dolphins and whales. It’s also a better place to learn surfing because there are more protected coves. Being in the water all day is a faraday cage. I suppose it could suck if you stayed in the wrong place and didn’t spend all your time in the ocean.
>
> nnEMF is coming to ES. There is a huge plan to put 5G all along the Surf City coast of El Salvador. Someone with inside info got to see their whole plan to destroy the coast with nnEMF. I hope you have some influence with Bukele to stop this. nnEMF is coming to a tropical location near you.
>
> I did however last night learn of an amazing island that is part of Puerto Rico called Vieques. 70% of the island is a protected wildlife preserve. Great snorkeling and diving there. And I did an antenna search and there are areas You can live with no towers in a 2 mile radius. It’s a small island and there is a small airport so like anywhere location needs to be measured. Less than 10,000 people on the whole island. As long as they keep that 70% protected, it might be a good spot. There are NO chain hotels on this island. All privately owned. At least there is a direct flight to PR from Denver unlike El Salvador. And then you can ferry over. It’s a gorgeous island with wild horses inland and protected reefs on the coast as well as some black sand beaches. I am excited to check this out. Maybe Incan document for a YouTube channel. Though I don’t trust that the algorithms would allow me to actual make any money from YouTube. It think I’m already on their shit list since 2016.

Vieques has massive tetrahertz arrays for the military. They use the areas for bombing raid and the water in the area is loaded with heavy metals.

### Woodstock — 2026-01-03T08:06:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-364499>

“I'd think he doesn't want you in Hawaii b/c you have so much going on, meaning it will only take you so far and then you'll need to move. So why not move to the A location first!”

sooooooooo……
I don’t live in Hawaii. I just took a vacation there with my husband on the 20 year anniversary of the last time we were there when my son was in my womb. I have been longing to go back with him and snorkel, despite how terrifying it was to see urchins inches from my pregnant belly then. I still loved it and we had an amazing time together that was much needed. I’m not a great swimmer but I love to snorkel in clear waters.

ive spent 4 months in El Salvador since last Feb. my husband isn’t sold on El Sal. He got a stomach bug the first visit. I’m probably Going to kill my 30 year relationship if I insist on that location. But I love the people and community so I will always go back and visit my mito fam and will be back in Feb. I’m looking for a place we can both be happy so we can be together. Hawaii was fantastic for our relationship and I felt much better there. And it was very calming to my nervous system. I am headed back to Kona and then back to El Sal. It will be two month away from home and that’s about my limit before I start to get really homesick for my family.

i Also have missed out on 2/3 of my life being sick not counting the 1/3 spent sleeping. and if I’m going to die I want to see more of the Earth first. It’s very challenging. El Salvador was extremely challenging for me. but I made a ton of friends and close bonds and made music and my vitamin D was over 70 there. I am focusing on the positive or I wouldn’t bother to come back. But my whole life will fall to pieces if I fuck up my marriage. It’s a two way street. Yet He needs the sun and low nnEMF just as much as I do.

I am going to be sleeping in a tent and doing yoga and snorkeling every day in Kona. I want to try to help my friend who lives in Maui but is joining me on the big island for at least a week there maybe longer. She already does a lot of mito stuff. i Don’t have to worry about about telling her to put her phone in airplane mode or convince her to get up at sunrise. She already does that. And I am afraid to snorkel without a buddy so it will be good to have a friend with me So I don’t get lost at sea.

maybe an island off Panama might be ideal for exploration. Supposedly those are good nnEMF according to Jack. but to do the research for our utopia means a lot of screen time researching. It becomes very OCD for me to figure it out. Which sucks my energy.

maybe Costa Del Sol would be ok but since there werent many mitos there last year I never explored It. I need community.

i Am interested in checking out the area. But I am pretty sure hubby is not gonna be on board even if the beach there is better. Also he needs to be able to work. He can work remotely for a week or two but he still needs to network and find clients local because I don’t think he can do what he does outside the USA.

i just learned of Vieques and then a day later found out about the military testimg and making the place toxic. It looked like a perfect spot amd still in the US which would have been better for his work situation.

back to the drawing board…

### Jaime C — 2026-01-03T08:39:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-364503>

Hey Anne-Marie, I've heard your name in the Q&As and since you're talking about Kona, thought I'd chime in here if you don't mind! We live in Gunnison, CO and have 2 boys with Type 1 Diabetes. We just got home (today!) from 6 weeks on the Big Island for a trial in the topics while waiting on passports to arrive. It was amazing and I'm happy for you to get to spend some time there too! We avoided Kona except for errands and grocery runs, even though my daughter lives there! We got an airbnb south of Captain Cook, in a very rural area. I checked 5G maps ahead of time and felt it was a good selection to avoid as much nnEMF as possible. Since you'll be in a tent, thought I'd recommend camping at Ho'okena Beach (reserve online in advance). It's where we spent the most time, as it quickly became our favorite spot. It's good for snorkeling (we swam with dolphins, when they were there)! Not too far north from Ho'okena is "2-Step" (it has a different official name but a quick google search will pull it up). Although there's no sand, just volcanic rock, (still awesome!) it is one of the top snorkeling spots on the big island. Both of these locations have fewer people, especially if you get there between 7-8 am, which isn't hard since we're all up for sunrise anyways! Happy to give specific details on where the reefs are, etc...we learned a ton! Enjoy the adventure!

I did not test Kona on my recent trip because I just wanted to have QT with hubby and be in the faraday cage of the ocean the whole time.

I can’t fly every two weeks Without repercussion. Flying is really rough on my redox. Also doing two flights in one day is harder for me. I am doing overnights in LA so I can fly Direct on the longer flights to Kona and ES. Amd I will have dinner with some Cali friends and sleep in between flights. Daytime flights are better for me. It Took a lot of planning but I figured out how to make it work best for me.

Thanks for your input! Yea I loved that area and I will be staying near two-step part of my first week there before I go to a yoga ashram for the class I’m doing there where I chose tent accommodations. We LOVED Two-step. We loved to paddleboard and then snorkel off the board one at a time. But I never got to swim with the dolphins there. We did see many dolphins from our board. Also we took a boat trip to get to the Captain Cook cove to snorkel. It was amazing. Saw a giant barracuda there.

we stayed at the Holiday Inn in Kona and I was ok as long as we got out early on the water. I had 8 free nights there and it was a low budget trip for us Because we really didn’t have the funds when I planned it. we brought our own inflatable paddleboard. Food is way more expensive there. I don’t think I could afford to move there. but I can’t afford to move anywhere unless we sold our house which isn’t happening. It’s not in my name. I would have to get divorced and force the sale.

I didn’t bring meters because I kind of forgot. I was way overdue for getting out of Colorado after 5 months back. I could barely pack. Brain fog. And really it was a blessing I forgot them because I just needed to focus on getting in the water and not stressing about the towers. But I am working on packing better this time since I won’t be back home for two months. Bringing all the meters this time and will teach my friend how to use them. I have a silver canopy I can put in my tent but I’m hoping EMFs will be lower in the ashram and there’s no electricity in the tents.

### Woodstock — 2026-01-04T09:07:13+0200

I finally found the right inflatable hot tub that is actually a SALTWATER system. It is truly an amazing tub. I tested just above the water level with safe and sound and it was in the green. not sure what happens to your head sticking out of the water. Or is it just the immersed parts of your body in the faraday cage?

anyway I highly recommend. I got it on sale cheaper at Walmart than direct from Intex. The price is up a bit now but still worth it. Comes in two sizes. Here is a link. I figured out how to test body voltage with my EM3. Zero standing on the location the tub sits while barefoot. But I am not sure how to test what it would be when you’re actually in the tub with water. Google said it would be dangerous to test while in the tub. So how do you test it?

I never fully understood the grounding wires we used for cold plunge and why you would need it if you don’t have any issues with grounding where you put the cold plunge.

I will keep experimenting with it.

### Anthony Smith — 2026-01-11T23:10:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-365168>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=365165)
>
> I figured out how to test body voltage with my EM3. Zero standing on the location the tub sits while barefoot. But I am not sure how to test what it would be when you’re actually in the tub with water. Google said it would be dangerous to test while in the tub. So how do you test it?
>
> I never fully understood the grounding wires we used for cold plunge and why you would need it if you don’t have any issues with grounding where you put the cold plunge.
>
> Should I unplug my hot tub when I’m using it to avoid E and mag fields? Or does being in the water protect you? Or make it worse? The tub is inflatable and made of some kind of pvc material.
>
> What is interesting is how much wearing socks increases the body voltage versus being barefoot. Be barefoot more!
>
> I will keep experimenting with it.

The body voltage meter gives the electric field (being induced into your body) a path of least resistance to ground so you can measure its strength. For an accurate reading you CANNOT be double grounded - you MUST wear ungrounded shoes.

Then when standing next to the tub, while holding the meter in one hand, put the other hand in the water. IF the tub is grounded the meter reading will drop (because you will be double grounded) IF the tub is not grounded and is picking up a field the reading will go up. IF this is the case turn off all close by circuits untill the reading drops AND then put a wire in the tub with the other end attached to a ground spike in the dirt NOT a house ground outlet.

Water is conductive so cutting off the source of any electric field close by Is IMPORTANT.

### Woodstock — 2026-02-14T06:07:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-366874>

Has anyone has success praying to Jesus? I’m not into dogmatic religion or even the Bible cause I know it’s been manipulated. And after many years of “plant medicine” when centralized medicine failed me and hurt me, I felt negative entities were invading my field and I was getting attacked in my sleep. I met a young woman on my flight that overcame addiction by turning to Jesus. She asked about me and she asked if she could pray for me. I thought she was going to say a prayer at bedtime or something and she moved over to the empty middle seat and held my hand and prayed for like 10 minutes. We were both crying. Then I got in my rental car and the radio was blasting a song about Jesus. I met some interesting folks along the way and had many come to Jesus moments. I’m just saying, if you haven’t tried praying to Jesus, maybe give it a shot. I know he saved me from MKUltra or DARPA or interdimemsional beings when I was 15 and something tried to suck me out of my body…it was demonic, and at the last moment when I could no longer resist I remembered Jesus and asked him to save me and the thing in my bedroom trying to take me immediately stopped.

This is actually the first thing I asked Jack about on the post Danny follow up before I was even a member. I asked about sleep paralysis. But it’s not just a neuro thing. It’s MKUltra or something. I don’t understand the answer I got in that pow wow because Jack blew it off as some neuro thing and yet I know what I experienced. It was the most terrifying thing in my life. I didn’t sleep for three months after that with my bible under my pillow. My teachers thought I was on drugs. I couldn’t tell anyone because it sounded crazy.

Now this new blog is out and it’s very sciencey and over my head as per usual. Why are they after me? What was after me? I am in a very safe place, living with Mitos who are kind and loving and listen politely to all my trauma stories. I am safe. And I’m not sitting around praying 24/7. I have not prayed to anyone but myself, my inner wisdom, my true spirit since around 2019 because I didn’t want to open my field and be vulnerable to energy vampires. But since that sweet girl from Guatemala prayed for me out loud on the plane my life has changed. I can’t remember where I put the note she wrote her instagram on. Hopefully I will find it so I can thank her. Praise Jesus.

I love El Salvador and everyone I’ve met here over the last year. But I also have another place calling me and I’m manifesting it. And my husband is happy. I am manifesting all my dreams. So much to share but that’s all for now. Trying to get through the latest blog and submit a question. Just wanted to say hi to anyone who reads my posts. And thank you for your support and encouragement and I really love getting the personal messages from people who appreciate that I am open and share my experiences in hopes that it might help someone else going through similar stuff. I haven’t been on the forum much lately because I AM LIVING!

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-15T21:07:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-366939>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=366874)
>
> Has anyone has success praying to Jesus? !

YES - Hundreds of answered prayers in my own life, my family and friends.

My daughter was born with Static Encephalopathy, we were told by “experts” she would never walk properly, run, dance, hop, skip or ride a bike etc. She was still young and we had not seen her do any of these normal childhood things BUT one day a famous healing evangelist came to our church. We all lined up and he prayed for everyone, people were “falling out” all over the place, he prayed for us, we felt nothing. My wife and I looked at each other and said - “Well I guess that was a waste of time” - We were at the back of a 5000 seat church and proceeded to watched our daughter hop, skip, jump down maybe a hundred steps to the front . . . Walking her down the idle and dancing with her on her wedding day was one of the greatest moments of my life.

I have personally prayed for a deff person and moments later they could hear. Had a possessed blood coven witch we were ministering to try to drown herself in my pool, I pulled her out, sat her in a chair and said “I bind you in Jesus’ name” - she literally could not move and was shouting for “whatever” to let her go.

In modern vernacular we live in a “matrix”, a hologram and “God” wrote the code.
So maybe prayer is asking God to “rewrite” a line or two of code - Or - accessing the already pre-programmed ability to rewrite our own code.

Institutional (centralized) religion sucks BUT God/Jesus is real and prayer works.

yesterday it was 5.13

i haven't seen it that low in maybe 2 or 3 years.

again, I've only been here less than two weeks but was visiting other tropics for snorkeling and volcanic activity in parts of dec and Jan

i have said like 10 times I have moved from chernobyl

would be nice to be able to visit but I can't figure out if you're trying to tell me that I shouldn't even visit ever again or if I will never ever get better no matter what I do. your messages on ask jack are confusing. i get it. i moved. i put down an offer. but I'm just gonna be fucked up forever because autism can't be cured? demyelination at birth can NEVER be healed?

my siblings think I am faking it

I get that my “brain is broken” in the scientific sense.

But also I was much more intelligent and introspective as a child than others around me.

Yes I want my left and right hemispheres to work in unison and it sucks that they don’t.

But I’m not sitting around whining about it. I’m not feeling sorry for myself for failing as a lawyer. Or all my other failures.

I’ve worked every day of my life to try to “fix myself”. I ran track for five years in middle-high school even though I came in last in every race.

I’ve been trying to figure it out my whole life and now I’ve finally found a place where I literally thrive. It made me glow. People were staring at me. I’m probably still 40 pounds overweight and 56. It was the fucking volcano.

I didn’t realize there was a part 2 to your MK Ultra blog. I thought it was the one I already read. I will read it. If you didn’t go overboard on the science terms and I can just read it without having to google every term, maybe I can get through it.

I try to tell him he forgets conversations we had last week and he is getting fried too and going to get dementia but he doesn’t listen. And his brain can’t comprehend.

I just sent him my labs showing I am no longer prediabetic and my vitamin D went up from 34 to 65.

Glad I got the labs done. Good to know. I know that wasn’t just from 10 days in Atami. It was from the lava spewing volcano lighting my brain on fire.

Maybe the labs will help him understand. But I could really use your help. He isn’t going to be able to get through that blog.

“My brain is broken” is not sufficient either. Please say it in a way that is helpful. I love my husband. We have made it 28.5 years. I would have been dead many years ago without him. I’m going to try to save him. But first I’m saving myself.

### Woodstock — 2026-02-20T16:23:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-367217>

Dear Uncle Jack,

You think I don’t get it.

Sunlight = TINA

LIGHT, WATER AND MAGNETISM

DUHHHHHH

I left Colorado. I left my entire life’s work behind. I left my home, my husband, my son, my two dogs, my 7 chickens, gave away my bees, left my Boulder community….

TO GO LIVE ON AN ACTIVE VOLCANO.

You told me that I CAN actually fix this with your Light, Water and Magnetism protocol. That I may someday be able to use BOTH hemispheres of my brain simultaneously instead of having to choose either being an analytical science geek or a creative music and art making person.

I would sure love to see what my FIXED brain could do.

I actually DO get it and also I GET TO CHOOSE to stop thinking about it and live in the side of my brain that won’t make me want to shoot myself in the mouth with a shotgun in my mothers garage like my sweet cousin did because he was treated wrongly by centralized medicine and like me was just trying to understand the world.

I CHOOSE LIFE.

And I’m doing EXACTLY what you told me to do.

So why are you “DONE WITH ME”?

I’m your biggest fan. I wrote a song about you. I wrote a play about you. I tell everyone you’re the smartest person on the planet and that I love you unconditionally no matter how much of a jerk you are to me.

Do you actually want me to wither up and die and lose my mojo again and hurt my brain and stifle my creativity and be a dried up prune just so I can stack your lessons?

Clearly I stacked enough lessons to decide to move to an active volcano that set me on fire and is calling me back to her.

You are holding back on some MKUltra knowledge that might help me understand how I came to be this way and why I have been attacked in my sleep my entire life. I was literally depressed for about 40 of my 56 years and have spent 2/3 of my life too sick to do much. I had to quit law because I was a nervous wreck. My body hurt so badly in my 20s it took me three hours shaking with a hot cup of herbal tea to just get in the shower to go to work. They called my gut issues “spastic colon” and gave me pills. Finally went to a chiropractor and he called it Fibromyalgia. I had severe POTS since childhood and only found out at age 50 when everyone started to get it after Covid that this had a name. And at 50 it went away or is very mild now due to menopause.

After I got married and the fibromyalgia diagnosis and had some support, I did acupuncture weekly and every kind of healing you could imagine. Yoga turned my life around but it didn’t totally heal me and I got really depressed again in 2000 when my Dad passed and exactly 6 months later my baby Siamese looking kitten was killed by a pack of raccoons. I went into a deep depression. Then 9/11 happened. So I went back to school to get a music/audio engineering degree which actually was my first choice when I was 18 as my major but I never had music theory lessons and couldn’t do the math and the music training at the same time so I chose law instead because I was good at arguing. And some family history that I won’t put on this forum.

Now you want me to read your blogs when I’m already doing what I need to do. I don’t live in Chernobyl anymore and I get that I will probably never get to live in my beautiful home ever again except to throw away all the items I don’t need and grab the ones I want.

My son will likely never want to leave Boulder. His dad wanted to give him the house anyway. It’s fully mitigated inside. I redid his room so his sleeping area will be EMF free with cut off switches for the electricity. I hope Boulder doesn’t kill him but he can lift a girl over his head with one arm so I’m not too worried about it.

My husband will join me on the volcano eventually. I will keep nagging him to be more mito. Someone still has to pay the bills. He is finally making progress in his career. Maybe due to his lifestyle changes thanks to a lot he has learned from you through me.

I appreciate your blogs, podcasts and all the rest but I’m not going to focus on it anymore.

IM LIVING YOUR WORD. WHY DOES THAT NOT MAKE YOU HAPPY?

IM HEALING MY METABOLIC ISSUES. A1c hasn’t been 5.1 for years. Every time I got it checked it was ticking up by .1 and in November it was up to 5.7 and now it’s 5.1

I AM HEALING

And you want me to read the blogs right now when I am on fire and making music and art and LOVING MYSELF AND MY LIFE AND BRINGING JOY TO THE WORLD AND EVERYONE I MEET?

You’re the one that’s retarded if you want me to do a 180 and flip to the analytical hemisphere of my brain instead of the creative one.

It’s not about dopamine. It’s about being my true self.

THANK YOU FROM THE DEPTHS OF MY SOUL!

I wouldn’t understand it and would still be trying to convince my family and would still be lost without you.

Do you get it now?

yes it's true that I've had some crazy experiences in my life and crossed paths with some very interesting people.

All I ever wanted was to not be in constant pain, dizziness and depression and low energy so that I could LIVE and not be stuck inside sick and depressed.

guess what??? I'm not stuck inside or depressed anymore. sorry if the truth of my life experiences is "too much" for anyone.

i share in hopes that it will help someone else. based on some of the people who have reached out to me and all the friends I've made in El Salvador, I don't think anyone would say I'm depressing.

yeah when I tried explaining to my Boulder friends 10 years ago that Hillary was cutting off little girls faces and eating babies they thought I was crazy and hated me. but now look who's talking about it on the Q and As?

but my posts are depressing?

I got my SCUBA diving certification today...

I never thought that would happen.

i've come so far. Somehow my success is depressing to you????

weird.

### Woodstock — 2026-02-23T05:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-367435>

What was so amazing about my dives today is that I didn’t panic. Even when things went wrong. And they did. My mask slipped off because of my big tangled messy hair amd mask filled up with water and going up my nose amd trying to blow it out and forgetting to breathe but I realized it slipped in the back and fixed it and didn’t need anyone to save me.

Once I mixed up the buttons that fill your vest or deflate it. Reminded me of my first week of driving a car and smashing into a parked car as I tried to slam on the brake and my brain reversed it and Imslammed on the gas instead. I realized what I was doing without panic and finally pushed the inflate button when I found myself on the bottom of the ocean floor. So glad there are no sea urchins here like in Hawaii! lol.

I emerged exhilarated and joyful. My instructor asked me on the way to Los cabanas if I liked diving and I was like “uhhh I think so” Amd after the giant grouper amd not panicking when things went wrong and all the fishies….I told him on the boat ride back….YES I LOVE DIVING!

Because my ears were always a problem on airplanes I never thought I could dive. And I didn’t trust my brain. And yes I made some mistakes….But I didn’t panic! Cool as a cucumber.

There is a whole other universe here that most people never explore. One of the first books Imlearned to read when I was 3 or 4 was about Jacques Cousteau.

Amd my favorite tv show was Julia Child. Maybe I should watch some of those episodes again. It might help me get my cooking mojo back. I lost it about 15 years ago when kids complained about my cooking and I was trying to cleanse myself back to health. The cleansing killed my appetite which always fueled my desire to cook and creativity in the kitchen.

You know what the problem is…just not enough hours in the day to do everything!

I’m sorry you don’t want to hear my song about you or watch the play I wrote inspired by your teachings. But we will just have to have all the fun without you.

Sooooo damn depressing. I know.

### Woodstock — 2026-03-04T13:26:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-368075>

for those out there with low vitamin D….El Salvador is a good place to be! Vitamin D went up from 65 to 87 in the last two weeks. I wasn’t testing as much last year, and I just wanted to see how much it changed even though I got it tested a couple weeks ago because I will be traveling again. It’s crazy how much it can drop going to a poor environment. And then go up so quickly. I definitely learned something from this and it was worth the money to do tests two weeks apart here.

Also I’m not sure when I will get to test again and wanted to know baseline of where Im leaving here at. I guess these tests are not always accurate for many reasons but when you get the results the same day…well that doesn’t happen much in the states. Especially not for Vitamin D. I wonder why they always take so long on that one in the US? (Detect sarcasm)

### Woodstock — 2026-03-14T21:48:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-368797>

Listened to this again…

There is so much in this podcast that resonates with my lifelong health issues. I listened to it in June when it first came out and just listened again.

I can use my right brain again. I realize that I am not in control of my left brain/right brain problem (where only one side works at a time…I’m either creative or analytical and it’s hard to do one if my brain is currently wired in the other hemisphere). It flips the switch all by itself and I can’t really flip it intentionally. So when it switches, it feels like I’m stuck there until something flips it back.

This is frustrating but also I am just going to accept it and do my best to maximize where I’m at instead of fighting it. So now I can read blogs and listen to podcasts.

I had some labs done before I leave to go off exploring the Pacific Seas on a sailboat with a one way ticket.

Ferritin came back really high. 240 from labcorp. Reference range is 15-150.

I recently had a bacterial infection and had to take an antibiotic. And I recall reading in one of the blogs about how iron feeds bacteria. So that makes sense that I was more vulnerable to bacterial infection. I want to understand this better.

Also I want to understand everything in this podcast that came up in my Google search on iron. I want to understand the iron in the +3 and +2 state but also I need to read more because you’re right Jack…I still don’t get it. I am not a scientist, but doing my best to read and research. This podcast is freaking me out a bit on how many of my boxes it checks, and now with the ferritin being so high, I am thinking this could help me stack the lessons if I could understand this process better.

I’m so glad I didn’t let some stupid psychiatric nurse prescribe me lithium a few years back.

I still don’t think I need a GLP-1 to jump start remyelination as long as I am in the right environment after listening to this. Risks outweigh benefits.

I do feel like I’m getting better overall. The high ferritin is concerning. I will monitor this one more closely from now on.

On the upside, I had calcitriol 1,25 Di-OH Vitamin D checked. It had been at 19 and couple years ago and is now 50. Standard vitamin D went up (from 34 in Nov) to 87. I wonder if I should bother with the standard test anymore or just check the 1,25 di-OH? That one was not available from the nurse that does house calls in El Salvador so I had that done in California before I left the mainland last week. I would be more concerned if that was still really low. Isn’t this a good sign that I am able to store the vitamin D?

T3 uptake is low again after first seeing this in November. Only recently has any thyroid lab ever indicated a possibility of hypothyroidism even though I’ve had a lot of the symptoms throughout my life. But how accurate is the testing and could I have been low thyroid for many years or decades and it just didn’t show up?

I tried taking a desiccated thyroid supplement from grass fed New Zealand cows starting in December or January but I think it was causing inflammation because my knees started hurting again after taking it when the pain had gone away after about a week in El Salvador. I had been really low energy in Boulder so I thought I would just see if it helped. I wonder if this caused my recent “manic” episode, if that is even what it was? Anyway, I stopped taking the supplement. Maybe the supplement was triggering my creative brain too? I miss my creative brain. But I guess I need my analytical brain to work right now so I can understand what is going on with my body and brain.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-15T03:37:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anne-marie%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30088/post-368827>

LESSON:

You see the last picture that JaSz posted above? It says, "If you dont' measure it, it don't get fixed."

Hopefully many of you now understand why Jansz got banned as a Gold member on my site. It wasn't because he was a nasty guy or a bad guy. It wasn't because he was dumb. It was because shown mountains of evidence he could not change his opinion so he tried to change many other member's minds with this picture he often posted about labs. He was a long term member who could never wrap his head around that fact we are not just a bag of biochemistry to be figured out. He could not fathom that biophysics is upstream to biochemistry.

He never could figure out why all his BS pushing of DUTCH testing was met with my anger on the forums.

Hopefully some of you are realizing why I gave him so much rope. I wanted him to realize nature's truths on his own learning time scale. He never did.

Why are lab tests superfluous when you understand biophysics > biochemistry?

When a photon of light hits the eye, the retinal chromophore inside opsin proteins changes shape in about 200 femtoseconds. That’s 0.0000000000002 seconds. To put that into perspective, a nerve impulse travelling along a neuron takes milliseconds. In other words, the very first step of vision happens around ten million times faster than an action potential in a nerve.

IMPLICATIONS?

Before a neuron fires.....Before the brain interprets anything…Before hormones or metabolism change…,

Light has already interacted and changed your biology. When you meet someone who thinks usiing a DUTCH test while getting ready to take an HRT program from a functional medicine moron can solve and environmental light story show them this. That tiny molecule retinal (derived from vitamin A) absorbs a photon and instantly flips shape. That microscopic structural change triggers the cascade that eventually becomes a nerve signal and, ultimately, sight.

the 200-femtosecond flip of retinal is 10^7 times faster than an action potential in any neuron in your body. This means by the time a lab tech or physician measures a hormone level or a "chemical imbalance, the photonic crime has been over for a biological eternity. Sit with this reality.
