### Jaime C — 2026-03-22T08:14:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-healthiest-spot-to-live-in-the-us.30104/post-369350>

> [Barbara888 said:](/goto/post?id=369309)
>
> What about Maui or Hawaii ?

We recently relocated from Colorado to the Big Island (Hawaii). We are in a fairly remote area and I have tested the home we are in with a Cornet meter. Everything here is 'green'. I don't see any towers on the locator map online that are near us, and we are in a 4G area.

I'm sure certain parts of Hawaii would be awful (island of Oahu where Honolulu is!). I've seen some amazing improvements in my boys while we have been here; trying to reverse Type 1 Diabetes for them both.

It seems to check the major boxes for healing:
in the tropics / good sun
black sand, volcanic rock and ocean to ground in (and the fun that comes with each of those things!)
plentiful seafood to eat
clean water
dark, quiet nights/no light pollution because we are remote
my daughter lives two towns over, and we have an instant community with her and her friends

For now, this is our best option. I'm doing most of this solo with the boys while my husband is in CO for necessary business (ie., to fund this whole thing!). International just isn't in the cards for us right now... until we know we can be together as a family.

Hawaii is expensive, but not that much more expensive compared to the resort town we are from in CO. If we can find a way to cut a few more ties (we are getting close!), ES would be the next stop. In the meantime, we are south of the Captain Cook area doing the best we can.

I know politically this isn't a great place to be, and I'm sure there are plenty of other reasons HI can be picked apart. For now, it's our "A".

Hi ! It does sound like it might be the best option for you right now. I just remember Jack's sentiment is not good about Hawaii because of military EMF's - which the cornet might miss? I am no where near an expert and do not want to be a party pooper haha but this is some things not covered by the cornet:

-   **Frequency Gap:** The Cornet only goes up to **8 GHz**. Most naval and aircraft radars (like X-band and Ku-band) operate between **8 GHz and 18 GHz**. Since they’re outside its range, the device stays green even if the radiation is high.
-   **Pulse Speed:** Military radar doesn't broadcast a steady signal like Wi-Fi. It sends out massive, ultra-short "pulses" in nanoseconds. The Cornet isn't fast enough to catch these—it averages the signal over time, which makes a huge peak look like a tiny, harmless reading.
-   **Low Frequencies:** There's a lot of "Over-The-Horizon" radar in Hawaii that uses very low frequencies (under 30 MHz) to track things thousands of miles away. The Cornet only starts measuring from **100 MHz**, so it misses that entire spectrum.

I really don't know how much of a problem that is, but I think Jack is warning on Hawaii for a reason :/
I don't think measuring with the cornet is enough to determine the problem

### Mat.I — 2025-02-07T23:48:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-about-treatment-for-jabs.30105/post-346383>

The percentages tell you the dose per volume. E.g. 1% solution is 1000mg active ingredient per 100ml which is 10mg per ml. 1.87% would be 18.7mg per ml.. Solution concentrations do not matter as long as you achieve your desired dose.. (end result achieved). Required dose ml = target dose per kg bw (mg/kg) x your bw / concentration mg/ml.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-16T20:27:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-about-treatment-for-jabs.30105/post-346845>

Pay attention to IVM/FenBen for cancer as the blogs lay out. New data is arriving and ties to my trip to the Amazon. IVM prevents SV40 promoters from entering the nucleus but blocking importin A/B. This pathway likely has many chemokines that can impact it.

Yes, ivermectin is known to inhibit importin α/β-mediated nuclear transport, which is relevant in the context of SV40 promoters.

Mechanism: •Importin α/β Pathway: This transport system is responsible for shuttling proteins with nuclear localization signals (NLS) into the nucleus. Many viruses, including SV40, hijack this pathway to deliver their regulatory proteins (e.g., Large T antigen) into the nucleus for replication and transcriptional activation.

•Ivermectin as an Inhibitor: Ivermectin binds to importin α/β and disrupts its function, thereby preventing nuclear entry of proteins that depend on this transport mechanism. Implications for SV40 Promoters:

•SV40 Promoters: The SV40 early promoter is often used in molecular biology due to its strong transcriptional activity in mammalian cells. However, its transactivation requires the nuclear localization of SV40 Large T antigen, which depends on importin α/β.

•Blocking Nuclear Entry: If ivermectin blocks importin α/β, it could prevent SV40 Large T antigen from entering the nucleus, thereby reducing SV40-driven gene expression and viral replication. Experimental Evidence:

•Studies have demonstrated ivermectin’s ability to inhibit nuclear import of viral proteins from various RNA and DNA viruses (e.g., HIV-1, Dengue, and even SARS-CoV-2).

•SV40 Large T antigen is known to require importin α/β for nuclear entry. If ivermectin blocks this pathway, it could theoretically interfere with any SV40-driven transcription or replication in systems using this promoter.

Certainly, here are some key studies that provide evidence on this topic:

1.Ivermectin as an Importin α/β Inhibitor: •A study by Wagstaff et al. (2012) demonstrated that ivermectin specifically inhibits importin α/β-mediated nuclear import. The researchers found that ivermectin effectively blocked the nuclear import of proteins dependent on the importin α/β pathway, without affecting other nuclear import pathways. This inhibition also correlated with a reduction in the replication of viruses such as HIV-1 and dengue virus, which rely on this pathway for nuclear entry of their proteins. ([http://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov](https://t.co/wfVYr4HOdF))

2.SV40 Large T Antigen and Importin α/β: •The SV40 Large T antigen contains a nuclear localization signal (NLS) that is recognized by importin α, facilitating its transport into the nucleus via the importin β pathway. This nuclear import is essential for the Large T antigen’s role in viral replication and cell transformation. ([http://en.wikipedia.org](https://t.co/pRWm3uS3A2))

These studies collectively suggest that ivermectin’s inhibition of the importin α/β pathway could impede the nuclear import of SV40 Large T antigen, potentially affecting SV40 promoter activity and viral replication.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-16T20:29:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-about-treatment-for-jabs.30105/post-346846>

Does Fenbendazole Upregulate p53? Yes, Fenbendazole (FenBen) has been reported to upregulate p53, a key tumor suppressor protein, in some cancer models.

**Mechanism of p53 Upregulation by Fenbendazole:**

1.Disruption of Microtubules: •Fenbendazole binds to tubulin, preventing microtubule polymerization in a manner similar to colchicine or vinblastine. •This leads to mitotic arrest, which can trigger cell cycle checkpoints and activation of the p53 pathway.
2.Induction of Cellular Stress & DNA Damage Response: •Microtubule disruption can cause mitotic spindle stress, leading to chromosomal instability. •This activates ATM/ATR kinases, which phosphorylate p53, stabilizing it and increasing its transcriptional activity.
3.Apoptosis and Autophagy Induction:
•Upregulated p53 can activate BAX/BAK pro-apoptotic proteins, leading to mitochondrial damage and caspase-dependent apoptosis. •Fenbendazole also promotes autophagy, which can contribute to cancer cell death.
4.Inhibition of Glucose Metabolism (Warburg Effect Reversal): •Some studies suggest Fenbendazole reduces glucose uptake by cancer cells, similar to metformin. •This metabolic stress can further activate AMPK, leading to p53-mediated tumor suppression.

**Fenbendazole’s Mechanism for Cancer Treatment**
1\. Microtubule Disruption (Primary Mechanism) •Fenbendazole binds to β-tubulin, disrupting microtubule formation. •This prevents proper mitotic spindle formation, leading to G2/M cell cycle arrest. •Cells stuck in mitotic arrest either undergo apoptosis or senescence.
2\. Apoptosis Activation via p53 & BCL-2 Inhibition •Cancer cells often overexpress BCL-2, an anti-apoptotic protein that prevents programmed cell death. •Fenbendazole inhibits BCL-2, shifting the balance towards apoptosis.
3\. Disrupting Glucose Metabolism in Cancer Cells •Fenbendazole has been shown to reduce glucose uptake and ATP production, increasing oxidative stress in tumors. •This effect weakens cancer cells that rely on glycolysis (Warburg effect), making them more sensitive to treatment.
4\. Synergistic Effects with Chemotherapy & Radiation •Some studies suggest Fenbendazole enhances the effects of radiation and chemotherapy by: •Increasing DNA damage accumulation. •Disrupting repair pathways (e.g., via p53 activation). •Weakening microtubule integrity, making cancer cells more vulnerable to other drugs.

**Supporting Studies & Evidence**
1.Fenbendazole inhibits tumor growth via microtubule disruption and p53 activation •Study in lung cancer cells showed that Fenbendazole caused mitotic arrest, increased p53, and induced apoptosis. •(Source: PubMed)
2.Fenbendazole enhances radiation sensitivity by targeting microtubules and p53 •Research demonstrated that combining Fenbendazole with radiation led to increased DNA damage and cell death. •(Source: PMC)
3.Mechanism of Fenbendazole in disrupting glucose metabolism •Study found Fenbendazole downregulates GLUT1, reducing glucose uptake in cancer cells. •
(Source: PubMed) Conclusion Fenbendazole upregulates p53 by causing mitotic stress, DNA damage, and metabolic inhibition, leading to cancer cell apoptosis. Its primary mechanism is microtubule disruption, similar to drugs like Vinblastine or Colchicine, but with lower toxicity.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-16T20:33:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-about-treatment-for-jabs.30105/post-346847>

Why is the Amazon a key player for the jabbed?

It is where unique CBD are created.

Here is the kicker… Other non toxic illegal compounds (in some backwards jurisdictions) that are treating cancer. Come to
[@cannmed](https://x.com/cannmed) to learn more.

Yes, cannabinoids exhibit some overlapping mechanisms with ivermectin (IVM) and fenbendazole (FenBen) in cancer treatment, particularly through microtubule disruption, apoptosis induction, and metabolic modulation.

Below are key comparisons:

1\. Microtubule Disruption (Similar to Fenbendazole) •Fenbendazole: Binds β-tubulin, disrupting microtubule polymerization, leading to mitotic arrest and cancer cell apoptosis. •Cannabinoids: Some cannabinoids, such as CBD (cannabidiol) and THC (tetrahydrocannabinol), have been reported to destabilize microtubules: •CBD disrupts tubulin polymerization, impairing mitotic spindle formation in glioblastoma and breast cancer models. •THC alters microtubule dynamics, potentially affecting cancer cell division. •Overlapping Effects: Cannabinoids may enhance the microtubule-disrupting effects of FenBen.

2\. p53 Activation & Apoptosis (Similar to Both IVM & FenBen) •FenBen & IVM: Upregulate p53, increasing apoptosis via BAX/BAK activation. •Cannabinoids: •CBD & THC upregulate p53, leading to mitochondrial dysfunction and apoptosis. •Activation of caspase-3 and caspase-9, triggering programmed cell death in multiple cancers. •Overlapping Effects: Cannabinoids can synergize with FenBen or IVM to enhance apoptosis in cancer cells.

3\. Metabolic Disruption & AMPK Activation (Similar to FenBen) •Fenbendazole: Blocks glucose metabolism in cancer cells, reversing the Warburg effect. •Cannabinoids: •CBD inhibits glucose uptake via downregulation of GLUT1 transporters. •AMPK activation by cannabinoids leads to mTOR inhibition, reducing cancer cell growth. •Overlapping Effects: Cannabinoids mimic FenBen’s metabolic disruption, making them potential synergistic agents.

4\. Anti-Inflammatory & Immune Modulation (Similar to Ivermectin) •Ivermectin: Modulates immune responses by shifting T-cell and cytokine activity, reducing cancer immune evasion. •Cannabinoids: •CBD reduces inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-α), potentially lowering tumor-promoting inflammation. •Enhancement of immune surveillance through interaction with CB2 receptors on immune cells. •Overlapping Effects: Cannabinoids may amplify IVM’s immune modulation in cancer therapy. Key Cannabinoids With Cancer-Treatment Potential 1.CBD (Cannabidiol): •Microtubule disruption •p53 activation → apoptosis •AMPK activation → metabolic inhibition •Anti-inflammatory effects (reduces IL-6, TNF-α) 2.THC (Tetrahydrocannabinol): •Microtubule destabilization •Apoptosis induction via CB1 receptor •Inhibition of angiogenesis in tumors 3.CBG (Cannabigerol): •Inhibits mitochondrial respiration in cancer cells •Synergizes with chemotherapy

4.THCV (Tetrahydrocannabivarin): •Reduces tumor cell proliferation •Modulates AMPK/mTOR pathway Potential Synergistic Treatment Approaches •CBD + Fenbendazole: Both disrupt microtubules and glucose metabolism. •CBD/THC + Ivermectin: Immune modulation + apoptosis enhancement. •Full-spectrum cannabinoids + Metabolic inhibitors: Combination therapy for aggressive cancers. Conclusion Cannabinoids share several anti-cancer properties with FenBen and Ivermectin, particularly microtubule inhibition, p53 activation, metabolic disruption, and immune modulation. This suggests they could be complementary in cancer treatment, though more research is needed to explore their combined effects.

5\. People in places on Earth are already doing this work and I have spent time meeting and talking with them.

# Thread: Please advise: heart fluid drain and wet cold feet.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-heart-fluid-drain-and-wet-cold-feet.30106/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kvutborg — 2025-01-13T22:04:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-advise-heart-fluid-drain-and-wet-cold-feet.30106/post-345140>

Fellow savages,

**i.** I learned that my father recently visited the hospital concerning his heart. I only know that he is in pain, near to having his heart fluid drained, and debating if he wants to consume prescription blood thinners. It has to be bad for him to actually go there, he generally stays away. I searched this forum, Kruse’s blogs, and elsewhere online and would very much appreciate guidance on two separate issues.

It seems to me, through my browsing, that his main issue is likely that one way or another his heart is experiencing inflammation. At a minimum, it seems that is how this started. I intend on attempting to persuade him to directly absorb sunlight, to plant his bare feet in the earth, and to direct him to the Leptin Rx. I also want him to learn about melanin and mitochondria.

In the last several weeks, I have noticed a massive difference in my blood circulation from being sandwiched by the sun and earth. I even accidentally experienced chi for the very first time! There was a minimal force pulling my palms toward each other, when I went to pull them apart after 25 minutes of direct sun and earth.

We can help him reduce inflammation, which should reduce the size of his heart (providing it is inflamed). I know there is more to this, though.

Is there a way to shrink the fluid without stabbing it?
If we reduce inflammation, should his pain lessen?
Will deuterium depleted water help his heart?

I remember reading or hearing about something that might be relevant a little while ago, but I have not found it again yet. Allegedly, introducing H2O2 to the body (topically, I think?) allows us to harvest extra oxygen. Do any of you know more about this and could it help him?

**ii.** I got excited to be in the cold (~0°c) yesterday at sunset and immediately played in water with my shins and feet. The idea was to increase conductivity between my body and the sand. I was there for at least an hour, only shorts/beanie/gloves, and now I am having symptoms of what google alleges to be trench foot.

It feels like my left lymph node is inflamed and there is some mucous clotting in the left side of my face (including nostril). I have had a migraine all day, and I have brain fog. My heart rate is elevated (96 bpm) and my forehead is warm. Pushing up on the left side of my forehead relieves pressure temporarily. As I am writing this, I am remembering that drinking hot water and maybe hot compresses might loosen it up. I will do that after posting this.

The sun did not peak through the clouds, today, so no sun for me. I assume the remedy is more earthing and a bunch of oranges (Vitamin C), is there anything else I should do?

I am planning to hop into the cold water tomorrow for at least 5 minutes. No sun, UV bone time! Would that help me or make this worse?

I live in a desert that is always dry and hot, so this windy cold environment is foreign to my body. I have not been taking ice baths or cold showers recently. I clearly shocked my body, but I am thinking it might need another shock.

Any guidance is immensely appreciated.

It is possible that I am not understanding the complicated language involved in this industry, but I definitely tried my best to find answers. Also, remember my brain is not functioning normally. Today, I am moving slow mentally and physically. Thank you for sharing your time with me.

P.S. Jack Kruse is an absolute legend for blowing the whistle as hard as he has. I will absolutely start spending money on his website when I climb out of my current financial position.

Edit:
i. Added increased heart rate and warm forehead for trench foot symptoms.

Really a guessing game without detailed info and bare in mind everyones n=1 is different in terms of the interaction with nnemf

This has been answered in so many Q&As that I think Jack is fed up of saying this

This is your definitive answer

As long as you are grounding on land at just before sunrise, and there are no cables , electricity or anything else below you and you're out there you don't have to see the actual sun. All you have the do is stay barefoot or both hands and feet on the ground for half hour past the sunrise time
Then eat your breakfast outside and then after that ground for as long as you want to. Then get on with your day.

It looks like we should at least be grounded while watching the sun rise for 30 minutes. After that, we can remain grounded as long as we want. I live in an apartment. I have a grounding mat from Clint Ober's website (he reintroduced the idea of grounding to the world). I watch the sunrise and ground from my balcony.

### Rusty — 2025-01-21T07:28:23+0200

You want to be outside until you see the transition from IR to UVA.

The benefits start a daybreak and continue from there. IF you only have a short time (10-15 mins) go with sunrise. The more you get the better off you are.

As the angle of sunlight gets over 15%, UVA shows up in the environment and changes the electronic induction in the eye and skin. When the sun gets over 30%, UVB light has arrived in most places on Earth. STRAIGHT FROM THE MITCHONDRIAL DIY THREAD ON THE FORUM.

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-05-06T08:55:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-sunrises.30109/post-352020>

> [nbr28 said:](/goto/post?id=345479)
>
> Oh! I didn't know we also had to be grounding. Its super cold outside right now, how long do you need to ground for?

On grounding, socks and shoes, one of the barefoot websites said they tested socks, just normal socks and natural fibre (wool) and found that if the socks were wet they lost 1% of conductivity, so if you go out in dry socks you lose 10%, so that still apparently a 90% grounding benefit and once wet they are pretty good so you may find that's enough and you don't need grounding shoes as such. that might just take the edge off the cold.
had to smile, I scythed the lawn last week early in the morning in bare feet and it was really cold and wet and i couldn't feel my feet at the end. I came up to the house and we have buckets of rain water by the back door, I dunked my feet in the water to get the grass off and water felt so lovely and warm compared to the wet grass.

I came up to the house and we have buckets of rain water by the back door, I dunked my feet in the water to get the grass off and water felt so lovely and warm compared to the wet grass.

Your feet will adapt to the cold over time. I barley feel it on my feet when we have frost on our lawn in winter likewise when I ramp up CT in winter.. All I feel is warmth in my feet as soon as they hit the grass. Keep at it and you will adapt to your environment.

# Thread: Hello Everyone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.30114/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jackson K — 2025-01-15T05:28:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.30114/post-345217>

**Hello everyone,**

I’m new here and wanted to share my journey and some of the struggles I’ve been dealing with. I’ve been a tech abuser for the past eight years, constantly in front of a screen—whether it’s my phone, computer, or TV. I played basketball and soccer in high school and have always been involved in athletics since I was young. These days, with college and my job, I’m on screens for 8+ hours a day, which I now realize has likely taken a toll on my health.

### **My Health Background**​

-   I’ve had **two varicocelectomies** that I deeply regret and wish I’d never done.
-   I had my **tonsils removed at age 5.**
-   This past June, I had a **lip laceration** and, unfortunately, got a tetanus shot despite my reservations. I haven’t taken any other vaccines since the ones I was “required” to get as a child. (No COVID vaccine for me—nice try, Fauci
-   I have **minor tinnitus** in my right ear from a gunshot.
-   I experience **floaters and BFEP** (Blue Field Entoptic Phenomenon).
-   I’ve had **three migraines**: two with aura and one without, all triggered by stress or excessive screentime.
-   **Poor posture** has always been an issue for me.

### **What Triggered It All**​

I’ve always struggled with stress and anxiety, but things got significantly worse last November. I suffered a panic attack at a dermatology appointment while getting moles removed. That night, I came down with a fever and chills, and I woke up with my heart racing, having another panic attack.

Since that day, I’ve had severe anxiety, and panic attacks because of the increase in visual symptoms I was experiencing. A week or two later, I had an ocular migraine, which made everything worse. Since then, I’ve experienced:

-   **Visual snow**: A mild graininess, especially in dim or dark lighting.
-   **Excessive BFEP** and **floaters**.
-   **Afterimages**, **starbursts**, **halos**, and **glare**, especially noticeable at night.
-   **Tinnitus** louder than ever before.
-   **Tension headaches** that come and go.
-   **Brain fog**, **low energy**, and **low libido**.
-   **Sore, tense eyes** and a **tense neck**.
-   **Heavy exercise** temporarily worsening symptoms.
-   ****Depersonalization****

I visited an ophthalmologist who confirmed that my vision is now 20/15 (improved from 20/20) and that my eyes are healthy. He suggested that my symptoms could be related to stress and anxiety, but there was nothing he could do aside from referring me to a neuro-ophthalmologist. After reading up on these issues, I realized that conventional doctors don’t have many solutions for this, so I decided to go the decentralized route.

### **Current Progress**​

Since then, my anxiety, stress, and symptoms have improved. My tinnitus, headaches, and even some of the visual disturbances have calmed down. I still deal with floaters, BFEP, glare, halos, starbursts, afterimages, and mild visual snow. I’ve learned that stress and anxiety make everything worse, so I’ve been focusing on keeping those in check.

Luckily, I live in a rural suburban area, so I’m not surrounded by excessive RF or EMF exposure. Here’s what I’ve been implementing so far:

-   **Cold Thermogenesis (CT).**
-   Religiously wearing **blue light-blocking glasses** when using screens or indoors.
-   Seeing the **sunrise** and grounding daily.
-   Using **red light therapy.**
-   Eating more **salmon** and other foods rich in DHA.
-   Limiting screentime and using **f.lux** on my devices.
-   Drinking a lot more water.
-   Doing **light exercise** and **neck stretches** to reduce tension.
-   Regulating and mitigating **nnEMF** in my bedroom and workspace.
-   Practicing **yoga** (I’ve always been into it and can even do the splits, but I’ve been doing it more consistently now).

### **Looking Ahead**​

I feel like I’ve torn my body down from years of stress, anxiety, screens, and EMFs. But since implementing these protocols inspired by Jack’s work, I already feel better. I know I still have a long way to go, but I’m optimistic about the progress I’ve made so far.

If anyone has suggestions or advice based on your own experiences, I’d love to hear them. Thanks for reading!

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-15T07:57:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.30114/post-345226>

Do less bad . . . Do more good . . . Recover.

How's your MEMORY?
I'm in the same boat but 5 years on. Personally I think it's hsv1.

### bennyhoffman — 2025-02-01T04:56:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.30114/post-346064>

Hey man, I saw your post and wanted to chime in. You mentioned having two varicocelectomies—what led you to that decision? Was it poor circulation to your extremities or varicose veins? I ask because I have a similar issue myself at 25 but haven’t resorted to surgery yet. I’m curious about what specifically drove you to go that route.

By the way, this is definitely the right place to post about health optimization. I genuinely believe [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) and this community are on the cutting edge of health and healing, so you’re ahead of the curve despite any setbacks.

I’m in a similar situation, though instead of tonsil removal, I had my wisdom teeth taken out. I’ve been wondering about the long-term consequences of that, though so far, I haven’t noticed anything significant. I understand that tonsils play a key role in immune function and brain detox. As for wisdom teeth removal, I’ve read that it’s almost like a neurological injury in the sense that the brain has to recalibrate after losing such important structural components (there’s probably a more scientific way to phrase this). It also plays a huge role in how we breathe and our overall jaw structure. Just another one of those Western medicine tricks used to screw over younger generations—right up there with vaccines, circumcision, blue-lit tech, EMFs, and so on.

Also, I’m 100% with you on the tetanus shot. I had a deep laceration on my shin about 3-4 months ago—super dirty, basically incubating in wet tile debris (prime tetanus environment)—so I ended up getting the tetanus vaccine. I went with the Td instead of the Tdap to avoid the pertussis component. If you check my post history, you’ll see how it messed me up for about two months. I’ve mostly recovered, aside from some lingering eye issues and energy problems, and that’s despite getting almost zero sun exposure all winter. No COVID shot for me either. I was briefly worried that traditional vaccines like the tetanus or flu shot might be contaminated with mRNA or something, but I got a SARS-CoV-2 antibody test and have zero antibodies to it—just an insane level of tetanus antibodies, way above the threshold for immunity.

Regarding tinnitus, I’ve had some Eustachian tube issues but no direct damage to the sensory hair cells, which they claim “can’t regenerate” (I have my doubts about that). I think melanin plays a role, just like it does in so many diseases—Jack would definitely be the best source for more on that.

As for the floaters and migraines, are those new? Did they start recently, maybe after the tetanus shot? Sorry to hear about the other symptoms—sounds neurological in nature. Maybe look into an Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) scan? Jack might recommend it. I considered getting one myself since it’s non-invasive and low risk, but I held off since my symptoms improved a bit.

Glad to hear your mental health is improving—that’s a solid sign. And good thing you’re not in Chernobyl (aka a major city). I’m just outside one (Pittsburgh), so not ideal, but at least I’m in a decent neighborhood with lower population density.

You’re on a good path, but I’m sure there are still tweaks you can make to your environment and lifestyle for better results. I saw [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) from EMFSafe already responded—you could try creating a sleep sanctuary or testing for EMFs in your house. That said, getting proper sun exposure should probably be a top priority, depending on where you live. Give it a year—limit blue light, ground daily, and see where you are. You’ll be surprised at the results.

You could also experiment with a ketogenic or carnivore diet. I’ve been on carnivore for eight months—lost 50 pounds and saw huge improvements in mental clarity and focus, especially when combined with sun exposure. More extreme options would be moving somewhere with better light stability and less nnEMF, like parts of Mexico or El Salvador. Even visiting these places during winter could be worthwhile. I haven’t done it yet, but I’m working on setting myself up financially to make that possible.

Anyway, keep us updated. I’m really curious about your progress—we’re all learning here, and the people in this community are super helpful and insightful.

### Jackson K — 2025-02-01T20:05:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.30114/post-346086>

>
> By the way, this is definitely the right place to post about health optimization. I genuinely believe [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) and this community are on the cutting edge of health and healing, so you’re ahead of the curve despite any setbacks.
>

I’ve been wondering about the long-term consequences of that, though so far, I haven’t noticed anything significant. I understand that tonsils play a key role in immune function and brain detox. As for wisdom teeth removal, I’ve read that it’s almost like a neurological injury in the sense that the brain has to recalibrate after losing such important structural components (there’s probably a more scientific way to phrase this). It also plays a huge role in how we breathe and our overall jaw structure. Just another one of those Western medicine tricks used to screw over younger generations—right up there with vaccines, circumcision, blue-lit tech, EMFs, and so on.
>
> Also, I’m 100% with you on the tetanus shot. I had a deep laceration on my shin about 3-4 months ago—super dirty, basically incubating in wet tile debris (prime tetanus environment)—so I ended up getting the tetanus vaccine. I went with the Td instead of the Tdap to avoid the pertussis component. If you check my post history, you’ll see how it messed me up for about two months. I’ve mostly recovered, aside from some lingering eye issues and energy problems, and that’s despite getting almost zero sun exposure all winter. No COVID shot for me either. I was briefly worried that traditional vaccines like the tetanus or flu shot might be contaminated with mRNA or something, but I got a SARS-CoV-2 antibody test and have zero antibodies to it—just an insane level of tetanus antibodies, way above the threshold for immunity.
>
> Regarding tinnitus, I’ve had some Eustachian tube issues but no direct damage to the sensory hair cells, which they claim “can’t regenerate” (I have my doubts about that). I think melanin plays a role, just like it does in so many diseases—Jack would definitely be the best source for more on that.
>
> As for the floaters and migraines, are those new? Did they start recently, maybe after the tetanus shot? Sorry to hear about the other symptoms—sounds neurological in nature. Maybe look into an Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) scan? Jack might recommend it. I considered getting one myself since it’s non-invasive and low risk, but I held off since my symptoms improved a bit.
>
> Glad to hear your mental health is improving—that’s a solid sign. And good thing you’re not in Chernobyl (aka a major city). I’m just outside one (Pittsburgh), so not ideal, but at least I’m in a decent neighborhood with lower population density.
>
> You’re on a good path, but I’m sure there are still tweaks you can make to your environment and lifestyle for better results. I saw [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) from EMFSafe already responded—you could try creating a sleep sanctuary or testing for EMFs in your house. That said, getting proper sun exposure should probably be a top priority, depending on where you live. Give it a year—limit blue light, ground daily, and see where you are. You’ll be surprised at the results.
>
> You could also experiment with a ketogenic or carnivore diet. I’ve been on carnivore for eight months—lost 50 pounds and saw huge improvements in mental clarity and focus, especially when combined with sun exposure. More extreme options would be moving somewhere with better light stability and less nnEMF, like parts of Mexico or El Salvador. Even visiting these places during winter could be worthwhile. I haven’t done it yet, but I’m working on setting myself up financially to make that possible.
>
> Anyway, keep us updated. I’m really curious about your progress—we’re all learning here, and the people in this community are super helpful and insightful.

I have a varicocele and varicose veins, which I believe are genetic since my cousins and other family members have them as well. I’m not sure which one you were referring to specifically. I had surgery on my varicocele after it was discovered during a physical, and shortly after, I started experiencing "pain" with it. Looking back, I believe it was mostly psychological, as I no longer have any pain now that my focus has shifted to my other symptoms.

The first surgery in 2019 was unsuccessful because the surgeon missed some veins, so I underwent a second procedure in 2020, which was successful. However, in 2024, the varicocele returned, though not as severely as before. My varicose veins in my leg aren’t too bad most of the time, but I have noticed that leg cramps occur more frequently, which suggests poorer circulation. I wouldn’t consider surgery for varicose veins unless circulation became a serious issue, but for now, any treatment would be more for aesthetic reasons. I’ve found that exercise, especially calf raises, helps with both circulation and appearance.

What you mentioned about tonsils is really interesting—I’ll have to look into that more. Regarding the tetanus vaccine, my family believes there’s no connection, but after getting it, I noticed a significant increase in anxiety and stress over the following months. That timing makes me believe there’s a link. I hadn’t received any vaccines in years before that, and it also coincided with me starting my work-from-home tech job, where I spend all day on screens. So, there are definitely multiple variables at play.

My tinnitus worsens when I’m sick or highly stressed, but it usually returns to baseline. I’ve always had slight tinnitus in my right ear from a gunshot when I was younger, but now I notice it in both ears, so I’m unsure of the cause now. I believe it’s a warning signal that my body needs a change. I also have some Eustachian tube issues—sometimes when I talk, my ears pop or crackle. My tinnitus also seems affected by my posture, as it gets louder when I clench my jaw or do chin tucks.

I’ve had floaters since I was about 15 or 16, but they were never this bad. I rarely noticed them unless I looked at the sky or bright white surfaces like screens or paper. They never really bothered me because I was told they were "normal." I rarely get migraines, and my last one before this recent episode was in 2021, which I believe was stress-induced. What made this recent migraine different is that it was an ocular migraine, and after that, I got new visual symptoms and my other symptoms worsened significantly. I had an OCT scan, which showed that my eyes are healthy. Because of that, I believe the migraine, combined with stress and anxiety, threw my brain off. While it may be somewhat neurological, I personally think my biggest issue is years of blue light exposure and mitochondrial dysfunction (low redox). I’m hoping to get bloodwork and other tests done to dig deeper and find more answers.

I live in central Pennsylvania, so I’m fortunate to be away from major EMF hotspots. However, I am a few miles from a small airport, which I suspect increases my EMF exposure. I’ve tested my home environment with my Trifield meter, Safe and Sound Classic III, and BVK meter to ensure my surroundings are as optimized as possible for now.

That’s awesome to hear about your success with the keto and carnivore diet! Is it expensive to maintain? I’ve been working on reducing inflammation through diet, eating more salmon and other fish, and cutting back on processed carbs and sugar. I believe my redox has been very low for years, and this situation has been the breaking point. I’d love to live closer to the equator, maybe in El Salvador for six months or more, to see if it helps. Mexico sounds like a good option too, but I’m not sure how welcoming they’d be to gringos after all the Trump deportations, lol.

For now, I’m implementing as many of Dr. Kruse’s protocols as possible. Hopefully, by next winter, I’ll be financially able to spend time in El Salvador to see if it makes a difference.

Thanks again for your insights and response—I really appreciate it, and I’ll keep you updated on my progress!

# Thread: Stetzerizer vs BVK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jackfoster — 2025-01-15T17:47:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/post-345238>

Can a BVK detect dirty electricity as well as a statsorizer can?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-15T19:03:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/post-345246>

> [jackfoster said:](/goto/post?id=345238)
>
> Can a BVK detect dirty electricity as well as a statsorizer can?

BVK is designed to measure body voltage NOT dirty electricity (DE), two different things. The Stetzerizer only measures in a narrow range of frequencies that their filters reduce. There could be a lot there that professional equipment would measure that the Stetzerizer is missing.

When you turn off a circuit you remove the electric field and 100% of the “DE”.

### jackfoster — 2025-01-15T22:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/post-345259>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=345246)
>
> BVK is designed to measure body voltage NOT dirty electricity (DE), two different things. The Stetzerizer only measures in a narrow range of frequencies that their filters reduce. There could be a lot there that professional equipment would measure that the Stetzerizer is missing.
>
> When you turn off a circuit you remove the electric field and 100% of the “DE”.

couldn't you detect d e spots with a BVK but you would just not be able to get a numbered measurement of it as you would with a stetzerizer?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-16T06:13:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stetzerizer-vs-bvk.30116/post-345269>

“DE” is an artifact/harmonic of the electric field and it is a tiny % of the nnEMF. The BVK measures the strength of the electric field being induced into your body. Maybe 1% - 2% of that is “DE”. The Stetzerizer measures the “DE“ (just the “DE”) on any circuit. Reduce the ”DE” and you have 98% of the nnEMF 60Hz (50Hz) electric field still in place. “DE” is part of the electric field which is measured separately from the magnetic field which is completely different than the RF.

People seam to be obsessed with “DE” . . . It is a mouse . . . The elephant in the room is the 50/60Hz AC electric field. Turn off the circuits, especially at night, and solve BOTH problems 100%.

# Thread: Nocturnal enuresis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nocturnal-enuresis.30118/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Caitlin (Metabolic Mastery) — 2025-01-15T22:57:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nocturnal-enuresis.30118/post-345261>

Hi Jack,

My 8 year old has always wet the bed and never had a dry night. We moved to the tropics when he was 3 and he was mouth breathing then, so we had tonsils and adenoids removed. I now know this was a mistake.
We have been doing all the right circadian things and eating animal based for 4 years however he’s still wetting the bed. I have seen a couple of airway dentists and they said visually his upper palate isn’t too narrow but would have to have imaging to see properly. I wanted to avoid this but will do X-rays if necessary. I just wanted to see if his palate would naturally expand and his nasal breathing improve as he gets older but I can still hear the turbulence through his nose at night so I know it’s narrowed. He was a breach baby and his cranium is misshapen and the osteo said he has one leg longer than the other so could it be coming from the spine ? They didn’t do any spine manipulation but the acupuncturist suggested this. Any suggestions would be appreciated
Thanks

### Mat.I — 2025-01-16T07:47:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nocturnal-enuresis.30118/post-345272>

If your son is not home schooled this could be a vasopressin issue from artificial blue light exposure at school? I have witnessed something similar.

# Thread: Dan C's Optimal Journal - Creating Art Outside, In the Sun, & Grounded
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-cs-optimal-journal-creating-art-outside-in-the-sun-grounded.30119/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dcetlin — 2025-01-16T04:11:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dan-cs-optimal-journal-creating-art-outside-in-the-sun-grounded.30119/post-345267>

Formal hello to this community

#### ​

#### **The past 6 months, I have been studiously lurking in the world of Dr. Kruse.**​

I have made a few posts here and there on the forum, been on a few Q&A's, but have largely kept to myself, an observer.

When I first heard Jack speak of the irreplaceable value of sunrise, I doubted my ability to change my sleep-wake rhythm. Yet now I find myself unable to miss a single sunrise. The transformation has been both subtle, and radical. I have always intuitively enjoyed spending my daylight outside. But now TINA and ALAN are the most fucking obvious axioms.

(Initially, I was writing this journal inside by the open window, but realized that made no sense. I'm now shirtless and feet in the grass at 3pm)

#### **My biggest takeaway these days: Embrace my time as my most important & precious resource!**​

Each moment presents a choice—conscious or unconscious: Whatever I find myself doing, wherever I find myself, I can persist, pivot, fully embrace the current path, or completely opt-out. When I act with aligned, inspired intention, I become the masterful architect of my finite time. Choice is inherent to my self-sovereign nature. Though I respect the past commitments I've made, and the potential future consequences of choices, I refuse to be bound by them, let alone the centralizing forces that seek to colonize my body, mind and reality. I want to make the most out of my time in this life. I want to feel that I am spending my time wisely, never wasted.

#### **Jack's quote on _ideation without execution_ was the distilled slap in the face I needed to hear.** ​

The decentralized wisdom is ultimately quite simple. Nature is god, so listen to her whispers. I will feel into what I want most in life, and take the steps to make it real. I will do so while cultivating an impeccable mind, recognizing that mind develops adaptively (or mal-adaptively) within its environment. I will also do so while building decentralized wealth.

I will build **financial wealth** through strategic reserves of cold-storage BTC, supported by income streams that respect the interconnected nature of health and wealth.

I will build **social wealth** via a tribe of high-trust brother and sister savages.

I will build **spiritual wealth** through net-maximizing my REDOX, increasingly living attuned to the cycles of nature, embracing myself as a solar powered being connecting to source, and minimizing bullshit energy & information from permeating my adaptive membrane.

I will build **emotional wealth** through embracing the suck, never lying to myself, rejecting the pull of lower vibrations, and striving to always be maximally compassionate towards myself and others (even if that means sharing or withholding hard truths from those I love, or cutting people out of my life).

Finally, I will build **intellectual wealth** through my incessant curiosity, desire to learn, seeking of the illuminated truth, and understanding of history.

#### **I appreciate Uncle Jack's rejection of _"balance"_ as an aspirational virtue.** ​

Having studied Ilya Prigogine, I see dynamic unfolding transformation through dissipative structures as a more compelling mental-model for how I construct my life. Instead of seeking a "balanced" life, I continually calibrate my clocks, fine-tuning the timing of my cycles, and lean hard into my edges. I am ready for life to test me. I will embrace leaps of faith, and learn to trust nature's wisdom buried deep within me. I will embrace the free-fall, the experience of reality as extemporaneous unfolding, and reject living in a pre-scripted simulation. Strategic ideation has its place, but at this point in my life, I need to orient more towards aligned action.

1\. ⬆doing - the investment of my time and energy into executing/acting-on my purest intentions & goals
2\. ⬆thinking - ideation / contemplation on past—via reflection—, and future—via visioning, planning, imagining, strategizing, dreaming, and praying.
3\. ⬆being - living into / embracing the spontaneous aliveness of life's unfolding

#### Lifestyle​

On one of the previous Q&As, I asked about nocturia. Jack quickly recommended I read [QE #23](https://www.patreon.com/posts/quantum-23-clock-76681761). Somehow, I always knew my condition of waking to pee in the night was circadian mismatch in nature, I just didn't have all the information to put it together. Even before I read the blog, I had a feeling the solution to my condition was to refine my clocks with a more impeccable relationship to sunlight, darkness, and technology. Now, I have a more intimate understandings of the mechanisms of vasopressin/ADH, the timing of water creation and retention, and cellular hydration, let alone nnEMF and Kruse 101. I was always curious why my bladder was strong during the day (infrequent urination), but hypersensitive at night. Now it's so fucking obvious. The crazy thing is that some aspect of this was always intuitive to me. Now it's a non-issue.

I am grateful for all the lessons I have been able to learn as a result of my tech abuse growing up. I am 26 today. My relationship has dramatically shifted. I am well aware of the environmental challenges as result of choosing to live in San Fransisco. But despite working a tech job on the computer, I spend 80% of daylight outside feet grounded in the park grass (no electric pollution via underground power-lines). I get as much skin in the game as I can. I cut out fluorinated water, currently Green Mountain Valley. My bedroom is dark. My wifi is off (though I live in an apartment and city where RF is unavoidable). I get my feet in the ocean regularly. I orient my vacations/travel around high UV and clean EMF.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

My current portable office. I'll connect via wired hotspot with long 12ft cabel, airtubes if I need them.

I am currently exploring the next 2-3 steps with regards to where to live, especially considering implications around starting a family in the next 2 years. Even at this point in my journey, given all of my environmental pollutants, I am living orders-of-magnitude more circadian in nature than I was in the past. It has been sufficient to dramaticly improve my sleep quality and efficiency. My mind thinks better, I don’t feel afternoon fatigue. I make better bottom-up choices more aligned with my wanting. I am much more intune with the messages from my body. Here's to living with greator and greator agency and proaction!

I’m also grateful how much more refined my bullshit-detector is. It helps me avoid wasting my time with clownshows (or Mockingbird assets).

#### Music​

I have been playing violin since the age of 3. I have played a lot of music outside, but always amplified with pedals plugged into the A/C power grid. Over the years, I have intuitively become increasingly attracted to accoustic violin. However, the wooden string instruments require indoor spaces to truly sound. Playing outside creates a dry sound given the lack of reverations. Nevertheless, I am finding ways to create music outside during the day, grounded and in the sun.

Even without my own backyard, the park works just fine as a portable recording studio with nice bird chirp ambiance. Feels amazing and sounds great! I just have to get lucky with the farting motorcycles!

At my heart I am an improvisor. This piece is a fully extemporaneous continuous take. I later produced the track, adding all sorts of layers including my voice, violin, synths, beats, bass, and the debut of my cello playing, much of which was also done outside!

I have developed myself as a multi-disciplinary artist (and human). I have studied many artists artists and genres over the years, incorperating all kinds of vocabulary into my personal style.

#### Gratitude To This Community​

Special shoutouts to Rusty, Anthony Smith. I've learned a lot from your posts! And of course Jack!

### dcetlin — 2025-02-26T06:00:29+0200

# Thread: SAAT - exceptional band aid for those suffering from food or seasonal allergies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gian — 2025-01-16T23:00:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/post-345299>

I just wanted to share this unique acupuncture in the ear treatment with the community as I presume many members on here also have or have had issues with food allergies and seasonal allergies. The procedure is abbreviated as SAAT and it has been around for a many decades but seems relatively unknown to most of us.

About a year ago (well before I discovered Dr. Kruse) I was struggling with my body reacting to most foods (lectins, all forms of sugars, gluten etc.) and through internet searching I read about a procedure that had proven effective for helping folks that were allergic to meat (alpha gal). I had never done any acupuncture before, nor did I believe in acupuncture as a modality, but I was desperate so I figured for a $200 procedure I was willing to roll the dice. The procedure involved muscle testing (which was also new to me) to determine what I was allergic to, then tiny needles were implanted in my ear at specific locations, covered by a small band aid and left there for three weeks. During the three weeks the needles were in my ear, I was told to avoid the foods that we were treating for (in my case I think it was gluten, sugar, sesame, wheat, lectins). After three weeks, my wife removed the small needles with tweezers and I began experimenting with foods. I was shocked with the results. Prior to the SAAT treatment, my stomach was so hypersensitive, and after the treatment, I was regularly having excellent BMs even while eating foods that I was allergic to prior to the procedure. I drank a craft beer for the first time in seven years (I wasn't even sure if I would still like the taste) and did fine.

I will not pretend like I know how it worked, but it sure did for me. The acupuncturist said for some patients the results last for a short while, while others it last for several years and some for a lifetime. For me, it was about a year and I started having issues again and was worse than ever with my system becoming intolerant of everything. I even became intolerant of bone broth (I was told it was the histamine in the bone broth). I went back and did another round of the SAAT, but this time, because of how angry my system was, he only put needles in for mast cell, and this round the needles stayed in for four weeks. Thanks be to God, the SAAT treatment was very successful and I went from the worst my stomach/BMs had ever been, to once again regularly having the coveted Bristol #4s Uncle Jack prides himself on.

The needles go in the right ear and the acupuncturist said that is because that spot in the right ear is associated with the liver. Although I don't believe this treatment is curative and does not likely address the underlying issue(s) with one's health problems, it vastly improved my situation and my quality of life. So, if you have bad seasonal allergies or food allergies, you may want to give the SAAT treatment a try and hopefully it can help you find some relief.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-17T01:25:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/post-345304>

I remember watching an open heart surgery years ago, the patient’s chest was open but he was awake and talking to the Dr.’s. There were no drugs used just a few needles. Changed my mind about acupuncture and at least considering other ”alternative” healing modalities.

### MikeR — 2025-01-18T21:10:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saat-exceptional-band-aid-for-those-suffering-from-food-or-seasonal-allergies.30120/post-345365>

It works for knee pain and the tiniest bit for my sense of smell. Really quite amazing. I stopped with the sense of smell treatment for now because the jaw needles were pretty uncomfortable and I wanted to concentrate on the knee more.

It works for knee pain and the tiniest bit for my sense of smell. Really quite amazing. ### Jon Hinman MD DVM — 2025-01-18T23:44:59+0200

### mitopower — 2025-01-19T18:57:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-results.30132/post-345398>

Hello, can anyone help me interpret these results?
Thank you,
Aaron (mitopower)

### Mat.I — 2025-01-24T07:54:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-results.30132/post-345683>

Disclaimer: I'm not a MD.. TSH is slightly elevated based on standard ref ranges ( & higher based on Jack's ranges).. Others appear within the standard ref ranges. Based on a decentralised approach - I would focus on fixing the light environment (especially ALAN) as disrupted melatonin signalling could elevate TSH as the pituitary gland compensates for this. nn-emf can also impair the function. Recommendations:

1\. Fix the light environment,
2\. Limit nn-EMF,
3\. Get all your clocks set via supporting a good circadian rhythm,
4\. Watch the sunrises, plenty of sun throughout the day (stay dark at night),
5\. Eat plenty of seafood
6\. grounding outside everyday

### Laudy Cincotta — 2025-01-24T17:47:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-panel-results.30132/post-345691>

> [mitopower said:](/goto/post?id=345398)
>
> Hello, can anyone help me interpret these results?
> Thank you,
> Aaron (mitopower)

Hey [@mitopower](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/37567/),

I am no expert or doctor however that screenshot is nowhere near enough for anyone to help you with.

Could you please share a screenshot including ranges to be able to put your results into perspective.

Also the following questions.

1\. Do you/have you had thyroid issues in the past?
2\. Why the lab test and wanting to interpret results? Are you hypo or hyper thyroid?
3\. Are you on any thyroid medication?
4\. Do you have other labs to compliment this? For example iron levels, Vitamin D, Vitamin B12, Zinc, Magnesium, Potassium, Sodium, Cortisol, Aldosterone, Renin??????
5\. Do you have liver tests and inflammation markers that could be driving things?

Depending on why you had the testing and your symptoms then alot of these other tests may be relevant.

Just looking at the screenshot without reference ranges it looks to me like both your free T3 and free T4 are low and your reverse T3 looks too high.

Your best to see if you could get a consult with Jack with more testing info so he can see a clearer picture.

Good luck.

It seems without any other info that there may be a conversion issue and instead of becoming useful your T3 is becoming reverse T3 indicating a move towards more hypo thyroid type symptoms.

I only know that becasue i am trying to work out my own thyroid issues and i may be incorrect as i am not a doctor however thats what i have understood so far.

# Thread: Subcellular melatonin and glutamate MDD connection
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subcellular-melatonin-and-glutamate-mdd-connection.30135/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emmanuela — 2025-01-20T03:46:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subcellular-melatonin-and-glutamate-mdd-connection.30135/post-345429>

Glutamate in the brain plays a big role in depression
And from the research I was reading, glutamate release increases in darkness and is suppressed by light.

Subcellular melatonin requires IR light and when UVA enters the eye, we make serotonin which then turns into melatonin.

Artificial blue light significantly reduces production of melatonin, seratonin and dopamine. And we can’t make subcellular melatonin either in these conditions.

So having dark dark nights increases glutamate more than light polluted nights and then in daytime, sunlight increases seratonin, dopamine, and melatonin.
Therefore improving mental health

More experienced Krusers -
am I understanding this correctly?

### Mat.I — 2025-01-20T05:27:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subcellular-melatonin-and-glutamate-mdd-connection.30135/post-345435>

Not my field of expertise, however, my understanding is generally as per the description above. **However, in relation to "dark dark nights increasing glutamate more than light polluted nights"** \- my thoughts are maybe this just prevents dysregulations that lead to ecotoxicity and melatonin is needed to protect neurons / mitochondria from ox damage and as I understand it also plays a role in regulating the glutamate ecotoxicity. Therefore ALAN would have a multilayer impact e.g. disrupted clocks > in balance between inhibitory (GABA) & excitatory (glutamate) neurotransmission > higher G levels > more excitatory activity in the brain or can go the other way. Others - poor sleep quality & reduced slow wave sleep, HPA axis & cortisol >sympathetic overdrive > accumulation of glutamate & increased release / excitotoxity etc..

### Emmanuela — 2025-01-20T05:38:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subcellular-melatonin-and-glutamate-mdd-connection.30135/post-345437>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=345435)
>
> Not my field of expertise, however, my understanding is generally as per the description above. **However, in relation to "dark dark nights increasing glutamate more than light polluted nights"** \- my thoughts are maybe this just prevents dysregulations that lead to ecotoxicity and melatonin is needed to protect neurons / mitochondria from ox damage and as I understand it also plays a role in regulating the glutamate ecotoxicity. Therefore ALAN would have a multilayer impact e.g. disrupted clocks > in balance between inhibitory (GABA) & excitatory (glutamate) neurotransmission > higher G levels > more excitatory activity in the brain or can go the other way. Others - poor sleep quality & reduced slow wave sleep, HPA axis & cortisol >sympathetic overdrive > accumulation of glutamate & increased release / excitotoxity etc..

I didn’t think about the eco toxicity and glutamate.
I just finished watching the mental illness/phsychadelicbsubstances webinar where Uncle Jack talks about how those with major depression have low glutamate and ketamine floods those parts of the brain with glutamate.
Then i was reading a research paper about how glutamate is released in darkness and inhibited by light and i thought dang.

Such cool stuff

### Mat.I — 2025-01-20T10:40:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subcellular-melatonin-and-glutamate-mdd-connection.30135/post-345450>

I need to get through all of the blogs and webinars.. Ketamine is a NMDA receptor antagonist. I assume the dosage would have to be carefully controlled to avoid excitotoxicity.

# Thread: Heads up regarding meeting members face to face
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heads-up-regarding-meeting-members-face-to-face.30136/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Magpie — 2025-01-20T07:18:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heads-up-regarding-meeting-members-face-to-face.30136/post-345438>

I have just had to file a complaint about a member of this site to the police in the local area of the. country in which this person resides. I am not going to name names as I believe that the psychopathic behaviour of this individual are the result of mental illness. So I do not want to identify the person involved. I have made a habit of not posting on the public forum because as an Australian doctor I felt that this put me at risk of professional difficulties and I am very cautious in my interactions with the Australian Government. I only started posting here after leaving the country and I am in the process of applying for residency in another country. Thanks to Jack's advice I have no final financial need to continue working although I intend to work in both of my professional careers just because I like helping people and I like entertaining people.
In the past I would message members directly if they were asking a question which I had some insight into. I have been following Jack since 2o11 because I remember that time that he was kicked off the cruise ship where he was supposed to be keynote speaker. And with my scaffolding of a medical degree and many years of clinical work, I think that I retain and remember a significant proportion of what he has been teaching via podcasts. I joined Patron once that was available and finally joined as a platinum member in 2019 I believe. Luke Storey said something that made it OK for me to spend the money on my membership. He said that buying all this biohacking gear is his hobby and so he justifies buying all the gear he has. Mind you I only started listening to Luke Storey's podcast because of his interview with Jack. Yeah I'm that much of a nerd. Most of the podcasts I listen to I only discovered because they interviewed Dr Jack Kruse.

I wish to ask all members to be very cautious when interacting with other members both on line and in real life. I have met several of my closest friends through this forum. But my last experience with meeting a member has resulted in me making a report to the police in Yorkshire in the UK. A member whom I befriended perhaps 12 months ago turned out to be floridly psychotic when I met them face to face. Most initial interaction was via text messaging initially. I get tired of texting because it is a slow and artificial form of communication without intonation from an audible voice so I also started phoning this person via Signal. Eventually I suggested that this person may benefit from spending some time in El Salvador and invited them to stay in my home there. I initially did the face to face meeting in a public place which raised no red flags. But visiting two of the homes of this person demonstrated someone with a hoarding habit of phenomenal danger. I say this because my step dad was a fire officer and taught me well that you want to minimise fire hazards in your home and you also do not want to lock yourself up to tightly. The risk of dying of carbon monoxide poisoning in a fire is worse when the fires cannot break into the building to save you. There were rooms in the house full of cardboard collected from years of online shopping. The corridors were lined with shopping bags full of whatever. But we have OCD or hoarding on a grand scale.

I was asked to clean the kitchen and then was derided because I didn't put everything away where it belonged. But I had no supervision to assist me.
I also was driven to another home of this individual which became a five hour drive in the middle of the night because this person is addicted to gaming and stayed up all night instead of going to bed early like a normal mitochondrial.
I ended up being advised by friends to not even get on the flight that would take us back to El Salvador. And I have not stayed in the airport hotel as planned the night before my flight home. Because I am postponing my flight with KLM. There is no way that I would be present anywhere where I am expected or invited to be in order to maintain my safety .

Deaher Rohen

Please cease and desist. Below is the screen shot of my report of your absolutely bizarre and pathological behaviour which I have discussed with thegiv local police. They are very aware of who you are and apparently your behaviour towards me was consistent with previous contact that you have had with the police. So please stop bothering me. Please stop slandering me. Or I will also be forced to take legal action against you. At present I can accept that your behaviour is consistent with someone who is unwell. Please contact each person that you have bothered with your bogus reports of myself and apologist to them profusely for you inappropriate behaviour.

I will next be contacting the mental health services to request that you are given a form of welfare check. Your obsessive behaviour is costing me a lot of money as I still have not been able to interact with KLM to post pone my flight. But I am not going to go anywhere where you might anticipate that I may go. My next move will be completely unpredictable. Because you cannot be trusted. Posting Jack's address on a public forum was sheer lunacy and I am also not impressed by you sending Laudy a photo of her home. No one is going to put up with your psychopathy. No one. Take yourself to hospital and perhaps there will be some kind of resolution to your insanity. You are fully cooked in the head mate and I am happy to phone psych services and request that they interact with you. You may be able to talk the talk and walk the walk but I know how to do it even better than you do. Because I am a GP I look after my patients holistically. You trained as a psychiatrist which can be pretty good. But a GP trumps you in the General Knowledge stakes when it comes to human nature and deciding who is real and who is fake. Sorry but I have been taught by patients from every economic strata. I have patients who are homeless on government benefits and I have patients who live in mansions with pontoons in the back yard so that they can moor their yachts at home and they also own a farm up the Sunshine Coast and run dairy cattle for fun. I look after people from cradle to grave. I look after patients who solely access specialists via the private health care system because they can afford private health insurance and are happy to spend up big on their health. I have patients who cannot even afford to buy their government subsidised medications. I have patients that can barely speak English and patients that hold a PhD in economics and hold university posts. There is very little that you can do to out manoeuvre me Rohen.

Give up have yourself admitted to hospital until you can recognise how psychotic your behaviour is. You are not going to win any game against me. Not now not ever not in the UK not in any other nation on earth. Because a GP wins in the game of who knows more about human nature than anyone. And I do pretty well in the understanding humanity and how to function as a decent human being because I listen to my patients, I try my very best to care for them, I respect them and my goal is to keep them all out of hospital. My work is a function of my compassion. And I did not go into medicine to make money. I went into medicine so that I can express my compassion in my day to day work. I am happy to do Pap smears all day if that is going to provide early identification of cervical tissue change and prevent a woman dying of cervical cancer. I will spend all day doing breast exams to identify cases of breast cancer and give that woman her best chance of beating it.

I love doing regenerative joint injections and doing prolotherapy and trigger point therapy because it can reduce someones pain and may sometimes mean that a person can avoid joint arthroplasty. I can also help with joint pain when someone is unable to have arthroplasty because of the anaesthetic risk.

I love doing antenatal care because I can provide pregnant women with the best advice on how to have a healthy and happy baby. And I love supporting women with breastfeeding because I am helping to feed their babies with an optimal diet.

I don't really love cardiology because I find aspects of it hard especially the pharmacology but all of my over 75 patients have a full medical every year to make sure that they haven't developed a valve murmur and signs of heart failure.

These are some simple examples of what I love about my job as a GP. I may complain about the building I used to work in because it was a really poorly designed building. But I miss my patients and they miss me. One of them messaged me via LinkedIn for gods sake to say thank you for helping him and his wife and her dad loose weight. The whole family are my patients from her parents, this lovely young couple and their two children. I regret needing to leave because I had to stop being a GP to people like them. But of course one has to look after ones self first. Which is why I have reported your behaviour to the police. I do not know if they will act on it but they were interested enough to make a lot of notes about everything that happened in Maltby. So have fun trying to slander me. I am very much respected by my patients. I have a lot of friends who will inform the authorities that you speak a whole lot of lies.

I had a great time talking to my godmother but do you think it is appropriate to be phoning an 80 yo lady to frighten her with lies about her goddaughter?
I tried to ring my sister Bronwyn multiple times today. It is unlikely that you spoke to her as she has not answered any of my calls and she has not messaged me either. And she would do if she had any concerns. Fortunately my other sister detests social media and does not even have any accounts so she is immune from your insanity.

I wonder why you didn't phone my mother. Probably because you cannot figure out who she is amongst the many "Facebook friends" that I have. She is 82 and would not be impressed by your behaviour. But you may feed her paranoia around me which would be a very very cruel thing to do.

Take yourself off to casualty and request a psych assessment. Because you are not going to win with me. Nobody can manipulate me. Nobody. If you have managed to reach Ivan and piss him off you will not win. Because my motivations for living and acting are pure and caring and compassionate. You have insulted me so much in the past few days that I was crying with frustration that someone would want to stop me from doing good in the world.

I had a long chat with Tristan today. I told him to speak with my friends like Maria-Teresa and Laudy and Jeremy Franco and decide for himself whether he can trust me and come and work for me. We will ask Eduardo to prepare an employment contract for Tristan which will include a profit sharing option so that his parents will be happy that he has an ethical boss. Because all of my business interests are about generating income so that I can employ people and improve their financial circumstances. I do not need any more money beyond what I already have. I only wish to generate more income so that I can help other people improve their lives.

I do not think that you understand who I am. I do not think that you have the capacity to understand who I am. So I am not even going to try and explain myself any more. I am going to live my life and fulfil my potential and you can watch me from wherever you chose to be. I have given you enough free advice to improve your health both physical and mental to a significant degree. Whether you choose to implement my suggestions is out of my control. But you do not win any game you play with me. Because I am a free spirit. No one can control me. They either attract me enough that I hang around or I come and I go as I please. And if I don't like someone I avoid them. If someone finds me annoying I avoid them. So you have explicitly told me that I am annoying and that I have exhausted you you qualify for my lifetime one time only total avoidance package. It's a special I created just for you because I loathe annoying and exhausting people so I very kindly decided that you never have to see me again. I do not understand why you demanded to know my location and then proceeded to threaten to contact Ivan and say slanderous things about me. That is completely evil to attempt to remove the one person from my life that makes me happy. So no I am never going to tell you where I am. I am never going to contact you again because you don't like me and you have no need for my services.

I suggest that you contact psych services and cease and desist from your slander and harassment. I am staying in a 5 star hotel with a very high level of security. Management has been informed of your psychopathy and no one will be allowed near me as I do not anticipate any visitors.
