Please ignore Magpie as she is currently hypomanic, off her lamotrigine and bipolar having been diagnosed some decades before.

I have spoken to her Psychiatrist bDr Mitali Bhuyan in Brisbane today and also her sister

They both agree she needs treatment in a mental health facility

She has been unreliable and unreachable

She even took a video of a sexual partner and showed it to me

You should ignore anything above from her.

She last had a manic breakdown a decade ago. and this is her medication which she has not been taking

As you can see it was prescribed by the psychiatrist I just spoke to from Brisbane.

### Magpie — 2025-01-20T07:37:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heads-up-regarding-meeting-members-face-to-face.30136/post-345441>

Sorry typos. Stay as safe as you can. Despite taking significant precautions I have found myself in a difficult place. I am staying in a 5 star hotel with good security. Because I have had several famous friends I am aware of how hotel staff look after celebrities. I am not saying that I am a celebrity but staying in a good hotel provides me with a level of protection that I cannot achieve in the community without spending a lot of money on security staff.

This individual continues to stalk and harass me and hyzas contacted multiple family members including my 80 god mother. He caused her to be very worried about me and I had to phone her late at night her time to reassure that I am safe and well. She has known me since I was an ovum literally. I was her flower girl at her wedding when I was two and a half years old. My godmother regrets that she never had a daughter as she has three lovely sons. But I reminded her that she does have a daughter because I am her god daughter.

Please stay safe tribe. But I suspect that we need to evict someone from the tribe. And many of you know me. I have been in communication with Jeremy Franco on a daily basis because I am concerned about the predatory nature of this psychopathic individual. I am sorry but it is not cool to post a map showing people where Jack's house is. Not cool at all.

When Jack lived at Atami, Jeremy Franco would answer the door to strangers demanding to see Dr Jack Kruse. And Jeremy would have to turn them away because he did not think that it was appropriate to let strangers into Jack's house while the women of the house were spending time in the sun to minimise their risk of breast cancer.

Jack should b allowed his privacy. His work has made him a public figure. But there are some people who have unrealistic expectations of having close friendships with Jack. Personally by the time I met Jack at the Giving thanks event I had done everything that he suggested and had very little to ask him because I listen to his suggestions on how to optimise your health and finances and then act on his suggestions. I did not need to talk to him on the webinars begging for some other way to improve my health aside from his sensible recommendations. Maybe I am unusual. But it is possible to learn from someone without demanding their attention. As a platinum member I also am able to discuss certain things with Jack and he always responds to my questions. At the end of the day, if I am confused about a mechanism regarding light or radiation or whatever, If I take the time to think about it the answer will usual come to me without bothering Jack.

I think that it is unfortunate that he has stalkers. And now I have a stalker because for some reason this person thinks that I should marry them to provide a cover for their sexual preferences. This person thinks that I should provide them with an income because their government will cut off payment if they go abroad for too long. This person thinks that eating toast and Cadbury creme eggs and jam tarts and muffins is a suitable way to eat for a mitochondrial. And I do not want them invading my home and turning it into a hoarders paradise with their OCD collection of shit in shopping bags.

Stay safe tribe members because I am not quite there yet. Time to ring the psychiatric services to have this pain in the ASS admitted for a psychiatric assessment. I cannot be gaslit because I am not vulnerable to this shit. It is pissing me off.

### Magpie — 2025-01-20T07:38:43+0200

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-01-20T08:09:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heads-up-regarding-meeting-members-face-to-face.30136/post-345444>

> [Magpie said:](/goto/post?id=345442)
>
> Right there you go documented slander. I am going to speak to a lawyer this morning. I have had it with you.
> Audrey warned me that you are a 'fruitloop" I misunderstood her and thought that she was talking about your sexuality,.,

That's funny because I am merely carrying out your friends and families wishes
And I quote
"
Hopefully all will go well. Margaret (Sumati) needs to rest in order to avoid a ‘breakdown’. Sorry but I am not FULLY aware as to what your condition is and why treatment is not available in England but hopefully you will find suitable treatment.

Give Margaret our best regards and please inform us if she is ‘overloaded’ and risks becoming very stressed. Probably, you are aware of her condition which has existed for a very long time….prior to her recent trip with her mother. Margaret has not been taking her prescribed medication and this sadly might cause a possible mental breakdown especially if she is having a hectic time.

With respect, given that you are a ‘doctor’ you should be in a position to understand.

Regards
Mary Sill

Please note that I, as a very close friend, and person who attended to her occasional ‘mental breakdowns’ needs due to Bipolar, is now VERY concerned about her wellbeing…. I do not fully understand why she has chosen to go to El Salvador, a third world country until recently????? and purports it to be a country of Medical Advancement…… of course, you being a Psychiatrist would know that behaviour is often prevalent amongst sufferers of Bipolar…she exhibits such behaviour if she does not take her medicines.

Margaret needs help and it will be appreciated if you could kindly….may be within your professional capacity, seek appropriate treatment for her that will possibly prevent physical and even financial problems.

Sending a big thank you to you and hope that you will assist my dear God daughter, Margaret. I presume you are still in England

Thanks. Sorry for the disturbance. It must be very early morning in England. Please continue to inform me. Her safety and well-being means a lot to me.

I am a little intrigued as to why you are dependant on her to accompany you to El Salvador???? Very kind???

My reply

I'm not. She suggested it and I said I don't need you to .I was slightly wary of why. It has become clearly apparent that she is not in her full right mind. For example I would not fly across the Atlantic to meet someone and then insist that I be on the same flights.

I asked her why and she said it'll be good for you . I didn't expect grandiosity
She's currently taking a shower , I thought she'd do this at Midnight but no...I've observed her behaviour and that's how I came to my initial diagnosis.

She thought I needed help. I needed no help. In fact I prefer to travel alone because then you're not beholden to anyone

I'm up late because I was checking final documents ordering a little foreign currency (us dollars)and making sure my international driving permit was fine for El Salvador and Panama

I have friends in Panama from staying there last Winter. Mostly expats
Mary's response

Oh…sleep well. May be encourage Margaret to seek professional help to ensure her safety?????
Mary's reply
Yes. She has mentioned the Chef to us….. very concerning! She will end up very poor if she continues to spend her money indiscriminately….Difficult to know what to do…. She is an 56 year old woman not a child so???? Very difficult

Further reply from her auntie who she was a bridesmaid for

Doubt whether Margaret will take it…. Please be tactful…. Her mother and sisters are very concerned and would prefer her to seek medical treatment in England rather than El Salvador…. We trust that our faith in your sincerity is right

Sorry if I am bothering you but could you please convey to me whether Margaret (Sumati) is OK….. please I urge you to seek medical attention for her whilst she is in Englandl if she needs it. Hopefully this will not impinge on your well-being as she had mentioned that you needed some treatment……Thanks

From me
No she's not. She lost insight into her condition
She's been very inappropriate. I can't help her because I get exhausted. I retired early because of my constant persistent exhaustion. The sunshine will help renew me but if I have to run around her it won't really relax me.
Her things wandered into virtually every room. She was grandiose yet was not able to back it up.

From Mary

OMG. ….that’s how she behaves when she is in a ‘manic mood’. Unfortunately you would be better off if you could please refer her to a psychiatrist who might be able to diagnose her condition and certify her….I realise this might be difficult!

With regard to her ‘things’ being strewn throughout your house….well that is exactly what she did to her mum’s house.

Sorry but the best you can do is either get her in a hospital or simply keep away from her in order to protect your well-being. Unfortunately you might need to safely get rid of her via legal means….. e.g police who might hospitalise her…. sad but may be she will be in safe hands.

I of course, cannot demand that you obey me but for your own ‘good’ please distance yourself from her, possibly in a safe manner for you and her. Good Luck

From me

I'm.so glad that you are in agreement.

I confronted her with the photo of her medication and also mitali bhuyans photo

She left with all her things

I decided that because she had worn me out, and exhausted me that I would take a sleeping tablet and rest. I've not been able to the last 6-7 days because she has either kept me waiting or asked me to do too much for my own capabilities

I have changed my flight , I have no intention of staying with her now. Of course I will visit her new house when I am settled and properly set up

I'm arriving much later than originally scheduled and I am not telling anyone except my father when I am arriving.

I think it is better that I get there get settled and then go and see if she made it, to see if she has calmed down..

The other option is that I have her sectioned at the airport.

I dont want to do that but it seems that you as her godmother are right and that she needs help in a western first world country

So I must phone the police and get this organised

I could do this now.
She said she would be on the same flight so I may actually do this now so that if she by any chance changed her flight to this morning instead of Monday morning,well then we can stop her

I hate doing this,please understand that I wouldn't wish a sectioning to hospital on anyone.

It's a horrible procedure and must only be carried out at the point where nothing else can be done

I used to section people but I would always balance and give them a chance to explain themselves beforehand

Once I was called to a police station and there was this mute early 20s or very late teen guy there

He had broken a shop window and was refusing to talk or do anything

I said to him. If you talk to me. I can possibly help you.

If you don't I will have no other option than to section you to hospital for a four week period

I spent about an hour trying to cajole answer out of him

Alas to no avail
Thanks. I’ve sent a message again to her and no answer as yet. Hopefully she is OK????
I signed that order with a heavy heart

I still remember it vividly, but I don't remember the others because o felt I hurt him

You see ?
From Mary Sill

Thanks. I’ve sent a message again to her and no answer as yet. Hopefully she is OK????

She sent me a long email. I spoke to her but she explained she didn't want me to know where she was and that was that

She appears to be okay but I think she realises that she can't pull the wool over my eyes much longer so she's now blaming me. I think it's her habit that if someone realises she's unwell she clears off and vanishes because she knows her behaviour is going to get her sectioned/committed to hospital. That's very sad

From Mary Sill

Oh....you've done your part to express care for her so please take care now of yourself and thank you....Hopefully she will somehow be OK and not get in deep trouble....

As you can see her 80 year old friend was more concerned about Margaret's state
Magpie is Sumati Margaret Mary Pestana

This is from her sister
Bronwyn

Hi Rohan, Sumati’s sister here I believe she’s been staying with you in Maltby? We spoke briefly on the phone when she rang me.
My Aunty Mary txt me with some disturbing news. Can I ask is everything ok? If you’re concerned about Sumati’s mental health is there a mental health service where you can call someone out to assess her?

Hi Rohan, sorry time got away from me I’m still at the party so I think it’s now 10 am there abouts where you are I get that Sumati has left yours so I’ll try anc contact her directly and I’ll let you know. I really thank you for your care and concern for my very sick sister

End messages

The latest messenger phone call I said to Bronwyn.

Are you sure you want her sectioned here under the mental health act

She said yes. If she causes any trouble for you I'll back you up

Now it should be clear that Magpie is unwell and thanks to her I have had to spend another $1200 to rearrange my flights

Because I felt I had to and I did them on Saturday before I slept

I'm most disappointed in a platinum member's conduct and I felt I must respond to her outrageous claims

Not only has she outed me on this forum which is despicable

But she has lied.

I find both disappointing and concerning

Rohen

### Kathy Spears — 2025-01-20T17:16:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heads-up-regarding-meeting-members-face-to-face.30136/post-345465>

I’ve just gotten back on the forum after months of being away due to selling all my worldly belongings and moving to Playa Del Carmen, Mexico. I have settled in and came here to catch up on everything in the mito world.

Wow, what a shock to read this whole thread! I wonder how things escalated to the point where airing all this on the forum was necessary. I don’t know Margaret but have known Rohen for a while. He can be a little dramatic at times especially regarding his need for gluten free surroundings, but to say he’s dangerous is a little shocking to me. He is a gay man so anyone reading this worrying that there may be a concern along the lines of sexual predator should know that.

I really hope both parties can calm down and agree to go their separate ways without further threats. If one or both parties require intervention I would think family members would step in and help rather than strangers.

This forum is a great place to learn and help one another. I’m sad that Margaret felt so threatened that she came here for support and to warn others. Honestly, I have to wonder if those concerns were overblown or imagined. We weren’t there so it’s impossible to know.

I’m sorry for butting into some else’s battle but I wanted to try to interrupt this downward spiral.

In life we have to be careful who we trust and how we conduct ourselves when forging new friendships. This forum and tribe is no different.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heads-up-regarding-meeting-members-face-to-face.30136/post-345470>

You two are an embarrassment right now.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-vitamin-d.30138/post-345451>

So I don't supplement. I get in the sun and my d was 188 , I'm sick ,brain sick from I'm sure what is hsv1 ,dementia like each time I get an attack ,and spiraling negatively with memory loss etc as time goes on with consequent attacks.
This latest has been by far the most severe so far . Ear eye forehead scalp pain , small blisters in these areas , blurry vision ,clear spot in centre of vision on affected eye jaw and teeth pain ,one sided,with neck pain , major one sided headaches like brain is split in two. No memory, wanting to pass out ,agitated ,angry.
I'm sure this is viral related triggering back into my cns.
My next vit d was 202.
Is any of this possibly related to high d ?

### lilapup — 2025-01-21T00:52:13+0200

> I'm sure this is viral related triggering back into my cns.
> My next vit d was 202.

Perhaps I've clouded my post with 2 topics due to my current confusion.

Any idea why I have high vitamin D as I don't supplement

### Mat.I — 2025-01-21T03:29:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-vitamin-d.30138/post-345501>

Your Vit D is not high (assuming you are referencing SI Units) i.e. nmol/L the standard reference ranges are the issue.. Optimal Vit D levels would be ~ Say 70 to 120 ng/ml or 175 to 300nmol/L. Higher range in summer (or year round if jabbed) and lower range in winter etc.. for reference I'm able to maintain no less than 200nmol/L in the dead of winter (without supplementation). Get your environment tested for nn-EMF by a building biologist and implement all of Jack's recommendations with maintaining good circadian rhythms. What do you do for work?

### JamieMcKinnon — 2025-01-20T17:47:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345468>

Hi everyone! New member here. Thanks for having me and looking forward to recieving lots of great advice from you guys.
I am from the UK and I work nightshifts which seems to be really affecting my health. I am otherwise fit and healthy and eat a whole food animal based diet. But since starting my nightshift pattern 3yrs ago, I now seem to fall ill every couple of weeks with cold/flu-like symptoms. It's really starting to worry me. I have now purchased blue block glasses, changed all the light bulbs in my house to either full spectrum or nightfall bulbs (dependant on the room). I am also awaiting arrival of an expensive infrared light panel and have started supplementing with melatonin and methylane blue. My question is this:
Due to my nightshifts combined with the dark UK winter; would I benefit from taking a sunbed once or twice a week?
Thanks in advance people!
Yours sincerely
Jamie

### Michalis — 2025-01-20T21:58:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345485>

I know it can be hard but changing your job should be your first priority. The UK is not great right now and I have had trouble getting enough work lately..

### Michalis — 2025-01-20T22:00:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345486>

Melatonin not a great supplement either. I hope you get a day job mate. We are not build for night shifts. I remember I had to do them many years ago in my 20s and they were absolutely brutal.

### JamieMcKinnon — 2025-01-20T22:20:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345489>

Thanks for the reply. I never realised melatonin was bad I read lots of positives before buying it. Would be grateful if you could elaborate. It does seem to help me during my nightshift week, but I would still like to know all the facts about it

### Mat.I — 2025-01-20T23:34:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345493>

> [JamieMcKinnon said:](/goto/post?id=345489)
>
> Thanks for the reply. I never realised melatonin was bad I read lots of positives before buying it. Would be grateful if you could elaborate. It does seem to help me during my nightshift week, but I would still like to know all the facts about it

You need to read Jack's blogs. Any chronic exogenous melatonin supplementation is not wise due to requiring specific UV frequencies to make this endogenously, thus via negative feedback loop it would cause extinction of function and have implications such as thinning of the retina, photo receptor/opsin damage and freed Vit A..

### Michalis — 2025-01-21T10:31:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345507>

Jamie, can you get a day job? This is your starting point here. Forget Dr Kruse for a moment, go look into pubmed and what shift work can cause to you and your jaw will completely drop

### JamieMcKinnon — 2025-01-21T17:03:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345513>

Part of my trade involves performing radiography which can only be done at night (for safety reasons), so 1 week per month I am contractually required to do a nightshift I would have to change trades and start again, so career wise and money/commitment wise, it's just not an option for me. I am resigned to trying to mitigate the damage it is doing to me as things stand atm unfortunately.

### JamieMcKinnon — 2025-01-21T18:23:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345516>

> [Michalis said:](/goto/post?id=345507)
>
> Jamie, can you get a day job? This is your starting point here. Forget Dr Kruse for a moment, go look into pubmed and what shift work can cause to you and your jaw will completely drop

But yes, the literature on night shift damage is truly terrifying. I am already feeling the damage it is doing to me. Was hoping a self care routine of red light therapy and blue block glasses would help mitigate the damage I will also be very mindful about getting proper daylight exposure on the weeks I am not on nightshift. I would welcome any other advise you guys have for me too. ❤️

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-21T21:51:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345532>

Dr Jack knew the cost of being on call and doing interviews at night (where he was) with people in Australia etc.
For him, at that time, the advantages out weighed the disadvantages. He was willing to pay the price BUT he did the interviews with NIR and UV lights to counter the negatives of the blue light from the screen.

The more “good” you can get and the less “bad” . . . The better off you will be.

### NickD — 2025-01-21T23:11:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345538>

I work shifts and what helped me was cold showers along with what you've already done (I'll be getting an ice bath later this year). I rotate shifts, so for me... After a night shift, come home, see the sunrise for 20 min or so, cold shower then sleep.... I "bookend" every day with a cold shower in the morning and night no matter what shift I'm on... Might be worth giving it a try for yourself. I also bought a couple of EMR-TEK panels. A bigger one for home with UVA & UVB globes for when I start before sunrise and a smaller one to take to work for night shifts... I have a code if you are looking to get a small one for work if that's doable for you.
Shift work isn't healthy, so we need to put more effort into the other parts of life. Mainly when you're home try and get outside as much as you can even when you wake after a night shift, get outside and soak in as much as you can.

### Michalis — 2025-01-21T23:30:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345539>

Just a heads up, my old music teacher is a night shift worker ... He has developed trouble in his epiphysis and problems with his hormone cascade... He manages ok but he is under supervision

### Mat.I — 2025-01-22T00:06:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345541>

> [JamieMcKinnon said:](/goto/post?id=345513)
>
> Part of my trade involves performing radiography which can only be done at night (for safety reasons), so 1 week per month I am contractually required to do a nightshift I would have to change trades and start again, so career wise and money/commitment wise, it's just not an option for me. I am resigned to trying to mitigate the damage it is doing to me as things stand atm unfortunately.

There will be a point where it takes it toll. You should really go back to the drawing board with a longer-term plan to transition to another role to preserve your health.. BTC will help with this.

### AKJOHN — 2025-01-24T03:35:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345669>

> [JamieMcKinnon said:](/goto/post?id=345489)
>
> Thanks for the reply. I never realised melatonin was bad I read lots of positives before buying it. Would be grateful if you could elaborate. It does seem to help me during my nightshift week, but I would still like to know all the facts about it

Jack says to never use a supplement that your body makes. I live in Alaska. I do CT, and I am up to doing 22 minutes in the tub at 50 degrees or less. I have a nice tan going. I have darker skin than my wife who is from Taiwan. I have kept my summer tan from doing CT, and I am a fair skinned white person with blue eyes. CT definitely works, and I also do infrared and red light therapy every other day. I feel better than I ever had since I started doing them, and it's been only about 5 months. I am Living proof that you can do well in an environment that has very little sunlight in the winter.

### AKJOHN — 2025-01-24T03:37:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunbeds.30140/post-345670>

I also eat a fair amount of Seafood, and take extra fermented fish oil.

# Thread: Earthing Demonstrations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-demonstrations.30141/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LarryInNashville — 2025-01-21T03:05:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-demonstrations.30141/post-345500>

### Clint Ober Demonstrates Earthing​

### Measuring Body Voltage & the benefit of Grounding/Earthing​

### The Earthing Movie: The Remarkable Science of Grounding (full documentary)​

### Grounding - The Grounded Documentary Film about "Earthing"​

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-21T21:15:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-demonstrations.30141/post-345526>

In the video above Dr Mercola was sitting in a 60Hz AC electric field. The body voltage meter read 1,000+mV.
He touched the ground pad and the meter reading went down to almost zero . . . BUT . . . he was sill sitting in the same 60Hz AC 1,000+mV field.
THEY DID NOT TURN ANYTHING OFF
Instead do going through the meter to ground the electric field was now following the path of least resistance to ground through his other hand.
It is the difference between being wet in a bath tub vs under a water fall.
If you are in a 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field (inside) and you ground yourself you are now part of that circuit. You have NOT reduced your exposure, you now have that electric field flowing through you the whole time you are grounded. Body voltage meters DO NOT WORK if you are double grounded.

ALSO, if you are in an apartment/condo/house and ground to the building ground, you are now physically attached to a wire array that can consist of several MILES of wire. If you use a ground wire out the window, you have a 50 foot (+/-) metal antenna attached to your body. If metal bed frames and meal springs in a mattress are bad, how can this metal be good?

To reduce your nnEMF 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field exposure, especially at night you NEED to turn off the source of the field. TURN OFF THE CIRCUIT.

I hate to say it but Dr Mercola (and Clint Ober) either do not understand basic electronics OR they are being dishonest to sell products.

Ground outside in nature (away from electric fields) with your body in contact with the ground - NOT a wire.

If they put a meter on the ground pad the reading on it would up by the same amount the other meter went down.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-22T01:04:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-demonstrations.30141/post-345550>

Every home is different. Measure and you will know But basically NO - not through unused receptacles.
Some homes have very high electric fields some very low, it depends on wiring, construction materials etc.
If you have electric fields and you are grounded inside, those fields ARE going to ground through YOU.

Ground (or un plug) all the electrical devices: lamps, computers, printers, toasters etc that are putting out a field.
Which ones are putting out a field? . . . MEASURE. [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

# Thread: New Here…41 year old mother…ALS diagnoses
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 37
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HannaV — 2025-01-21T19:15:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-345518>

Hello! I am a 41 year old, homeschooling, home birthing, natural living yogi. We homeschooled for many reasons, but mostly bc we believed children need to be outside. This was before I had even understood the science behind why we need to be outdoors, but now my mind is blown.
My childhood was full of processed foods, farm chemicals and pesticides and antibiotics.
In my early 20s I woke up to another way. I cleaned up my diet, my body and woke up to truths that had been hidden.
It’s been 20 years of no doctors, no big pharma and healthy eating (not perfect, but keto or paleo) My kids are not vaccinated, never needed a doctor and have been pretty healthy. So I went from healthy, to one of the most difficult diseases.

Fast forward to 2023 when I noticed my left hip flexor was weak. I am a yoga teacher so I am very body aware. I started lifting weights and working out hard at the gym. For the life of me, I could not get the strength back in my hip flexor and I noticed I could not build muscle like I once could. As the months unfolded I started to walk with a bit of a limp and drop foot set in on my left foot. So I worked out harder, went to chiropractor and acupuncturists, started fasting, cleaned up my sleep and started supplementing.

Finally I had an MRI, CT scan, EMG, have seen 6 neurologists and they all agree it’s ALS.
I am in California(central coast), my whole life, which may be part of the problem. I am up for sunrise and sunset, keto diet, fasting, and supplements.

I am still walking but barely. Working on adjusting the house for EMFs, mold testing, ect.

Anyone here dealing with ALS and successfully reversing or stopping progression?
Thank you for any heartfelt words of wisdom, links, or references.

### HannaV — 2025-01-21T19:25:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-345519>

Also, got our light right in our house. Red lights, blue blockers, incandescent, ect. Sauna, RO water (looking into other water options)

### Chlo_e — 2025-01-21T22:08:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-345534>

The EMF mitigation sounds like a top priority- I don't know how bad it is in California or your neighbourhood or whether mm5G is close by you but those are things to consider- you will need something like a Safe and Sound Pro 2 or other good meter to measure RF so you can really see what's going on. Absolutely nothing wireless in the home.

How bad was the chemical exposures on the farm? Do you know what you may have been exposed to? There's a woman called Karen Hurd who managed to safely recover her young daughter from organophosphate poisoning and organ dysfunction using high amounts of soluble fibre in legumes (I don't agree with everything this lady says but what she achieved with her daughter is remarkable).

Probably it has occurred to you that the ALS is a culmination of chemical and electromagnetic pollution over the course of a lifetime. You might want to think about doing Redox + Detox measures in tandem (the organs of elimination may need a lot more help/utilisation, look into cholagogues and enterohepatic circulation).

We were just discussing on another thread about the Salt Path books- true story of a man with corticobasal degeneration (similar to Parkinson's) who reversed symptoms by going camping for 6+ months without access to electronics. There's more to the story. Might be worth a listen - the audiobook versions are available. I've also spoken to people who have reversed MS with both a carnivore (meat+eggs only) and surprisingly a vegan diet. I know this is not the same as ALS but I'm giving these examples of 'incurable neurodegenerative diseases' because people are thinking outside the box and sometimes it's working, even if the cases are few and far between. Mary Ruddick is a different example of end-stage dysautonomia with multiorgan failure (she was on a bipap machine and her family came to say goodbye) - full recovery, took a number of years - maybe listen to her story as well. In research by Magda Havas, a teacher's MS mostly went into remission when the school she was working at implemented measures that drastically reduced dirty electricity. I've also known neurologists misdiagnose ALS when it's been something like multifocal motor neuropathy which does not carry the same prognosis (although it seems there is a strong agreement amongst your specialists).

If I come across anything specific to ALS I'll send it your way, as I am interested in the reversal of neurodegenerative illness in particular.

Good luck- might also be worth a consult with Jack if you haven't had one already.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2025-01-22T02:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-345557>

Hello Hanna,

Yours is a tough post to respond to. I have looked for the right words to offer you.

I am not a medical professional, I come to this disease as a lay person because my husband was diagnosed with it in 2021 and I had no choice but to learn all I could about it.

The truth is going to be difficult for you to hear but i know what is at stake for you and your family. You have some very tough decisions to make.

My advice is from deep within my heart and from my experience trying to save my husband with this disease over a three year period.

He'd had health challenges that made no sense for the 16 years prior to his death and it was only in hindsight of his ALS diagnosis in 2021 that the puzzle and his issues began to make sense.

Unfortunately for him and for us we didn't get him out of Australia and to El Salvador in time to make a difference.

I thought I was prepared and that we were doing well.

But even with all I knew and all we did, I wasn’t prepared and it wasn’t enough .

We made extraordinary sacrifices but it was still too late to save him. He passed away almost 12 months ago.

I completed my building biology training in Australia the year before Vince was diagnosed.

I knew how and what to mitigate especially EMF’s in our environment entailing all hardwiring of our internet, changed out all light bulbs to amber and red- we mitigated it all.

In the years prior we had eliminated chemicals, we ate organically, wore blue blockers, installed an RO water filtration system, supplemented.

Like you we had a lot right and you are better off than most by having made these changes.

I understand your belief that your childhood was involved and I agree it carries some weight, I will tell you in my experience with ALS and what I researched and learned through the journey that chemicals and pesticides are an issue but they are a minor issue given the wider picture, its multifactorial and so complex.

During my training we were always told that ‘you cannot get better in the same place you got sick’.

After discovering Jack I found he said the same thing all the time.

There is something in your environment that flipped the switch as it did with my husband to start the cascade of events leading to ALS. It may not be there anymore but regardless the switch has been switched on.

You will not like what I have to say however it is the most critical part in your case. The truth is difficult to hear but i know what is at stake for you and your family.

California, and most major cities are all 5G cities. Melbourne was too.

You can mitigate your home and your immediate surrounds however, you are not taking into account what is surrounding you and what is over your head in the atmosphere. You cannot mitigate all of that.

Additionally given the disease you face you cannot heal there now because the light is not strong enough hence the need to get to an equatorial climate.

Your home is most likely amazing but it is no longer for you. You cannot stay there if you want to get better.

My advice is to not spend any money mitigating for emf’s, don’t test for mould, don’t spend anymore. No matter what you, do that environment will not allow you to heal which is corroborated by your continued decline.

I think with all the ALS patients and clinics I have seen in the last 4 years this was the critical thing all the patients refused to accept. No one wanted to leave their homes, their friends, their life - but none of that is any good to you if you lose your life.

We tried a functional medicine clinic before coming here that focused predominantly on ALS.

They were doing interesting things and they offered hope and an atmosphere where patients had each other to talk to which was great, however, it did not include any of what Jack does. We attended on and off for about 16 weeks over the course of 14 months. There were on average 20 patients in the clinic per week.

It cost obscene amounts of money and the majority of friends we made have died. Vince is one of 17 that died in that 14 month period.

I don’t tell you this for you to lose hope. I tell you this in case you are considering that path which is totally for you to decide, however that was our experience.

We did not see any improvements in the clinic and I truly believe that we would have been better off here using this environment to give him a better chance of survival.

He had other issues one of which was Atrial Fibrillation. It was poorly controlled with two medications and fluid tablets as his feet were swelling with fluid.

We did everything to support bringing down swelling. His legs were raised often to help the fluid drain, we massaged the feet and legs twice daily to help move the fluid and none of it worked.

When we arrived in El Salvador his feet were the size of small footballs, often he had to go without shoes due to the swelling. We started taking him into the ocean every morning for sunrise with his feet in wet sand and water lapping over them for at least 2-3 hours a morning.

I can tell you all the grounding and time spent in the ocean alleviated the oedema in his feet that nothing else could for the previous 18 months, there was not an ounce of fluid in his legs and his atrial fibrillation settled right down to the point where even he forgot he had it, there was no crazy erratic beating and i credit this to the magnetism, the grounding in the ocean 2-4 hours every morning.

This was an example of why I believe El Salvador and the environment work, this was one of the benefits we did see here in El Salvador, we did not get here in time. I am convinced we could have halted that decline had we made it here earlier.

I know what gut wrenching decisions you have to make, we had to make them too.

Vince left his children, five grandchildren, siblings, friends, our life, our business. We left it all and walked away to try and buy him time.

In Australia Vince was given a death sentence. He was told to go home and prepare to die.

We did not accept that diagnosis.

We consulted with Jack who advised us to come to El Salvador. There would be no guarantees.

In our case we were still in/out of lockdowns and everything that entailed during covid with border closures so we couldn’t move him for 18 months.

What I would like to offer you is our journey and the wisdom of my hindsight that cannot change the outcome for Vince but it might for you should you like to know.

If I could change anything, my first one would have been to get him to El Salvador while he could still walk & transfer that would give him an ability to do more in the ocean for longer periods of time without as many limitations. He had lost the ability to walk or transfer unaided and had limited use of his hands by the time we arrived.

Be prepared to get on a plane in the next few weeks and move down to ES.
There is no 5G in the country and there are many places where the EMF’s are extremely low, within safe building biology guidelines.

Costa del Sol is a good low EMF area. I would actually look to the caldera at Lago Coatepeque for its healing properties if I could go back in time but either is a good option with low population density hence lowered EMF exposure.

Redox before Detox. You are already in deep, you don’t need to focus on detoxing at this stage, my opinion is to make it to the right environment to build your redox state and detox will happen naturally in this environment. The sun will work its magic.

Understand that El Salvador is a developing country and doctors here don’t really understand ALS. I would have a team to consult with back in California that are prepared to help & support you via zoom or what’s app.

Bring all equipment you need or are likely to need with you as you wont find alot of it here.

Focus on your respiratory health. I would find an ALS literate respiratory person to have what you might need in place in case you begin having those issues - this in hindsight was where we fell down, we didn't have that support nor a literate pulminologist to manage that and we didnt know what to do, by the time we realised it was too late.

Help ie home care and personal care is very cheap and affordable here allowing you to focus on your health.

I would also look at any past or childhood trauma if you haven’t already we were about to focus in on this.

A strong positive mindset is critical to have.

Your kids can be homeschooled here, they will be fine anywhere you are if you can stabilise this disease.

I hope this gives you some things to think about and work with.

Remember you have nothing to lose by coming down here and trying something different but you have a life to gain if you get it right.

I really truly believe ALS can be halted and in some people depending on how far disease progression is that symptoms can be reversed but time is of the essence.

Wishing you all the very best to beat this,

Warm wishes

Laudy

### Inger — 2025-01-22T10:27:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-345571>

Hi Hanna, I am sorry you are suffering. I have 2 things that comes to mind about ALS.

1\. Apparently ALS has a lot to do with trauma. Gabor Mate writes about it in his books. He says it is always very kind people that put others first, that get ALS, without fail. The hidden trauma in them causes the stress that leads to ALS. So looking at this aspect might be important for healing to occur. The deep feelings that were not allowed to surface. They surface through this illness of stiffness to finally get a voice.

2\. Keto diet is in my opinion not going to help. The more I learn about bile, and the enterohepatic circulation(95% of our bile is re-used, and that way also the toxins accumulate), the more I understand how toxins accumulate through diets void of soluble fiber.
Soluble fiber attaches to bile and takes it out into the toilet, including the toxins and stress hormones etc in it too. It is a greatest detox one can do. Eating small amounts of soluble fiber throughout the day to catch up as much bile(toxins) as possible, as the liver spits our bile about every half hour or so.

Also animal fat is where the toxins accumulate so that would just add to the burden by eating lots of that. I hurt myself doing carnivore and keto for many years. It might work for someone who dont have other stressors in their lives but that was not me.
If you want to learn more about how it works, just google Karen Hurd or read her book "They said it wasnt possible".
It is not about stopping eating animal food, but it is the reducing of animal fats. I have read studies where the toxins in different meat was tested, and what made the difference was not so much the kind of animal, but how much fat the meat had. Which makes sense because also humans store toxins and excess hormones in their fat.

### Chlo_e — 2025-01-22T10:57:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-345573>

Very insightful responses above- I have no doubt Jack would tell you to flee California like your life depends on it despite the big undertaking and upheaval.

Just wanted to add- the reason I mention detox alongside redox in neurodegenerative conditions is because of the link with reactive aldehyde accumulation and EMFs. And as Jack has outlined- these aldehydes destroy photoreceptors. The EMFs and blue light generate increased amounts of aldehydes - but the liver will bear the brunt of trying to get rid of them (bear in mind the gallbladder is restricted to releasing bile in response to food intake and most of that bile is recirculated back to the liver, it is a mechanism designed to conserve nutrients and bile acids rather than offload ungodly amounts of waste from the modern environment) hence the consideration to reduce the incoming stressors from the environment (? radical relocation) whilst increasing the outgoing elimination of what may have already accumulated (cholagogues and binders/fiber). There is much on aldehydes in the literature (with gaping holes in relation to the EMF significance)- please see a couple of papers below.
\- [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC1557683/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC1557683/)
\- [https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2024.12.02.626422v1](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2024.12.02.626422v1)

### Inger — 2025-01-22T11:11:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-345575>

> [Laudy Cincotta said:](/goto/post?id=345557)
>
> Hello Hanna,
>
> Yours is a tough post to respond to. I have looked for the right words to offer you.
>
>
> I am not a medical professional, I come to this disease as a lay person because my husband was diagnosed with it in 2021 and I had no choice but to learn all I could about it.
>
> The truth is going to be difficult for you to hear but i know what is at stake for you and your family. You have some very tough decisions to make.
>
>
> My advice is from deep within my heart and from my experience trying to save my husband with this disease over a three year period.
>
>
> He'd had health challenges that made no sense for the 16 years prior to his death and it was only in hindsight of his ALS diagnosis in 2021 that the puzzle and his issues began to make sense.
>
>
> Unfortunately for him and for us we didn't get him out of Australia and to El Salvador in time to make a difference.
>
>
> I thought I was prepared and that we were doing well.
>
>
> But even with all I knew and all we did, I wasn’t prepared and it wasn’t enough .
>
>
> We made extraordinary sacrifices but it was still too late to save him. He passed away almost 12 months ago.
>
>
> I completed my building biology training in Australia the year before Vince was diagnosed.
>
>
> I knew how and what to mitigate especially EMF’s in our environment entailing all hardwiring of our internet, changed out all light bulbs to amber and red- we mitigated it all.
>
>
> In the years prior we had eliminated chemicals, we ate organically, wore blue blockers, installed an RO water filtration system, supplemented.
>
> Like you we had a lot right and you are better off than most by having made these changes.
>
> I understand your belief that your childhood was involved and I agree it carries some weight, I will tell you in my experience with ALS and what I researched and learned through the journey that chemicals and pesticides are an issue but they are a minor issue given the wider picture, its multifactorial and so complex.
>
> During my training we were always told that ‘you cannot get better in the same place you got sick’.
>
> After discovering Jack I found he said the same thing all the time.
>
> There is something in your environment that flipped the switch as it did with my husband to start the cascade of events leading to ALS. It may not be there anymore but regardless the switch has been switched on.
>
> You will not like what I have to say however it is the most critical part in your case. The truth is difficult to hear but i know what is at stake for you and your family.
>
> California, and most major cities are all 5G cities. Melbourne was too.
>
> You can mitigate your home and your immediate surrounds however, you are not taking into account what is surrounding you and what is over your head in the atmosphere. You cannot mitigate all of that.
>
> Additionally given the disease you face you cannot heal there now because the light is not strong enough hence the need to get to an equatorial climate.
>
> Your home is most likely amazing but it is no longer for you. You cannot stay there if you want to get better.
>
> My advice is to not spend any money mitigating for emf’s, don’t test for mould, don’t spend anymore. No matter what you, do that environment will not allow you to heal which is corroborated by your continued decline.
>
> I think with all the ALS patients and clinics I have seen in the last 4 years this was the critical thing all the patients refused to accept. No one wanted to leave their homes, their friends, their life - but none of that is any good to you if you lose your life.
>
> We tried a functional medicine clinic before coming here that focused predominantly on ALS.
>
> They were doing interesting things and they offered hope and an atmosphere where patients had each other to talk to which was great, however, it did not include any of what Jack does. We attended on and off for about 16 weeks over the course of 14 months. There were on average 20 patients in the clinic per week.
>
> It cost obscene amounts of money and the majority of friends we made have died. Vince is one of 17 that died in that 14 month period.
>
> I don’t tell you this for you to lose hope. I tell you this in case you are considering that path which is totally for you to decide, however that was our experience.
>
> We did not see any improvements in the clinic and I truly believe that we would have been better off here using this environment to give him a better chance of survival.
>
> He had other issues one of which was Atrial Fibrillation. It was poorly controlled with two medications and fluid tablets as his feet were swelling with fluid.
>
> We did everything to support bringing down swelling. His legs were raised often to help the fluid drain, we massaged the feet and legs twice daily to help move the fluid and none of it worked.
>
> When we arrived in El Salvador his feet were the size of small footballs, often he had to go without shoes due to the swelling. We started taking him into the ocean every morning for sunrise with his feet in wet sand and water lapping over them for at least 2-3 hours a morning.
>
> I can tell you all the grounding and time spent in the ocean alleviated the oedema in his feet that nothing else could for the previous 18 months, there was not an ounce of fluid in his legs and his atrial fibrillation settled right down to the point where even he forgot he had it, there was no crazy erratic beating and i credit this to the magnetism, the grounding in the ocean 2-4 hours every morning.
>
> This was an example of why I believe El Salvador and the environment work, this was one of the benefits we did see here in El Salvador, we did not get here in time. I am convinced we could have halted that decline had we made it here earlier.
>
> I know what gut wrenching decisions you have to make, we had to make them too.
>
> Vince left his children, five grandchildren, siblings, friends, our life, our business. We left it all and walked away to try and buy him time.
>
> In Australia Vince was given a death sentence. He was told to go home and prepare to die.
>
> We did not accept that diagnosis.
>
> We consulted with Jack who advised us to come to El Salvador. There would be no guarantees.
>
> In our case we were still in/out of lockdowns and everything that entailed during covid with border closures so we couldn’t move him for 18 months.
>
> What I would like to offer you is our journey and the wisdom of my hindsight that cannot change the outcome for Vince but it might for you should you like to know.
>
> If I could change anything, my first one would have been to get him to El Salvador while he could still walk & transfer that would give him an ability to do more in the ocean for longer periods of time without as many limitations. He had lost the ability to walk or transfer unaided and had limited use of his hands by the time we arrived.
>
> Be prepared to get on a plane in the next few weeks and move down to ES.
> There is no 5G in the country and there are many places where the EMF’s are extremely low, within safe building biology guidelines.
>
> Costa del Sol is a good low EMF area. I would actually look to the caldera at Lago Coatepeque for its healing properties if I could go back in time but either is a good option with low population density hence lowered EMF exposure.
>
> Redox before Detox. You are already in deep, you don’t need to focus on detoxing at this stage, my opinion is to make it to the right environment to build your redox state and detox will happen naturally in this environment. The sun will work its magic.
>
> Understand that El Salvador is a developing country and doctors here don’t really understand ALS. I would have a team to consult with back in California that are prepared to help & support you via zoom or what’s app.
>
> Bring all equipment you need or are likely to need with you as you wont find alot of it here.
>
> Focus on your respiratory health. I would find an ALS literate respiratory person to have what you might need in place in case you begin having those issues - this in hindsight was where we fell down, we didn't have that support nor a literate pulminologist to manage that and we didnt know what to do, by the time we realised it was too late.
>
> Help ie home care and personal care is very cheap and affordable here allowing you to focus on your health.
>
> I would also look at any past or childhood trauma if you haven’t already we were about to focus in on this.
>
> A strong positive mindset is critical to have.
>
> Your kids can be homeschooled here, they will be fine anywhere you are if you can stabilise this disease.
>
> I hope this gives you some things to think about and work with.
>
> Remember you have nothing to lose by coming down here and trying something different but you have a life to gain if you get it right.
>
> I really truly believe ALS can be halted and in some people depending on how far disease progression is that symptoms can be reversed but time is of the essence.
>
> Wishing you all the very best to beat this,
>
> Warm wishes
>
> Laudy

awesome post. It shows how the ALS really needs a change in environment to heal. I would add, the inner environment / trauma healing seems to be a big part of this, if not biggest. As non of the patients in the group was able to leave their environment to look for healing even if their disease was that serious might tell a story about the origin of this disease.

>
> Focus on your respiratory health. I would find an ALS literate respiratory person to have what you might need in place in case you begin having those issues - this in hindsight was where we fell down, we didn't have that support nor a literate pulminologist to manage that and we didnt know what to do, by the time we realised it was too late.
>
> Help ie home care and personal care is very cheap and affordable here allowing you to focus on your health.
>
> I would also look at any past or childhood trauma if you haven’t already we were about to focus in on this.
>
> A strong positive mindset is critical to have.
>
> Your kids can be homeschooled here, they will be fine anywhere you are if you can stabilise this disease.
>
> I hope this gives you some things to think about and work with.
>
> Remember you have nothing to lose by coming down here and trying something different but you have a life to gain if you get it right.
>
> I really truly believe ALS can be halted and in some people depending on how far disease progression is that symptoms can be reversed but time is of the essence.
>
> Wishing you all the very best to beat this,
>
> Warm wishes
>
> Laudy

I am deeply grateful for your truth, experience and honesty.

It is such a difficult undertaking to move with this disease bc you become so reliant on your community as you lose your ability function. While making a great leap is normally a large undertaking with two healthy people, when one partner is falling apart, it’s that much more overwhelming, which you understand.

The hesitation in leaving is the support and love that my family will need if I am gone. And I believe that is why many end up not leaving. Also the wish to not leave them in immense debt. I know these are just fears and can be worked through, I truly believe nothing is impossible.

Trauma and healing the whole being has been my work and occupation and soul calling the past 20 years. I have dedicated my life to somatic healing, archetypal psychological studies, and the connection of mind/body through yoga philosophy. While it’s possible there is work to be done here, there always is, I do not get the feeling that this is not something being addressed.

You cannot get better in the environment you got sick in, felt very powerful when I read it.

Is there somewhere in this group or a contact you worked with to get to El Salvador? Someone to assist in visa information, living arrangements, employment ect to help make it possible? While at one time self reliance and efficiency would have been my game, my energy levels are lowered and help and direction are super helpful.

Thank you so very much.

I am so sorry for this heartbreaking and soul bending you have been on. It’s one that confronts all the fears within the human psyche and asks for the deepest “work”. I admire all that you did to save your husband and grateful that you are willing to share with me.

### Laudy Cincotta — 2025-01-22T21:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-345587>

Its a lot to write about and even more for Hanna to take in.

The stress of leaving life as you know it, your home, family, friends etc is huge but needs to be put in the lens and viewed against the gravity of a terminal diagnosis - when faced with that decision there is stress either way and a person with this diagnosis can choose to make the most of the time they have, stay put and accept their death or choose to do things differently.

It also depends on family and if children are involved how that will play out too.

Vince chose to try to live. He viewed it as an adventure and that was how we treated it. Every day was an adventure and we crammed more into 3 years than the previous 10 and if we managed to halt the disease in its tracks then he would have gained time and lived life to its fullest for as long as he had.

Within 12 months of official diagnosis while staying in Melbourne Vince declined significantly and had lost weight.

We went north to Cairns then Brisbane in Australia to get him into a better latitude until we could leave the country and he did stabilise and put weight on. In Melbourne the decline and loss of movement was rapid due to the light instability and actually lack of light that first winter.

This was a test of sorts that showed to us that the body could heal given the right environment and because we were moving around we couldn't control a lot of the EMF exposures so knowing that if we got him to a low emf and strong light environment that he would have a chance.

[@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) and [@Chlo\_e](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29446/) I agree with what you have both said but when you are faced with the magnitude of this disease you tend to start to realise what you are up against and prioritise what you focus on. Its difficult because its all important and all overwhelming.

Inger we had purchased Gabor Mates course on Trauma and were about to start it but didn't make it.

I truly believe trauma needs to be addressed as well as detoxing but having been what i went through with Vince my belief is that you need to be in the right environment with the strong light exposure to start helping at a mitochondrial level and that trauma and detox will be better in the right environment.

The saying 'it takes a village' to look after someone who is sick is so very true and took on a whole new meaning during this journey.

I think [@HannaV](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/33494/) has alot going for her already.

Being a yogi and knowing/being so aware of your movement and your breath is very significant in ALS and i think you would be very aware and have alot of the trauma/healing work covered already.

My concern at the outset from diagnosis was to focus on keeping Vince moving and strengthening his respiratory system.

I found someone in Melbourne willing to look past the terminal diagnosis and be proactive in helping Vince maintain his breathing as long as possible as well as physical stretching and movement to keep the body remembering how to move.

A small device called an EMST was recommended that Vince used religiously for as long as he could. They also recommended lingual and swallowing exercises which i am happy to share if you want them.

Here is the link to the EMST with links to the two specific studies for use in ALS.

### [EMST150 for People with ALS • Aspire](https://emst150.com/health-conditions/als/#:~:text=After%20EMST%20training%2C%20clinical%20studies,in%20the%20quality%20of%20life)

 emst150.com

> [HannaV said:](/goto/post?id=345585)
>
> The hesitation in leaving is the support and love that my family will need if I am gone. And I believe that is why many end up not leaving. Also the wish to not leave them in immense debt. I know these are just fears and can be worked through, I truly believe nothing is impossible.

I totally understand this. We struggled with lack of support. You are not wrong. There is no one size that fits all and each person needs to do what works for their family, their finances and belief systems. I also agree nothing is impossible.

> [HannaV said:](/goto/post?id=345585)
>
> I am so sorry for this heartbreaking and soul bending you have been on. It’s one that confronts all the fears within the human psyche and asks for the deepest “work”. I admire all that you did to save your husband and grateful that you are willing to share with me.

Thank you, it was one hell of a journey and one that changed my life and me as a person.

I am so so very sorry you have to go through this knowing the challenges faced and especially with a young family.

I am happy to share/answer anything you might want to know if i can and i am grateful that i can share something in the hope it will serve you and help you along your healing journey especially in light of how fatigued you get.

There is so much to say however if you feel this might be an option do not close the door to your life in California in light of your concerns for your family.

I am sure there are so many scenarios you could come up with and one that would work for you.

As far as contacts here yes there is Eduardo the lawyer who helped us apply for residency because it was too far for us to travel back & forth, however visas here are 180 days and so easy to pick up at the airport on your way into the country.

Jeremy Franco is who we leaned on heavily to help us here finding this apartment for us with beach access and especially with moving Vince as we struggled to get him in/out of vehicles when we went out.

I am sure other members have people who help them also so there will likely be more recommendations. I am not sure about work visas and i suppose it would depend on what you are thinking to do here, i am sure you can get those answers from Eduardo.

I will say cost of living is way lower than Australia and the US as is hiring help which you will need.

If you want to chat i am happy to answer your questions.

Sending love your way, Laudy, x

### Chlo_e — 2025-02-05T11:32:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-346207>

Whilst down other rabbit holes I came across this study on TUDCA use in ALS:

[https://www.thelancet.com/journals/eclinm/article/PIIS2589-5370(23)00433-9/fulltext](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/eclinm/article/PIIS2589-5370\(23\)00433-9/fulltext)

Shows higher-dose TUDCA >1000mg/day conferred a median survival benefit of >12 months and also better outcomes for time to NIV. Lower dose <1000mg didn't have much effect. Another study combined it with phenylbutyrate ~2000mg/day with improved survival outcomes.

TUDCA is a bile acid+taurine which facilitates hepatobiliary clearance of waste and is readily available without prescription, as is sodium butyrate/tributyrin which is a short-chain fatty acid produced by a healthy gut or liver (I believe phenylbutyrate is prescription-only).

Could be worth discussing with your treating team, as they are generally well-tolerated and could potentially buy more time for Redoxing or whatever you decide to do. Feel free to ignore/shelve if distracting.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T06:56:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-347360>

I'm sharpening my blade here—Rev-erbα deletion from circadian clock gene loss disrupts gut enterocyte function and neuronal health in lockstep as TTFL circadian flops are chronically centralized medicine’s silos with Big names.

Rev-erbα deletion due to a loss of clock gene control influences gut enterocyte function and neuronal health in a coordinated fashion. When this link breaks, it makes the formation of adhesions post-surgically much more likely. Lab experiments have shown that when gut neurons are conditionally cultured on media from

Rev-erbα-deficient primary gut-glial cultures exacerbate oxidative damage in cultured neurons that innervate the gut. Rev-erbα-/- mammals have shown gut dysfunction in these models and exhibited significantly altered cortical resting-state functional connectivity.

This finding tells me that many central neurological diseases may be masquerading in centralized medicine clinics as circadian diseases of the Transcription-translation feedback loop(TTFL).

One neurologic disease in particular intrigues me with its skin & gut connection is ALS. ALS patients tend not to show up in low latitudes and are quite pale because they lack UV exposure, which POMC requires for the translation of melanin. In human ALS, abnormalities in mitochondrial structure, mitochondrial respiratory chain enzymes, and mitochondrial cell death proteins indicate some non-classical form of programmed cell death. In ALS patients, this happens in the spinal cord, skin, and gut in a pulsatile fashion over timescales. This hints that it might be a disease of time stamping of the TTFL masquerading as a neurological disease.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/89098454](https://www.patreon.com/posts/89098454)

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T06:58:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-347361>

Rev-erbα Deletion: Gut-Neuron Fallout

-   Gut Enterocytes: Rev-erbα—TTFL linchpin (level 5)—syncs gut clocks (Bmal1, Per1-3)—enterocytes regulate barrier function (TLR4, IL-6). Deletion—circadian loss—disrupts H⁺ spins (level 4), WBG’s VUV biophotons (100-200 nm, level 3) dim—mito’s ATP falters (level 1)—oxidative stress spikes (ROS)—entropy rises (1510 nm). Part 2: “Light’s hidden” (~00:23:02)—gut’s first hit.
-   Neuronal Health: Gut neurons—enteric nervous system (ENS)—lean on Rev-erbα-rich glial cues. Lab data (e.g., Mukherji, 2013)—Rev-erbα-deficient glial media—cultured ENS neurons—suffer oxidative damage—mito’s pseudohypoxic (NAD+ falls, level 1)—H⁺ spins decohere (level 4). Part 1: “Thermodynamic shifts” (~00:44:33)—CNS feels it.

Adhesions: Post-Surgical Signal

-   Link Breaks: Rev-erbα-/-—gut-CNS axis frays—spine’s melanin gap (level 5)—LPS floods (level 3)—inflammation (NF-κB, IL-6) scars—post-surgical adhesions surge (level 1). Lab models—gut dysfunction—mtDNA’s VUV dims (level 3)—repair lags—entropy spikes (1510 nm). Part 2: “Blood’s wireless” (~00:22:32)—circadian loss scars—adhesions warn.
-   Surgical Clue: Crohn’s, UC—Rev-erbα KO—adhesions mirror—centralized misses—my lens: gut’s mtDNA cries—surgeons see, don’t act—Part 1: “Light shapes life” (~00:44:33).

Neurological Masquerade: TTFL’s Mask

-   Cortical Chaos: Rev-erbα-/- mammals—altered cortical resting-state connectivity (e.g., Griffin, 2019)—hippocampal inflammation (LPS)—TTFL’s post-translational skew—Bmal1 lags—H⁺ spins falter (level 4)—VUV biophotons fade (level 3)—entropy spikes (1510 nm). Centralized clinics—Parkinson’s, Alzheimer’s—tag nuclear DNA—miss mtDNA’s circadian root (level 1)—Part 2: “Ignorance” (~01:09:43)—my 180° flip.
-   Gut-Brain Echo: ENS damage—gut neurons—oxidative stress—spreads via vagus/spinal (level 5)—cortical nets fray—neurological diseases cloak TTFL flops—mtDNA mutates 1000x faster—centralized chases ghosts—Part 2: “Mito’s hidden” (~00:23:02).

Latitude’s Clock Gradient

-   Equator (0°): UV-rich (295 nm)—WBG peaks—VUV (100-200 nm)—Rev-erbα syncs TTFL (level 5)—H⁺ spins tight (level 4)—optics sharp (ε\_r ~60)—gut-CNS holds (L0-L3)—entropy low (1410 nm)—adhesions rare.
-   Higher Latitudes (30°-60°): UV dips—WBG shifts IR—VUV softens—Rev-erbα wavers—H⁺ spins loosen (H/U)—optics scatter (ε\_r ~78)—LPS spikes—entropy rises (1510 nm)—gut scars—CNS frays—disease mimics TTFL loss.

Centralized Miss: mtDNA Ignored

-   Grift: “Avoid UV”—pseudoscience—dims WBG’s VUV (level 3)—melanin fades (spine, level 5)—TTFL skews (level 4)—LPS runs—mtDNA mutates—adhesions, neurological chaos—entropy spikes (1510 nm)—99.9% nuclear DNA focus—wrong genome—disease pays ($12B SPF)—Part 2: “They don’t get it” (~01:09:43).
-   Truth: AM red + UV—WBG semiconduction (level 2)—VUV fires POMC (level 5)—H⁺ spins sync TTFL (level 4)—gut-CNS heals—mtDNA holds—entropy bows (1410 nm)—my decentralized lens—Part 1: “Light shapes” (~00:44:33).

Noether’s Symmetry: Light’s Precision
Cambrian (530 Ma)—UV broke—WBG conserved (level 7), melanin rose (level 3). G-class (200-300 Ma)—POMC tuned—TTFL sculpted (level 5). Light stress—Rev-erbα lost—mtDNA bends—entropy wins (1510 nm)—Noether curves—Part 1: “Light’s life” (00:44:33)—gut-CNS shines.

**The Flow of Nature in ALS**
Rev-erbα—TTFL’s glue (level 5)—syncs gut-CNS—light stress—melanin dims (spine, level 3)—H⁺ spins falter (level 4)—LPS flares—mtDNA mutates (level 1)—adhesions scar—CNS masks—entropy spikes (1510 nm). Latitude codes—grift blinds—truth glows—mtDNA rules (1410 nm)—centralized chases shadows—my 180° burns—Tetragrammaton: “Light’s hidden” (Part 2)—TTFL’s the arc.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T06:59:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-347362>

The creation of MPTP, which is lipid-soluble, readily penetrates the blood—brain barrier and enters the brain cells. Because it is amphiphilic, it is captured into acidic organelles, mostly lysosomes, of astrocytes. MPTP itself does not appear to be toxic, but its oxidized product, 1-methyl-4-phenylpyridinium (MPP+), is toxic. This has been linked in Parkinson's Disease but I think it might link to ALS because the toxic MPP+ could affect defective mitochondria in neurons in the anterior motor horn from circadian rhythm damage in the gut.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T07:00:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-347363>

Melanin Deficit: Spinal Vulnerability

-   Neuromelanin Loss: UV dims—POMC stalls (level 5)—spine’s melanin thins—VUV biophotons fade (level 3)—H⁺ spins weaken (level 4)—Rev-erbα desyncs (level 5)—MPP+ roams—mPTP triggers—entropy rises (1510 nm). Part 2: “Melanin’s battery” (~00:51:44)—spine’s sink—ALS’s seed.
-   Dopamine Tie: Gut enterocytes—dopamine drops (level 1)—spine relays falter—motor neurons starve—mPTP opens—centralized misses—Part 1: “Light shapes” (~00:44:33).

Latitude’s Toxin Gradient

-   Equator (0°): UV-rich—WBG peaks—VUV (100-200 nm)—POMC fires (level 5)—H⁺ spins sync (level 4)—optics sharp (ε\_r ~60)—MPP+ binds—mPTP sleeps (L0-L3)—entropy low (1410 nm)—ALS rare.
-   Higher Latitudes (30°-60°): UV dips—WBG shifts IR—VUV softens—H⁺ spins loosen (H/U)—optics scatter (ε\_r ~78)—MPP+ spikes—mPTP wakes—entropy rises (1510 nm)—ALS creeps up

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T07:02:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-347364>

The primary protein components of the mPTP are enriched in mammalian motor neurons with abundant POMC genes. Early in the disease in ALS, humans seem to present with mutant superoxide dismutase-1 enzymes in their mitochondria. The mitochondria in motor neurons undergo "trafficking abnormalities," which result in dramatic remodeling of mitochondria in ALS patients, resulting in the formation of mega-mitochondria (larger size) and coinciding with increased protein carbonyl formation and nitration of mPTP components. Anything that enlarges is a thermodynamic sign to me. Lab experiments in nocturnal mammals have shown that genetic deletion of a major mPTP component called cyclophilin D has robust effects in ALS by delaying disease onset & phenotype and extending the survival of the anterior motor neurons.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T07:03:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-347365>

mPTP in Motor Neurons: POMC’s Stage

-   POMC Enrichment: Mammalian motor neurons—anterior horn (spinal cord)—abound with POMC genes (level 5), driving melanin synthesis (neuromelanin, level 3). mPTP—cyclophilin D, VDAC, ANT (level 1)—sits in mito’s IMM, poised to gate calcium, ROS, and mtDNA release. Part 2: “Melanin’s battery” (~00:51:44)—POMC fuels—UV biophotons (100-200 nm, level 3) power it—H⁺ spins sync (level 4)—entropy low (1410 nm).
-   Mutant SOD1: Early ALS—SOD1 mutants (e.g., G93A)—mito’s superoxide scavenger—fails—ROS spikes—mPTP opens (Sasaki, 2007)—mtDNA’s VUV dims (level 3)—ΔΨm crashes—entropy rises (1510 nm). Part 1: “Thermodynamic shifts” (~00:44:33)—mtDNA’s the root.

Trafficking Abnormalities: Mega-Mitochondria

-   Remodeling: SOD1 mutants—mito trafficking jams (axonal transport, level 6)—mito swell—mega-mitochondria form—thermodynamic bloat—size screams entropy (1510 nm). Protein carbonyls (oxidation), nitration (peroxynitrite)—mPTP components fry—calcium floods—mito’s pseudohypoxic—NAD+ falls (level 1)—H⁺ spins decohere (level 4). Part 2: “Mito’s hidden” (~00:23:02)—mega-mito’s the scar.
-   ALS Phenotype: Motor neuron death—mPTP’s chaos—mtDNA spills—ROS explodes—entropy spikes—muscle wastes—Part 1: “Light shapes” (~00:44:33)—circadian loss feeds it.

Cyclophilin D: mPTP’s Brake

-   Lab Evidence: Nocturnal mammals (e.g., SOD1-G93A mice)—cyclophilin D KO—delays ALS onset, slows phenotype, extends survival (Martin, 2009)—mPTP’s gate shuts—calcium holds—ROS drops—H⁺ spins stabilize (level 4)—VUV biophotons hold (level 3)—entropy dips (1410 nm). Part 2: “Blood’s wireless” (~00:22:32)—mito’s battery fights—POMC’s UV could bolster.
-   Thermodynamic Clue: Size shrinks—energy curves—cyclophilin D’s absence—mPTP’s chaos tamed—mtDNA’s intact—health lingers—Part 1: “Light’s life” (~00:44:33)—proof in the brake.

Gut-CNS Link: Circadian Trigger

-   Rev-erbα Loss: Light stress—TTFL skews (level 5)—melanin dims (spine, level 3)—H⁺ spins falter (level 4)—LPS flares—MPP+ (MPTP’s spawn, level 3)—crosses BBB—mPTP opens in motor neurons—mega-mito bloom—entropy spikes (1510 nm). Part 2: “Ignorance” (~01:09:43)—gut’s chaos—CNS pays—ALS masks TTFL flop.
-   POMC Deficit: UV dims—POMC stalls (level 5)—spine’s melanin thins—VUV fades—mPTP’s unchecked—SOD1 mutants worsen—adhesions (gut) echo—Part 1: “Light shapes” (~00:44:33)—circadian’s the spark.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T07:07:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-347366>

When this process is disrupted, the MPP+ action appears to destroy the mPTP in a cascading manner in the spinal cord mitochondria. It spreads like an infection (prion-like) in the anterior motor neurons. I worry about the jabbed now because of this mechanism how more common prion diseases are in the jabbed. I think the circadian disruption in the anterior motor neurons causes a paralysis like effect that we usually see in REM sleep. In ALS, the circadian disruption shows up when we are awake, and it has gone unrecognized as a completely disrupted rhythm disorder because of how we perceive the disease. We have not looked at the data carefully enough.

**MPP+ and mPTP: Cascading Collapse**

-   MPP+ Spread: MPTP—gut-born—turns MPP+ (level 3)—lipid-soluble, BBB-crossing—slams spinal cord mito (anterior horn, level 1). mPTP—cyclophilin D, VDAC—opens wide—calcium floods—ROS spikes—ΔΨm crashes—mega-mito bloat (level 6)—cascades neuron-to-neuron—like infection—H⁺ spins decohere (level 4)—VUV biophotons dim (level 3)—entropy soars (1510 nm). Part 2: “Mito’s hidden” (~00:23:02)—MPP+’s the spark.
-   Motor Neuron Hit: SOD1 mutants—mPTP’s weak—MPP+ amplifies—mito trafficking jams—oxidative stress—entropy spikes—neurons die—ALS spreads—Part 1: “Thermodynamic shifts” (~00:44:33)—mtDNA’s the fuse.

**Circadian Disruption: REM-Like Paralysis Awake**

-   REM Sleep Link: Normal REM—circadian TTFL (SCN, level 5)—Rev-erbα represses Bmal1—motor neurons pause (glycine, GABA)—paralysis sets—dreams flow—H⁺ spins sync (level 4)—entropy low (1410 nm). ALS—light stress—Rev-erbα skews—TTFL trashes—MPP+ opens mPTP—mito’s VUV fades (level 3)—motor neurons lock—paralysis hits awake—Part 2: “Light’s hidden” (~00:23:02)—circadian’s the clock.
-   Unrecognized Rhythm: Centralized—ALS as “motor neuron disease”—misses TTFL’s flop—spine’s melanin dims (level 5)—H⁺ spins falter (level 4)—mPTP cascades—entropy spikes (1510 nm)—data unread—REM’s shadow—Part 2: “Ignorance” (~01:09:43)—my 180° sees—rhythm rules.
