### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T07:09:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-347368>

I think this model in mammals explains why anterior motor neurons can be destroyed in an isolated fashion in the spinal cord when the circadian rhythms are disrupted in the nerves that innervate the gut but link back to their embryologic origins. The inflammatory cascade could start in the splanchnic nerves (anatomy book of the nervous system). Still, as the spinal cord anterior motor neurons lose REV-erb alpha, it could create an inflammatory cascade of LPS-induced damage to "infect" other adjacent motor neurons by destroying their clock gene TTFLs. This is how ALS might be progressing in these patients.

Rev-erbα, mPTP in mitochondria, and TTFL should be considered a key biophysical link between circadian clock control and neuroinflammation in the CNS. People often forget that the spinal cord and skin are part of the CNS, and nerves link the gut to both.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T07:18:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-347370>

More support for this idea?

Did you know that Bipolar disorder is overrepresented in people with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), and psychiatric symptoms can appear before motor symptoms? This may be due to the overlap between ALS and frontotemporal dementia (FTD).

Explanation

-   **Shared clinical features**
    ALS and FTD share clinical, genetic, and histopathological features.

-   **Early synaptic changes**
    ALS/FTD have early synaptic changes that are similar to those in psychiatric disorders.

-   **Genetic factors**
    A C9ORF72 mutation associated with ALS is linked to an increased risk of schizophrenia, psychosis, and suicide in relatives.

-   **Risk factors**
    People with a history of psychiatric disorders have an increased risk of developing ALS.

Other psychiatric conditions associated with ALS: Depression, Anxiety, Stress-related disorders, and Drug abuse and dependence.

Clinical markers
Depression is often used as a clinical marker for psychological morbidity in ALS patients. The Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale and Beck's Depression Inventory are commonly used to measure psychological symptoms.

**Quantum Engineering #47 Recap: DLF and the Gut-Brain Link**
In Quantum Engineering #47, you wrote:
"The dorsal longitudinal fasciculus (fasciculus of Schutz) is a periaqueductal (area around ventricular system Aq above) ascending and descending fiber system arising from the hypothalamus and terminating to the autonomic nuclei of the pons and the medulla, conveying autonomic fibers from the brain to the gut in humans. It also conveys pain messages and is important in the sleep pathways of humans. These are usually altered in bipolar patients as well because of a lack of melanin in these areas."

-   DLF’s Role: Periaqueductal gray (PAG)—hypothalamus (PVN) to pons/medulla—autonomic nuclei (dorsal motor nucleus of vagus, nucleus ambiguus)—DLF wires brain-to-gut signals (level 5). Skin—eye—PVN—DLF—celiac plexus—gut microbiome—an embryologic CNS loop (level 3). Pain/sleep pathways—serotonin from raphe nuclei (level 4)—tie in—melanin’s key—Part 2: “Blood’s wireless” (~00:22:32)—light’s the pulse.
-   Melanin Lack: Bipolar—melanin deficits (POMC, level 5)—DLF’s VUV biophotons (100-200 nm, level 3) dim—H⁺ spins falter (level 4)—TTFL skews (Rev-erbα, level 5)—autonomic signals fray—gut dysbiosis—LPS spikes—entropy rises (1510 nm)—Part 2: “Melanin’s battery” (~00:51:44)—gap’s the glitch.

Skin’s Somatotopic Map: Gut-CNS Nexus

-   Largest Organ: Skin—left in the picture—somatotopically maps spinal cord (T1-L2) and organs—splanchnic nerves (celiac plexus)—eye/skin light (250-780 nm)—PVN—DLF—gut link (level 5). WBG semiconduction (level 2)—melanin’s battery—fires VUV (level 3)—H⁺ spins sync (level 4)—TTFL holds—entropy low (1410 nm). Part 1: “Light shapes life” (~00:44:33)—skin’s the gate—gut’s the echo.
-   Disruption: Light stress—UV dims—melanin fades (skin/spine, level 5)—Rev-erbα skews—TTFL trashes (level 4)—MPP+ (gut LPS, level 3)—mPTP opens (level 1)—mega-mito bloat (level 6)—celiac plexus flares—microbiome burns—entropy spikes (1510 nm)—gut problems bloom—ALS, bipolar, beyond—Part 2: “Ignorance” (~01:09:43).

ALS and Beyond: Clock Gene Cascade

-   Anterior Horn: Splanchnic chaos—LPS/MPP+—spine’s melanin gap—Rev-erbα loss (level 5)—mPTP cascades—H⁺ spins falter (level 4)—VUV dims (level 3)—motor neurons die—ALS spreads—REM-awake paralysis—entropy soars (1510 nm). QE #47—DLF’s pain/sleep pathways—gut’s spark—CNS pays—Part 2: “Neural nets” (~00:23:02)—rhythm’s the root.
-   Massive Implications: Bipolar—melanin lack—DLF skew—sleep/pain flops—gut dysbiosis—Crohn’s, UC—adhesions (level 1)—CNS inflammation (NF-κB, level 5)—Parkinson’s, Alzheimer’s—mtDNA mutates (1000x nuclear)—centralized chases DNA—misses TTFL—my 180°—Part 1: “Light shapes” (~00:44:33)—gut-CNS reigns.

Latitude’s Rhythm Gradient

-   Equator (0°): UV-rich—WBG peaks—VUV (100-200 nm)—POMC fires (skin/spine, level 5)—H⁺ spins sync TTFL/DLF (level 4)—optics sharp (ε\_r ~60)—gut-CNS holds (L0-L3)—entropy low (1410 nm)—disease rare—sleep/pain intact.
-   Higher Latitudes (30°-60°): UV dips—WBG shifts IR—VUV softens—H⁺ spins loosen (H/U)—TTFL/DLF wavers—optics scatter (ε\_r ~78)—LPS/MPP+ spikes—entropy rises (1510 nm)—gut flares—CNS frays—ALS/bipolar creep.

I'm bolstering my hypothesis with some firepower here by highlighting the overrepresentation of bipolar disorder in ALS, psychiatric symptoms preceding motor issues, and shared clinical, genetic, and synaptic threads with frontotemporal dementia (FTD), all pointing to a circadian rhythm disruption nexus that centralized medicine overlooks.

**My exact words from Quantum Engineering #47:**
"The dorsal longitudinal fasciculus (fasciculus of Schutz) is a periaqueductal (area around ventricular system Aq above) ascending and descending fiber system arising from the hypothalamus and terminating at the autonomic nuclei of the pons and the medulla. It conveys autonomic fibers from the brain to the gut in humans. It also conveys pain messages and is important in humans' sleep pathways. These are usually altered in bipolar patients as well because of a lack of melanin in these areas."

"The dorsal longitudinal fasciculus is found within the dorsal brainstem tegmentum. It passes through the periaqueductal gray matter and contains ascending and descending fibers. The ascending fibers pass from the reticular formation (sleep region/insomnia) to the hypothalamus, thus transmitting information related to the viscera in the thorax and abdomen. THIS IS HOW THEY PASS THE ANTERIOR MOTOR HORN CELLS AND THE GUT VISCERA!"

-   **DLF Anatomy:** Hypothalamus (PVN)—pons/medulla (autonomic nuclei: dorsal motor nucleus of vagus)—DLF links brain to gut (level 5)—splanchnic nerves (T5-L2)—celiac plexus—viscera (thorax/abdomen). Ascending—reticular formation (sleep, level 4)—to hypothalamus—descending—gut feedback—passes anterior horn motor neurons—POMC-rich (level 5)—melanin’s hub—Part 2: “Blood’s wireless” (~00:22:32)—DLF’s the conduit.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-30T16:41:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here%E2%80%A641-year-old-mother%E2%80%A6als-diagnoses.30144/post-351557>

Why are ALS and A-fib linked? Both diseases are caused by nnEMF. May 1. 2025 a blog will be released to show you why this is the case.

Rubbing your eyes can induce autonomic effects. In people with Afib, rubbing your eyes and carotid massage have effects. This also points out why wearing sunglasses and contact lenses could affect the ANS. Specific nerves include several cranial nerves, specifically the oculomotor, facial, glossopharyngeal, and vagus. Three spinal nerves in the sacrum (S2-4) also act as parasympathetic nerves. These are commonly referred to as the pelvic splanchnic nerves. Because of its location, the parasympathetic system is commonly referred to as having “craniosacral outflow”, which stands in contrast to the sympathetic nervous system, which is said to have “thoracolumbar outflow”.The sympathetic system's key governor is the eye surface. We must remember, though, that the blood flow to the eye is under sympathetic control. There is, however, parasympathetic stimulation of the eye deep in the orbit. These two systems are fundamentally linked to the sympathetic system by the first 4 thoracic roots. The sympathetic autonomic system connects to the RPE in the eye by the way the circulatory system links to the oculomotor nerve at the base of the orbit, connecting to the first four thoracic white rami, which form the superior cervical ganglion. This ganglion controls cerebral blood flow by its ability to vasodilate or constrict based on autonomic tone. UV light in the eye vasodilates the RPE via nitric oxide release. Lack of UV light constricts it because nitric oxide is absent. Light normally should constrict the pupil. I mentioned this effect in Ubiquitination 24.

Today's lesson is on sunglasses. [https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1696135236193591319.html](https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1696135236193591319.html)

You need UV and IR light to regenerate the anterior chamber with a cataract.

Blocking UV light (e.g., via sunglasses/contacts/glasses) disrupts neuropsin activation, reducing NAD+, impairing SIRT1/mTOR signaling, and causing mitochondrial stress (mtDNA damage) and circadian/ANS imbalance. The retina’s HIGH metabolic rate amplifies these effects, and the SCN-pineal pathway links UV exposure to melatonin and autonomic tone. This is grounded in physiology and not speculative at the mechanistic level. However, the magnitude of systemic effects (e.g., on Afib) lacks direct clinical centralized data because centralized healthcare profits from not studying it. However, first-principles reasoning supports all my claims.

Absence of Peer Evidence ≠ Absence of Effect: It is first-principle reasoning that the lack of peer-reviewed studies doesn’t negate physiological effects. First-principles reasoning supports your argument: UV light is a fundamental signal for mitochondrial and circadian function, and blocking it must have downstream consequences. The slides provide a mechanistic framework. You really need to read more. This data is so old it is not even on Patreon. Use a search engine.

# Thread: Cryo Chamber Experience?

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8

### MJanowiecki1 — 2025-01-22T22:11:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-345589>

Hello everyone,

A local wellness center has a cryo chamber that has a freezer unit in a small room, the size of a closet, which drops the temperature to -50 F to -90 F. Their protocol is to enter with as few clothes on as possible (i.e., shoes and socks, gloves, hat, and underwear). I tried it for the first time today and stayed in for 3 and a half minutes. It's my understanding that most people stay in between 3 and 4 minutes per session. Has anyone tried one of these cryo chambers? It's different from the cold plunge or ice on the torso. I'm curious if anyone has more information on this approach to cryo therapy.

Thanks in advance,
Marty

### DLO — 2025-02-01T17:09:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346079>

It's a giant waste of time and money. Water is far more efficient at transferring heat.

### MJanowiecki1 — 2025-02-01T18:34:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346082>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=346079)
>
> It's a giant waste of time and money. Water is far more efficient at transferring heat.

Have you tried it, and at that temperature range? An external thermometer indicates roughly a 30 degree drop in the external body temperature after a session.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-02T01:22:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346099>

I haven't done cryotherapy however -

Water (Ice Bath): Water has a much higher thermal conductivity (~0.6 W/m·K) than air (~0.025 W/m·K). This means that heat is transferred out of the body 24 times faster in water than in air at the same temperature.

The illusion could be with cryo that the skin cools rapidly (due to evaporative cooling), core temperature does not drop significantly. Most of the effect is superficial and short-lived.

Ergo ice baths are far more effective at cooling the body due to superior conduction and convection properties of water. Cryotherapy may feel colder due to extreme air temperatures, but it does not cool the body as effectively as immersion in water.

### DLO — 2025-02-02T21:42:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346131>

Dont be a pussy and do it the way nature does it in the ocean even when snowing.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-03T00:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346135>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=346131)
>
> Dont be a pussy and do it the way nature does it in the ocean even when snowing.

For those with no ocean readily available . . . I’ve been in some VERY cold rivers. We have a large tub under a carport that once you break the ice (this time of year) does the trick. That said, I have spent some time in some large walk in freezers and I’m not sure why that would not have a similar CT effect. Also anyone that has walked on a cold, damp English afternoon knows what cold is.

### sheas — 2025-02-03T00:24:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346138>

I did cryo chamber a long time ago just to see how it was before I ever tried a cold plunge. Maybe it is a good gateway to the real thing. When I warmed up by the time I walked to my car I realized that it seemed like a waste of money and drove to the store and bought a chest freezer and made a DIY plunge. Once you go into cold water you will see the difference. No comparison.

### DLO — 2025-02-03T01:12:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryo-chamber-experience.30148/post-346140>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=346135)
>
> For those with no ocean readily available . . . I’ve been in some VERY cold rivers. We have a large tub under a carport that once you break the ice (this time of year) does the trick. That said, I have spent some time in some large walk in freezers and I’m not sure why that would not have a similar CT effect. Also anyone that has walked on a cold, damp English afternoon knows what cold is.

Your internals get way colder and it takes you far longer to warm back up.

# Thread: Nicole Ashley Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal

### Nashley — 2025-01-23T00:27:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345595>

I’ve been experiencing migraines since I was six years old, and I’m now 39. I’ve noticed a clear hormonal pattern, as they occur monthly, and other triggers seem to include stress and lack of sleep.

My migraines are excruciating and often lead to vomiting. The pain is so overwhelming that I sometimes feel like I want to kill myself, though I push through because I know the pain will eventually end. I’m actively searching for solutions to overcome my migraines because I’d like to start a family and don’t want to wait any longer. However, I worry that pregnancy might make my migraines worse or that this condition could somehow be passed to my children, which I want to prevent. A few other issues that might be connected are cold hands and feet, difficulty staying warm, and having no libido.

I’m incredibly grateful to have come across Jack Kruse’s work and feel optimistic about finding answers.

### Nashley — 2025-01-24T03:13:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345668>

### I listened to “Getting Started with Cold Thermogenesis” webinar today. If was so helpful and inspiring. One of my questions I had for Jack in the up coming powpow is using cold for the pain when I have a migraine attack. In the webinar he said one of the best ways is to put ice packs on your carotid artery and femur artery, going to give this a try next time I have an attack! On the webinar Jack talk about a lot of the problems ct helps, I’m going to make it a part of my life and become cold adapted. I’m still not sure what the cause is of the migraines but I’m certain Ct will in improve this and many other things. I heard Jack say in a Q and A, “treat your condition as if you already have cancer”, that’s how I am going to eradicate the migraines. I’m writing this entry as a sit in cold tub of water, legs only today , tomorrow ice woman!​

### Nashley — 2025-01-26T21:17:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345778>

Had a migraine this morning, tried ice on both sides of carotid artery, reduce the pain a little but still ended up throwing up and resorting to aspirin, so disappointed. I really was hoping it was going to work and wouldn’t need other pain relieve.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-01-26T22:04:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345780>

I had a client that got bad migraines and NIR light on the temples helped them.

### Nashley — 2025-01-30T17:36:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-345992>

While I agree that EMF and blue light aren’t good for anyone, I believe there’s more to my migraines than just that. I started experiencing them at six years old, which could have been influenced by classroom lighting and EMF exposure from devices. However, if that were the main cause, it would stand to reason that most of the girls I grew up with—who lived the same lifestyle—would have had migraines too, yet I don’t know of anyone else who did. Additionally, I’ve spent summers deep in the wilderness, far from cell phones and technology, sleeping on the ground, yet I still experienced migraines during those times.

This also doesn’t explain why my migraines are primarily tied to my cycle. If blue light and EMF were the main causes, I would expect to experience them constantly rather than in a distinct pattern. While I do occasionally get migraines from lack of sleep or stress, those triggers are rare for me. Given this, I truly believe something isn’t right with my hormones.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-10T06:11:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicole-ashley-optimal-journal.30149/post-346524>

However, I worry that pregnancy might make my migraines worse or that this condition could somehow be passed to my children, which I want to prevent. A few other issues that might be connected are cold hands and feet, difficulty staying warm, and having no libido.
>
>
> I’m incredibly grateful to have come across Jack Kruse’s work and feel optimistic about finding answers.

Just my thoughts that may help you look into a few different areas -

Have you looked into the role of magnesium on the NMDA receptor? it plays a role in stabilizing this receptor involved in neural signalling (movement of ions through these receptors can influence electrical signals within the brain). A deficiency in Mg can lead to the over stimulation of the NMDA receptor (potentially contributing to pathophysiology of migraines). This link has been studied and published in papers (plug it into a search engine).

Are you around lots of nn-EMF ? have you had your environment checked by a building biologist? As nn-EMF will interact with the VGCCs (Voltage Gated Calcium Channels) leading excess inward leaking of calcium within the cell & excess Ca can displace Mg ions leading to Mg deficiency within the cell etc..

The link to your cycle could be -
(Ovulation) ~ day 14 (out of 28) - progesterone can increase Mg excretion through urine. This can carry through luteal phase up until menstruation.

If you drink caffeine - this will be further exacerbated.

If not linked to Mg deficiency, there could be many other possibilities, however this could be a place for you to look into.
Hope this helps.

Hi there,

How are you doing now?

It took me maybe 20 years to link nnEMFs + external stressors to on and off serious fatigue and sleep problems.

Some ideas I gathered on this site and elsewhere which may be helpful/true or not.
\- estrogen (first half of cycle) is protective against nnEMFs. Are your symptoms worse second part of your cycle?
\- menses are a way a women detox and gets rid of deuterium too. That to me indicates that in women the felt impact of any external toxins will fluctuate with their cycle.
\- any stressor, environmental or mental, will generate calcum efflux and nnEMF will do so 24/7 unless mitigated/reduced
\- in Dr. Sircus book on transdermal magnesium I read that magnesium deficiency has very similar effects as nnEMFs. He also refers to Mg in chlorophyl and to the sun and it reminded me of Dr. Kruse mentioning hemoglobin and iron as the human counterpart for chlorophyl.

I know of a man with strong migranes who is a vegetarian working and relaxing with computers and screens in the city for about 30years now.
He saw many MDs and seems to have never found help in centralized medicine.

Regarding non-environmlental factors, I wonder about individual differences in magnetite crystals in the brain for instance.

Hope you found/find relief.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345599>

My daughter has juvenile myoclonic epilepsy. She has taken generic keppra for 12 years. We recently discovered grounding. But one post mentioned after an hour and a half on a grounding sheet, a girl had a seizure. My daughter lives with a lot of fear and anxiety. Does anyone have any information concerning a connection? We have been grounding outside, and she reports a calmness, but I'm worried about anything that plugs in. Any help or direction would be so appreciated!
Thank you

Our daughter has Benign Rolandic Epilepsy. What has helped us is time outside, grounding (feet on the ground), and candlelight at night.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epilepsy-seizures-and-grounding-indoors.30151/post-345985>

If you want to be grounded at night . . . Sleep on the ground - outside.
Getting natural light requires being outside under the sun NOT sitting inside under artificial light.
Being grounded in Nature requires being OUTSIDE in Nature with your body in contact with the ground NOT a metallic pad attached to a wire connected to the ground. A mat connected to a wire is NOT the ground.
If metal bed frames and metal springs in mattresses are BAD how can a metallic pad connected to a metal wire be good? The wire acts as an antenna just like the springs in a mattress. Think about it.
Even if you do turn off the AC current, IF you do use a “grounding” pad attached to a wire connected to the house ground - a 2,000ft2 house has about 1/2 a mile of wire in it. That is a very large antenna . . . Not very natural. A 50ft (+/-) wire out the window is still an antenna and not natural.

# Thread: Unable to turn off current to my bedroom because of GFI's setup
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-turn-off-current-to-my-bedroom-because-of-gfis-setup.30156/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jackfoster — 2025-01-23T05:04:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-turn-off-current-to-my-bedroom-because-of-gfis-setup.30156/post-345611>

It might be difficult to explain here and I have really looked into it but I am unable to turn off the current to my bedroom because of the way the GFIs are set up in the home I live in. My body voltage in bed is around .1 I think I have done as much as I can with moving the positioning of my bed unplugging and removing all electronics but is there anything else I could do to try to lower the voltage in my room even further without turning off the current?

### Lukassss — 2025-01-23T08:13:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-turn-off-current-to-my-bedroom-because-of-gfis-setup.30156/post-345615>

The cost version is:Grounded canopy.

Economy version:
If your bed is in the corner of the room, you can cover the walls and floor with aluminum foil and ground it.

### Mat.I — 2025-01-23T08:28:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-turn-off-current-to-my-bedroom-because-of-gfis-setup.30156/post-345616>

> [jackfoster said:](/goto/post?id=345611)
>
> It might be difficult to explain here and I have really looked into it but I am unable to turn off the current to my bedroom because of the way the GFIs are set up in the home I live in. My body voltage in bed is around .1 I think I have done as much as I can with moving the positioning of my bed unplugging and removing all electronics but is there anything else I could do to try to lower the voltage in my room even further without turning off the current?

Is your BV 100mV (0.1 V)? If so and you have measured it correctly I wouldn't be too concerned providing no contact currents are present or use of grounding mats inside. The SBM BB guidelines aim for <10mV for no concern however this is generally not realistic. The slight concern range is 10mV to 100mV, severe is 100mV to 1000mV, extreme is over 1000mV.

Given your earlier posts on the forum you may have to be a bit stricter and mitigate nnemf as much as possible..

### Mat.I — 2025-01-23T08:32:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-turn-off-current-to-my-bedroom-because-of-gfis-setup.30156/post-345617>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=345616)
>
> Is your BV 100mV (0.1 V)? If so and you have measured it correctly I wouldn't be too concerned providing no contact currents are present or use of grounding mats inside. The SBM BB guidelines aim for <10mV for no concern however this is generally not realistic. The slight concern range is 10mV to 100mV, severe is 100mV to 1000mV, extreme is over 1000mV.
>
> Given your earlier posts on the forum you may have to be a bit stricter and mitigate nnemf as much as possible..

# Thread: NEW from tiny Parkville, MO.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-tiny-parkville-mo.30157/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### js92014 — 2025-01-23T05:35:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-tiny-parkville-mo.30157/post-345612>

Hello fellow truth seekers!
I guess I will start at the beginning, seems like a logical place. I am Jerry, I am a consumer commodity relocation specialist- a class A professional driver for a global LTL company. My wife is an RN, currently working from home in a hospice leadership position. She has also done infusion work, but went back to hospice because that's what she loves. We have 3 kids- boys, all boys... all three born in May and 8 years apart. We have MY 24 year old, HER 16 year old and OUR 8 year old. We just built our dream home FULL of LED lights- ugh! My wife has several ailments, mainly migraines. Anyone here who themselves suffers from them or has a loved one who does knows how painful it is to watch someone you love hurt and you're helpless. Of course doctors just want to poison her with meds. Several years ago we found the migraine food list, and number one on it was onions which we ate a ton of. Armed with that list and our new paleo/gluten free diet we have managed to cut her migraine frequency way down. Now, were not perfect... we have had a hard time sticking to gluten free because some of the alternatives are terrible- but we try. We get all our meat fresh from a friend who raises them free range grain/antibiotic free. We are a seafood family to a degree, my wife can't have shellfish because of the histimines in it cause migraines (or so we've read years ago). We eat a lot of salmon, but I know its not wild caught- we will shop better for this from now on. We do not consume any raw meat, poultry or seafood. There are other fish types we eat as well, no tilapia ever as we heard its an engineered fish. We try to shop farmers markets for produce, but that's tough this time of year. We do not cook from boxes or by way of a microwave, all of our meals are prepared at home using fresh (as possible) ingredients with no or few items with any preservatives. We make our own chili, spaghetti, fajita, and jerky seasonings as every one in the store contains preservatives. We get no vaccines and our 8 year old has not ever had a shot in his life. His pediatrician told us not to bring him back after he was born unless we we're going to let him poison our child. I guess this is a good time to say that due to my wife working from home she also home schools our youngest. Our two older boys have had very minimal vaccines in their younger years, and NO FAUCI OUCHY for any of us.

Two weeks ago I heard a snippet of Dr. Jack Kruse on a podcast, it was about a 15 minute cut. That was all I needed to head out on a broad search for more. I was delighted to find lots of podcasts on Rumble and Spotify, which made me extremely happy because those are two platforms I subscribe to. I am not a fan of social media at all. Anyway, I started listening and 2 hours went to 4 went to days and days now. I have largely given up trying to talk to anyone anymore about health or politics, its generally a complete waste of my time. However, I am passing on links to Dr. Kruse videos and podcasts and its truly catching on. We are die hard conservatives and I never thought anyone would drag me away from my political podcasts- until now.

We are eager to start this process, and we think were at a good jumping off point because of the years of paleo and gluten free practice. I am REALLY wanting to start light therapy on all of us, and get screen protectors for our devices. I have done many hours of research awaiting approval to access these forums, but am very excited to communicate with good folks who have long started this particular therapy. I have not spent a dime as of yet because I've no idea where to begin, and finding anything in the 380-400NM rating is nearly impossible. I am finding lots of stuff in the 600 plus up to about 960, but nothing higher either. I know more than one podcast I listened to Dr. Kruse mentions the importance of both 380 and 1000. Some of these lights are very, very pricey and I don't want to buy without knowing the prescription for each wavelength- distance from the light, need full body or no, how long of exposure, which wavelengths require eye protection, etc. I am more interested in lighting that can be easily moved versus installing one, so my wife can easily move it about the house as she works, teaches, cooks etc. Reversing the affects of blue light is something we can easily do now, then we can move on to refining diet, increasing sun exposure, waking for sunrise, and other regimens as we learn from this community.

So- this is us.

I would like to thank everyone in advance for any advice on lighting and screen protectors that you all recommend, I assure you that you won't be wasting your time as I will begin buying immediately. We look forward to a long and healthy future right here! I apologize in advance for the length of my intro and I hope I didn't violate any TOS in posting it.

### LoriLee — 2025-01-25T04:28:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-from-tiny-parkville-mo.30157/post-345729>

We look forward to a long and healthy future right here! I apologize in advance for the length of my intro and I hope I didn't violate any TOS in posting it.

I bought the Sperti UVA lamp which is I think 370 mm light. 1280 is the other range Dr Kruse always mentions which is present with sunlight I believe. The Sperti lamp is only a crutch. Nothing compared to 380 from the sun. Go to El Salvador. We just went for a month and it was incredible. Check out block blue light for a full spectrum light you can move about the house. Get daytime and nighttime blue blockers. I got ours from EMR tek. Welcome!

# Thread: Hello! Healing CFS/ME
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### darrenFC — 2025-01-23T10:16:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345622>

Hello all, I am a long time reader of Dr. Kruse's work. I am from India but lived abroad in Stockholm and Paris for a few years. As Jack predicts, I got sick at these high latitudes. Specifically my symptoms match those of CFS/ME quite closely. I find that I am highly sensitive to nnEMF, to the point where turning on the 4g on my cellphone gives me a pretty severe reaction causing intestinal distress, hives, etc. As I reduced my nnEMF exposure I find that I become more sensitive to when I am around Wifi or 4g (such as visiting friends etc.) I am not really sure if it's the sensitivity that causes CFS or it is a consequence of CFS.

Jack has a post on CFS on linkedin which I've tried really hard to understand, it is tricky with the decrease in cognitive ability. He says you must renovate melanin externally and then internally before you can use grounding and cold thermogeneis or you will hinder your recovery instead of helping it. I didn't understand this point although I am attempting to do so at the 15th latitude. along with eating more fat and seafood. I use redlight, creatine, lions mane which help with my physical and cognitive ability. I also removed all blue lights using only candles and incandescents.

The healing journey has taught me so much about myself and the world. In many ways, it has been a great gift that allowed me to know myself better, and become who I really am.

Thank you for reading, I would appreciate any comments, reading recommendations, personal experiences etc, that can put me in the right direction to heal.

much love,

Darren

### NickD — 2025-01-23T14:44:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345626>

Hey man, I'm from India as well. Although we live in Australia now, the same concept applies to different latitudes. I work shifts so for me the priority is getting out in the sun as much as possible. How's your sun exposure going?

### jackfoster — 2025-01-23T18:37:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345640>

I would also like to better understand the concept of renovating melanin externally the internally before using grounding and CT

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345649>

### NickD — 2025-01-24T06:49:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345681>

I think renovating melanin means getting out in the sun and building up your melanin production...

### darrenFC — 2025-01-24T10:24:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345685>

> [NickD said:](/goto/post?id=345681)

> I think renovating melanin means getting out in the sun and building up your melanin production...

hello Nick! yep, that's basically what it is, and also avoiding a few foods that interfere with melanin production. I was confused about why this needs to be done before beginning grounding or CT though. Here is the link to the relevant article: [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-short-jack-kruse/](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-short-jack-kruse/)

I can empathise with you about shift work! I did three years of it in the oil & gas industry. I think that played a big part in my current illness but sounds like you have the tools and information to mitigate the damage
Regarding sun exposure, I try to get 3-4 hours a day which seems to be the time required for my Fitzpatrick skin type

what foods, pls? i also have me/cfs

### darrenFC — 2025-01-28T04:18:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345861>

> [Sunny Day said:](/goto/post?id=345779)

The list includes chocolate, ginger, red wine, MAO inhibitors, and antihistamines. Also tattoos

it might be worth buying the melanin renovation Rx blog on patreon, i think it is 25USD for the standalone blog, there are some nuances in there

I got a lot of sun the last two months, it helped up to a point and then plateaued. What helped me next was installing a grounding rod outside and connecting it to a grounding mat. I don't get any symptoms of post-exertional malaise anymore if i ground after activity. I also have a TENS unit that helps with the pain, and they are super cheap. I noticed a big difference after moving to a nnemf clean (relatively) environment.

Next I'm going to try and make my own magnetico type thing because it's just incredibly expensive and I don't even think they ship to India. working my way through Light>magnetism>water

Let's stay in touch so we can compare notes  it is a complex disease for sure but I am optimistic

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-01-28T10:08:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345871>

> [darrenFC said:](/goto/post?id=345861)
>
> The list includes chocolate, ginger, red wine, MAO inhibitors, and antihistamines. Also tattoos
>
> it might be worth buying the melanin renovation Rx blog on patreon, i think it is 25USD for the standalone blog, there are some nuances in there

I don't get any symptoms of post-exertional malaise anymore if i ground after activity. I also have a TENS unit that helps with the pain, and they are super cheap. I noticed a big difference after moving to a nnemf clean (relatively) environment.
>
> Next I'm going to try and make my own magnetico type thing because it's just incredibly expensive and I don't even think they ship to India. working my way through Light>magnetism>water
>
> Let's stay in touch so we can compare notes  it is a complex disease for sure but I am optimistic

Where in India are you ? Because don't get me wrong, India is people soup

You're never far away from anyone. Anyone could be in your area using their devices.
I get a distinct worsening when my brother comes home sets up.all.his laptops leaves them plugged in and has enough wiring to create a van Allen belt ( I exaggerate here for literary licence, )

In just one corner of the kitchen dining room area. And this is a house on an Indian scale. Not a British one ,even the inverse square law doesn't work because he's on it day in day out for 4 days of the week. I heave a sigh of relief when he's away but it's not enough

Plus he's jabbed three times. 2 astra Zeneca and one Pfizer

Thankfully he's fat. He weighs 93kg
And he's slighter shorter. But it has had its effects. And they're awful. He's completely centralised with a touch of supplementitis

He's always irritable, always irritating and threatens violence. Just like the teenagers addicted to their mobile phones. Of which he has three
And which he spends more time on , at dinner table etc

His brain has been affected badly. Sadly he doesn't realise he's killing himself slowly with his tech

He's behind a screen from 7.30 am to almost midnight at night. **Why do you think a non resident desi like me is aching to get on the plane to central America?**

For my health. I'm hopefully leading by example

Within 9 months of joining as a member I had gone to central America, Panama the area you see around Rio Hato and spent 3 months there Found it great

Then time in El Salvador but I had bad luck. I ended up with an Airbnb that had a damn transmitter within eyesight . I had to cancel it at some cost and decided that I'd look around but I had to curtail the time in El Salvador. Looking back on it. Bad move. I should have just moved around until I got an area I preferred.

Retrospectroscope vision is 20/20. Sometimes 35/20

Get out of the people soup.

This time. I've made sure to choose where I l live carefully and even if it costs more than I originally planned. You want to ask me where DM me

See the difference between one month in and three months in

Great Airbnb but turn 90 degrees and that great big 4G transmitter tower is there ,I felt crap despite the sunshine

### Inger — 2025-01-28T11:35:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345876>

> [Sunny Day said:](/goto/post?id=345779)
>
> what foods, pls? i also have me/cfs
>
> reading the linkedin article you posted. idk about light being a simple fix of me/cfs. lots of us get lots of light and still sick. i've also lived at equator and got lots of sun, and still sick.

I have been able to almost completely heal my cfs in 2 years and I live in Finland. I have done it with leaving out all stimulants like chocolate, coffee, alcohol, nicotine, sugar in any form, stopping keto/carnivore and adding 3 cups of legumes (no soybeans) to my diet spread out over the day about 6 times to soak up bile(here is where the excess stress hormones and toxins are). Also I do animal protein 3 times a day at least, a palm big portion. Eating a cup of veggies at each meal. Eating other carbs like quinoa, sourdough bread etc if I feel the need. No dairy.
No supplements! Just drinking water and eat real food like a little kid.
I do have some small cheats once in a while but I have pretty much followed this through.

Rest and rest and rest. Working way less. Working on my trauma (beans/soluble fiber is a big help here as they are so calming)

I of course do sun and grounding too when possible and mind my light environment etc. But I got cfs even if I did this because of pushing my body too hard and fighting reality.
I highly recommend Karen Hurds bean protocol. It doesnt cost anything if you search the information for free on youtube or instagram (uniquehammond), also there is a Karen Hurd facebook group where I have posted my success story.

### Inger — 2025-01-28T12:53:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345881>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=345876)
>
> I have been able to almost completely heal my cfs in 2 years and I live in Finland. I have done it with leaving out all stimulants like chocolate, coffee, alcohol, nicotine, sugar in any form, stopping keto/carnivore and adding 3 cups of legumes (no soybeans) to my diet spread out over the day about 6 times to soak up bile(here is where the excess stress hormones and toxins are). Also I do animal protein 3 times a day at least, a palm big portion. Eating a cup of veggies at each meal. Eating other carbs like quinoa, sourdough bread etc if I feel the need. No dairy.
> No supplements! Just drinking water and eat real food like a little kid.
> I do have some small cheats once in a while but I have pretty much followed this through.
>
> Rest and rest and rest. Working way less. Working on my trauma (beans/soluble fiber is a big help here as they are so calming)
>
> I of course do sun and grounding too when possible and mind my light environment etc. But I got cfs even if I did this because of pushing my body too hard and fighting reality.
> I highly recommend Karen Hurds bean protocol. It doesnt cost anything if you search the information for free on youtube or instagram (uniquehammond), also there is a Karen Hurd facebook group where I have posted my success story.

I have to add to this, if one would have the possibility to spend 3 months resting on a beach doing this protocol without any cheats, the healing would be complete. That is what Karen says and i believe her! The adrenal gland cells renew in 3 months. So that would be a super quick healing. But the reality look different for the most of us so the healing often takes around 2 years.
Through a friend I got the opportunity to spend a couple weeks on beaches in central America last fall and i can tell I felt how the healing speed up!
In one week I will be in Mexico again camping on a beach for maybe 6 weeks or so with another friend that invited me, and I am going to complete my healing there i am sure. I have promised my former boss to work full time at the oyster beach again in Germany, so that will be the test how resilient I have become
I used to be so exhausted I could barely walk to the mailbox some days. I only felt my deep exhaustion when i stopped all stimulants. They are like speed. It is not true energy and it is fooling oneself. I was so drug addicted. To caffeine, nicotine, and alcohol. And I denied it for myself. Until the suffering became too big and my body crashed.

I am a little hesitant to put up my pictures here but I will do it anyways because it might inspire somebody. I gained quite a bit of weight in the beginning and it was not fun, but I knew it belonged to the process so I did not fight it.

I took a picture after one year on the protocol, and then one picture yesterday, after 2 years bean protocol.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/photo_2025-01-08_18-36-38-jpg.29925/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/photo_2025-01-27_13-52-07-jpg.29926/)

What I forgot to say is, no exercise is allowed. Only slow walks, and slow yoga/stretching. I started using my mini rebounder a year ago just a minute slow jumps and I felt so bad after. I slowly got used to it and now I do slow jumps for about 10 minutes a day most days, it does not raise my heart beat and it feels so good for the lymph. I highly recommend it after the initial resting phase. This summer i will build my muscles again. It all has to go very slow and little by little

### NickD — 2025-01-28T13:28:03+0200

I had a little chuckle at this. It's exactly how I grew up LOL. 1 room, filling up water in the morning for the day. Heat up water if you want warm water. Communal toilet. It was a real spin-out when we moved to Australia and there was hot water in the tap! I had my own room! Crazy stuff! hahaha Although, I was happier in a simpler life. That was 20 years ago now of course. All the nnEMF etc would definitely be affecting me.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-01-28T13:45:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-healing-cfs-me.30159/post-345885>

> [NickD said:](/goto/post?id=345884)

> I had a little chuckle at this. It's exactly how I grew up LOL. 1 room, filling up water in the morning for the day. Heat up water if you want warm water. Communal toilet. It was a real spin-out when we moved to Australia and there was hot water in the tap! I had my own room! Crazy stuff! hahaha Although, I was happier in a simpler life. That was 20 years ago now of course. All the nnEMF etc would definitely be affecting me.

Mum had Dad build a palace of a bungalow. With large airy reception rooms that could all be opened up into one space much like Jack's current house and a patio door in glass that could be opened too ,to bring the outside in .

So she didn't live in "pigeon holes" like the general population

I upgraded from a boxroom in a 3 bed semi to a room that I thought was small when I compared it to my mum's the builder had a laugh at me and said it was a big room and even now the Noddy boxes that are built have smaller master bedrooms

It's a bugger to heat though , but that wasn't a consideration 44 years ago

Even 44 years later it's the biggest bungalow in the town.
It holds loads of memories and loads of things because partition turned my parents into hoarders. Paper, rubbish, gets thrown out but some of our appliances date from the 1970s and stil work fine ...

Trauma messes with peoples heads

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-01-28T14:05:17+0200

That's the difference between me in Panama. The first one was when I was starting to recover. After a month. Then on the last day when I could walk up and down a hill to the beach with no problems.

I had relaxed and spent 2-3 hours on the beach every morning. And then in the afternoon I'd spend time in the pool with another retired French Canadian who was born in French Algeria and who doesn't use sunscreen and sunglasses.

There's a mismatch between him and his wife who has Hungarian roots

This particular guy, kept begging me to return . So let's see what the future brings . They became good friends in just 6 weeks and actually saw me off before I left for the airport.

India of course is people soup, but it's a very big country. For instance, I live on the west coast in a sleepy village by the beach. We only get one tiny bar of phone signal here across all cell service providers. My bigest EMF concerns are the WiFi router next door and the AC grid which appears to be poorly grounded. I have a custom made faraday cage for the router en route, and I stay away from anything plugged into the grid as much as possible.

I have thought about moving many times. it is difficult to explain, I would rather stay here and make the best of the situation as far as I can. I am able to help some of my friends and family with all the information I've learned from Jack and others. In short, I am able to be of service here even if it's not the absolute best place for me

I think most people with CFS have unresolved trauma and slowing down is key no matter what protocols you are following

Unresolved trauma resonates strongly with me. My CFS came swiftly on not long after three major deaths in the family one of them a murder in less than 5 months , loss of my career by the GMC for nothing more than being a nice guy to the clients /patients
and pissing off the pharmaceutical industry with Vitamin D3 and heartbreak in 2010

I really had lost direction and control of my destiny at that point in time , questioning why I had failed and what I really wanted to do for the next 50-55 years ahead of me.
Looking back this probably explains why I didn't just Google on How to buy bitcoin in 2011. Darn and blast it. But I probably would have spent it when it got higher

I somehow knew that I needed to buy it but I was too far gone from higher functioning by 2011 I couldn't even remember 3 minute monologues whereas before I could quote long sections word perfectly

Bitcoin and finding Dr Jack Kruse just under 2 years ago has appreciably improved matters

I remember when I was happiest at a holiday in Nice. My parents dropped me off at a beach, I think by myself. Because I don't remember them joining me, and I spent a long time there chatting to the other adults and just pottering around

Hands off parents in the 1970s. Nowadays you wouldn't leave an eight year old by themselves. I had plucked out a lot of hair from my scalp on the right hand side so I looked badly tonsured ( I knew it was called trichotillomania as a kid ). But 8 weeks in Europe cured the trauma and I felt better. Bullying at school was awful. I talked to the adult teachers. The peers weren't truly interested unless they were the years above or below. Another paper now released to show you why you should avoid the bad advice coming from Peter Attia and Bryan Johnson. Staying out of the sun and into manmade light directly impacts longevity.

Sunlight increases longevity by controlling the epigenetic longevity clocks linked to the sex steroid hormones. Select loci throughout the genome undergo predictable and consistent methylation gain or loss, which, when considered individually or collectively, have enabled the development of molecular age predictors commonly referred to as epigenetic clocks. Sunlight affects the sex steroid clocks in all mammals.

Initially developed for estimating chronological age, these epigenetic clocks are now understood to capture elements of biological age and may be precisely tailored to measure either form of aging or even to predict human mortality risk. This is why a lack of sunlight leads to less longevity in men and why it causes PCOS in women. Science is beginning to catch up to Uncle Jack's wisdom. If you are pale and do not understand POMC biology and abuse blue light and nnEMF, you will die sooner. My Decentralized Thesis and the Garden of Eden: A Parallel of Human Design and Modern Disruption

Imagine the Garden of Eden (GOE) as the blueprint for humanity’s optimal state, perfectly attuned to Nature’s rhythms, where sunlight, circadian alignment, and cellular harmony sustained life as God or evolution intended. In this divine or evolutionary design, 20% of hemoglobin (Hb) delivers oxygen from the heart to the brain, fueling cognition, consciousness, and our unique mammalian identity. But what happens when that 20% is preserved in volume yet corrupted in function, with Hb oxidized to metHb (+3 state), unable to carry oxygen? Can we still embody the fullness of humanity as planned? This question mirrors the decentralized thesis we’ve been exploring: a vision of systems, biological, social, or technological, operating in harmony with Nature’s principles, free from centralized distortions. Like a flawed centralized protocol, modern life disrupts this design, and the consequences are profound.

The Fall from Eden: Modern Life’s Assault on Biology

Just as the GOE represents humanity’s symbiosis with Nature, our decentralized thesis champions systems that align with natural laws—autonomous, resilient, and adaptive. Today’s world, however, imposes a “centralized” override: artificial light, non-native EMF (nnEMF), and 24/7 tech divorce us from daylight and drown us in nightlight. This mirrors the epigenetic fall from Eden, where each generation strays further from the original blueprint. Chronic diseases, cancer, neurodegeneration, and metabolic collapse emerge not as random failures but as predictable outcomes of this mismatch, accelerating transgenerationally as tech and light abuse intensify. The decentralized thesis warns that centralized systems (like modern tech-driven lifestyles) erode resilience, creating fragility where Nature intended antifragility.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-01-24T20:08:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shedding-due-to-jabs-is-real.30166/post-345700>

Shedding due to jabs is real.

Peters et al. found that women with daily close proximity (within 6 feet) to vaccinated individuals outside their household had:
\- 34% higher risk of heavier bleeding.
\- 28% higher risk of menstruation starting over 7 days early.
\- 26% higher risk of bleeding lasting more than 7 days. The authors concluded,
“Our findings suggest possible indirect transmission of ingredients or products of the COVID-19 vaccines, presumably through shedding, from people who received one or more of the COVID-19 injections.”

### [Menstrual Abnormalities Strongly Associated with Proximity to COVID-19 Vaccinated Individuals | International Journal of Vaccine Theory, Practice, and Research](https://ijvtpr.com/index.php/IJVTPR/article/view/113)

 ijvtpr.com

Time, Light, the JAB, and Mitochondrial Hypoxia

Time theft is irreplaceable nature, which directly extends to how light impacts mitochondrial function at the mtDNA level, where ultraweak photon emissions (UPEs) are generated in mammals. Mitochondria rely on circadian timing to optimize energy transformation via the electron transport chain (ETC), using sunlight to drive photobioelectric currents. Sunlight, rich in red and near-infrared wavelengths (600–1000 nm), penetrates tissues and enhances cytochrome c oxidase (CCO) activity, boosting ATP production and reducing reactive oxygen species (ROS). This process is disrupted by hypoxia at the mtDNA level, where UPE spectra shift, often narrowing and weakening, mimicing the current solar cycle and that manifests in us as a chronic disease.

Light stress caused all 5 extinction events on earth because it lead to a metabolic stress in living things they could not adapt to fast enough.

Artificial light, like LEDs, lacks the full spectrum of sunlight, particularly red/infrared wavelengths, and emits high levels of blue light (400–500 nm). Blue light inhibits CCO by increasing ROS, leading to hypoxia and mitochondrial dysfunction (heteroplasmy). This “subtracts time” from our life spans by accelerating cellular aging: disrupted circadian rhythms (e.g., via melanopsin activation in the retina) desynchronize the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN), impairing mitochondrial energy production. Over time, this manifests as chronic diseases—diabetes, autoimmunity, or turbo cancers because energy flow, governed by time, is disrupted in a myriad of ways. here is a lesson of how sunglasses, glasses, IoL, and contacts can murder you rapidly if you've been jabbed. That being said…Racine, WI is where I live… They just announced that we may be the first “Smart City”. Microsoft data center is currently being built in our backyard. What are your thoughts on this progression with technology. I want to educate my community on the effects of all this non-native EMF and what the possibility of this development of our city may do to our health.

Honestly…it makes me want to move away…but I feel it is my duty to educate the community about the possibility of a Smart City effecting the future of their health.

Thoughts?

Everything “smart” uses WiFi and Bluetooth which both utilize energy at 2.4Ghz.

R-eporting
T-echnology

### China’s DeepSeek Bombshell Rocks Trump’s $500BN AI Boondoggle​

[by Zero Hedge](https://www.zerohedge.com/technology/chinas-deepseek-bombshell-rocks-trumps-500bn-ai-boondoggle)January 25th, 2025 12:04 PM
AI was supposed to be the fast-track to absolute societal control and oligarchic rule into the next millennia, but now those pesky Chinese have overturned the applecart leaving western elites with a problem they might not be able to fix.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Image Credit: imaginima/Getty Images
SHARE
PostTweetMessageEmail
LIVE
[](https://gab.com/RealAlexJones)
_**The future of humanity is being decided as we speak. And it is not being decided on a battlefield in Eastern Europe, or the Middle East or the Taiwan Strait, but in the data centers and research facilities where technology experts create “the physical and virtual infrastructure to power the next generation of Artificial Intelligence.”**_
This is a full-blown, scorched-earth free-for-all that has already racked up a number of casualties though you wouldn’t know it from reading the headlines which typically ignore recent ‘cataclysmic’ developments. But when [President Trump announced the launching of a $500 billion AI infrastructure project (Stargate) on Tuesday](https://www.cnn.com/2025/01/21/tech/openai-oracle-softbank-trump-ai-investment/index.html) just hours after China had released its **DeepSeek R1—which “outperforms its rivals in advanced coding, math, and general knowledge capabilities”**—it became painfully obvious that the battle for the future ‘is on’ in a big way. And this is not a battle that either side can afford to lose. Here’s how technology expert Adam Button summed it up:

> _**Imagine we’re back in 2017 and the iPhone X was just released. It was selling $999 and Apple was crushing sales and building a wide moat around its ecosystem.
> Now imagine, just days later, another company introduced a phone and platform that was equal in every way if not better and the price was just $30.
> That’s what unfolded in the AI space today. China’s DeepSeek released an opensource model that works on par with OpenAI’s latest models but costs a tiny fraction to operate.** Moreover, you can even download it and run it free (or the cost of your electricity) for yourself.
> The product is a huge leap in terms of scaling and efficiency and may upend expectations of how much power and compute will be needed to manage the AI revolution. **It also comes just hours before Trump is expected to unveil a $100 billion investment in US datacenters.** The model shows there are different ways to train foundational AI models that offer up the same results with much less cost. It also opens up far more applications for AI that would have been too expensive to run previously, which should broaden the applications in the real economy.
> [– China’s DeepSeek may have just upended the economics of AI,](https://www.forexlive.com/news/chinas-deepseek-may-have-just-upended-the-economics-of-ai-20250121/) forex live_

**Imagine the panic that is spreading across western tech capitals right now.** AI was supposed to be the fast-track to absolute societal control and oligarchic rule into the next millennia, but now those pesky Chinese have overturned the applecart leaving western elites with a problem they might not be able to fix. (See—[Unchecked AI will lead us to a police state](https://edri.org/our-work/unchecked-ai-will-lead-us-to-a-police-state/), edri ) They expected that their microchip sanctions would sabotage China’s AI efforts for at least a decade-or-so but, instead, China has come roaring back with a system that has left the tech giants gasping for air.

Of course, China’s eye-popping strides in technological development are nothing new as editor Ron Unz pointed out in a recent article where he noted that **“between 2003 and 2007, the US led in 60 of the 64 technologies.” Whereas, as of 2022, “China led in 52 of the 64 technologies.” That’s not a competition; that’s a beat-down in a parking lot**. Here’s Unz:

> _China now leads the world in many of the most important future technologies. The success of its commercial companies in telecommunications (Huawei, Zongxin), EV (BYD, Geely, Great Wall, etc.), battery (CATL, BYD) and Photovoltaics (Tongwei Solar, JA, Aiko, etc.) are directly built on such R&D prowess.
> Similarly, the Chinese military’s modernization is built on the massive technological development of the country’s scientific community and its industrial base…. With its lead in science and technology research, China is positioned to outcompete the US in both economic and military arenas in the coming years…. [American Pravda: China vs. America](https://www.unz.com/runz/america-vs-china-a-comprehensive-review-of-the-economic-technological-and-military-factors/), Ron Unz, Unz Review_

None of this should come as a surprise, although the timing of DeepSeek’s release (preempting Trump’s Stargate announcement) shows that the Chinese don’t mind throwing a wrench in Washington’s global strategy if it serves their regional interests, which it undoubtedly does. Here’s a bit more background from an article by Benj Edwards at _Ars Technica_:

> _On Monday, Chinese AI lab DeepSeek released its new R1 model family under an open MIT license, with its largest version containing 671 billion parameters. **The company claims the model performs at levels comparable to OpenAI’s o1 simulated reasoning (SR) model on several math and coding benchmarks….**
> The releases immediately caught the attention of the AI community because most existing open-weights models—have lagged behind proprietary models like OpenAI’s o1 in so-called reasoning benchmarks. …
> The R1 model works differently from typical large language models ….They attempt to simulate a human-like chain of thought as the model works through a solution to the query. This class of what one might call **“simulated reasoning” models, or SR models for short,** emerged when OpenAI debuted its o1 model family in September 2024. …
> **DeepSeek reports that R1 outperformed OpenAI’s o1 on several benchmarks and tests, including AIME (a mathematical reasoning test), MATH-500 (a collection of word problems), and SWE-bench Verified (a programming assessment tool)….**
> TechCrunch reports that three Chinese labs—DeepSeek, Alibaba, and Moonshot AI’s Kimi—have now released models they say match OpenAI’s o1’s capabilities, with DeepSeek first previewing R1 in November. [Cutting-edge Chinese “reasoning” model rivals OpenAI o1—and it’s free to download](https://arstechnica.com/ai/2025/01/china-is-catching-up-with-americas-best-reasoning-ai-models/), ars technica_

This is a very big deal. The United States intends to dominate the world in this critical technology and yet **the upstart Chinese have not only produced a system that is every bit as good as America’s best, but have made it more affordable, more accessible and more transparent. What’s not to like?**

_(Note—OpenAI is an American artificial intelligence (AI) research laboratory. It is made up of the non-profit OpenAI Incorporated and **its for-profit subsidiary corporation OpenAI Limited Partnership. OpenAI has emerged to be one of the primary leaders of the generative AI era. OpenAI is a privately held company that has open sourced some of its technology, but it has not open sourced most of its technology…. In contrast, DeepSeek AI R1 is open source which means its code is publicly accessible—anyone can see, modify, and distribute the code as they see fit**. Open source software is developed in a decentralized and collaborative way, relying on peer review and community production.)_

Here’s more from political analyst Arnaud Bertrand in a post on X:

> **Most people probably don’t realize how bad the news (about) China’s Deepseek is for OpenAI. They’ve come up with a model that matches and even exceeds OpenAI’s latest model o1 on various benchmarks, and they’re charging just 3% of the price. It’s essentially as if someone had released a mobile on par with the iPhone but was selling it for $30 instead of $1000. It’s this dramatic.
> What’s more, they’re releasing it open-source so you even have the option – which OpenAI doesn’t offer – of not using their API at all and running the model for “free” yourself.**
> If you’re an OpenAI customer today you’re obviously going to start asking yourself some questions, like “wait, why exactly should I be paying 30X more?”. This is pretty transformational stuff, it fundamentally challenges the economics of the market….
> **So basically, it looks like the game has changed. All thanks to a Chinese company that just demonstrated how U.S. tech restrictions can backfire spectacularly – by forcing them to build more efficient solutions that they’re now sharing with the world at 3% of OpenAI’s prices.** As the saying goes, sometimes pressure creates diamonds. [@RnaudBertrand](https://twitter.com/RnaudBertrand/status/1881709223152878000)

Get the picture?
**Everything the US has done to stymie China’s development—including economic sanctions, chips embargoes, military provocations, political meddling, even arresting a Huawei executive (truly pathetic)—has blown up in their faces.**
China’s well-educated, highly motivated, technologically adept workforce have produced a model of AI that equals or exceeds the best the West has to offer at a fraction of the cost and with open sourcing that allows users to
modify, and distribute the code as they see fit.
So, which version of AI sounds like a genuine benefit to humankind and which sounds like another scheme for transforming the world into a dystopian police-state controlled by aspiring tyrants and psychopathic control freaks? Here’s more from Bertrand on ‘why China is making AI available so cheap:

> …**.it speaks to a different philosophy/vision on AI: ironically named “OpenAI” is basically about trying to establish a monopoly by establishing a moat with massive amounts of GPU and money. Deepseek is clearly betting on a future where AI becomes a commodity, widely available and affordable to everyone.** By pricing so aggressively and releasing their code open-source, they’re not just competing with OpenAI but basically declaring that AI should be like electricity or internet connectivity – a basic utility that powers innovation rather than a premium service controlled by a few players. And in that world, it’s a heck of a lot better to be the first mover who helped make it happen than the legacy player who tried to stop it. [@RnaudBertrand](https://twitter.com/RnaudBertrand/status/1881726325117026509)

So, it’s basically like everything else in this sick, twisted world where a handful of money-grubbing miscreants muscle their way into a new technology so they can fatten their own bank accounts while planting their bootheel firmly on the neck of humanity. It seems to me that China’s approach is vastly superior in that it’s clearly aimed at providing the benefits of AI to the greatest number of people at the lowest possible cost. Here are a few random comments on China’s DeepSeek AI that I picked off X that show how excited people are about this groundbreaking version:

> _The ramifications of this are huge. Every day China does something incredible, totally unlike the stagnation of the EU, talking all day while accomplishing nothing, or the latest evil plan oozing out of DC. **This is just brilliant. & inspiring. & it WILL earn them more goodwill** @CaptainCrusty66_

> _**It’s the china recipe book for success for every industry where western oligopolies have dominated**. @bbooker450_

> _AI will become a part of everyday infrastructure like electricity and tap water. **DeepSeek is a signficant step towards that, thanks to its cost reduction and open source nature** @MrBig2024_

> _We are living in a timeline where **a non-US company is keeping the original mission of OpenAI alive – truly open, frontier research that empowers all**…. @DrJimFan_

> _This is cool…this isn’t just another open source LLM release. this is o1-level reasoning capabilities that you can run locally, that you can modify and that you can study…
> **that’s a very different world than the one we were in yesterday.** Al, comments line_

> _Price comparison of OpenAI o1 and DeepSeek AI R1: R1 is significantly cheaper across all categories (96–98% savings). Now you know why big organizations don’t want open-source to continue, If humanity is ever going to benefit from AI, it will be from open-source . [@ai\_for\_success](https://twitter.com/ai_for_success/status/1881371370120216618)_

> _China is overturning mainstream development theory in astonishing ways. **China’s GDP per capita is only $12,000. That’s 70% less than the average in high-income countries. And yet they have the largest high-speed rail network in the world. They’ve developed their own commercial aircraft. They are the world leaders in renewable energy technology and electric vehicles. They have advanced medical technology, smartphone technology, microchip production, aerospace engineering…** China has a higher life expectancy than the USA, with 80% less income. We were told that this kind of development required very high levels of GDP/cap. But over the past 10 years China has demonstrated that it can be achieved with much more modest levels of output. **How do they do it? By using public finance and industrial policy to steer investment and production toward social objectives and national development needs. This allows them to convert aggregate production into development outcomes much more efficiently than other countries, where productive capacity is often wasted on activities that may be highly profitable to capital, or beneficial to the rich, but may not actually advance development.** Of course, China still has development gaps that need to be addressed. And we know from some other countries that higher social indicators can be achieved with China’s level of GDP/cap, by focusing more on social policy. But the achievements are undeniable, and development economists are taking stock. @jasonhickel_

**Unfortunately, the intensity of the competition between the US and China, ignores the inherent risks of Artificial Intelligence and its looming threat to human survival.** In a recent analytical piece by the Rand Corporation titled [AI and Geopolitics: How Might AI Affect the Rise and Fall of Nations?](https://www.rand.org/pubs/perspectives/PEA3034-1.html), the authors provide a disturbing window into a future in which “AI-enabled machines—of equivalent or greater intelligence and, potentially, highly disruptive capabilities” could pose a threat to our own existence. Keep in mind, the line between our historic reality and science fiction has already been crossed just as the probability that our own creation, AI, is likely “to become an actor, not just a factor” in the existential challenges faced by our species. Here’s a short blurb from this truly unsettling article:

> _Although technology has often influenced geopolitics, the prospect of AI means that the technology itself could become a geopolitical actor. **AI could have motives and objectives that differ considerably from those of governments and private companies. Humans’ inability to comprehend how AI “thinks” and our limited understanding of the second- and third-order effects of our commands or requests of AI are also very troubling. Humans have enough trouble interacting with one another. It remains to be seen how we will manage our relationships with one or more AIs**….
> **We are entering an era of both enlightenment and chaos…
> The borderless nature of AI makes it hard to control or regulate.** As computing power expands, models are optimized, and open-source frameworks mature, the ability to create highly impactful AI applications will become increasingly diffuse. In such a world, well-intentioned researchers and engineers will use this power to do wonderful things, **ill-intentioned individuals will use it to do terrible things, and AIs could do both wonderful and terrible things.** The net result is neither an unblemished era of enlightenment nor an unmitigated disaster, but a mix of both. Humanity will learn to muddle through and live with this game-changing technology, just as we have with so many other transformative technologies in the past….
> **The potential dangers posed by AI are many. At the extreme, they include the threat of human extinction, which could come about by an AI-enabled catastrophe, such as a well-designed virus that spreads easily, evades detection, and destroys our civilization.** Less dire, but considerably worrisome, is the threat to democratic governance if AIs gain power over people….
> **AI cannot be contained through regulation,** so the best policy will aim to minimize the harm that AI might do. This will probably be most critical in biosecurity,\[3\] but harm reduction also includes countering cybersecurity threats, strengthening democratic resilience, and developing emergency response options for a wide variety of threats from state and sub- and non-state actors…..
> In light of the likely very widespread proliferation of advanced AI capabilities to private- and public-sector actors and well-resourced individuals, governments should work closely with leading private-sector entities to develop advanced forecasting tools, wargames, and **strategic plans for dealing with what experts anticipate will be a wide variety of unexpected AI-enabled catastrophic events**. [](https://www.rand.org/pubs/perspectives/PEA3034-1.html)[AI and Geopolitics: How Might AI Affect the Rise and Fall of Nations?,](https://www.rand.org/pubs/perspectives/PEA3034-1.html) RAND_

In other words, humanity should encourage their business and political leaders to exercise sound judgement and prepare for unexpected disasters that could terminate the species.
That is simply not sufficient defense for the challenge we face
