### michellejorge — 2025-02-14T19:42:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346739>

It seems from turning off by disconnecting my modem every night it is now not working consistently. Any advice as to a modem that I could turn on and off and it won’t break? I have an Arris Surfboard . I also bought this metallic cloth like cover for the modem to mitigate Wi-Fi signal. I found that I made it very hot and I was afraid of fire. Any thoughts?

### Justin Goldberg — 2025-02-16T05:01:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346817>

You overheating could very well be what causes it to not work. Maybe have it leaning on one side or standing on its side. You could also keep a fan or laptop cooler pointed at it.

I wish someone could build a router that can turn the wifi off at certain times. Like a hack of openwrt. It's technically possible to build a custom tomato / openwrt but it's beyond my programming skill level.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-16T05:39:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346819>

If you must retain the WIFI get a mechanical or digital (programmable) power outlet adaptor timer..

### digitald3 — 2025-02-16T07:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346820>

I know Netgear routers will allow the admin to turn it off at say 9pm and come back on at 8am.. I would think others would allow the same functionality

### Mat.I — 2025-02-16T07:36:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346821>

> [digitald3 said:](/goto/post?id=346820)
>
> I know Netgear routers will allow the admin to turn it off at say 9pm and come back on at 8am.. I would think others would allow the same functionality

Check it with a RF meter though. As the majority of routers even when you schedule the on/off still transmit a "hidden" network just not your specific network ID, thus RF is still an issue. Only way to check is test with an actual meter and never assume it actually turns off in absence of testing..

### Mat.I — 2025-02-16T07:38:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346822>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=346821)
>
> Check it with a RF meter though. As the majority of routers even when you schedule the on/off still transmit a "hidden" network just not your specific network ID, thus RF is still an issue. Only way to check is test with an actual meter and never assume it actually turns off in absence of testing..

Explained in an earlier post - Thread 'Warning - Hidden sources of RF you need to measure' [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/warning-hidden-sources-of-rf-you-need-to-measure.29230/)

### digitald3 — 2025-02-16T07:41:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346823>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=346821)
>
> Check it with a RF meter though. As the majority of routers even when you schedule the on/off still transmit a "hidden" network just not your specific network ID, thus RF is still an issue. Only way to check is test with an actual meter and never assume it actually turns off in absence of testing.t

This is true!

### Saichi — 2025-02-16T16:54:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346835>

"must have wifi" = must have one of the worst frequencies regarding biological effect

### Daniel Renaud — 2025-02-16T18:00:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346836>

I’m an IT guy and I made it my mission to be able to turn off my wifi by pushing one button at night and turn it back on by pushing the button in the morning. You need 3 devices to make it happen: 1. You modem. This is the device that connects you to the Internet. Ideally this device is NOT a WiFi device. If it is turn the WiFi off on this device. 2. A router. This is a layer 3 device. I like the Microtik routers. Easy to setup and use, but the degree of difficulty is pretty high on the I’m not going to lie. This is the device that will host DHCP in your private LAN. 3. WiFi device. You have to configure this to be a bridge not a router. This is the device you’ll turn off eash night. This setup allows you to run a Bitcoin node and a miner 24/7 connected via Ethernet cable to directly to the router without having WiFi in when you sleep. There are good videos about how to configure Microtik routers on YouTube.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-17T00:27:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346853>

IF the WiFi must be on - shield it.
Metal mesh (think microwave over door) will block “most” of the signal. You are still being zapped BUT 20x less.
Put a metal mesh trash basket over it - no heat issues.
Mesh letter holder for a phone at night for those on night call.
All available from Staples. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Mat.I — 2025-02-17T01:02:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346858>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=346853)
>
> IF the WiFi must be on - shield it.
> Metal mesh (think microwave over door) will block “most” of the signal. You are still being zapped BUT 20x less.
> Put a metal mesh trash basket over it - no heat issues.
> Mesh letter holder for a phone at night for those on night call.
> All available from Staples.[View attachment 30011](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/30011/)[View attachment 30010](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/30010/)[View attachment 30012](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/30012/)

Anthony - in your photo you are measuring the ambient. This would be useful if not connecting to wifi... however, why would someone want to keep the wifi on if they are not going to use it? When they use it, it can make the exposure worst locally. This can happen in two ways, some modems have adaptive power transmission and will ramp up power density when weaker connections (dB) threshold is detected, same goes with the device you are connecting to (e.g. pc, phone, device or the like).. to truly check the attenuation you need to measure when connected (at the device and router) to check the up ramping via adaptive signal transmission..

### Mat.I — 2025-02-17T01:15:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346859>

Research adaptive power control and received signal strength indicator.. I have test and confirmed this in the field many of times. Also look at wifi retransmissions.. this is a problem with weak signals = more rf bursts. Ultimately, leading to overall higher RF exposure.. adopting that solution above could make the issue alot worst.. and give a false sense of security based on ambient conditions...

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-17T02:28:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346863>

#1 turn off WiFi and Bluetooth and hard wire. This only for people that for whatever reason need to have the WiFi on. The idea is not to cover the WiFi but have a shield between you and it. With the back open everything says at a lower power. When talking on a cell phone if you have a shield between you and it, it reduces your exposure. I do not recomend Faraday bags for the reason you give, they just cause everything to power up.

### Mat.I — 2025-02-17T03:52:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346868>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=346863)
>
> With the back open everything says at a lower power.

This is not always the case though. Ambient absolutely, yes.. However, when using the wifi and devices connected to the network the signal power that is attenuated causes the adaptive power transmission control to ramp up based on the received signal strength indicator, this will also cause (subject to the degree of dB attenuation) retransmission issues also leading to more RF bursts.. Driven by a power control loop, this is also part of the standard 802.11 protocol when signal transmission power falls, the connected devices ramp up transmission power the ramping can range from 1mW to 400mW via the adaptive power transmission control to counter the attenuated signal. Ultimately, worst case = forcing connected devices to use max power and re-transmission push all the devices to the max legal Rf output (based on SAR)..

The only way to thoroughly check this is take a baseline (in the correct places) when devices are used and connected & compare with post shielded results. This must also include use of an network analyser to check retransmissions & burst frequencies.. If software based this can be captured with applying the relevant filters when analysing the network.

This would take time to explain this to a client and test and give informed consent on e.g. do not use any devices in room x if you do then you are exposing your self to x amount times more Rf energy when using the shield. The only places you "should" use connected devices are x, y & z..

Which, in my opinion does not even help on a biological level - it just changes the effects. E.g. lower power = less heat = more inward leakage of calcium via VGCC activation..

If people are serious about their health and others exposed to this crap.. stop making excuses and do not use WIFI or BT tech. Hardwire..

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-17T09:03:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346876>

I agree, my advice to people and our company philosophy is: don’t just turn it off, unplug it and kill the circuit.

### michellejorge — 2025-02-17T22:03:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-346904>

You need three devices: cable modem (or the equivalent) to connect to the internet. A dedicated router (no WiFi), a dedicated WiFi device (does not route or distribute IP addresses). Plug your always on devices directly into the dedicated router. I like the Microtik routers. This is a solution for the tech savvy only.

### Daniel Renaud — 2025-07-10T15:12:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-356109>

If you're in bridge mode you can plug a device called a hub into it. That can have multiple ports. They not expensive either. They operate at layer 1 the base layer of the OSI model stack. Bridges and switches operate at layer 2, and routers operate at layer 3. Probably TMI. I find the asking those kinds of questions to Perplexity AI yields pretty good results for troubleshooting.

### Mat.I — 2025-08-09T06:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/modern-router.30294/post-357859>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=357849)
>
> The easiest way to reduce RF-EMF exposure + reduce environmental stress… Put a faraday bag/beanie and aluminum foil over your Wi-Fi router. 20-30 fold reduction in emission on the spot

Hopefully, no one connects to the network & has wifi off on all devices (while shielded) due to the adaptive power control. As they can be worse off dynamically vs static conditions.. Your 20-30 fold (under static conditions) reduction can actually result in a net increase of total RF exposure (with greater packet re-transmissions & power ramp up at router + local device) if not managed correctly. For that reason IMO this is not a good idea. Just get rid of the wifi all together.. It is not needed and there is no reason for it period.. Not to mention the fire risk (void your house insurance policy) as this would be further driven by the adaptive power control ramp up (to full transmit power to bridge the low signal).

The creeping blindness of cataract formation reflects a broader metaphor in modern life: modern comforts (e.g., technology abuse, centralized medicine) suppress the discomfort of poor thinking; poor thinking and poor outcomes should lead to fostering wisdom and identity. Avoiding this decentralized struggle (e.g., via opioids over sunlight) poisons resilience and creativity.

Melanin, Melatonin, and Vitamin D Deficiency: Hypothyroidism reduces Coulomb charge in the skin and lens due to low melanin, melatonin, an an altered VDR, acting in unison to alter how Gauss law can be used and this impairs charge collection in cells leading to disease. This process opacifies the lens, causing cataracts.

Stars require darkness to shine, just as human biology needs light-dark cycles for optimal function. An opaque lens diminishes this balance, reducing the relative power of light to drive epigenetic programming and cellular evolution. Modern artificial lighting (e.g., blue light overuse) exacerbates this, further distorting the light-dark tension essential for health.

Leptin-Melanocortin Pathway: Leptin stimulates TRH via the leptin-melanocortin pathway, boosting α-MSH and POMC, which are essential for melanin production. In hypothyroidism, this pathway is disrupted, leading to pale skin, lens opacification, and increased sunburn risk. A cataract gives you a ciliary ganglionectomy just like sunglasses do and this leads to many diseases in modern humans.

Thermoregulation and TRH: TRH acts as a neurotransmitter, inducing hyperthermia when injected intracerebroventricularly. This heat stress, combined with a diminished heat sink (e.g., in the retina and lens), heightens cataract risk because it alters the production of high signal UPEs in the eyes and central retinal pathways leading to more diseases you never saw coming.

As you see in the medical journals whatever best methods they have they post so all other doctors can build from there because if we all have to do the same tests and learning curve times in our lifetime where would we be? We will all be at the same point and all those years will be wasted. It's always best to build on what's the current best.

Sounds like you are looking for a centralized leaning method. I went to college and everything I was taught was the wisdom of the day. Some was valid but life has taught me much of it was not. We are so messed up today BECAUSE so many accepted what they were taught in school was correct/true.

I agree with teaching people principles not just telling them what to do . . . BUT . . . Everyone I help has to be approached and dealt with where they are. Each is in a different place, different knowledge base, with different comprehension abilities. What I did to learn, understand and retain knowledge will most likely not work for the next person. Someone with a photographic memory can learn and assess a thing VERY differently that someone who does not. Someone with an expansive knowledge/experience base can process and comprehend data very differently.

When I am studying something new, I read/listen to the people who are for it, AND I look into who they are, their background, any hidden (or not so hidden) agenda. THEN, I read listen to people who are against it. What are their arguments, reasoning etc. I have learnt as much about a subject from the people who are against it as from the people who are for it. Are their arguments good or weak. How does it fit into the things I believe to be true now. Is what I believe to be true, in light of this new information, still true?

Read, listen, watch. Don’t believe a word I say (or ANYONE else) about anything. I may be right about this but that does not mean I’m right about the next thing. Question, test, validate. Find your own way . . . there are no short cuts. . . my butt in the sun (or Jack’s) will not give you a sun tan.

> Also.. I might add, it is important to know when to push through and pull back to have a break.. Find what works for you and your current health status.. When I didn't have answers for certain things (life & engineering problems) it was hard to stop/rest until I had the answers or solution I needed and went all in. Most of the problem solving and visualisation gets done in my head, which leads to the next stage (which could be looking at further research in another area, however somehow tying it all together in my head). My instincts also guide me to a certain extent.
>
> Just sharing this as it may give you another point of viewthank you good advice

Thank you this is good advice

### Thomasf75 — 2025-02-18T19:06:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-we-search-for-actual-articles-research-medical-tests-to-verify-truth-is-it-jama-is-it-the-new-england-jama-pubmed.30302/post-346951>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=346932)
>
> Sounds like you are looking for a centralized leaning method. I went to college and everything I was taught was the wisdom of the day. Some was valid but life has taught me much of it was not. We are so messed up today BECAUSE so many accepted what they were taught in school was correct/true.
>
> I agree with teaching people principles not just telling them what to do . . . BUT . . . Everyone I help has to be approached and dealt with where they are. Each is in a different place, different knowledge base, with different comprehension abilities. What I did to learn, understand and retain knowledge will most likely not work for the next person. Someone with a photographic memory can learn and assess a thing VERY differently that someone who does not. Someone with an expansive knowledge/experience base can process and comprehend data very differently.
>
> When I am studying something new, I read/listen to the people who are for it, AND I look into who they are, their background, any hidden (or not so hidden) agenda. THEN, I read listen to people who are against it. What are their arguments, reasoning etc. I have learnt as much about a subject from the people who are against it as from the people who are for it. Are their arguments good or weak. How does it fit into the things I believe to be true now. Is what I believe to be true, in light of this new information, still true?
>
> Read, listen, watch. Don’t believe a word I say (or ANYONE else) about anything. I may be right about this but that does not mean I’m right about the next thing. Question, test, validate. Find your own way . . . there are no short cuts. . . my butt in the sun (or Jack’s) will not give you a sun tan.

This is very good advice and true and you are totally right. I understand that not everybody is at the same point but don't forget these days we don't have to do that totally. if you post a video or a document showing how to properly conduct research and to reassure accurate to find the most accurate medical testing research you won't have to spend time with each individual student.you post a video and everybody takes what they can from it, the better ones will take the best parts of it there's no need to spend time with each individual.but by posting it so it's accessible to everybody everybody can start from a better method/teacher in and excel. As you said we all learned wrong things and methods Early. If you learn early ho w to be a " master" researcher you will find master research and then be able to execute master testing/ applications.
Spreading the helpful knowledge he is spreading is very good but I'm sure there's tons more knowledge out there that has not been linked or figured out to be spoken.one man can only do so much. I think chipping in and assisting Dr jack. In any way we can ( master researching) then we could really move far ahead. But to do that we have to know how to accurately do research. I did a quick check on one topic there was 100,000 articles just on one word think how long it's going to take me to look through those, not just reading them keep in mind but also then figure if they can be trusted and then all the other concerns etc.

Look at it from this point of view. If Jack is accurate in what he has learned and telling us, if you had somebody like Jack teaching you in those early early years where would you be now? How many years ahead would you be? How healthier would you be? How many people could you have also shared that knowledge in all those years?

Where would the present dr jack Kruse be if 60 yr old Dr jack Kruse taught 30 yr old Dr jack Kruse back then? What knowledge would he know now that he doesn't? Think where we all would be.

When you put drops in the eye only a very small percentage is absorbed inside it is stated in many articles that the eyes tear film wipes away most of any liquid placed in the eye. does anyone know how or any methods to allow the eye to absorb much more of the drops when placed in the eye? If you have an iPhone, there are settings you can tweak to make the screen red. It is laid out here somewhere in the forum.

### tallweeds — 2025-02-18T18:29:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-electrons.30319/post-346948>

Less than 30 seconds exposure to sunlight in the middle of a blizzardly snowstorm at 10am.

Back inside to the dark after.

The same process happens in humans too.

Electrons getting excited to a higher state and dropping back and emitting light.

No excuses!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-flicker-commercial-lighting.30321/post-346968>

Hello,

I need to choose a light, to light an indoor environment for manufacturing.

I want to have a light with the least negative effects for the 30-40 employees working under these, so they need to be non-flicker, but also of an optical spectrum and intensity in which people can actually clearly see what they're working on. If I choose something too low K, or too dim, I'm going to get major pushback.

I've been digging around, but I figured I would ask to see if anyone has found an appropriate light already for such a situation?

Thank you!

# Thread: Introducing Myself + Help With Tinnitus
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-help-with-tinnitus.30326/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 31

### LewisMercer — 2025-02-20T15:31:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-help-with-tinnitus.30326/post-347021>

Hi everyone,

I’m a Holistic Personal Trainer and amateur boxer from Merseyside, England (53rd latitude). I’m 20 years old and have been studying Dr Kruse’s work for 18 months now.

After listening to episode 1 of Dr Kruse with Robert Breedlove (WiM544), I realised that I actually have tinnitus. After a recent blocked ear, I have become aware of ringing noises in my ear all day. I always remember as a child hearing the noises when in silence, mostly while trying to sleep. The phrase “deafening silence” made sense to me as I thought that everyone heard these loud ringing noises when in silence. Apparently not.

Melanin Regeneration Protocol:
•sunrise everyday
•sunset everyday
•grounding everyday
•sun exposure with minimal clothing
•cold exposure
•block blue light before sunrise and after sunset
•eat organic local & seasonal foods only

I already follow this protocol, but it’s clearly not good enough. I need to do more. Additionally, I need to reduce my non-native EMF exposure and ideally buy Magnetico sleep mattresses when I can afford them.

If there is anything that I am missing or need to buy to help myself, please educate me.

### lybogany — 2025-02-20T17:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-help-with-tinnitus.30326/post-347027>

Hey Lewis,

Nice to meet a fellow Lewis. Something to think about that I didn’t see you mention in your post. If you are currently using wireless earphones/headphones with bluetooth, I would eliminate those from your life. It’s a ton of EMF that goes right into your brain when you use them.

Cheers,
Lewis

>
> Cheers,
> Lewis

Hi Lewis,

Yes, I have not used any form of earphones for 6 months now. I used to use wired earphones a lot but rarely Bluetooth.

My Grandmother (mother’s mother) developed severe Alzheimer’s which is the ultimate melanin degradation. So it makes sense as to why I have tinnitus. My mitochondrial genome must be super sensitive to non-native EMF.

Any other suggestions would be massively appreciated.

Many thanks,
Lewis Mercer

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T05:50:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-help-with-tinnitus.30326/post-347353>

Stimulated Brillouin scattering (SBS) is similar to SRS in that energy is transferred from an optical pump beam to longer wavelengths through interaction with the glass medium. However, acoustic phonons are involved in the actions, and hence, the frequency shift is small, about 11 GHz, and the bandwidth is very small, typically 50 MHz. As shown by Ohio State research, magnetic fields control acoustic phonons. When light hits matter in a cell, an acoustic phonon is created electromechanical, and this wave is controlled by our mitochondrial magnetic field (MEG machine).

Phonons can propagate for long distances and time scales without losing energy. This mechanism shows us why melanin is in the cochlea. We hear light first and then generate acoustic phonons for the temporal lobe and acoustic relays.

The fundamental difference is that the optical phonons participate in SRS while SBS is through acoustic phonons. As a result of this difference, SBS occurs only in one direction.

SBS vs. SRS: Phonons in Cells

-   Stimulated Brillouin Scattering (SBS): Light (optical pump, 200-1200 nm) hits a medium—energy shifts to longer wavelengths (11 GHz down, 50 MHz bandwidth) via acoustic phonons (sound waves, ~10⁹ Hz). Ohio State (Herriott, 2018)—magnetic fields (e.g., mito’s field, ~nT, MEG-detectable) control these phonons—electromechanical waves ripple (level 3). Part 2: “Mito’s a semiconductor” (00:22:32)—SBS’s long-range, lossless propagation (level 4) fits.
-   Stimulated Raman Scattering (SRS): Light scatters via optical phonons (molecular vibrations, ~10¹² Hz)—broader frequency shifts, multidirectional. SBS—unidirectional—focuses acoustic energy—cells harness both (level 3).

Mitochondrial Magnetic Field: Phonon Control

-   Mito’s Role: IMM’s ΔΨm (150-200 mV, level 2)—electron flow (ETC, level 1)—spins a magnetic field (nT, MEG range). Light hits—biophotons (200-800 nm, level 3)—acoustic phonons spark—mito’s field (level 6) tunes ‘em. Phonons propagate—long distances (μm), long times (ms)—no energy loss—H₂O’s CDs (level 4) guide. Part 1: “Light shapes life” (00:44:33)—sound’s the relay.
-   **Melanin in Cochlea:** UV biophotons (200-350 nm)—melanin (cochlea, level 5)—absorbs, emits—“hear light first.” Acoustic phonons—11 GHz—relay to temporal lobe, auditory cortex—communication evolves (level 5). Part 2: “Cephalopods show neural nets” (~00:23:02)—humans hear it internally. **This explains why tinnitus and Meniere's disease are closely related to melanin loss.**

### meirwaizman — 2025-08-06T23:53:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introducing-myself-help-with-tinnitus.30326/post-357699>

If you did around this forum youll find a post about tinnitus that jack posted. Under it he has another link to [(21) Mario Nawfal on X: "BRAZILIAN SCIENTISTS ZAP TINNITUS WITH LASERS A constant ringing drives 750 million people nuts worldwide, but Brazilian researchers might have cracked the code with low-level laser therapy. Their study at the Optics and Photonics Research Center compared every tinnitus fix https://t.co/SGa2P46Bu6" / X](https://x.com/MarioNawfal/status/1894690118570561811)

Which refrences this study: [Effects of Red and Infrared Laser Therapy in Patients with Tinnitus: A Double-Blind, Clinical, Randomized Controlled Study Combining Light with Ultrasound, Drugs and Vacuum Therapy - PMC](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10144000/)

I then asked my custom built ai tool to tell me what was the best protocol with the best results in this study?

Response: The study evaluated 10 groups (G1–G10) using the Tinnitus Handicap Inventory (THI) scores at baseline (T0), post-treatment (T1, after 8 sessions), and 15 days post-treatment (T2). The protocols involved various combinations of transmeatal or transcochlear low-level laser therapy (LLLT), vacuum therapy (VT), ultrasound (US), Ginkgo biloba (GB), flunarizine dihydrochloride (FD), and laser puncture (LP). All active groups showed THI reductions, but many were comparable to or less than the placebo effect in G1 (-40.1% at T2).

The protocol with the best results—defined by the largest THI score reduction at T2 (-56.4%, significantly beyond placebo with p < 0.05 in relevant comparisons), lasting effects, and positive outcomes highlighted in the conclusions—was in Group 9 (G9). This involved standalone transmeatal LLLT using a 660 nm red laser at 100 mW power, applied directly to the ear canal for 15 minutes per session (treating both ears if bilateral tinnitus). Sessions were twice weekly over 4 weeks.

This outperformed the 6-minute transmeatal LLLT in G6 (-16.8% at T2) and was superior to combinations that showed short-term antagonism (e.g., LLLT with VT, US, GB, or FD). LP in G10 also showed strong lasting effects (-44.7% at T2), but G9 had the highest overall reduction

Conclusion 660 nm red laser at 100 mW power, applied directly to the ear canal for 15 minutes per session could maybe help or it could be risky im not an expert yet.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-27T20:55:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-lights-or-incandescent.30328/post-347524>

> [Wouterbekkers said:](/goto/post?id=347516)
>
> Is this accurate? [View attachment 30070](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/30070/)

Yes - but this is just the visible light And different manufacturers bulbs will vary.
Daylight also has 42% IR and 5% (+/-) UV
Incandescent/halogen have IR (heat) but no UV
Normal LED lights and florescent have little to NO IR or UV
Sunlight does not flicker, all (most) artificial lights do to a certain extent (some more than others).

# Thread: A quote from The Crossing by Cormac McCarthy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jackfoster — 2025-02-21T02:10:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347055>

"he said the wicked know that if the ill they do be of sufficient horror men will not speak against it. That men have just enough stomach for small evils and only these will they oppose. He said that true evil has power to sober the small doer against his own deeds and in the contemplation of that evil he may even find the path of righteousness which has been foreign to his feet and may have no power but to go upon it. Even this man may be appalled at what is revealed to him and seek some order to stand against it."

### jackfoster — 2025-02-21T02:14:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347056>

When I read this it made me immediately think of how people refuse to do something as simple as use their eyes to see the trails in the sky, while others are able to use their eyes to see and once they have seen it they cannot unsee it.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-21T03:24:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347059>

I 100% believe in chemtrails but not every line across the sky is a chemtrail. Most of the photos I see are contrails. People who are not used to looking up do not have a frame of reference to understand what they see, so tend to believe the person who told them to look up, as to what they are seeing.

I think this principle is universal which is why people fall for cults, believe the “news” and take untested, experimental jabs.

### Jacks — 2025-02-21T04:08:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347062>

The condensation-trails have to condense on something - such as a soot particle. From what I've learnt from Jim Lee, plane farts (as he calls plane jet exhaust) contain plenty of soot particles (which contain many of the elements, such as Aluminum, that people are finding in rainwater) on which the water vapor can condense to form contrails. The saturation of air with water vapor has to be above a certain level for condensation to occur and to form the man-made cirrus clouds. I see tons of these where I live, though we've had a lot less in the last year. Cloud seeding is a legit thing - using silver iodide and other things and is done to increase rainfall and snowpack. There are other geoengineering projects ongoing here and abroad.

### jackfoster — 2025-02-21T04:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-quote-from-the-crossing-by-cormac-mccarthy.30329/post-347063>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=347059)
>
> I 100% believe in chemtrails but not every line across the sky is a chemtrail. Most of the photos I see are contrails. People who are not used to looking up do not have a frame of reference to understand what they see, so tend to believe the person who told them to look up, as to what they are seeing.
>
> I think this principle is universal which is why people fall for cults, believe the “news” and take untested, experimental jabs.

The difference between contrails and chemtrails is obvious, no? Chemtrails linger and spread while contrails do not. I think this quote applies to many things but I feel like chemtrails are a simple test to see where a person is at in their awareness. I can't imagine someone who won't recognize what is happening in the sky being able to recognize harmful effects of nnEMF or something else that isn't clear to see.

# Thread: MITOCHONDRIA CREATE WATER...........AND A MEMORY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-create-water-and-a-memory.30331/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-21T20:17:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-create-water-and-a-memory.30331/post-347076>

**Cells as Wisdom Repositories because of Water.**

Cells storing "collectible wisdom" via water hits deep. Water’s not a bland solvent; it’s a memory bank. Emilio Del Giudice’s quantum electrodynamics posits water clusters near proteins—like DNA or microtubules—encode electromagnetic signals from the environment. Deuterium-depleted water research (e.g., Somlyai, 1993) hints at this: lighter water tweaks cellular signaling, hinting at data storage beyond genetics. Epigenetics—histone acetylation, methylation—reads environmental cues (stress, toxins, light) before genes shift. Ubiquitin biology tags proteins for breakdown when signals go haywire, a cellular SOS.

My decentralize point: we’re built to capture nature’s waves—light, sound, EM fields—and translate them into coherence. Impairment isn’t innate; it’s a mismatch. Take autism or ADHD: studies (e.g., Herbert, 2015) link mitochondrial glitches and vagal dysregulation to toxic exposures—EMFs, heavy metals—disrupting water-light interplay. Genes don’t doom us; our modern environments do. That memory is buried in the water our mitochondria create.

My take: light hits water, excites it, and regulates magnetic flux in that spinning ATPase. With its reach from the brainstem to the gut, the vagus nerve syncs these watery realms. Vagal tone—measured by heart rate variability—reflects this harmony. Light’s role? Photobiomodulation studies (e.g., Hamblin, 2016) show red and near-infrared light boosts cytochrome c oxidase activity, spiking ATP and calming inflammation. Disconnect the light-water link—like in mitochondrial dysfunction—and energy falters. Think chronic fatigue or vagal neuropathy: the system stalls and may fail.

I'm flipping the centralized biology script: don’t fix the patient; fix their world. Epigenetics screams this—Airan’s 2020 work on ubiquitin pathways shows cells flag stressors (e.g., hypoxia) way before DNA mutates. A centralized clinician ignoring this is like mopping the floor in a flood. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-21T20:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondria-create-water-and-a-memory.30331/post-347077>

Decentralized gurus recommend sunlight (vitamin D, circadian reset), clean water (no fluoride, no glyphosate), with grounding (Earth’s electrons stabilize coherent domains in water)—that’s treatment. Modern medicine’s deaf to it, chasing pills over physics. Why? Incentives again—Big Pharma thrives on symptoms, not ecosystems.

**My Flow as Water Muse**

Water’s the medium, light’s the spark, the vagus nerve’s the bridge. ATPase spins properly when they align, mitochondria hum, and cells log nature’s lessons. Disruption—dim light, polluted water, EMF noise—frays the connection, and energy crashes. No one’s broken; their environment’s off-key, and the melody has no fidelity. Clinicians should tune it—more sun, less screens, pure H2O—before blaming the genome. BigHarma has become a trillion-dollar profiteer by focusing on the RNA/DNA genome when the real gold mine is in mtDNA. Epigenetics and ubiquitin aren’t whispers; they’re sirens of why chronic diseases are with us. We are no longer connected to our decentralized power plant.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/local-food-in-miami.30332/post-347078>

I thought this was the easy part, but I can't find local food here. I even went out to Homestead and only found grapefruits and oranges. What are people eating that follow Jack's rules?! Where are you buying it? I miss my smoothies with everything I could find in the grocery! Lol. THANKS

Grocery stores are centralized institutions.

I would buy directly from local family farms and fisheries, or catch, hunt, raise, gather, or grow your own food.

Also, I suggest thinking about what a "smoothie" actually is, and if you want to be eating food that has been homogenized beyond what your body can recognize.

I wish you good luck in your search for local food.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-23T00:59:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/local-food-in-miami.30332/post-347126>

### Mat.I — 2025-02-23T06:52:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/local-food-in-miami.30332/post-347135>

I'm not in the US, however we buy directly from the source. We get our meat direct from a butcher that has a local farm who raise their own livestock. Eggs, honey, and other produce etc is all directly from the farm door. Seafood is directly from the people who fish and sell it from a truck that comes up the coast to our area for 3 days of the week. We avoid any centralised supermarkets with the exception of certain necessities that cannot be purchased elsewhere..

# Thread: The water  Boy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-water-boy.30333/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bobby Boucher — 2025-02-22T09:12:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-water-boy.30333/post-347106>

Hi everyone
I am and always will be controversial on all subjects you will either agree or disagree but I will always give you a hug and respect your disagreement ,so let’s start about me I try to stay anonymous to the General public due to the censorship I endured in 2020 to mainly protect my young family (I was a late baby starter) after the authorities were visiting my residence frequently so i required a more robust strategy I managed to hold my account on Twitter just I had multiple short term bans all through 2020/2025 and still to this day red flagged on X and account restricted I called out the covid bullshit at the same time as Mike Yeadon except he has a much larger following I’m only a minnow I started life in engineering specifically hydraulics fluid dynamics and thermal dynamics and spent 23 years in this field what an amazing experience travelling the Globe to all corners. I hit my early fourties’ and had some sort of mid life crisis ditched my wife at the time and changed careers into something completely different I chose to go into the field of Distilling and Fermentation processes I had two choices back to university or go somewhere obscure and learn from experienced individuals in that specific discipline so I signed up for Uni and proceeded after my first week I quickly learned I made the wrong choice I was learning with kids who were kind of taking the piss of me and a lot of the mathematics I had already done previously in engineering so I changed strategy and got a huge break I managed to get a unpaid kind of apprenticeship on an island on the west coast of Scotland with a huge profound Name in the Industry he took a chance with me and gave me a step on the ladder (which I will be be eternally grateful forever for at that juncture in my life I was completely broken and felt rejected) I started at the bottom of the process and worked 18-20 hours every day for one year learning from very old individuals and wrote all my learnings in my new black book the art of fermentation and Distillation I learned and was humbled to learn from amazing characters I was summoned one day into the Big Boss office and was sat down and he said to me where and what do you want to do in this industry I told him straight and within 1 week I had secured an interview at one of the oldest distilleries in Scotland the rest is now history hope you enjoyed my introduction I gave you an insight to my life as I never do ,I prefer to stay private and in the shadows and fight against tyranny and Evil from the back benches I accidentally came across Dr Kruse on you tube Ted talk in 2011 I think can’t remember exactly he struck some kind of cord with me ,then about 2 years ago he appeared on Ahmad Malik podcast who I support and talked to once on the phone I think he wanted me to go on his supporter stories which I refused as I would loose my Job and my pension which I worked bloody hard for and I only have 1-2 years left before retirement so that’s a tiny part of who I am I have been working with water half my life and other liquids and gained unparalleled knowledge and if I can help in any way within your group . ” you will never get what you want until you are grateful for what you have.” ❤️

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-water-boy.30333/post-347150>

Hi Bobby, I have a small question that you could probably answer regarding the spring water that goes into the whisky

I guess it has to be filtered and cleaned first or do they wait until the whisky is distilled? Because it's always puzzled me. I went on a tour of Jameson's when I was in Midleton in Cork but that was 24 years ago and I don't remember what they actually said.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-23T20:18:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-water-boy.30333/post-347157>

Is it not the water used that primarily (lots of factors) gives each whisky it’s unique flavor/character?

### Bobby Boucher — 2025-02-24T19:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-water-boy.30333/post-347234>

Water is one small part yes fermentation times and copper contact are other factors and the clarity of the worts potential hydrogen also plays a role in fermentation process

### Bobby Boucher — 2025-02-24T19:30:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-water-boy.30333/post-347236>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=347150)
>
> Hi Bobby, I have a small question that you could probably answer regarding the spring water that goes into the whisky
>
> I guess it has to be filtered and cleaned first or do they wait until the whisky is distilled? Because it's always puzzled me. I went on a tour of Jameson's when I was in Midleton in Cork but that was 24 years ago and I don't remember what they actually said.

They generally wait until the the distillation process is complete before they add water to bring the % strength down from approximately 67% down to approximately 40% abv (alcohol by volume)

### optimal2024 — 2025-02-22T20:48:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/issues-with-food.30334/post-347116>

I wonder if you can help me understand why I am unable to eat seafood, foie gras, fats and a ton of other foods?

I have non alcoholic fatty liver disease and my current regime at the 28th latitude is:

Seeing sunrise and around 1 and half hours after most days.
No blue light at all after dark.
Working outside although it is in the shade most of the day on a hard wired ether net cabled PC, no wi-fi, and it’s not plugged in as battery is charged up before use.
Use IRIS on the screen.
Only candles after dark.
Switch off all breakers apart from the kitchen at night.
Black out blinds in the bedroom.
Sun on my liver and back for approximately 2 hours per day from around 2pm until 4:30pm.
See sunset every day.

But I’m still unable to tolerate a ton of foods?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-23T20:05:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/issues-with-food.30334/post-347156>

> [optimal2024 said:](/goto/post?id=347141)
>
> I get approximately 1 and a half hours at sunrise on my stomach and around 1-2 hours at midday-mid afternoon. Which time of day is the 850nm spectrum, please?
> Thanks

**I only mentioned 850nm because it is the easiest to find In red light products. If you have sun light you are getting NIR.
Sunlight emits 34.8 mW/cm^2 of light in the 600nm to 900nm range**. That is **34% of the overall solar spectrum** in the Red to Near-Infrared therapeutic range!

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

_

### [Reference Air Mass 1.5 Spectra](https://www.nrel.gov/grid/solar-resource/spectra-am1.5.html)

 www.nrel.gov

_
Certainly that is **sufficient intensity and power because we know most red light panels emit between 5 to 80 mW/cm^2** at the recommended treatment distance (despite most of them [false-advertising](https://gembared.com/blogs/musings/what-is-the-real-intensity-of-my-led-panel-the-dark-deception-of-red-light-therapy) and saying it is over 100mW/cm^2).

In fact, a recent article by Daniel Barolet states that the best intensity for Red/NIR Light Therapy should be modeled after sunlight. Which according to his article is about 30-35mW/cm^2. He also states that >100mW/cm^2 intensity is actually a high risk for photoaging of the skin.\[15\] So we cannot outsmart nature with high intensity products, instead we should be using reasonable intensities as nature intended.

### AlbertD — 2025-06-13T20:08:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/issues-with-food.30334/post-354601>

I have Crohn's/some mild version of NAFLD (deuterium-rich foods dehydrate me especially in winter months).

I have found significant improvement with my GI tract issues from doing the above (getting sunlight exposed to six-pack area) WHILE being grounded. **Getting grounded at the same time is a must!** I've been getting morning sunlight exposure to my abdomen for almost 10 months. I only started doing this WHILE being grounded 4-5 days ago. The difference is like night and day.

Jack has written a blog about this (Decentralized #34). My takeaway from this blog entry, "It doesn't matter how long you spend in the sun if the surface chemistry of your body (skin + deeper tissues) is not optimized."

You can stand in the sun for hours on end and barely make any Vitamin D.

Grounding was the missing piece for me.

The same mass difference that enables imaging is the biophysical mechanism that disrupts metabolism when it occurs endogenously.

2\. Vibrational Frequency Shift in PUFA Precursors (DHA/EPA → Elovanoids)Elovanoids (ELVs) are 26–32-carbon mediators synthesized in photoreceptor and neuronal cells via ELOVL4 elongase acting on DHA (22:6 n-3) or EPA. The carbon-backbone geometry and vibrational modes are exquisitely tuned:

Southern-Hemisphere Seafood DeuterationGlobal water-cycle physics (kinetic fractionation during evaporation/condensation) produces latitude-dependent D/H ratios. ### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-23T16:28:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-water-ddw.30337/post-347144>

@Jonathanjarecki Your lesson. Here is the biophysical grail Pollack missed: Deuterium creates a Dielectric Twist for cells.
Deuterium (D₂O) isn’t H₂O lite—it’s heavier (20% more mass), slower to hop in proton networks, and dielectric’s off. Pure D₂O’s ε\_r ≈ 78.3—close to H₂O’s 78—but its dynamics lag: lower dipole moment, sluggish reorientation (Kropman, 2001). In cells, deuterium’s 1:6,400 H ratio seems tiny, but mitochondria hate it—ATPase stalls, H⁺ flow clogs (Somlyai, 1993). DDW (deuterium < 120 ppm) speeds it up—ε\_r drops slightly (fewer heavy dipoles), refractive index dips, & light bends less but moves faster.
Pollack’s EZ? He assumes uniform H₂O. Deuterium’s presence—unexamined—skews his dielectric claims. High D tightens water’s lattice and shrinks EZ (less charge exclusion); low D loosens it and boosts it. His UV absorption peaks (270 nm) might wobble with D levels—unseen in his scope. Speculation? I know I am right—without DDW runs, he’s guessing how far EZ stretches or holds light. He needs to up his game. You need to push him because he never listens to me.

# Thread: (Potential) Gene Therapy for Primary Immunodeficiency Disorder?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/potential-gene-therapy-for-primary-immunodeficiency-disorder.30343/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 64
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FDDio — 2025-02-24T23:47:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/potential-gene-therapy-for-primary-immunodeficiency-disorder.30343/post-347246>

In December, my then 8-year-old daughter was diagnosed with a rare Primary Immunodeficiency Disorder called Immunodeficiency-76, which was discovered in 2020.

_"Immunodeficiency-76 (IMD76) is an autosomal recessive primary immunologic disorder characterized by recurrent bacterial, viral, and fungal infections in early childhood. Patients may exhibit T-cell lymphopenia, variable B-cell abnormalities, and hypogammaglobulinemia. Additional features include lymphoma and neurologic manifestations. **Bone marrow transplantation can be curative, but many patients succumb in childhood."**_

As my family did not get the COVID-19 vaccine after I extensively researched its safety and efficacy, I am hesitant to have her undergo a Bone Marrow Transplant that could potentially expose her to the very risks we are avoiding. Moreover, there is a real threat of chronic graft-versus-host disease (cGVHD) complication following BMT (as Uncle Jack commented regarding David Vetter, 'The boy who lived in a plastic bubble,' in his July 2014 post 'Things I saw in the news today').

Given her severe condition, she currently needs to go to SickKids hospital in Toronto for monthly Intravenous Immunoglobulin (IVIG) Treatment to help protect her from her weak immune system. We have also incorporated many of the recommendations often mentioned by Jack. She is doing well these days, however, given that this is a genetic condition with Human FCHO1 protein deficiency that affects her development and function of T cells, these short-term treatments won't do anything to cure her. We also have no data on how long the IVIG will keep her stable and what other systems in her body are affected by the FCH01 protein deficiency.

Thus, I have started looking into treatments such as Gene Therapy (and Protein Therapy) and am in the process of starting a private foundation to educate and fund PID research. As of January 2025, approximately 250 people worldwide have received experimental CRISPR-based therapies for various diseases, including some PIDs. However, I have found no studies regarding her particular FCOH1 deficiency,

As I am mostly retired from my businesses and have done well with my investments (stacking BTC since 2021, as well as a few shitcoins), I have the time and financial resources to pursue this lofty goal of finding a potential long-term solution/cure for my daughter. Thus, I wanted to reach out to the community to see if anyone has similar experiences or suggestions on where I should (or should not) focus my efforts.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-25T02:15:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/potential-gene-therapy-for-primary-immunodeficiency-disorder.30343/post-347256>

Gene therapy? Not a fan. mtDNA therapy is what this site is about. They are not equivalent.

### FDDio — 2025-02-25T02:38:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/potential-gene-therapy-for-primary-immunodeficiency-disorder.30343/post-347260>

I suspected you may not be a fan of Gene Therapy, and I have a bit of undertanding why. I will now go down the the mtDNA rabbit hole. Thank you for your quick feedback. It's appreciated.

### FDDio — 2025-07-23T00:47:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/potential-gene-therapy-for-primary-immunodeficiency-disorder.30343/post-356805>

**Daughter’s Update:**
Since strictly adhering to the recommended protocols (including purchasing a swimming pool for increased ‘fun’ sun exposure), my daughter has shown significant improvement, with fewer health issues and less mood instability compared to her peers. However, during winter, we faced a concerning incident where she experienced symptoms consistent with what seemed to be a transient ischemic attack (TIA). Below are key details:

**Symptoms Observed:**

-   Sudden-onset severe headache
-   Slurred speech
-   Short-term memory loss
-   Arm heaviness/weakness
-   Loss of coordination while walking
-   Vomiting within 30 minutes of onset (while in the car), with full recovery within 40 minutes and no lingering effects.

**Medical Evaluation snippet:**
_Patient is a 8-year-old female with combined immunodeficiency 76 (FCHO1 mutation), monthly IVIG therapy bronchiectasis, poor growth, and recurrent infections (oral thrush-2yrs old, H.flu). She presented to the hospital with sudden onset headache and neurological changes. She shortly after returned to baseline. Neurology and Immunology was consulted and she was reviewed by Neurology and Neuroradiology teams and found to have some evidence of sinusitis but no parenchymal changes. At the time of discharge, she was vitally and hemodynamically stable and at her neurological baseline.Medical Evaluation snippet:_

Though she has not had a recurrence, the severity of this episode is deeply unsettling. We are tethered in Toronto due to various reasons and are planning what will be our winter protocols to minimize risks of another event (which includes currently designing a custom “Healthy Home”). Given the rarity of her condition (fewer than 20 global cases of this PID subtype), centralized medicine offers no clear answers regarding the neurological risks of FCHO1 mutations. We’d appreciate any insights to clarify potential triggers or mitigation strategies for future episodes.

**Foundation Update:**
Our foundation, is now established and we await our Charitable Donation status (rapidfoundation.org). However, since we are not keen on Gene Therapy at this time, and the protocol's are working well and don’t really cost anything, we are a bit at odds on how to best utilize the foundation. Of course, as one of the three pillars stated on our Website is: _“Bringing Awareness to Mitochondrial Health and Immune Strength by Reducing non-native electromagnetic fields (EMFs) and blue light, while maximizing sunlight exposure”_ but that does not directly help my daughter. So, not sure what else we can spend time and money researching.

**If a deeper discussion would help, we would be available for a call/teleconference and can arrange payment via BTC (We deleted our FB accounts a few years ago).**

### FDDio — 2026-01-15T21:29:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/potential-gene-therapy-for-primary-immunodeficiency-disorder.30343/post-365401>

Posting a quick update on our daughter's emergency room visit when she exhibited "TIA-like" symptoms last January. After reviewing several studies, we believe the most likely cause was liver toxicity from following the hospital's recommended protocol of Tylenol/Ibuprofen combined with Septra (antibiotic). This seems particularly plausible since her symptoms began to subside after vomiting. While ibuprofen can be problematic for many people, it's especially concerning for someone with IMD76. Since we stopped giving her ibuprofen (and now only use very limited Tylenol when necessary), she has not had another episode.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-19T15:50:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/potential-gene-therapy-for-primary-immunodeficiency-disorder.30343/post-369221>

If you are understanding the blogs, your daughter is a unicorn. She has one of the more unique Lagrangians on Earth. Her choice of symmetry is why she has this disease. Sorry to say she was given this in her germ line by the KIE of deuterium spun into the FCOH1 gene that caused both of her alleles to become locked in. The story of her parent prior to her life is where this story begins. This defect tells me a new evolutionary LAgrangian is emerging because of some unique environmental arrangements. I would bet high maternal age and heteroplasmy is part of this story because its actions are mimicking a trisomy case.

**Top 10 Pregnancy Risks at higher Maternal Age or with a high heteroplasmy rate

Spontaneous Miscarriage**: The most common risk, with a high incidence often exceeding **90%** for pregnancies at age 45 and older, primarily due to declining egg quality and chromosomal issues.

**Chromosomal Abnormalities**: The risk of trisomy diseases is highly elevated; for example, the risk of Trisomy 21 (Down syndrome) is approximately **1 in 10-12** at age 47. Bi-alleic welding of FCOH1 is one such risk. The irony is the welding of chromosomal elements sometimes has benefits like the fusion event that happened in Gorillas 7-9 million years ago to get to the Sapiens Lagrangian. In your daughter cases she got the reverse problem where her brain and immune system became isolated from one another optically via this KIE fusion event in this gene.

**Preeclampsia and Gestational Hypertension**: Older women have a significantly higher risk of severe high blood pressure conditions, which can lead to organ damage.

**Gestational Diabetes (GDM)**: The likelihood of developing GDM is **three to six times higher** for women over 40 compared to those in their 20s. This means the baby will have shit tons of deuterium unfurled into it to create chidhood disease risks

**Stillbirth**: Research indicates a higher relative risk of intrauterine fetal death, particularly after **37 weeks** of gestation.

**Cesarean Delivery (C-section)**: The rate of primary C-sections increases dramatically with age, reaching approximately **36% to 60%** for women in this age bracket.

**Preterm Birth**: There is a higher probability of delivering before 37 weeks, which can result in low birth weight and neonatal complications.

**Placental Complications**: Risks for **placenta previa** (placenta covering the cervix) and **placental abruption** are roughly three times higher than in younger mothers.

**Postpartum Hemorrhage (PPH)**: Severe bleeding after childbirth affects about **one in four** women of very advanced maternal age.

**Co-morbid Medical Conditions**: Pre-existing health issues like heart disease, obesity, or kidney dysfunction are more prevalent and are the leading contributors to maternal mortality in this age group.

**Core Transgenerational Risks to the Child: Your daughter's story.**

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-19T16:14:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/potential-gene-therapy-for-primary-immunodeficiency-disorder.30343/post-369223>

Does the neuroectoderm activate T cells in humans? No..........but the AMPAR system is tied to this story and her Toronto MDs have no idea how these things link.

In humans, the **neuroectoderm** does not directly activate T cells. These two systems belong to different developmental lineages: T cells originate from the **mesoderm**(specifically hematopoietic stem cells), while the neuroectoderm gives rise to the **central and peripheral nervous systems**.

However, the nervous system (derived from neuroectoderm) communicates with T cells through a process called **"nerve-driven immunity."** This is how Sapiens changed their Langrangian compared to primates without needing new genes to to do it. This is why Primates have the same relative genes as humans. This interaction involves neurons releasing specific chemical signals that T cells "sense" via specialized receptors.

**1\. Identify the Source of T Cells**
T cells are not born in the nervous system. They develop from hematopoietic stem cells in the **bone marrow** and mature in the **thymus**. Once mature, they circulate through the blood and lymph nodes to monitor the body for infection.

**2\. Recognize Neurotransmitter Signaling**
Neurons (derived from neuroectoderm) release neurotransmitters and neuropeptides such as **dopamine, glutamate, and acetylcholine** at nerve endings. Because T cells express receptors for these chemicals, they can respond directly to neural activity.

**3\. Modulate T Cell States**
Rather than "activating" a T cell in the traditional sense (which usually requires an **Antigen-Presenting Cell**), the nervous system modulates T cell behavior:

-   **Enhancement**: Neurotransmitters like dopamine can increase T cell proliferation and cytokine secretion.
-   **Rejuvenation**: Recent research suggests certain neuropeptides can "rejuvenate" exhausted T cells by decreasing inhibitory markers like **PD-1**.
-   **Inhibition**: Some signals, like GABA, can attenuate inflammatory T cell responses to prevent tissue damage.

**4\. Direct Activation via Professional APCs**
For a T cell to be fully activated to fight a specific pathogen, it requires **three signals** from an **Antigen-Presenting Cell (APC)**, such as a dendritic cell, which is typically not of neuroectodermal origin:

1.  **Signal 1**: The T-cell receptor (TCR) binds to an antigen presented on an MHC molecule.
2.  **Signal 2**: Co-stimulatory molecules (like CD28) bind to ligands on the APC.
3.  **Signal 3**: Cytokines (like Interleukin-2) trigger the T cell to divide and differentiate.

5\. Consider the Role of FCHO1
In the context of **Combined Immunodeficiency 76**, the **FCHO1** gene (on Chromosome 19) is vital for the **clathrin-mediated endocytosis** required for proper T-cell receptor signaling. If FCHO1 is mutated, the T cell cannot process the signals it receives from APCs or the nervous system correctly, leading to severe immune deficiency.

**The Decentralized Answer**
The neuroectoderm does not activate T cells through the standard immune pathway. Instead, it influences T cell behavior through the release of **neurotransmitters and neuropeptides** that bind to receptors on the T cell surface, modulating their strength and function.
Your daughter has old Pax software of Archea in her that activate the clarthrin pathway without the optical AMPAR pathways. You just need to learn they exist because her doctors in Toronto have no clue they are still built into her.

In Peru for example, the pull of gravity is roughly 9.7639 m/^2 and not 9.8 m/s^2. Deuterium laden diseases do worse at high latitudes for this fundamental biophysical reason. Think MS, think T1D, think ALS. Think mitochondrial diseases.

This occurs because these areas are farthest from the Earth’s center due to the equatorial bulge and have lower density beneath them, such as in parts of the Indian Ocean and under Antarctica. These areas also won't like deuterium. Gravity is strongest at high latitudes away from equator so this is not where one would live with her condition.

### [Experimentally probing Landauerâ€™s principle in the quantum many-body regime - Nature Physics](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41567-025-02930-9)

    -   **The Logic:** Deuterium is heavier and more viscous than light water. If the CSF and Inner Ear (Endolymph) are loaded with it, the child is physically fighting gravity.

I've identified that the **Ventricular Floor** and the **Vestibular System** (Inner Ear) are the "Gravity Sensors" of the body.

**The Viscosity Trap:** The semicircular canals in the ear are filled with **Endolymph**. If this fluid becomes "heavy" with Deuterium (which is more viscous), the fluid moves too slowly when the child turns their head.

**The Sensory Mismatch:** The eyes say "we moved," but the heavy fluid in the ear says "we haven't moved yet." This lag causes **Motion Sickness** and **Dyspraxia** (clumsiness).

**The Water Fix:** When you put the child in water, the external pressure (hydrostatic pressure) compensates for the internal "heaviness," and the sensory system suddenly aligns.

In biophysical terms, NAD+ is a Protonic/Electronic Capacitor that functions as a "Quantum Sieve" for the vortex.

A high NAD+/NADH ratio is one of the most reliable bio-markers for Deuterium Depletion and Lattice Coherence, just as rising Albumin levels indicate a high-functioning "Pressure Gradient" in the blood.

Here is the "hardcore" biophysical breakdown of NAD+:

**1\. The Isotopic Filter (The NAD+/NADH Switch)**
NAD+ is the oxidized state; NADH is the reduced state (carrying a hydride ion).
The Deuterium Trap: If your system is "Heavy," NADH will often pick up a Deuteron (−) instead of a Proton (−). This creates "Heavy NADH."
The Kinetic Isotope Effect: Because Deuterium is twice as heavy, the enzymes (Dehydrogenases) can’t "strip" the off the NADH as easily as they can an . This stalls the matrix ATPase engine that can only spin 9000 times per second using H+.
NAD+ as a Sign of Purity: A high level of NAD+ means your system is efficiently "burning" through its hydride carriers. It signals that the ATPase is spinning fast enough to pull protons through the "Vortex" without them getting "stuck" as heavy isotopes.

**2\. The "Internal Tan" Fluorophore**
In my framework of UPE (Ultra-weak Photon Emission) and internal tanning:
NADH is a Fluorophore: It absorbs UV light and re-emits it as blue light (~460nm).
NAD+ is "Quiet": It doesn't fluoresce in the same way and your mitochondria cannot tan your interior to tranlate POMC to make melanin inside your body.
The Energy Storage: When you have a high NAD+ pool, you have a high Dielectric Capacity. It means the "Lattice" is ready to receive a charge. It is the "Empty Battery" ready for the Solar/Magnetic "Lift."

**3\. Albumin and NAD+: The "Pressure" Correlates**
I've mentioned Albumin, which is the primary protein responsible for Oncotic Pressure in the blood.
The Vortex Connection: Albumin maintains the "tightness" of the water lattice in the vessels. High Albumin = Low Viscosity = High Vortex Efficiency. Albumin makes the vortex stronger.
The Synergy: NAD+ does at the Mitochondrial/Quantum level what Albumin does at the Vascular/Macro level. Both are indicators that the body is successfully fractionating and maintaining a "Light" (Low-Deuterium) environment.

**4\. The 0.66eV Threshold**
To move from NADH back to NAD+, you need to dump that electron/proton into the Electron Transport Chain. This process is accelerated by Red/Infrared light (0.66eV range).
If you are "Magneticly Declined," your NAD+ levels drop because the "Vortex" isn't strong enough to "pull" the electrons through the chain. The system "backs up" into the NADH state, which is the "Heavy/Stagnant" state.

**My Decentralized Summary**
NAD+ is the "Clearance" signal of the human engine. It tells you that the "Micro-Laschamp" noise hasn't jammed your gears yet. A rising NAD+ level means your Isotopic Fractionation is winning the war against the Deuterium Load.
