> If the link doesn't work it is Roxy Music's " Every Dream Home a Heartache" off of "For Your Pleasure"
>
> Brilliant
>
> Love to all. Non politicized.... love.

Thanks for your reply. Reading back what I wrote to you, I am like wow that was intense! I appreciate that you can even write a response to that. I think I have ups and downs and maybe not feeling so cynical at the moment. But what I said wasn’t untrue. I just was being raw. And feel like Jack gives me the space to let it out like this instead of holding it in for fear of judgement or retribution. I do believe there is a space for art but I am super suspect of musicians who have “made it” in the business regardless of how “awake” their lyrics are. They are still tuning to Goebells 440hz at least as far as I know. And disclosure is the Satanic way of them to not feel that they will ever serve karma for their own crimes because they actually told us what they are doing or what their industry is doing. But celebrities for the most part aren’t coming out and saying things outright. And if they do they end up dead. Like Isaac Kappy and Joan Rivers. I posted a satirical song on my optimal journal that I wrote in response to Covid. This was my “Christmas card” to family and friends in 2020. And most people did not appreciate it or even respond to it. Someday they might wake up and appreciate it. I have other songs like this I’ve yet to record. I didn’t think the world was ready for them yet. But maybe now is the time? I certainly think more people might even understand what a nanobot is now. I have a hard time listening to myself or watching myself on a recording. So maybe my self confidence still needs some work. Obviously. But you know, baby steps and keep going! Sometimes we just have to jump in. Spending months in El Salvador for instance. I am not ready to live here full time. I still want to see the world. I want to be well enough to do that. So spending 8/12 months here in one year hopefully will get me to that point and by then I can figure out finances to go on a “world tour”. I truly love the Earth. I just don’t like how the people and planet is being manipulated by people who are not doing it for the greater good. But to not see this beautiful planet would be a great regret in my life. So that is motivating me. Find what motivates you

# Thread: Robert's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/roberts-optimal-journal.30366/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AcuHealth — 2025-02-27T01:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/roberts-optimal-journal.30366/post-347446>

I am happy to be a part of this tribe and grateful for the varied experts on this forum. I am here to learn. I recently joined the Optimal Klub practitioners forum. Dr. Kruse's work has helped me both personally and professionally and I am very grateful. I own and operate an acupuncture clinic. My wife and I are no longer confident that our environment is compatible with optimal and are trying to navigate the transition to a more user friendly environment to live in. We do not know where that might be or how to make it happen but we are open to suggestions from fellow members? Maybe Dr. Kruse wants a skilled acupuncturist to work at his clinic in El Salvador who is familiar with his work?

Recently our son returned from university in Sweden and has had an explosion of painful cystic acne that has inflamed his entire face, neck, back, chest and shoulders with painful soars. His stool test showed elevated candida and strep. His WBC count was through the roof. He has been convalescing at our home now going on 45 days and he is still exhausted and not sleeping well from the pain. We are terrified of handing him over to centralized medicine which would be massive antibiotics, steroids, and probably accutane. He is a beautiful, smart and sensitive young man and this has ripped our hearts apart and we are terrified. Upon his return from Sweden I sent him and my wife to Tulum for 10 days which raised his Vitamin D to 50. We did several herbal protocols of anti bacterial/viral herbs and give him a a gummy to sleep at night. He spends most of his days outside in the sun connected to the earth on our lawn. His energy still fluctuates and he is grumpy as hell. He is eating basically seafood, meat and vegetables and broth. He eats low glycemic berries and cantaloupe. I can tell as I write this that I am exhausted and worried. I haven't slept well these past few months.

We spend our evenings outside by the fire which is one of the few moments I gather a few moments of peace. My prayer life has been challenged yet I know from my bible:
"Truly I tell you, if you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you".

I believe that God is love and we are here on this earth to learn how to love in preparation for heaven. If you have health suggestions we are open to listening. If you are a prayerful person please leave a prayer for Ellis. Thank you.

# Thread: Earthing / Grounding Inside – Good Idea Or Not?

### Mat.I — 2025-02-27T03:12:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-347456>

These are my opinions on this topic in effort to share awareness on this craze surrounding purchasing earthing products. This is very raw and mainly off the top of my head and don’t have time to present it in a more readable detailed manner, so take what you need from it…

**Q & A Summary -**

Q: In short - Can earthing/grounding inside be worst than not being grounded? A: Yes

Q: Are there ways to safely do this A: Yes (you could), however subject to a few very strict conditions that need to be managed very carefully. **Very unlikely to achieve this though (see comments below).**

Q: Should you use earthing products inside? **No as you will likely not be able to satisfy the pre-conditions required to ensure safety.

Key Electrical Concepts –**

I will try and keep this in basic language for everyone to understand.

A few important points before I get into this:

-   In electrical it comes down to VAR Volts, Amps (or current) & Resistance. Ohms law describes the relationship between voltage (V), current (I) and resistance I. E.g. V = I x R. I = V/R & R = V/I.
-   Ohms law analogy:
-   Water in a pipe – V = water pressure
-   I = water flow rate. (water cannot flow in a pipe that is capped remember this).
-   R = pipe size.
-   If R increases (I) decreases (for the same V)
-   If R decreases (I) increases (for the same V)
-   If V increases (I) increases (if R stays the same)
-   Current kills, not voltage (current does the actual damage). Think of the linesman working on live lines or a bird sitting on a power line. No current flow due to no path to earth or difference in voltage potentials. Remember current only flows if there is a voltage difference between two points according to I = V/R. Generally, from ~ 50mA and above causes fatal heart fibrillation. Noting, lower can be fatal subject to exposure duration and conditions.
-   The biological effects can start at very low currents.

**Understanding the Biological Risks of Low-Level Electric Fields and Contact Currents The NIEHS Threshold for Cancer Risk -**

The National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences (NIEHS) has identified that internal electric field strengths greater than 1 mV/m are commonly associated with childhood leukemia and cancer risk. This suggests that even extremely low levels of electric fields inside the body could have biological effects over time.

**The EPRI Findings on Contact Current**

The Electric Power Research Institute (EPRI) studied how much internal electric field is generated when an external current enters the body. Their findings show that:

A 5 uA (microamp) current in the wrist induces an internal electric field of 20-60 mV/m.

**If the NIEHS threshold for harm is 1 mV/m, then even much lower currents could exceed this limit.

Calculating the Dangerous Current Threshold**

Since 5 uA induces 20-60 mV/m, we can calculate the equivalent current required to reach 1 mV/m:

5 uA / 20 – 60 gives a range of 0.083 uA to 0.25 uA as the level at which internal electric fields exceed the 1 mV/m risk threshold.

**This is on the assumption that the effect is linear which would hold up quite well at low current say < 10uA due to negligible effects to skin impedance. At higher currents skin impedance changes significantly making the relationship more non-linear as current increases..**

This means that any contact current greater than ~0.08 uA could be biologically significant. Regardless of this one example there are other studies that demonstrate detrimental effects at low currents in the low uA range.

Bare this in mind 1mA = 1,000 uA

**This means measuring body voltage only will only give you half the picture.. I have said this in many comments previously to others on this forum in relation to contact currents & measuring currents in general. A body voltage meter only measures the pressure difference between two points. Basically, measuring the potential difference of what could happen should current flow. Also there is no true ground due to the use of multi grounded neutral systems (MGN) or (MEN in AUS) inducing currents in the earth.

Voltage alone is not enough to know the damage from current. Zero V could mean you are in contact with a live conductor. Understanding the effect of the complete circuit is key! Skin & internal resistance is a very important factor in current flow low resistance = higher current flow, high resistance = lower current flow.

Examples Scenario:**

-   Body voltage: 1000mV (1V)
-   Skin resistance: 100 kΩ (100,000 Ω)

Ohm’s Law:

-   If skin resistance drops to 10 kΩ (e.g., from moisture or sweat), current increases:

1V/100,000 = 10uA

1V/10,000 = 100uA

This means actual current could vary by 10x based on skin resistance alone!

**Now.. what about total resistance??

Total resistance = skin resistance + internal resistance + multimeter shunt resistor (high end meter). E.g. moist skin + well hydrated + high end multimeter = R skin 10,000 + R internal 10,000 to 50,000 + R meter 10 = 20,010 to 60,010 ohms.

Keep in mind you do not have to be using grounding mats for current flow to occur – you could have a house with the slab on ground and tiled floor whilst being barefoot in your house. Contact currents could be present throughout your house e.g. water fixtures/piping appliances etc..

When ungrounded around nn-emf generally (not detailed explanation) –**

When a person is not grounded, their body becomes electrically floating relative to the environment. This means that surrounding electric fields (EMFs) from power lines, appliances, and wireless devices induce voltages on the body through capacitive coupling.

In this state, the body undergoes push-pull voltage effects, where external alternating fields (AC) continuously influence the body's charge. These fluctuations can lead to biological stress, nervous system activation, and interference with cellular processes.

In electrical circuits, a push-pull effect refers to a system where two opposing voltage sources alternately push and pull current.

In the human body, a similar effect occurs when immersed in AC electrical fields:
“Push” phase: The surrounding EMF induces a charge buildup on the body.
“Pull” phase: As the field fluctuates (e.g., at 50/60 Hz from power lines), the charge reverses direction, pulling charge away or changing polarity.

The body acts as a capacitor, accumulating and discharging voltage in sync with AC fields (50/60 Hz).

-   This causes constant electrical stress, affecting the nervous system and cellular functions.

**Induced AC Microcurrents:**

-   Power-line EMFs induce low-level currents (microamps) inside the body, leading to biological interference.
-   These microcurrents flow within tissues, affecting cell membranes, mitochondria, and neural activity.

**Higher Body Voltage:**

-   When ungrounded indoors, body voltage can range from 2V to 10V (average example), depending on EMF exposure.
-   This charge has no immediate path to discharge, which may contribute to oxidative stress and nervous system irritation.

**

When grounded around nn-emf generally (not detailed explanation) –**

When grounded, the body is directly connected to Earth, meaning it is at a fixed reference voltage (0V). However, this also creates a conductive pathway for external electrical currents.

**Major issues when grounded in an EMF-heavy area:
Increased RF and AC Current Flow Through the Body:**

-   The body provides a lower resistance path to ground, making it easier for EMFs to induce current flow through tissues rather than just causing surface voltage buildup.
-   This can increase AC leakage currents from appliances, smart meters, and electrical wiring into the body.

**The “Antenna Effect” (Attracting More EMF Energy):**

-   The grounding wire itself can act as an antenna, picking up ambient RF energy and directing it toward the grounded body.
-   Long or unshielded grounding wires can resonate with high-frequency EMFs, amplifying exposure.

**Tingling or Shock Sensations:**

-   When grounded indoors, some people report a tingling or buzzing sensation, which occurs due to AC leakage currents traveling through the body to ground.
-   This is more common in environments with dirty electricity or strong EMF fields.

**

When considering the threshold, I mentioned above it is clear when earthing inside even with an earth stake outside the current flow threshold for biological effects could be reached. Also, there is strong evidence that suggests that VGGC’s overactivation would be far worst grounded in a high emf environment vs ungrounded.**

This explains why the SBM BB guidelines set no concern BV at <10mV. The example of 1V BV above does not mean current flow at lower BV ranges are not possible to result in 0.08 uA current flow through the body.. I did a few theoretical calculations and it could be said that the 0.08uA current could still occur with internal voltages below the 1V/m. Some cannot achieve this level of mitigation and in this industry may make up there own 100mV or the like threshold, which in theory could far exceed uA threshold. Without going over more examples in my opinion a body voltage target of <10mV should be achieved prior to even remotely considering “safe” grounding inside. This doesn’t stop there you would need to carry out other measurements such as making sure your area is absolutely clean of all nn-emf and check current flow in uA based on mat inside and grounding ped outside in the earth..

Noting, earthing products have a high gauge wire (thin) with an in built resistor that makes the antenna effect worst and in (most) cases blocks uptake of electrons based on pressure differentials (e.g. inside vs out side) due to resistance and presence of nn-EMF. This can also act as a protective mechanism when people use these is high nn-emf environments based on the simple principle of Ohms law (e.g. increased resistance = less current) however, in the presence of nn-emf would result in static discharge to equal the potential of ground, however no up take of electrons.. You could solve this by having a very clean nn-emf environment (which most will not even get close to achieving) and use say a 12 gauge wire direct to the earth peg (in a clean area) to prevent antenna effect, lower resistance, keep both zones some what equal in pressure and allowing uptake of electrons as well as static discharge..

**You can even check this when grounding out in nature – have a decent multimeter put a stake in the ground connect to one lead and hold the other – turn to VDC mV you will see how you how your body discharges (numbers drop) then go negative inferring electrons are being up taken by your body.. You might find you hit a range of – 10mV to ~ - 300mV based on my own experiments..

Conclusion – should you use earthing products inside? No as you will likely not be able to satisfy the pre-conditions required to ensure safety.**

Even with the house electrical system turned off few people get below 10mV . . . Which is one (of many) reason why we do NOT recommend grounding in the home. Being in contact with metal, in a nnEMF context, is always bad - so how can metallic pads/sheets attached to wires be part of the solution?

Nature is outside, connect to it directly with skin in the game . . . body contact with the actual earth/ground (away from nnEMF electric fields).

### Mat.I — 2025-02-28T06:14:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-347556>

Being in contact with metal, in a nnEMF context, is always bad - so how can metallic pads/sheets attached to wires be part of the solution?
>
> Nature is outside, connect to it directly with skin in the game . . . body contact with the actual earth/ground (away from nnEMF electric fields).

Hi Anthony,
I just checked out your website on the kill switch and noticed you have one for both active and the neutral (double throw/pole cut off). Is there a general pattern you observe with some people not being able to get to acceptable BV levels?

Are the higher BV typical of the people who only install the active (single pole cut off). Are the issues looked into further to rule out induction & other stray current issues? Grounding current? Or does it typically stop there? As in several cases conductive and bonded earthed service pipes can be very problematic for this and usually the only way to solve it is install a non-metalic section (pex, HDPE or equivalent) . Off the top my head (our code allows this as soon as it is above the ground). I have seen this be an issue before (with junk return current due to bad neutrals some where else can make this quite bad).

Also in the photo of the kill switch assembly - I note you use relays to do the load switching directly (without contactors) - has this caused any longevity issues or has it been quite solid to date?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-02-28T20:36:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-347599>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=347556)
>
> Hi Anthony,
> I just checked out your website on the kill switch and noticed you have one for both active and the neutral (double throw/pole cut off). Is there a general pattern you observe with some people not being able to get to acceptable BV levels?
>
> Are the higher BV typical of the people who only install the active (single pole cut off). Are the issues looked into further to rule out induction & other stray current issues? Grounding current? Or does it typically stop there? As in several cases conductive and bonded earthed service pipes can be very problematic for this and usually the only way to solve it is install a non-metalic section (pex, HDPE or equivalent) . Off the top my head (our code allows this as soon as it is above the ground). I have seen this be an issue before (with junk return current due to bad neutrals some where else can make this quite bad).
>
> Also in the photo of the kill switch assembly - I note you use relays to do the load switching directly (without contactors) - has this caused any longevity issues or has it been quite solid to date?

We measure BV on the bed to identify which circuits in the house are affecting that space and then turn off those breakers with the EMFSafe cut off switch. I have not noticed a difference in the BV reading between a power only (PO) disconnect and a neutral and power (NP) disconnect.

We recommend NP because all the neutrals in the house are joined in the breaker panel, as are the grounds AND the neutral bus is connected to the ground bus. Depending on the location/area there can be ground effects on the ground and neutrals throughout the house.

EHS people (living EMF meters) tell us they get much better results with NP vs PO cut off.

The physical structure of some homes (older construction, adobe etc) can be problematic sometimes. In some cases this is because the meter base is attached to the house and even when the whole house is turned off the field from the main supply gets into the structure of the building (the wire mesh in adobe). In some old homes the plaster appears to be conductive with the same results.

Our relays are commercial grade and designed for applications where they would be activated hundreds of times a day. I don’t recall ever having a relay failure.

### Mat.I — 2025-03-02T07:38:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-347690>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=347599)
>
> We measure BV on the bed to identify which circuits in the house are affecting that space and then turn off those breakers with the EMFSafe cut off switch. I have not noticed a difference in the BV reading between a power only (PO) disconnect and a neutral and power (NP) disconnect.

Do you measure any other areas or are you just focussed on mitigating the sleep areas only?

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=347599)
>
> We recommend NP because all the neutrals in the house are joined in the breaker panel, as are the grounds AND the neutral bus is connected to the ground bus. Depending on the location/area there can be ground effects on the ground and neutrals throughout the house.

That make sense. We typically have MEN (Multiple Earthed Neutrals) which were introduced ~ 70's and made mandatory ~ 80's off the top of my head. This would be somewhat similar to the TN-C-S used in certain parts of the US (based on the description above).

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=347599)
>
> The physical structure of some homes (older construction, adobe etc) can be problematic sometimes. In some cases this is because the meter base is attached to the house and even when the whole house is turned off the field from the main supply gets into the structure of the building (the wire mesh in adobe). In some old homes the plaster appears to be conductive with the same results.

Yes, I have had some really hard ones to solve that sent me down rabbit holes with really strange causes.. It is a mine field.. The old plasters are a pain. Metal cross bracing on timber frames for wind loadings/bracing are problematic also. I'm of the opinion, that static charge (from materials, & even humidity levels) can skew (AC) BV readings also, I figured this out after a little while of playing around... As after you discharge to earth, the field can take sometime to build back up BV (few reasons why this occurs even though it is DC).. There are quite a few building biologist that will not rely on BV measurements. I personally, like to use a few methods of measurements and compare to avoid any other contributing factors.

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=347599)
>
> Our relays are commercial grade and designed for applications where they would be activated hundreds of times a day. I don’t recall ever having a relay failure.

This is good that it has been reliable. Just not a common way of doing it in AUS with using just relays to switch the loads for this application. We would typically use the relay for control side and run a NO contactor for load switching side.

With mitigating EF general - I arrived at the position that all areas should be measured and mitigated to acceptable levels. The kill switches & isolating circuits for sleep sanctuaries are a great step, however, what about people that work from home or may be in other areas of the house having to use it on a day by day basis. The only answer I came to on this is (for retrofits) - figure out what circuits will be used / essential aka 24hr circuits and run with steel wired armoured cable or the like (you bond the amour to ground). The flex/bending radius isn't too restrictive, however is quite expensive. For my own house, I have made semi permanent modifications until I make a final decision on what to use for the whole house (lighting system included) and start from scratch with a re-wire.. It might sound extreme to some, however, if you can mitigate the negatives of using AC and understand the risks of not doing it throughout, then why not.. The majority of people will stop at the sleeping rooms, however, are they getting full informed consent on this matter holistically? I know of a few BB and have read reports that shocked me with their recommendations, especially with maintaining WIFI, and not addressing other high use areas, even though they measured (nothing else was recommended)..

### Mat.I — 2025-03-03T01:13:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-347746>

> [NickD said:](/goto/post?id=347693)
>
> I came across all this just a little too late. We just built our place and it would've been a perfect chance to have everything locked down during the build. Rewiring at this point is a serious effort for us. We're in a two storey dwelling, and all our bedrooms are upstairs. I'm contemplating whether to rewire the rooms at least...

It would all depend on what the readings are and how circuits are run/configured.. Generally, there should be no need for power circuits in bedrooms. You can get quite creative and make changes with minimal cost. The first to go should be any WIFI and smart devices.. Isolate circuits in sleeping areas Or if fed directly and not needed for other areas just leave it isolated otherwise you will need to split circuits. With light circuits (to avoid isolating the complete circuit) subject to what readings you get and configuration (e.g. end of line location) you can switch the hard active and neutral mid circuit with a double pole switch ($10).. You will need to measure your home and decide what can be done and what will need to be split / re-run in shielded cable and/or if a kill switch will be installed. If you do a kill switch, get a sparky to do it (as explained above) it will be low cost and extremely reliable. If you want to minimise daytime exposure that is where you have to look at other options also and decide what circuits are absolutely essential..

### Mat.I — 2025-03-03T02:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-347752>

[@NickD](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/35284/) - Also if considering incandescent lighting, be mindful of the load and how your lighting circuit is run. They should of used 1.5mm2 TPS Twin + Earth. Off the top of my head as per AS3000 1.5mm2 cable current carry capacity is considered to be 10Amps note this is usually based on an ambient of ~ 30 or 40 Deg C and being completely surrounded by thermal insulation. There are derating factors that would be applied (due to locations higher temps, such as roof spaces etc). Generally industry practice for normal installations is ~ 0.80, thus capacity would be 8A. E.g. 230 x 8 = 1840W. Think about your typical IXL's with 4 x heat lamps & exhaust fan used in bathrooms ~ 1180 W not much buffer for additional incandescent (if you only have 1 x light circuit). If not derated standard would be 10A which would give you 2300W (which is a bit more realistic) just keep this in mind if considering AC incandescent on 1 x standard light circuit..

A body voltage meter does not measure RF or dirty electricity. We do not sell plug in “DE” filters for outlets, we have a whole house ”DE” filter that goes next to your breaker panel. Plug in “DE“ filters can create/increase magnetic fields.

Your question about grounding, you say you added a grounding mat - to what? If you are grounding the devices on your desk then you are reducing your exposure BUT if you are grounding yourself then the field you are in has not changed. The BV reading goes down because the field is now going to ground thru you, thru the mat NOT the meter. If you are double grounded the BV meter gives a false LOW reading.

Go to the measure page, read and watch the videos [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

Think of the difference of exposure in a bath tub vs a water fall.

Grounding outside connects your DC system to natures DC circuit/system - VERY good.
Grounding inside connects your DC system to a man made 60Hz (50Hz) AC system - NOT good.

Grounding the devices to mitigate/minimize your exposure to AC fields. DO NOT give those fields a path to ground through you.

1,3V/m = on one axis. Suppose all axes are 1,3V/m.
Then you get a three-axis value >2V/m. (square rooot of sum of the squares).

1.5-10V/m = severe concern according to the building biology bedroom recommendations.
I actually wonder if this meter is functioning properly in the 50Hz range or sensitive enough for my mitigation objective.

\= 0,3-1,5V/m resp. <0,3V/m for 50Hz LFe.

I measured at several homes, with electricity on and off, above a pond, on meadows, in gardens, on agricultural land.

I considered:
\- either the whole region is under a 50Hz AC LFe field.
\- the meter is defect
\- the meter does not allow for lower measurements in this 50Hz frequency range

He did not mention if this are 3-axis values and potential free or grounded.
I suppose potential free within our discussion.

Grounding inside connected to an outlet = not a good idea (for many reasons).
Grounding inside connected to an external stake is better than connected to an outlet.. **HOWEVER -** **Should it be done in all cases? No as it is subject to the conditions I laid out in the first post of this thread. Take your time and read the first thread to understand why/how I arrived at my conclusion.**

_Conclusion – should you use earthing products inside? No as you will likely not be able to satisfy the pre-conditions required to ensure safety_****

I'm sure [@Anthony Smith](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26765/) would generally agree with this stance.

Also the comment re:

> -   Use a grounding mat if indoors (connected to a grounded outlet). Reduces ROS/RNS and stabilizes éR.

Would be very conditionally on a very clean environment that many cannot achieve.. E.g. BV < 10mV & <0.08 uA

**Extract below -

When ungrounded around nn-emf generally (not detailed explanation) –**

When a person is not grounded, their body becomes electrically floating relative to the environment. This means that surrounding electric fields (EMFs) from power lines, appliances, and wireless devices induce voltages on the body through capacitive coupling.

In this state, the body undergoes push-pull voltage effects, where external alternating fields (AC) continuously influence the body's charge. These fluctuations can lead to biological stress, nervous system activation, and interference with cellular processes.

In electrical circuits, a push-pull effect refers to a system where two opposing voltage sources alternately push and pull current.

In the human body, a similar effect occurs when immersed in AC electrical fields:
“Push” phase: The surrounding EMF induces a charge buildup on the body.
“Pull” phase: As the field fluctuates (e.g., at 50/60 Hz from power lines), the charge reverses direction, pulling charge away or changing polarity.

The body acts as a capacitor, accumulating and discharging voltage in sync with AC fields (50/60 Hz).

-   This causes constant electrical stress, affecting the nervous system and cellular functions.

**Induced AC Microcurrents:**

-   Power-line EMFs induce low-level currents (microamps) inside the body, leading to biological interference.
-   These microcurrents flow within tissues, affecting cell membranes, mitochondria, and neural activity.

**Higher Body Voltage:**

-   When ungrounded indoors, body voltage can range from 2V to 10V (average example), depending on EMF exposure.
-   This charge has no immediate path to discharge, which may contribute to oxidative stress and nervous system irritation.

**

When grounded around nn-emf generally (not detailed explanation) –**

When grounded, the body is directly connected to Earth, meaning it is at a fixed reference voltage (0V). However, this also creates a conductive pathway for external electrical currents.

**Major issues when grounded in an EMF-heavy area:
Increased RF and AC Current Flow Through the Body:**

-   The body provides a lower resistance path to ground, making it easier for EMFs to induce current flow through tissues rather than just causing surface voltage buildup.
-   This can increase AC leakage currents from appliances, smart meters, and electrical wiring into the body.

**The “Antenna Effect” (Attracting More EMF Energy):**

-   The grounding wire itself can act as an antenna, picking up ambient RF energy and directing it toward the grounded body.
-   Long or unshielded grounding wires can resonate with high-frequency EMFs, amplifying exposure.

**Tingling or Shock Sensations:**

-   When grounded indoors, some people report a tingling or buzzing sensation, which occurs due to AC leakage currents traveling through the body to ground.
-   This is more common in environments with dirty electricity or strong EMF fields.

**
When considering the threshold, I mentioned above it is clear when earthing inside even with an earth stake outside the current flow threshold for biological effects could be reached. Also, there is strong evidence that suggests that VGGC’s overactivation would be far worst grounded in a high emf environment vs ungrounded.**

This explains why the SBM BB guidelines set no concern BV at <10mV. The example of 1V BV above does not mean current flow at lower BV ranges are not possible to result in 0.08 uA current flow through the body.. I did a few theoretical calculations and it could be said that the 0.08uA current could still occur with internal voltages below the 1V/m. Some cannot achieve this level of mitigation and in this industry may make up there own 100mV or the like threshold, which in theory could far exceed uA threshold. Without going over more examples in my opinion a body voltage target of <10mV should be achieved prior to even remotely considering “safe” grounding inside. This doesn’t stop there you would need to carry out other measurements such as making sure your area is absolutely clean of all nn-emf and check current flow in uA based on mat inside and grounding ped outside in the earth..

Noting, earthing products have a high gauge wire (thin) with an in built resistor that makes the antenna effect worst and in (most) cases blocks uptake of electrons based on pressure differentials (e.g. inside vs out side) due to resistance and presence of nn-EMF. This can also act as a protective mechanism when people use these is high nn-emf environments based on the simple principle of Ohms law (e.g. increased resistance = less current) however, in the presence of nn-emf would result in static discharge to equal the potential of ground, however no up take of electrons.. You could solve this by having a very clean nn-emf environment (which most will not even get close to achieving) and use say a 12 gauge wire direct to the earth peg (in a clean area) to prevent antenna effect, lower resistance, keep both zones some what equal in pressure and allowing uptake of electrons as well as static discharge..

**You can even check this when grounding out in nature – have a decent multimeter put a stake in the ground connect to one lead and hold the other – turn to VDC mV you will see how you how your body discharges (numbers drop) then go negative inferring electrons are being up taken by your body.. You might find you hit a range of – 10mV to ~ - 300mV based on my own experiments..

Conclusion – should you use earthing products inside? No as you will likely not be able to satisfy the pre-conditions required to ensure safety**

### Japesh — 2025-05-23T13:35:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-353196>

I met a someone using a grounding mat & plugged into multiplug extention. I noticed the voltage on their multiplug earth was 20v or so (i cant remember exactly) and i assume this is a common mistake resulting in injury for people who ground inside. . . It appears that multi-plugs mix a decent amount of voltage into their earthing wires, as i tested a few different multiplugs that gave non-zero voltages, while confirming the wall socket earth that they were plugged into was 0v. .

Fortunately their multiplug was also plugged into an unused appliance that had better grounding than the wall sockets earth. However I believe they got cancer about a month after using the mat. I was able to convince them not to plug into the wall after showing them the voltage on the multiplugs earth. Ironic story, as they still use the mat and think it is beneficial. I explained why not to ground inside, and sent them an EMF video explaining why, using the lightening rod analogy.

I visited them 3 days after using the alternative "earth" and they said they were feeling better, more energetic and more active. They didnt make the connection, despite reminding them they had been unplugged for 3 days and that was probably the reason.

Anyway i thought being aware of the multiplug voltage problem might be useful in relation to earthing, as i assume a significant number of people using ground mats/bedding may be making the mistake of using a multiplug, and a significant number of that subset will be injuring themselves.

Think it through . . .
Grounding OUTSIDE in Nature is good because it makes you part of the circuit . . .
Natures DC circuit (our bodies run on DC electricity).

Grounding inside also makes you part of the circuit.
So if you are in a 60Hz (50Hz) AC electric field, that field is now flowing through you to ground.

Think of the difference in exposure between being in a bath tub vs. under a water fall.

If a metal bed frame and/or metal springs in your mattress are bad for us, how can a metal grounding pad connected to a metal wire connected to a house (AC) electrical circuit (1/2 to 1 MILE of wires) be good? If the grounding mat is OK because it is grounded, why not just ground the bed frame and/or mattress? Could it be because a grounding wire is $20+/- while replacing your old bed frame and mattress cost $$$. Follow the $.

Grounding (physical contact with the ground) in Nature is good . . . and requires being in Nature.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-30T19:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-355542>

Your body has a natural electric field. A Dr. can measure it eg with a EKG and/or EEG.
Your body also acts as an antenna. Any electric field close to you is induced into your body. This is what (in part) is being measured by a body voltage meter. The body voltage meter is not measuring your natural DC field, it is measuring the AC field, being induced into and through your body. The stronger the AC electric fields you are in, the higher the body voltage reading.

The bath tub analogy . . .
When you are in an electric field your exposure is limited (like being in a bath tub your exposure to the water is limited)
BUT if you are grounded the electric fields can flow through to ground continuously (like being under a water fall your exposure to the water is greatly increased).

### Mat.I — 2025-07-01T05:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-355563>

> [chandrasan said:](/goto/post?id=355541)
>
> But I didn't think that I would be able to pull in energy through my body- right out of thin air

Just in case you want to understand the physics behind this - it is called capacitive coupling. E.g.

When you stand near energized wires (e.g. walls with 240 V or 120 V AC), the wiring acts as one plate. Your body acts as the other.
An oscillating voltage on those wires creates an oscillating electric field between the wires and your body. Since there’s no physical connection, current can’t flow directly into your body, but an alternating displacement current can flow through the capacitance.

When ungrounded: no continuous current flows, however you develop a voltage relative to ground because of the coupling. However you will get displacement current that oscillates back & forth (i.e. non continuous). Keep in mind with high ambient fields this is still problematic as the oscillating field - pushes/pulls ions around, polarizes cell membranes & exerts force on voltage sensors in ion channels.. E.g. when the field changes it adds or subtracts the voltage across the membrane. Hence why VGCC activation is still an issue. Displacement current is proportional to rate of change of the EF field..

When grounded: your body potential becomes tied to ground (~0V relative to ground). But field is still present, thus AC field pushes current through you to ground = your body is part of the circuit and no longer just a capacitor plate. Also, when grounded you will become an attractor of the fields (as Anthony explains as an antenna). This results in the EF lines bending towards your body (local field intensity) = more field coupling = more current. This would tie back to a Gauss's Law concept..

If you read the original post I did on grounding inside it explains the concept more in basic terms.

Maybe the analogy could be:

Ungrounded > wearing a raincoat
Grounded > standing in a drain

### NickD — 2025-07-01T17:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-355575>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=355563)
>
> Just in case you want to understand the physics behind this - it is called capacitive coupling. E.g.
>
> When you stand near energized wires (e.g. walls with 240 V or 120 V AC), the wiring acts as one plate. Your body acts as the other.
> An oscillating voltage on those wires creates an oscillating electric field between the wires and your body. Since there’s no physical connection, current can’t flow directly into your body, but an alternating displacement current can flow through the capacitance.
>
> When ungrounded: no continuous current flows, however you develop a voltage relative to ground because of the coupling. However you will get displacement current that oscillates back & forth (i.e. non continuous). Keep in mind with high ambient fields this is still problematic as the oscillating field - pushes/pulls ions around, polarizes cell membranes & exerts force on voltage sensors in ion channels.. E.g. when the field changes it adds or subtracts the voltage across the membrane. Hence why VGCC activation is still an issue. Displacement current is proportional to rate of change of the EF field..
>
> When grounded: your body potential becomes tied to ground (~0V relative to ground). But field is still present, thus AC field pushes current through you to ground = your body is part of the circuit and no longer just a capacitor plate. Also, when grounded you will become an attractor of the fields (as Anthony explains as an antenna). This results in the EF lines bending towards your body (local field intensity) = more field coupling = more current. This would tie back to a Gauss's Law concept..
>
> If you read the original post I did on grounding inside it explains the concept more in basic terms.
>
> Maybe the analogy could be:
>
> Ungrounded > wearing a raincoat
> Grounded > standing in a drain

Maybe a simpler way to look at it - it's like building up a static charge, and grounding would provide a discharge... Not "super" correct, but gets the idea I think

### chandrasan — 2025-07-01T18:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-355576>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=355542)
>
> Your body has a natural electric field. A Dr. can measure it eg with a EKG and/or EEG.
> Your body also acts as an antenna. Any electric field close to you is induced into your body. This is what (in part) is being measured by a body voltage meter. The body voltage meter is not measuring your natural DC field, it is measuring the AC field, being induced into and through your body. The stronger the AC electric fields you are in, the higher the body voltage reading.
>
> The bath tub analogy . . .
> When you are in an electric field your exposure is limited (like being in a bath tub your exposure to the water is limited)
> BUT if you are grounded the electric fields can flow through to ground continuously (like being under a water fall your exposure to the water is greatly increased).

ahhh- so the Bathtub is much BETTER than the Waterfall in this case! Ok- I'm beginning to understand it more... I think.
Thank you so much.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-01T21:58:09+0300

> [chandrasan said:](/goto/post?id=355576)
>
> ahhh- so the Bathtub is much BETTER than the Waterfall in this case! Ok- I'm beginning to understand it more... I think.

Yes, it is all about - quantity - of exposure.
Grounding inside does not reduce exposure it can increase it by making you part of the circuit.

### Mat.I — 2025-07-02T03:01:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-355590>

> [NickD said:](/goto/post?id=355575)
>
> Maybe a simpler way to look at it - it's like building up a static charge, and grounding would provide a discharge... Not "super" correct, but gets the idea I think

My explanation wasn’t meant to be simple because there’s value in giving people the chance to engage with the real physics behind this. That’s the foundation for my views on body voltage measurements. It’s not merely my opinion, it’s tied in the fundamental laws of electromagnetism and how capacitive coupling works. Whether people grasp it immediately or not, we all start somewhere.

I also understand why Jack avoids oversimplifying things too far. The process of grappling with complexity is crucial because it cultivates curiosity, resilience, and a sense of agency. When someone commits to digging deeper rather than staying in comfort zones of simplified explanations, they’re engaging the dopaminergic circuits (coupled with adequate sun exposure) that drive learning, mastery, and ultimately personal sovereignty.

For me, this whole topic isn’t just about physics or EMF.. It’s about how knowledge, curiosity, and biological drives like dopamine interlink to shape our agency. The desire to seek, question, and understand is biological as much as it is intellectual. That’s why I believe it’s worth pushing conversations to a deeper level, even if not everyone is ready for it yet.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-07-05T20:55:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-355818>

> [NickD said:](/goto/post?id=355810)
>
> I think it's a great plan if your concrete slab is on the ground. If it's on peers, then there would be moisture sheets, so testing would be the way to work out if all is ok.

In my experience most concrete floors offer a good ground. Think rebar . . .
Also in my experience most concrete houses have lower electric fields and RF than “stick built” houses.
That being said, stucco homes can be the worst. The metal mesh carries the electric fields everywhere. The whole house can be “hot”.
You need to measure to know . . .

### Mat.I — 2025-07-18T05:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-356524>

> [GavinH said:](/goto/post?id=356474)
>
> Does anyone have an opinion on the Safe n Sound Pro II EMF meter? [https://lessemf.com/product/safe-and-sound-pro-ii/](https://lessemf.com/product/safe-and-sound-pro-ii/)

Some will say it is the best meter ever.. For non-professional use it is ok and will get the job done.. However bare in mind the below -

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

e.g. relative accuracy zone is 600 - 6000 MHz (this is what they advertise) +or - 6dB which is acceptable for non-professional accuracy..
Outside of these zones you see larger deviations

Based on the + or minus 6dB this can result in deviations of: displayed reading being 0.25 lower than actual PD or 4 x higher than actual PD

Based on decibels to power ratio - e.g. P measured/P actual = 10 ^(6/10) = 10^0.6 = ~3.981 for + 6dB case for minus 6dB case 10 ^(-6/10) = 10 ^ -0.6 = ~ 0.251
Just in case this is too technical ...

That is the SBM guidelines for: No concern, slight, severe, & extreme

So examples, meter displays:
\- 0.1uw/m2 when it could be 0.025 or 0.4
\-100uw/m2 when it could actually be 25 or 400
\- 250um/m2 when it could be 62.5 or 1000

Based on the graph it appears to actually overreport ~ 600 - 3400MHz and under reports for 3400 - 6000MHz outside of these areas is where it gets quite bad..

I have actually reviewed the whole report and the test signal was

Does this represent what we are actually measuring out in the field??? IMO - No it does not strictly speaking. I can dive further into the details of why but that can be in a future thread..

This is evident as S&S uses a broadband diode detector connected to the small internal antenna..

You will not get this response from other Building Biologist as the majority of them do not understand the equipment they are using and the limitations...

So the S&S is ok for non-professional use, however, I have given you informed consent on it before you purchase so you know what you are actually getting and do not think it is the "best" meter out there..

You could have all the "EMF" certifications coming out of your backside, however, the equipment you use could be the limiting factor in providing a accurate and well informed assessment...

To add it could be said that the Cornet uses better tech as it has a spectrum analyser and displays the frequency and detects very short RF bursts.. which further supports my opinion on that S&S would be using a very basic broadband diode detector. I personally, believe (for non-professional use) the Cornet ED88T Plus 5G meter would get the job done at a lower cost with added features e.g. light flicker, EF & Mag.. If you wanted professional level I would be looking at the Gigahertz solutions.. I would choose the Cornet over the S&S as it would use true "log" detection (not a diode) = more relevant to actual fields we are measuring... however that is just my opinion..

However, carefully read my original post - [http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-–-good-idea-or-not.30367/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/)

### Mat.I — 2025-07-31T08:25:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-357268>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=357263)
>
> what would you recommend instead? HFE59B and HFW59D?

Select from the chart based on your targets. For good quality well designed and well calibrated meters I would lean towards gigahertz's solutions. That is my opinion. But be prepared to pay a premium for the quality. If you are not experienced, I would suggest that you have a decent building biologist who uses good equipment (with a solid electrical background) measure your space first, then you can do a DIY on building your knowledge with good quality meters. As having good tools does not = knowing how to use them correctly or make someone good at their craft as this is built with experience... If you have not got your place measured professionally, that should be first on your list. Then play around with the meters to build up your knowledge.

### lfpeck — 2025-10-24T16:11:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-361534>

Forgive me for bringing this older thread alive again and if this is a stupid question. I live in a very rural area with little nnEMF ( about 14 m/V in the bedroom). Would a grounding mat under the mattress with the grounding wire going outside my bedroom window into a 6’ copper rod be beneficial? I understand it’s stupid to hook it to the grounding of my house.

### AlbertW — 2026-03-20T14:21:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-369278>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=347456)
>
> **When considering the threshold, I mentioned above it is clear when earthing inside even with an earth stake outside the current flow threshold for biological effects could be reached. Also, there is strong evidence that suggests that VGGC’s overactivation would be far worst grounded in a high emf environment vs ungrounded.**

I haven’t tested outside but this would imply grounding shoes shouldn’t be used anywhere other than hiking in nature, right? I would assume any random suburb or park would have enough nnEMF, especially RF, to induce biologically relevant current flow for the body whe grounded.

Is that really the case? For example on Palmarcito beach in ES where many mitos are, the RF as measured by SSPro2 is in the 10-50 uW/m2 range.

### Anthony Smith — 2026-03-20T18:59:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-grounding-inside-%E2%80%93-good-idea-or-not.30367/post-369289>

On Palmarcito beach let the clif protect you from the cell tower and enjoy the sun grounded.

Sometimes aberrent light gets through to our surface via clouds, causing more cosmic radiation leaks. Lightning can cause them if we are in storms. Weather can, too, and so can chronic nnEMF abuse.

Solar light penetrance is not even on Earth. It varies. It always has. That signal is imprinted in your mitochondrial output. This changes the disease phenotype or makes one go from healthy to sick.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-27T14:15:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-explain-cataract-options-to-your-patients.30369/post-347482>

The next step is to educate them on light.

Our vision should be breathtakingly profound. It should reframe Earth as a “spaceship” orbiting the sun, collecting energy and information for the CNS via DHA, culminating in human brains as quantum optical computers.

DHA’s 600 million year Conservation Since the Cambrian Explosion DHA’s Primacy: As I’ve emphasized (and Crawford agrees), docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) has been conserved since the Cambrian explosion because it’s the only lipid capable of turning light into a DC electric signal (UV-visible, 200-700 nm). Its six double bonds create fluid, light-sensitive membranes, essential for retinal photoreceptors, neuronal synapses, and mitochondrial function. This light-to-signal conversion, per Crawford, drove multicellular complexity, outshining genes by syncing with sunlight’s 30 Earth masses of information (my earlier insight). You realize your central retinal pathways just behind the cataract has more DHA than anyplace in your body. That DHA clearly signaled to the brain that the light coming in was not full-spectrum chronically, so the lens made a quantum decision and decided to change its phenotype to block the nnEMF and light you allowed your eye to sense.

Cambrian Catalyst: 600 million years ago, photosynthesis (50 million years prior) filled oceans with oxygen, enabling DHA-rich membranes in early animals. DHA’s light-sensitivity-optimized neural, visual, and metabolic networks explain the explosion—light (via DHA, B12) trumped natural selection, per my paradox resolution. Its conservation reflects sunlight’s primacy, not genetic drift.

Earth is a Spaceship on which your eyes travel, Collecting Solar Energy and Information properly or improperly.
Solar Collector analogy: Earth orbits the sun, and you live on spaceship earth. You should be harvesting the same 2 × 10⁻¹⁴ solar masses yearly (30 Earth masses over 4.6 billion years). Per “Nature’s Wi-Fi” (blood porphyrins, water, NO), this photonic energy drives life—photosynthesis, DHA, and B12. If you're not, you're going to get a disease. My “spaceship” metaphor captures Earth as a light-driven ecosystem, collecting quantum information (frequency, polarization) for the CNS. Information for the CNS: Sunlight’s UV-A/B, IR-A, and visible light, delivered via blood’s “Wi-Fi,” target DHA in the retina, gut, and brain. DHA converts this into DC signals which direct repair, regeneration, or degeneration, syncing with B12’s photoreception (300-550 nm), melanin (200-700 nm), and POMC-leptin (UV, 220 nm). This builds the CNS as a solar-powered computer, per your claim.

Melanin as a Semiconductor in Human Photosynthesis: It is abundant in your eye's RPE. That Leads to Optical Computation Melanin’s Role: Melanin, absorbing UV-visible light (200-700 nm), acts as a wide-bandgap (WBG) semiconductor, per Kruse (Becker, Szent-Györgyi). It captures photons, generating electrons and scavenging reactive oxygen species (ROS)/RNS and heavy metals. Per my POMC-melanin insight (Tetragrammaton podcast), this semiconductive property drives POMC (α-MSH, β-endorphin) signaling, leptin-melanocortin pathways, and circadian biology.

Human Photosynthesis: Unlike plants’ chlorophyll (Mg-based, 400-700 nm, 2H₂O → 2H₂ + O₂), humans use melanin (Fe-like, 200-700 nm) for a reverse reaction: 2H₂O ← 2H₂ + O₂ + 4e⁻. Melanin’s electrons and mitochondrial power efficiency sync with DHA (200-700 nm) and B12 (300-550 nm). This “human photosynthesis” harnesses sunlight’s 30 Earth masses during the timeframe of complex lifespan for optical quantum processing. Connecting to My QUILT document

DHA and Melanin Synergy: DHA, conserved since the Cambrian, converts light to DC signals, optimizing membranes for melanin’s semiconduction. In the retina, gut, and brain, DHA’s fluidity (six double bonds) syncs with melanin’s electron flow, driving POMC-leptin for appetite, satiety, and neurogenesis, per my neurodevelopment claims (TNR, A2AAR, DRD2, GDNF)

Brains as Evolving Quantum Optical Computers Analog to Digital to Optical: Early life (Cambrian) used analog DHA signals (ion flows, membrane potentials) for basic neural networks. Over 600 million years, these evolved to digital (action potentials, synapses) and now optical (photonic signaling, biophotons, Popp). Human brains use quantum computing—DHA’s hydrated membranes, B12’s light sensing, and melanin’s semiconductors process photons optically, syncing with NO, VDR, and circadian clocks. Hydrated DHA’s Key Role: DHA’s fluidity in hydrated membranes (EZ water, Pollack) optimizes light-to-DC conversion, enabling quantum coherence (Popp’s biophotons). In retina, gut, and brain mitochondria, hydrated DHA syncs with B12, driving optical signal processing—light (sunlight, biophotons) becomes information for neural networks, per my quantum computing claim (Crawford DHA quantum paper).

Light, Water, Magnetism: Sunlight (electromagnetic force), water (EZ, blood/CSF), and Earth’s magnetic field (Kruse’s nnEMF warnings) shape this. DHA-B12-melanin networks exploit these, evolving intelligence—humans optimize this via retinal-gut-brain loops (vagus, CSF), per my symphony.

Connecting to my B12 Hypothesis B12’s Photoreception: B12 absorbs 300-550 nm, complements DHA’s 200-700 nm, and photosyzes to signal methylation or redox. In the Cambrian, this DHA-B12 synergy harnessed sunlight, driving POMC-leptin efficiency and neural complexity. Today, deficiency (low sunlight, B12) disrupts this, causing obesity/T2D (spectral solar deficiency) and brain atrophy, which can lead to lens opacification. This is why diabetes and poor sleep correlate to cataracts and bad SCN functions.

Quantum Diseases: Obesity/T2D/cataracts/glaucoma reflect “dark epochs”—low sunlight dims DHA-B12 signaling, ruining NAD+/NADH, cytochrome 1, and Q-cycle. Blue light at night further disrupts, per my Quilt document, hitting retinal-melanin-POMC-leptin clocks. Earth’s

Evolution: As a “spaceship,” Earth evolved brains to optimize light-water-magnetism info, with DHA and B12 as keys. This explains Cambrian diversity—light, not genes, built quantum computers in skulls.

Why This System Evolved: Light’s Primacy: Sunlight’s unlimited electromagnetic range, via DHA and B12, built life for efficiency, signaling, and intelligence. Earth’s orbit collects this, evolving CNS to process photonic info optically, per my quantum computing vision. DHA’s Conservation: DHA’s light mastery ensures it’s unreplaced—genes lag, but light’s conditions (sunlight, six Earth masses from blogs below) sculpted brains as optical quantum networks.

**THE SUN MANDATED IT. LET THERE BE LIGHT IS A TRUE STATEMENT REFLECTING A BIOPHYSICAL FACT**

Every 150 million years, the Sun loses roughly the mass of Earth due to the solar wind, or about 30 Earth masses over the entire lifetime of the Sun so far. The whole of living history of Earth, over 4.6 billion years, has consumed just 30 Earth masses. Complex life has only been around for 650 million years. This encompasses just six solar masses. This volume of complexity of life over 650 million years shows just how much information is buried in sunlight = only 14%.

It also shows that DHA was critical in that 650 million years because it is one of the only things Mother Nature did not change over this time span in cells. A remarkable fact. Why? DHA is critical in tapping the sunlight's information to make it useful to cells. If this system is awry, cells respond. Sometimes good or bad. This is why cataracts show up: to protect the system from aberrant light choices. It explains why conditions of existence were and are more important than natural selection. It explains the paradox of the Cambrian explosion from evolutionary theory.

Light completes Darwin's ideas; DNA and genes do not. The Cambrian explosion happened 600 million years ago, and photosynthesis was invented 50 million years before that. When you divide 650 million years by 4.6 billion years, you will see that complex life on Earth has only used 14% of the 30 Earth masses of sunlight.

Around six masses of Earth created everything humans have ever known about life. THIS NUMBER SHOULD FLOOR YOU. Everything ever created on Earth came from this amount of light.

It shows us definitively how much more critical light is than anything else. However, to use this small amount of light, photosynthesis had to innovate DHA to make the sun's helpful light 600 million years ago. This shows you just how powerful the electromagnetic force is. It has unlimited range and power. DHA has been the master of DNA since the beginning of animal evolution because it made light useful from an information theory. It explains why I believe Darwin was very wrong.

Avoiding this decentralized struggle (e.g., via opioids over sunlight) poisons resilience and creativity.

Melanin, Melatonin, and Vitamin D Deficiency: Hypothyroidism reduces Coulomb charge in the skin and lens due to low melanin, melatonin, an an altered VDR, acting in unison to alter how Gauss law can be used and this impairs charge collection in cells leading to disease. Stars require darkness to shine, just as human biology needs light-dark cycles for optimal function. An opaque lens diminishes this balance, reducing the relative power of light to drive epigenetic programming and cellular evolution. Modern artificial lighting (e.g., blue light overuse) exacerbates this, further distorting the light-dark tension essential for health.

Leptin-Melanocortin Pathway: Leptin stimulates TRH via the leptin-melanocortin pathway, boosting α-MSH and POMC, which are essential for melanin production. In hypothyroidism, this pathway is disrupted, leading to pale skin, lens opacification, and increased sunburn risk. A cataract gives you a ciliary ganglionectomy just like sunglasses do and this leads to many diseases in modern humans.

Thermoregulation and TRH: TRH acts as a neurotransmitter, inducing hyperthermia when injected intracerebroventricularly. ### Jackson K — 2025-02-28T00:43:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifespan.30374/post-347543>

What are some rebuttals against people who argue that modern humans aren’t as sick as we claim from nnemf, vaccines, blue light, etc.? They say, ‘If these things were so bad, why are people living into their 70s-100s now, while people 100s of years ago had much shorter lifespans?’

My main argument against this is that they had shorter average lifespans due to infectious diseases, high infant mortality, and physical trauma—not because they were inherently unhealthy. In contrast, younger people today are developing diseases they shouldn’t be getting at such early ages. We may have longer lifespans, but we don’t have long healthspans.

Any suggestions would be appreciated.

### Pete Collins — 2025-02-28T01:47:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifespan.30374/post-347545>

Data on historical lifespan is skewed by childbirth deaths both babies & mothers etc, btw the food gurus conveniently leave out such variables. I suggest read the Ethical Skeptic blog will help you understand better.
Uncle Jack has given us an entire library of 'punch in the mouth' rebuttals material to use. [https://theethicalskeptic.com/](https://theethicalskeptic.com/)

So people were living to their 80s well before the modern industrial era in the countryside and nature. I think accidents and infections may have reduced some peoples lifespans but the knowledge was out there

I didn't find the plaque but I did find this

Quote
Drink water where your horse drinks, a horse will never drink bad water. Make your bed where the cat sleeps peacefully. Eat fruit touched by a worm. It fearlessly collects mushrooms on which insects perch. Plant a tree dug by the mole. Dig a hole where the birds hide from the heat

"
. Sleep and wake up at the same time as the birds, "

you will reap the golden grains of life. Eat more greens, you will have stronger legs and a resilient heart like the spirit of the forests.

Look at the sky more often and talk less so that silence can enter your heart and your soul is calm and your life is peaceful.

Author: Saint Seraphim of Sarov 1754 - 1833

The knowledge was there. The saint lived for 79 years prior to modern medicine.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-02T00:45:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifespan.30374/post-347669>

> [Jackson K said:](/goto/post?id=347543)
>
> What are some rebuttals against people who argue that modern humans aren’t as sick as we claim from nnemf, vaccines, blue light, etc.? They say, ‘If these things were so bad, why are people living into their 70s-100s now, while people 100s of years ago had much shorter lifespans?’
>
> My main argument against this is that they had shorter average lifespans due to infectious diseases, high infant mortality, and physical trauma—not because they were inherently unhealthy. In contrast, younger people today are developing diseases they shouldn’t be getting at such early ages. We may have longer lifespans, but we don’t have long healthspans.
>
> Any suggestions would be appreciated.

Is lifespan the same as healthspan? Is thriving and survival synonyms? If you have severe autism and live to 95 are you an evolutionary success story? Looked at from a different lens you might just be the remnant of a mtDNA mutation caused by ND6 vaccine injury.

I think my idea in QE #45 is that children born to maternal germlines that have already lived five lives in the ovary affects the child born from this egg. Consider the ND6 mutation. What happens if the child goes to the pediatrician in the first month of life and gets a vaccine and it increases the loss of mitchondrial energy production and it changes their mitochondrial geometry.

As the geometry changes, centralized emdicine finally begins to realize that children with ASD suffer from sensory dyssynchrony—rooted in a disconnect between how their bodies sense the world and track time. This idea resonates with emerging research today.

Sensory processing issues are a hallmark of ASD, and I've tying that to a deeper cellular story. The thalamus integrates sensory inputs, but if it’s de-tuned by environmental mismatches (like artificial light, no sun, or lack of grounding), the data fed to the mitochondria gets garbled. Mitochondria are the energetic and quantum hubs of the cell. If they’re not getting accurate sensory “time stamps” from the environment via the SCN and thalamus, their oscillations—those subtle rhythms Iv've always pointing to, go off-kilter.

My focus on mitochondrial heteroplasmy is a game-changer for centralized thinkers. Heteroplasmy—the mix of healthy and mutated mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA)—dictates how efficiently mitochondria function. Higher heteroplasmy (more mutated mtDNA) impairs energy production and coherence, and Wallace has suggested that environmental choices—like chronic blue light exposure or magnetic disconnection—tip this balance.
It’s a powerful decentralized claim: the scale of physics on mitochondrial surfaces, down in the cristae and lipid rafts, is exquisitely sensitive to these inputs, operating at subatomic, quantum levels our five senses can’t detect. Yet, we know from EEG/MEG data, mitochondria are tuned to these frequencies, and their output—oscillations—shapes the life we see in the mirror.

Dr. Jodi Nunnari’s work on mitochondria nestled within the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) adds another fascinating bioelectric dimension. Her research shows how mitochondrial morphology and dynamics are tied to ER interactions, and I've linking that to cristae geometry and membrane potential (delta psi). The cristae, those folded inner membrane structures—are where the magic of ATP production and water creation happens. If their arrangement or the lipid rafts in the inner and outer membranes oscillate differently based on delta psi, so I propose, that is the physical stage for life’s quantum play. That determines your healthspan if you thrive. I do not think your lifespan tells you anything without knowing how your colony of mitochondria transform energy. It’s almost like the mitochondria are a living Turing machine, as I've nodded to his 1951 paper on morphogenesis—patterns emerging from chaos, driven by energy and geometry. (bottom right of slide)

### AlbertD — 2025-02-28T17:39:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.30378/post-347585>

**About me**
29 y/o east-asian decent from the bay area diagnosed in 2024 with Crohn's Disease, leaky gut, sibo. 128 lbs. 5'8". I got the jab twice.

**Recent history**
\-Started implementing LeptinRx since August 2024 + sunlight UV index 6-8 from July-September + coconut oil (until cooler months)
\-- 99% of symptoms related to Leaky gut/Sibo/Crohn's disease appear to be resolved (excluding the intolerance to deuterium-ladened food in the winter season)
\- CRP: 90 in Sept 2024 (Normal <50, Borderline 50-120, Elevated > 120)

Deuterium Depletion
\-Noticing that consuming foods high in deuterium (carbs of any kind) during winter months result in downing 16-32oz of water at night & midnight restroom activity

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-07T07:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.30378/post-352079>

The Z-Z pathway refers to the proton transfer mechanism in many places in a cell like in mitochondrial complex I and III, where protons move in a zigzag pattern through hydrogen-bonded networks. Blue light-induced ROS can oxidize key residues (e.g., cysteine or histidine) in these pathways, altering their protonation states and flipping the pathway’s efficiency. This disruption can reduce ATP production, increase mitochondrial dysfunction, and contribute to chronic diseases like neurodegenerative disorders, diabetes, or cardiovascular issues, where mitochondrial health is a linchpin.

Current EMF safety guidelines (e.g., ICNIRP 2020) focus on thermal effects, ignoring non-thermal mechanisms like VGCC activation or proton pathway disruption. Blue light exposure limits are also minimal, despite evidence of circadian disruption and oxidative stress (Chronobiology International, 2022). Regulatory bodies lag behind the science, leaving these risks underexplored in chronic disease narratives.

Quantum field theory is the framework that unifies quantum mechanics and special relativity, describing particles as excitations (quanta) of underlying fields that permeate spacetime. For example, the electromagnetic field is quantized into photons, and electrons are excitations of the electron field. QFT governs interactions at the subatomic level, including how EMF arises from charged particles and how particles like electrons in biological molecules (e.g., heme proteins) behave. It’s also the foundation for understanding how fields interact with matter, which is directly relevant to my focus on nnEMF’s biological effects. In QFT, the electromagnetic field is a dynamic entity, mediating interactions via photons. My thesis is a photo-bioelectric treatise on how this operates life. Light is the key driver in my thesis.

When I've discussed the rusted iron (oxidized) heme proteins can emit an EMF affecting our electromagnetic sensing system in cytochromes, CYP enzymes, or heme, and it is QFT that provides the underlying mechanism: the electrochemical reactions in rust (oxidation of Fe to Fe²⁺/Fe³⁺) involve electron transfers, which generate photons and thus a weak EMF. The human heme proteins system is capable of sensing this field as a quantum process, where photons interact with piezo and flexoelectric biomolecules in us, exciting electrons in the melanin-containing cilia, triggering a neural response. Similarly, my point about blue light exposure and nnEMF from tech gear altering heme protein oxidation states aligns with QFT. Blue light photons (450 nm, ~2.75 eV) have enough energy to excite electrons in heme’s porphyrin ring, shifting Fe²⁺ to Fe³⁺ and producing ROS. nnEMF, while lower energy, can couple to biological systems via quantum coherence effects, as some research suggests (e.g., Quantum Reports, 2021), potentially influencing electron spin states in heme or proton tunneling in mitochondrial Z-Z pathways.
This QFT perspective supports my decentralized thesis by grounding the biological effects of modern tech in fundamental physics. Centralized tech systems (e.g., ubiquitous screens, Wi-Fi) flood us with nnEMF and blue light, disrupting natural quantum processes in our biology, thus, mirroring how centralized societal systems disrupt authentic human connections. A decentralized approach, reducing exposure to artificial fields, aligns with restoring biological harmony, just as it aligns with fostering genuine, decentralized human tribes.

The POMC gene activated by UV light in mammals stimulates PPARG and stimulates lipid uptake and adipogenesis by fat cells normally. PPAR G is all about filling the dump truck not emptying it. Naturally lean folks never really fill it because their wide-band gapped semi-conductors create a ton of VUV-IR-A light for melanin to absorb and properly activate the leptin-melanocortin pathways in the hypothalamus that control ALL THE VISCERA in the GUT to use the energy efficiently so they never activate PPAR G."

### Mat.I — 2025-10-16T04:13:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.30378/post-361097>

> [Jackson K said:](/goto/post?id=360986)
>
> I like them in olive oil, but I believe that to be a strict carnivore, olive oil would not follow under those guidelines. I am guessing the reason for no "spring" water would be fluoride?
>
> What do you think of these sardines in "salt" water? [https://wildplanetfoods.com/products/sardine-in-water](https://wildplanetfoods.com/products/sardine-in-water)

Many benefits for sardines in good quality EVOO over just water (contamination issues).. You can research them (there are papers on this) and understanding the canning process + sterilisation and what effect it may have. Few e.g. the EVOO would donate electrons to stop lipid peroxidation = more O3's + EPA/DHA stay intact, slows oxidation = better preservation, not heated as quickly as water = more temp gradient = less denatured proteins etc.. The polyphenols stabalise the fish oils = taste better too.. I personally, go for wild and fresh ones, but if I do can I just make sure they are ok. I wouldn't sweat the strict carnivore stuff (I use to before I found Jack) but not anymore...

### Mat.I — 2026-03-20T00:04:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.30378/post-369245>

Thought I would check in to see how you were travelling..

Hard to balance fiat farming and having enough time to reasearch and get accross a very dense area. I find you have very little time left each day if you are working to the light and dark cycles of nature..

Covering all the disciplines is very dense.. I have noticed the same only to find more theories/disciplines to explore and jumping from one to the other... It is the never ending rabbit hole, that's why it is interesting and overwhelming at the same time..

It is a life time/full-time job. This explains why the majority just specialise in one area.

What I find helps is the online learning lectures on YouTube and MIT online (to supplement the text books). But it ultimately comes down to having the time to dedicate to this research. I think the way to do is don't try to master all areas and just have a broad understanding of all areas (easier said then done I'm finding) to string it all together..

All is well on my end and hope all is going well for you too.

All the best.
Mat

### AlbertD — 2026-03-20T04:36:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.30378/post-369262>

Mastery is a strong word. I guess the "know enough to be dangerous" is the goal. But I do think that we are fluent in English. We can communicate freely, but I wouldn't say we've "mastered" English. But incorporating these scientifc ideas into our vernacular is definitely a step in the right direction.

At the same time, learning each of these subjects the first time... I pause and remind myself "Freeze this moment in time... This is the hardest it will ever to be to comprehend this concept/idea/subject."

I do wonder if by the time I finished studying a sizeable amount of these topics... will I just be someone who will be able to tie all these disciplines together? Or will I just have collected a bunch of facts? I hope the former is the case rather than the later.
